Task instruction sent to agent 
# Task: Kubernetes Server-Side Apply Configuration Guide

**Repository:** kubernetes/kubernetes (stripped snapshot)
**Output:** Write your document to `/workspace/documentation.md`

## Objective

Produce a deep guide for applyconfigurations and Server-Side Apply semantics, including conflict handling and workflow patterns.

## Scope

Focus on the applyconfigurations and Server-Side Apply subsystem.
Your document must explain component responsibilities, end-to-end flow, and extension or operational tradeoffs.

## Content Expectations

Address all of the following in your own structure:
- subsystem purpose, boundaries, and upstream/downstream dependencies
- key components and how responsibilities are split
- end-to-end control/request flow with concrete repository evidence
- failure modes, limits, and design tradeoffs
- extension points, customization hooks, and associated risks
- a concise map of the most important files/directories used in your analysis

## Quality Bar

- Cite concrete file paths from the repository.
- Explain behavior and interactions, not just API signatures.
- Include at least one section on operational implications (performance, reliability, or maintainability).
- Do not fabricate classes, functions, or files.

## Anti-Requirements

- Do not output `doc.go`.
- Do not provide shallow bullet dumps without system flow explanation.
- Do not rely on external docs not present in the workspace.

    32→// DefaultMaxUpdateManagers defines the default maximum retained number of managedFields entries from updates
    33→// if the number of update managers exceeds this, the oldest entries will be merged until the number is below the maximum.
    34→// TODO(jennybuckley): Determine if this is really the best value. Ideally we wouldn't unnecessarily merge too many entries.
    35→const DefaultMaxUpdateManagers int = 10
    36→
    37→// DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability defines the default probability that the field management of an object
    38→// starts being tracked from the object's creation, instead of from the first time the object is applied to.
    39→const DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability float32 = 1

    24→
    25→// Managed groups a fieldpath.ManagedFields together with the timestamps associated with each operation.
    26→type Managed interface {
    27→	// Fields gets the fieldpath.ManagedFields.
    28→	Fields() fieldpath.ManagedFields
    29→
    30→	// Times gets the timestamps associated with each operation.
    31→	Times() map[string]*metav1.Time
    32→}
    33→
    34→// Manager updates the managed fields and merges applied configurations.
    35→type Manager interface {
    36→	// Update is used when the object has already been merged (non-apply
    37→	// use-case), and simply updates the managed fields in the output
    38→	// object.
    39→	//  * `liveObj` is not mutated by this function
    40→	//  * `newObj` may be mutated by this function
    41→	// Returns the new object with managedFields removed, and the object's new
    42→	// proposed managedFields separately.
    43

   466→	newObj = p.fieldManager.UpdateNoErrors(currentObject, newObj, managerOrUserAgent(p.options.FieldManager, p.userAgent))

   470→func (p *smpPatcher) createNewObject(_ context.Context) (runtime.Object, error) {
   471→	return nil, errors.NewNotFound(p.resource.GroupResource(), p.name)

   474→func newApplyPatcher(p *patcher, fieldManager *managedfields.FieldManager, unmarshalFn, unmarshalStrictFn func([]byte, interface{}) error) *applyPatcher {

   479→		creater:             p.creater,

   485→	}

    28→type capManagersManager struct {
    29→	fieldManager          Manager
    30→	maxUpdateManagers     int
    31→	oldUpdatesManagerName string
    32→}
    33→
    34→var _ Manager = &capManagersManager{}
    35→
    36→// NewCapManagersManager creates a new wrapped FieldManager which ensures that the number of managers from updates
    37→// does not exceed maxUpdateManagers, by merging some of the oldest entries on each update.
    38→func NewCapManagersManager(fieldManager Manager, maxUpdateManagers int) Manager {
    39→	return &capManagersManager{
    40→		fieldManager:          fieldManager,
    41→		maxUpdateManagers:     maxUpdateManagers,
    42→		oldUpdatesManagerName: "ancient-changes",
    43→	}
    44→}

    47→func (f *capManagersManager) Update(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, managed Ma

    31→
    32→// ExtractInto extracts the applied configuration state from object for fieldManager
    33→// into applyConfiguration. If no managed fields are found for the given fieldManager,
    34→// no error is returned, but applyConfiguration is left unpopulated. It is possible
    35→// that no managed fields were found for the fieldManager because other field managers
    36→// have taken ownership of all the fields previously owned by the fieldManager. It is
    37→// also possible the fieldManager never owned fields.
    38→//
    39→// The provided object MUST bo a root resource object since subresource objects
    40→// do not contain their own managed fields. For example, an aut
... (truncated)

     1→/*

    17→package merge
    18→
    19→import (
    20→	"fmt"
    21→	"sort"
    22→	"strings"
    23→
    24→	"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath"
    25→)
    26→
    27→// Conflict is a conflict on a specific field with the current manager of
    28→// that field. It does implement the error interface so that it can be
    29→// used as an error.
    30→type Conflict struct {
    31→	Manager string
    32→	Path    fieldpath.Path
    33→}
    34→
    35→// Conflict is an error.
    36→var _ error = Conflict{}
    37→
    38→// Error formats the conflict as an error.
    39→func (c Conflict) Error() string {
    40→	return fmt.Sprintf("conflict with %q: %v", c.Manager, c.Path)
    41→}
    42→
    43→// Equals returns true if c == c2
    44→func (c Conflict) Equals(c2 Conflict) bool {
    45→	if c.Manager != c2.Manager {
    46→		return false
    47→	}
    48→	return c.Path.Equals(c2.Path)
    49→}
    50→
    51→// Conflicts accumulates multiple conflicts and aggregates them by managers.
    52→type Conflicts []Conflict
    53→
    54→var _ error = Conflicts{}
    55→
    56→// Error print
... (truncated)

    60→type Updater struct {
    61→	// Deprecated: This will eventually become private.
    62→	Converter Converter
    63→
    64→	// Deprecated: This will eventually become private.
    65→	IgnoredFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set
    66→
    67→	// Deprecated: This will eventually become private.
    68→	IgnoreFilter map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter
    69→
    70→	returnInputOnNoop bool
    71→}
    72→
    73→func (s *Updater) update(oldObject, newObject *typed.TypedValue, version fieldpath.APIVersion, managers fieldpath.ManagedFields, workflow string, force bool) (fieldpath.ManagedFields, *typed.Comparison, error) {
    74→	conflicts := fieldpath.ManagedFields{}
    75→	removed := fieldpath.ManagedFields{}
    76→	compare, err := oldObject.Compare(newObject)
    77→	if err != nil {
    78→		return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to compare objects: %v", err)
    79→	}
    80→
    81→	var versions map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*typed.Comparison
    82→
    83→	if s.IgnoredFields != nil && s.IgnoreFilter != nil {
    84→		return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("IgnoreFilter and IgnoreFilter may not both be set")
    85→	}
    86→	if s.IgnoredFields != nil {
    87→		versions = map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*typed.Comparison{
    88→			version: compare.ExcludeFields(s.IgnoredFields[version]),
    89→		}
    90→	} else {
    91→		versions = map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*typed.Comparison{
    92→			version: compare.FilterFields(s.IgnoreFilter[version]),
    93→		}
    94→	}
    95→
    96→	for manager, managerSet := range managers {
    97→		if manager == workflow {
    98→			continue
    99→		}
   100→		compare, ok := versions[managerSet.APIVersion()]
   101→		if !ok {
   102→			var err error
   103→			versionedOldObject, err := s.Converter.Convert(oldObject, managerSet.AP
... (truncated)

44-assistanttext-
The documentation has been written to `/workspace/documentation.md`. Here is a summary of what it covers:

**Structure:** Nine sections covering every aspect of the SSA and applyconfigurations subsystem.

**Key content by section:**

1. **Purpose and Boundaries** — explains what SSA solves over client-side apply (field ownership, conflict surfacing), maps the three layers (client library, HTTP handler, field management core), and identifies upstream/downstream dependencies.

2. **Key Components** — traces the `FieldManager` public facade, the nine-layer `Manager` decorator chain (`structuredMergeManager` → `StripMetaManager` → `ManagedFieldsUpdater` → `BuildManagerInfoManager` → `CapManagersManager` → `ProbabilisticSkipNonApplied` → `LastAppliedManager` → `LastAppliedUpdater` → `VersionCheckManager`), the SMD `Updater` type, the HTTP `PatchResource` handler, the `ManagedFieldsEntry` wire format, and admission integration.

3. **End-to-End Flow** — eight concrete steps from client payload construction through `GuaranteedUpdate` → `applyPatcher` → `FieldManager.Apply` → `structuredMergeManager.Apply` → `merge.Updater.Apply` → storage, with exact function signatures and file:line references.

4. **Conflict Detection** — the exact algorithm in `merge.Updater.update` showing the intersection logic (`managerSet.Set().Intersection(compare.Modified.Union(compare.Added))`), how `force` strips ownership, the HTTP 409 format, and the `lastAppliedManager` CSA migration bypass.

5. **ApplyConfigurations Client Library** — pointer-field design rationale, `ExtractInto` mechanics, read-modify-apply pattern, and code generation pipeline.

6. **Failure Modes and Tradeoffs** — conflict storms, managedFields size cap (`DefaultMaxUpdateManagers=10`), version conversion silent drops, force-a
... (truncated)
45-systemtask_notification-
46-systeminit-
47-assistanttext-
The document has been written to `/workspace/documentation.md`. Both background research agents completed successfully and their findings are fully incorporated into the guide.

The document covers all required areas with concrete file citations:

- **Subsystem purpose and boundaries**: explains the three-layer architecture (client library → HTTP handler → field management core) and the upstream dependency on `sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6`
- **Component responsibilities**: the nine-layer `Manager` decorator chain is traced from `structuredMergeManager` through every wrapper up to `VersionCheckManager`, with each layer's role explained
- **End-to-end flow**: eight concrete steps from client payload construction through `GuaranteedUpdate` → `applyPatcher.applyPatchToCurrentObject` → `FieldManager.Apply` → `structuredMergeManager.Apply` → `merge.Updater.Apply` → etcd, with file:line references throughout
- **Conflict detection**: the exact intersection algorithm in `vendor/.../merge/update.go:73-160`, how `force` strips ownership from competing managers, and the `LastAppliedManager` CSA migration bypass
- **ApplyConfigurations client library**: pointer-field design, `ExtractInto` mechanics for read-modify-apply patterns, and the code generation pipeline
- **Failure modes**: conflict storms, the 10-manager update cap, silent version-deletion drops, force oscillation, noop timestamp noise, and the managedFields reset escape hatch
- **Extension points**: `TypeConverter`, `resetFields`/`IgnoreFilter`, subresource isolation, CRD vs built-in divergence, and admission constraints
- **Operational implications**: write pressure from noop applies and the `IgnoreManagedFieldsTimestampsTransformer` optimization, controller retry patterns, `UpdateNoErrors` graceful degradation,
... (truncated)

{
  "description": "Explore applyconfigurations subsystem",
  "prompt": "Explore the Kubernetes applyconfigurations and Server-Side Apply subsystem in /workspace. I need a thorough understanding of:\n\n1. The directory structure of staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/applyconfigurations/ - what's in there, what are the key files\n2. The Server-Side Apply implementation in the API machinery - look in pkg/registry/, staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/, staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/\n3. The field manager / field ownership tracking - look for fieldmanager, managedfields related code\n4. How apply configurations are generated - look for generator scripts or code generation tooling\n5. The structured merge patch / SMD (structural merge diff) implementation\n6. How conflicts are detected and handled\n7. Key interfaces and types\n\nPlease read actual file contents (not just list them) for the most important files. Focus on:\n- staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/applyconfigurations/ (key Go files, not doc.go)\n- staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/ (apply handler)  \n- staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/ (apply logic)\n- Any fieldmanager package\n- structured-merge-diff related imports/usage\n\nGive me concrete file paths, function names, type names, and explain what each component does. Read enough file content to understand the actual implementation.",
  "run_in_background": true,
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}

16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package managedfields\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal\\\"\\n    28\u2192)\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192// FieldManager updates the managed fields and merges applied\\n    31\u2192// configurations.\\n    32\u2192type FieldManager = internal.FieldManager\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192// NewDefaultFieldManager creates a new FieldManager that merges apply requests\\n    35\u2192// and update managed fields for other types of requests.\\n    36\u2192func NewDefaultFieldManager(typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter, objectCreater runtime.ObjectCreater, kind schema.GroupVersionKind, hub schema.GroupVersion, subresource string, resetFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter) (*FieldManager, error) {\\n    37\u2192\\tf, err := internal.NewStructuredMergeManager(typeConverter, objectConverter, objectDefaulter, kind.GroupVersion(), hub, resetFields)\\n    38\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    39\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create field manager: %v\\\", err)\\n    40\u2192\\t}\\n    41\u2192\\treturn internal.NewDefaultFieldManager(f, typeConverter, objectConverter, objectCreater, kind, subresource), nil\\n    42\u2192}\\n    43\u2192\\n    44\u2192// NewDefaultCRDFieldManager creates a new FieldManager specifically for\\n    45\u2192// CRDs. This allows for the possibility of fields which are not defined\\n    46\u2192// in models, as well as having no models defined at all.\\n    47\u2192func NewDefaultCRDFieldManager(typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter, objectCreater runtime.ObjectCreater, kind schema.GroupVersionKind, hub schema.GroupVersion, subresource string, resetFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter) (_ *FieldManager, err error) {\\n    48\u2192\\tf, err := internal.NewCRDStructuredMergeManager(typeConverter, objectConverter, objectDefaulter, kind.GroupVersion(), hub, resetFields)\\n    49\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    50\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create field manager: %v\\\", err)\\n    51\u2192\\t}\\n    52\u2192\\treturn internal.NewDefaultFieldManager(f, typeConverter, objectConverter, objectCreater, kind, subresource), nil\\n    53\u2192}\\n    54\u2192\\n    55\u2192func ValidateManagedFields(encodedManagedFields []metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry) error {\\n    56\u2192\\t_, err := internal.DecodeManagedFields(encodedManagedFields)\\n    57\u2192\\treturn err\\n    58\u2192}\\n    59\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"c4ad3b40-4b71-4b36-9988-6c871c9e7519\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:47.447Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"9acef929-2806-4fe8-9026-fd2f6755d89a\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"c4ad3b40-4b71-4b36-9988-6c871c9e7519\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_015nscBd8kYNTJeaQEmynNVA\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01ABeYDVzuV5ZQXbPFL1eFfx\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go\",\"limit\":150},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":2851,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":16405,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":2851},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG56HQy6iHWK81juCVSz\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"6e5f6774-7122-41c7-9e70-b862fb6f5323\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:47.785Z\"}\n",

    1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2018 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this 16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package managedfields\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal\\\"\\n    28\u2192)\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192// FieldManager updates the managed fields and merges applied\\n    31\u2192// configurations.\\n    32\u2192type FieldManager = internal.FieldManager\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192// NewDefaultFieldManager creates a new FieldManager that merges apply requests\\n    35\u2192// and update managed fields for other types of requests.\\n    36\u2192func NewDefaultFieldManager(typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter, objectCreater runtime.ObjectCreater, kind schema.GroupVersionKind, hub schema.GroupVersion, subresource string, resetFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter) (*FieldManager, error) {\\n    37\u2192\\tf, err := internal.NewStructuredMergeManager(typeConverter, objectConverter, objectDefaulter, kind.GroupVersion(), hub, resetFields)\\n    38\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    39\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create field manager: %v\\\", err)\\n    40\u2192\\t}\\n    41\u2192\\treturn internal.NewDefaultFieldManager(f, typeConverter, objectConverter, objectCreater, kind, subresource), nil\\n    42\u2192}\\n    43\u2192\\n    44\u2192// NewDefaultCRDFieldManager creates a new FieldManager specifically for\\n    45\u2192// CRDs. This allows for the possibility of fields which are not defined\\n    46\u2192// in models, as well as having no models defined at all.\\n    47\u2192func NewDefaultCRDFieldManager(typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter, objectCreater runtime.ObjectCreater, kind schema.GroupVersionKind, hub schema.GroupVersion, subresource string, resetFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter) (_ *FieldManager, err error) {\\n    48\u2192\\tf, err := internal.NewCRDStructuredMergeManager(typeConverter, objectConverter, objectDefaulter, kind.GroupVersion(), hub, resetFields)\\n    49\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    50\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create field manager: %v\\\", err)\\n    51\u2192\\t}\\n    52\u2192\\treturn internal.NewDefaultFieldManager(f, typeConverter, objectConverter, objectCreater, kind, subresource), nil\\n    53\u2192}\\n    54\u2192\\n    55\u2192func ValidateManagedFields(encodedManagedFields []metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry) error {\\n    56\u2192\\t_, err := internal.DecodeManagedFields(encodedManagedFields)\\n    57\u2192\\treturn err\\n    58\u2192}\\n    59\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. I need detailed understanding of:\\n\\n1. Find and read the fieldmanager package - likely at staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/fieldmanager/ or similar location\\n2. How managedFields are stored and tracked in ObjectMeta\\n3. The conflict detection algorithm - what constitutes a conflict, how it's detected\\n4. The \\\"force\\\" flag behavior for conflicts\\n5. The structured-merge-diff library usage - find imports of \\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff\\\"\\n6. How apply differs from update semantics\\n7. Manager identity / field manager name\\n8. The Apply endpoint handler - find where PATCH with apply content type is handled\\n\\nPlease actually READ the content of key files. Look at:\\n- Any file with \\\"fieldmanager\\\" in its name or path\\n- staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go or similar\\n- The types for ManagedFieldsEntry in staging/src/k8s.io/api/meta/v1/types.go\\n- Any \\\"apply.go\\\" files in the apiserver\\n\\nGive me concrete function signatures, type definitions, and explain the flow of an Apply request from the HTTP endpoint through field management to storage.\"},\"uuid\":\"fa7300de-8d20-4494-bb6c-a86d93b936a4\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:45.470Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"fa7300de-8d20-4494-bb6c-a86d93b936a4\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01N8f3XLBA3yuQ8wSFDGpqVM\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"I'll conduct a thorough exploration of the Server-Side Apply field manager and conflict handling in the Kubernetes repository.     "prompt": "Explore the Server-Side Apply field manager and conflict handling in the Kubernetes repository at /workspace. I need detailed understanding of:\n\n1. Find and read the fieldmanager package - likely at staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/fieldmanager/ or similar location\n2. How managedFields are stored and tracked in ObjectMeta\n3. The conflict detection algorithm - what constitutes a conflict, how it's detected\n4. The \"force\" flag behavior for conflicts\n5. The structured-merge-diff library usage - find imports of \"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff\"\n6. How apply differs from update semantics\n7. Manager identity / field manager name\n8. The Apply endpoint handler - find where PATCH with apply content type is handled\n\nPlease actually READ the content of key files. Look at:\n- Any file with \"fieldmanager\" in its name or path\n- staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go or similar\n- The types for ManagedFieldsEntry in staging/src/k8s.io/api/meta/v1/types.go\n- Any \"apply.go\" files in the apiserver\n\nGive me concrete function signatures, type definitions, and explain the flow of an Apply request from the HTTP endpoint through field management to storage.",

22\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal\\\"\\n    28\u2192)\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192// FieldManager updates the managed fields and merges applied\\n    31\u2192// configurations.\\n    32\u2192type FieldManager = internal.FieldManager\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192// NewDefaultFieldManager creates a new FieldManager that merges apply requests\\n    35\u2192// and update managed fields for other types of requests.\\n    36\u2192func NewDefaultFieldManager(typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter, objectCreater runtime.ObjectCreater, kind schema.GroupVersionKind, hub schema.GroupVersion, subresource string, resetFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter) (*FieldManager, error) {\\n    37\u2192\\tf, err := internal.NewStructuredMergeManager(typeConverter, objectConverter, objectDefaulter, kind.GroupVersion(), hub, resetFields)\\n    38\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    39\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create field manager: %v\\\", err)\\n    40\u2192\\t}\\n    41\u2192\\treturn internal.NewDefaultFieldManager(f, typeConverter, objectConverter, objectCreater, kind, subresource), nil\\n    42\u2192}\\n    43\u2192\\n    44\u2192// NewDefaultCRDFieldManager creates a new FieldManager specifically for\\n    45\u2192// CRDs. This allows for the possibility of fields which are not defined\\n    46\u2192// in models, as well as having no models defined at all.\\n    47\u2192func NewDefaultCRDFieldManager(typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter, objectCreater runtime.ObjectCreater, kind schema.GroupVersionKind, hub schema.GroupVersion, subresource string, resetFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter) (_ *FieldManager, err error) {\\n    48\u2192\\tf, err := internal.NewCRDStructuredMergeManager(typeConverter, objectConverter, objectDefaulter, kind.GroupVersion(), hub, resetFields)\\n    49\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    50\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create field manager: %v\\\", err)\\n    51\u2192\\t}\\n    52\u2192\\treturn internal.NewDefaultFieldManager(f, typeConverter, objectConverter, objectCreater, kind, subresource), nil\\n    53\u2192}\\n    54\u2192\\n    55\u2192func ValidateManagedFields(encodedManagedFields []metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry) error {\\n    56\u2192\\t_, err := internal.DecodeManagedFields(encodedManagedFields)\\n    57\u2192\\treturn err\\n    58\u2192}\\n    59\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package handlers\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\tjsonpatch \\\"gopkg.in/evanphx/json-patch.v4\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\tkjson \\\"sigs.k8s.io/json\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\tmetainternalversionscheme \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion/scheme\\\"\\n    33\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured\\\"\\n    35\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/validation\\\"\\n    36\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    37\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n    38\u2192\\tcbor \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer/cbor/direct\\\"\\n    39\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\\\"\\n    40\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\\\"\\n    41\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/mergepatch\\\"\\n    42\u2192\\tutilruntime \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\\\"\\n    43\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n    44\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/strategicpatch\\\"\\n    45\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation/field\\\"\\n    46\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/yaml\\\"\\n    47\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\\\"\\n    48\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\\\"\\n    49\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\\\"\\n    50\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/fieldmanager\\\"\\n    51\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/finisher\\\"\\n    52\u2192\\trequestmetrics \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/metrics\\\"\\n    53\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/negotiation\\\"\\n    54\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\\\"\\n    55\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\\\"\\n    56\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\\\"\\n    57\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/dryrun\\\"\\n    58\u2192\\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n    59\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\\\"\\n    60\u2192)\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192const (\\n    63\u2192\\t// maximum number of operations a single json patch may contain.\\n    64\u2192\\tmaxJSONPatchOperations = 10000\\n    65\u2192)\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192// PatchResource returns a function that will handle a resource patch.\\n    68\u2192func PatchResource(r rest.Patcher, scope *RequestScope, admit admission.Interface, patchTypes []string) http.HandlerFunc {\\n    69\u2192\\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n    70\u2192\\t\\tctx := req.Context()\\n    71\u2192\\t\\t// For performance tracking purposes.\\n    72\u2192\\t\\tctx, span := tracing.Start(ctx, \\\"Patch\\\", traceFields(req)...)\\n    73\u2192\\t\\treq = req.WithContext(ctx)\\n    74\u2192\\t\\tdefer span.End(500 * time.Millisecond)\\n    75\u2192\\n    76\u2192\\t\\t// Do this first, otherwise name extraction can fail for unrecognized content types\\n    77\u2192\\t\\t// TODO: handle this in negotiation\\n    78\u2192\\t\\tcontentType := req.Header.Get(\\\"Content-Type\\\")\\n    79\u2192\\t\\t// Remove \\\"; charset=\\\" if included in header.\\n    80\u2192\\t\\tif idx := strings.Index(contentType, \\\";\\\"); idx > 0 {\\n    81\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontentType = contentType[:idx]\\n    82\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    83\u2192\\t\\tpatchType := types.PatchType(contentType)\\n    84\u2192\\n    85\u2192\\t\\t// Ensure the patchType is one we support\\n    86\u2192\\t\\tif !sets.NewString(patchTypes...).Has(contentType) {\\n    87\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.err(negotiation.NewUnsupportedMediaTypeError(patchTypes), w, req)\\n    88\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n    89\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    90\u2192\\n    91\u2192\\t\\tnamespace, name, err := scope.Namer.Name(req)\\n    92\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n    93\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n    94\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n    95\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    96\u2192\\n    97\u2192\\t\\t// enforce a timeout of at most requestTimeoutUpperBound (34s) or less if the user-provided\\n    98\u2192\\t\\t// timeout inside the parent context is lower than requestTimeoutUpperBound.\\n    99\u2192\\t\\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, requestTimeoutUpperBound)\\n   100\u2192\\t\\tdefer cancel()\\n   101\u2192\\n   102\u2192\\t\\tctx = request.WithNamespace(ctx, namespace)\\n   103\u2192\\n   104\u2192\\t\\toutputMediaType, _, err := negotiation.NegotiateOutputMediaType(req, scope.Serializer, scope)\\n   105\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   106\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n   107\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   108\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   109\u2192\\n   110\u2192\\t\\tpatchBytes, err := limitedReadBodyWithRecordMetric(ctx, req, scope.MaxRequestBodyBytes, scope.Resource.GroupResource(), requestmetrics.Patch)\\n   111\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   112\u2192\\t\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"limitedReadBody failed\\\", attribute.Int(\\\"len\\\", len(patchBytes)), attribute.String(\\\"err\\\", err.Error()))\\n   113\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n   114\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   115\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   116\u2192\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"limitedReadBody succeeded\\\", attribute.Int(\\\"len\\\", len(patchBytes)))\\n   117\u2192\\n   118\u2192\\t\\toptions := &metav1.PatchOptions{}\\n   119\u2192\\t\\tif err := metainternalversionscheme.ParameterCodec.DecodeParameters(req.URL.Query(), scope.MetaGroupVersion, options); err != nil {\\n   120\u2192\\t\\t\\terr = errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\\n   121\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n   122\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   123\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   124\u2192\\t\\tif errs := validation.ValidatePatchOptions(options, patchType); len(errs) > 0 {\\n   125\u2192\\t\\t\\terr := errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{Group: metav1.GroupName, Kind: \\\"PatchOptions\\\"}, \\\"\\\", errs)\\n   126\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n   127\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   128\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   129\u2192\\t\\toptions.TypeMeta.SetGroupVersionKind(metav1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind(\\\"PatchOptions\\\"))\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192\\t\\tadmit = admission.WithAudit(admit)\\n   132\u2192\\n   133\u2192\\t\\taudit.LogRequestPatch(req.Context(), patchBytes)\\n   134\u2192\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"Recorded the audit event\\\")\\n   135\u2192\\n   136\u2192\\t\\tvar baseContentType string\\n   137\u2192\\t\\tswitch patchType {\\n   138\u2192\\t\\tcase types.ApplyYAMLPatchType:\\n   139\u2192\\t\\t\\tbaseContentType = runtime.ContentTypeYAML\\n   140\u2192\\t\\tcase types.ApplyCBORPatchType:\\n   141\u2192\\t\\t\\tif !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.CBORServingAndStorage) {\\n   142\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// This request should have already been rejected by the\\n   143\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// Content-Type allowlist check. Return 500 because assumptions are\\n   144\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// already broken and the feature is not GA.\\n   145\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tutilruntime.HandleErrorWithContext(req.Context(), nil, \\\"The patch content-type allowlist check should have made this unreachable.\\\")\\n   146\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tscope.err(errors.NewInternalError(errors.NewInternalError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"unexpected patch type: %v\\\", patchType))), w, req)\\n   147\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   148\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   149\u2192\\n   150\u2192\\t\\t\\tbaseContentType = runtime.ContentTypeCBOR\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"4625354d-2d9b-4b70-b6a1-a762977fe0ef\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:47.788Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"6e5f6774-7122-41c7-9e70-b862fb6f5323\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"4625354d-2d9b-4b70-b6a1-a762977fe0ef\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_015nscBd8kYNTJeaQEmynNVA\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01VB7ibfxJFJ8JrBRRujMKnk\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/fieldmanager/admission.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":2851,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":16405,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":2851},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG56HQy6iHWK81juCVSz\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"7b876f47-8005-43b1-8fd3-2abacfa15baa\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:48.114Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"7b876f47-8005-43b1-8fd3-2abacfa15baa\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01VB7ibfxJFJ8JrBRRujMKnk\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01VB7ibfxJFJ8JrBRRujMKnk\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:48.117Z\",\"uuid\":\"b44e3b8b-4671-491f-8c6a-b2c3aaa90655\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"7b876f47-8005-43b1-8fd3-2abacfa15baa\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01VB7ibfxJFJ8JrBRRujMKnk\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2021 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package fieldmanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/warning\\\"\\n    27\u2192)\\n    28\u2192\\n    29\u2192// InvalidManagedFieldsAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat is the warning that a client receives\\n    30\u2192// when a create/update/patch request results in invalid managedFields after going through the admission chain.\\n    31\u2192const InvalidManagedFieldsAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat = \\\".metadata.managedFields was in an invalid state after admission; this could be caused by an outdated mutating admission controller; please fix your requests: %v\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192// NewManagedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController validates the managedFields after calling\\n    34\u2192// the provided admission and resets them to their original state if they got changed to an invalid value\\n    35\u2192func NewManagedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController(wrap admission.Interface) admission.Interface {\\n    36\u2192\\tif wrap == nil {\\n    37\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n    38\u2192\\t}\\n    39\u2192\\treturn &managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController{wrap: wrap}\\n    40\u2192}\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192type managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController struct {\\n    43\u2192\\twrap admission.Interface\\n    44\u2192}\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192var _ admission.Interface = &managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController{}\\n    47\u2192var _ admission.MutationInterface = &managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController{}\\n    48\u2192var _ admission.ValidationInterface = &managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController{}\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192// Handles calls the wrapped admission.Interface if applicable\\n    51\u2192func (admit *managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController) Handles(operation admission.Operation) bool {\\n    52\u2192\\treturn admit.wrap.Handles(operation)\\n    53\u2192}\\n    54\u2192\\n    55\u2192// Admit calls the wrapped admission.Interface if applicable and resets the managedFields to their state before admission if they\\n    56\u2192// got modified in an invalid way\\n    57\u2192func (admit *managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController) Admit(ctx context.Context, a admission.Attributes, o admission.ObjectInterfaces) (err error) {\\n    58\u2192\\tmutationInterface, isMutationInterface := admit.wrap.(admission.MutationInterface)\\n    59\u2192\\tif !isMutationInterface {\\n    60\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n    61\u2192\\t}\\n    62\u2192\\tobjectMeta, err := meta.Accessor(a.GetObject())\\n    63\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    64\u2192\\t\\t// the object we are dealing with doesn't have object metadata defined\\n    65\u2192\\t\\t// in that case we don't have to keep track of the managedField\\n    66\u2192\\t\\t// just call the wrapped admission\\n    67\u2192\\t\\treturn mutationInterface.Admit(ctx, a, o)\\n    68\u2192\\t}\\n    69\u2192\\tmanagedFieldsBeforeAdmission := objectMeta.GetManagedFields()\\n    70\u2192\\tif err := mutationInterface.Admit(ctx, a, o); err != nil {\\n    71\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n    72\u2192\\t}\\n    73\u2192\\tmanagedFieldsAfterAdmission := objectMeta.GetManagedFields()\\n    74\u2192\\tif err := managedfields.ValidateManagedFields(managedFieldsAfterAdmission); err != nil {\\n    75\u2192\\t\\tobjectMeta.SetManagedFields(managedFieldsBeforeAdmission)\\n    76\u2192\\t\\twarning.AddWarning(ctx, \\\"\\\",\\n    77\u2192\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(InvalidManagedFieldsAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat,\\n    78\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\terr.Error()),\\n    79\u2192\\t\\t)\\n    80\u2192\\t}\\n    81\u2192\\treturn nil\\n    82\u2192}\\n    83\u2192\\n    84\u2192// Validate calls the wrapped admission.Interface if aplicable\\n    85\u2192func (admit *managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController) Validate(ctx context.Context, a admission.Attributes, o admission.ObjectInterfaces) (err error) {\\n    86\u2192\\tif validationInterface, isValidationInterface := admit.wrap.(admission.ValidationInterface); isValidationInterface {\\n    87\u2192\\t\\treturn validationInterface.Validate(ctx, a, o)\\n    88\u2192\\t}\\n    89\u2192\\treturn nil\\n    90\u2192}\\n    91\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"d229aec5-28ae-4843-a6be-a8dbe16552a2\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:48.117Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"7b876f47-8005-43b1-8fd3-2abacfa15baa\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d229aec5-28ae-4843-a6be-a8dbe16552a2\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_013D54sCU5Uqc961yZkNbFvq\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Let me continue reading key implementation files:\"}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":8220,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":19256,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":8220},\"output_tokens\":3,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG56UUUsDS6mVqaw8uUo\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"600d7cc1-60d9-4c7c-b9c7-e30b63cc577f\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:49.072Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"600d7cc1-60d9-4c7c-b9c7-e30b63cc577f\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_013D54sCU5Uqc961yZkNbFvq\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01LvmXuiAwRTTQ61xxLWRKeR\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/fieldmanager.go\",\"limit\":200},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":8220,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":19256,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":8220},\"output_tokens\":3,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG56UUUsDS6mVqaw8uUo\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"65259757-8229-405e-ae74-9bcee0c7f2e4\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:49.554Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"65259757-8229-405e-ae74-9bcee0c7f2e4\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01LvmXuiAwRTTQ61xxLWRKeR\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01LvmXuiAwRTTQ61xxLWRKeR\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:49.558Z\",\"uuid\":\"b5a35b91-ef1d-4b1b-9b42-a53d8741ce69\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"65259757-8229-405e-ae74-9bcee0c7f2e4\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01LvmXuiAwRTTQ61xxLWRKeR\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package internal\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"reflect\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge\\\"\\n    30\u2192)\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192// DefaultMaxUpdateManagers defines the default maximum retained number of managedFields entries from updates\\n    33\u2192// if the number of update managers exceeds this, the oldest entries will be merged until the number is below the maximum.\\n    34\u2192// TODO(jennybuckley): Determine if this is really the best value. Ideally we wouldn't unnecessarily merge too many entries.\\n    35\u2192const DefaultMaxUpdateManagers int = 10\\n    36\u2192\\n    37\u2192// DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability defines the default probability that the field management of an object\\n    38\u2192// starts being tracked from the object's creation, instead of from the first time the object is applied to.\\n    39\u2192const DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability float32 = 1\\n    40\u2192\\n    41\u2192var atMostEverySecond = NewAtMostEvery(time.Second)\\n    42\u2192\\n    43\u2192// FieldManager updates the managed fields and merges applied\\n    44\u2192// configurations.\\n    45\u2192type FieldManager struct {\\n    46\u2192\\tfieldManager Manager\\n    47\u2192\\tsubresource  string\\n    48\u2192}\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192// NewFieldManager creates a new FieldManager that decodes, manages, then re-encodes managedFields\\n    51\u2192// on update and apply requests.\\n    52\u2192func NewFieldManager(f Manager, subresource string) *FieldManager {\\n    53\u2192\\treturn &FieldManager{fieldManager: f, subresource: subresource}\\n    54\u2192}\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192// newDefaultFieldManager is a helper function which wraps a Manager with certain default logic.\\n    57\u2192func NewDefaultFieldManager(f Manager, typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, objectCreater runtime.ObjectCreater, kind schema.GroupVersionKind, subresource string) *FieldManager {\\n    58\u2192\\treturn NewFieldManager(\\n    59\u2192\\t\\tNewVersionCheckManager(\\n    60\u2192\\t\\t\\tNewLastAppliedUpdater(\\n    61\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tNewLastAppliedManager(\\n    62\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNewProbabilisticSkipNonAppliedManager(\\n    63\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNewCapManagersManager(\\n    64\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNewBuildManagerInfoManager(\\n    65\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNewManagedFieldsUpdater(\\n    66\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNewStripMetaManager(f),\\n    67\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t), kind.GroupVersion(), subresource,\\n    68\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t), DefaultMaxUpdateManagers,\\n    69\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t), objectCreater, DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability,\\n    70\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t), typeConverter, objectConverter, kind.GroupVersion(),\\n    71\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n    72\u2192\\t\\t\\t), kind,\\n    73\u2192\\t\\t), subresource,\\n    74\u2192\\t)\\n    75\u2192}\\n    76\u2192\\n    77\u2192func decodeLiveOrNew(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, ignoreManagedFieldsFromRequestObject bool) (Managed, error) {\\n    78\u2192\\tliveAccessor, err := meta.Accessor(liveObj)\\n    79\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    80\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n    81\u2192\\t}\\n    82\u2192\\n    83\u2192\\t// We take the managedFields of the live object in case the request tries to\\n    84\u2192\\t// manually set managedFields via a subresource.\\n    85\u2192\\tif ignoreManagedFieldsFromRequestObject {\\n    86\u2192\\t\\treturn emptyManagedFieldsOnErr(DecodeManagedFields(liveAccessor.GetManagedFields()))\\n    87\u2192\\t}\\n    88\u2192\\n    89\u2192\\t// If the object doesn't have metadata, we should just return without trying to\\n    90\u2192\\t// set the managedFields at all, so creates/updates/patches will work normally.\\n    91\u2192\\tnewAccessor, err := meta.Accessor(newObj)\\n    92\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    93\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n    94\u2192\\t}\\n    95\u2192\\n    96\u2192\\tif isResetManagedFields(newAccessor.GetManagedFields()) {\\n    97\u2192\\t\\treturn NewEmptyManaged(), nil\\n    98\u2192\\t}\\n    99\u2192\\n   100\u2192\\t// If the managed field is empty or we failed to decode it,\\n   101\u2192\\t// let's try the live object. This is to prevent clients who\\n   102\u2192\\t// don't understand managedFields from deleting it accidentally.\\n   103\u2192\\tmanaged, err := DecodeManagedFields(newAccessor.GetManagedFields())\\n   104\u2192\\tif err != nil || len(managed.Fields()) == 0 {\\n   105\u2192\\t\\treturn emptyManagedFieldsOnErr(DecodeManagedFields(liveAccessor.GetManagedFields()))\\n   106\u2192\\t}\\n   107\u2192\\treturn managed, nil\\n   108\u2192}\\n   109\u2192\\n   110\u2192func emptyManagedFieldsOnErr(managed Managed, err error) (Managed, error) {\\n   111\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   112\u2192\\t\\treturn NewEmptyManaged(), nil\\n   113\u2192\\t}\\n   114\u2192\\treturn managed, nil\\n   115\u2192}\\n   116\u2192\\n   117\u2192// Update is used when the object has already been merged (non-apply\\n   118\u2192// use-case), and simply updates the managed fields in the output\\n   119\u2192// object.\\n   120\u2192func (f *FieldManager) Update(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, manager string) (object runtime.Object, err error) {\\n   121\u2192\\t// First try to decode the managed fields provided in the update,\\n   122\u2192\\t// This is necessary to allow directly updating managed fields.\\n   123\u2192\\tisSubresource := f.subresource != \\\"\\\"\\n   124\u2192\\tmanaged, err := decodeLiveOrNew(liveObj, newObj, isSubresource)\\n   125\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   126\u2192\\t\\treturn newObj, nil\\n   127\u2192\\t}\\n   128\u2192\\n   129\u2192\\tRemoveObjectManagedFields(newObj)\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192\\tif object, managed, err = f.fieldManager.Update(liveObj, newObj, managed, manager); err != nil {\\n   132\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   133\u2192\\t}\\n   134\u2192\\n   135\u2192\\tif err = EncodeObjectManagedFields(object, managed); err != nil {\\n   136\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to encode managed fields: %v\\\", err)\\n   137\u2192\\t}\\n   138\u2192\\n   139\u2192\\treturn object, nil\\n   140\u2192}\\n   141\u2192\\n   142\u2192// UpdateNoErrors is the same as Update, but it will not return\\n   143\u2192// errors. If an error happens, the object is returned with\\n   144\u2192// managedFields cleared.\\n   145\u2192func (f *FieldManager) UpdateNoErrors(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, manager string) runtime.Object {\\n   146\u2192\\tobj, err := f.Update(liveObj, newObj, manager)\\n   147\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   148\u2192\\t\\tatMostEverySecond.Do(func() {\\n   149\u2192\\t\\t\\tns, name := \\\"unknown\\\", \\\"unknown\\\"\\n   150\u2192\\t\\t\\tif accessor, err := meta.Accessor(newObj); err == nil {\\n   151\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tns = accessor.GetNamespace()\\n   152\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tname = accessor.GetName()\\n   153\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   154\u2192\\n   155\u2192\\t\\t\\tklog.ErrorS(err, \\\"[SHOULD NOT HAPPEN] failed to update managedFields\\\", \\\"versionKind\\\",\\n   156\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tnewObj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind(), \\\"namespace\\\", ns, \\\"name\\\", name)\\n   157\u2192\\t\\t})\\n   158\u2192\\t\\t// Explicitly remove managedFields on failure, so that\\n   159\u2192\\t\\t// we can't have garbage in it.\\n   160\u2192\\t\\tRemoveObjectManagedFields(newObj)\\n   161\u2192\\t\\treturn newObj\\n   162\u2192\\t}\\n   163\u2192\\treturn obj\\n   164\u2192}\\n   165\u2192\\n   166\u2192// Returns true if the managedFields indicate that the user is trying to\\n   167\u2192// reset the managedFields, i.e. if the list is non-nil but empty, or if\\n   168\u2192// the list has one empty item.\\n   169\u2192func isResetManagedFields(managedFields []metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry) bool {\\n   170\u2192\\tif len(managedFields) == 0 {\\n   171\u2192\\t\\treturn managedFields != nil\\n   172\u2192\\t}\\n   173\u2192\\n   174\u2192\\tif len(managedFields) == 1 {\\n   175\u2192\\t\\treturn reflect.DeepEqual(managedFields[0], metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry{})\\n   176\u2192\\t}\\n   177\u2192\\n   178\u2192\\treturn false\\n   179\u2192}\\n   180\u2192\\n   181\u2192// Apply is used when server-side apply is called, as it merges the\\n   182\u2192// object and updates the managed fields.\\n   183\u2192func (f *FieldManager) Apply(liveObj, appliedObj runtime.Object, manager string, force bool) (object runtime.Object, err error) {\\n   184\u2192\\t// If the object doesn't have metadata, apply isn't allowed.\\n   185\u2192\\taccessor, err := meta.Accessor(liveObj)\\n   186\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   187\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"couldn't get accessor: %v\\\", err)\\n   188\u2192\\t}\\n   189\u2192\\n   190\u2192\\t// Decode the managed fields in the live object, since it isn't allowed in the patch.\\n   191\u2192\\tmanaged, err := DecodeManagedFields(accessor.GetManagedFields())\\n   192\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   193\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to decode managed fields: %v\\\", err)\\n   194\u2192\\t}\\n   195\u2192\\n   196\u2192\\tobject, managed, err = f.fieldManager.Apply(liveObj, appliedObj, managed, manager, force)\\n   197\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   198\u2192\\t\\tif conflicts, ok := err.(merge.Conflicts); ok {\\n   199\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, NewConflictError(conflicts)\\n   200\u2192\\t\\t}\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"36910c9d-ccbe-4e2a-90b0-088ad67460a8\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:49.558Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"65259757-8229-405e-ae74-9bcee0c7f2e4\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"36910c9d-ccbe-4e2a-90b0-088ad67460a8\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_013D54sCU5Uqc961yZkNbFvq\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01PCyLUHYBqkQ8GSHfy6tudq\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go\",\"limit\":150},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":8220,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":19256,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":8220},\"output_tokens\":3,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG56UUUsDS6mVqaw8uUo\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"21a2cae1-b2d1-4693-b752-02a545e27254\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:49.873Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"21a2cae1-b2d1-4693-b752-02a545e27254\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01PCyLUHYBqkQ8GSHfy6tudq\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01PCyLUHYBqkQ8GSHfy6tudq\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:49.878Z\",\"uuid\":\"3f2e99af-cf21-4ba1-9358-49ff0d257d13\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"21a2cae1-b2d1-4693-b752-02a545e27254\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01PCyLUHYBqkQ8GSHfy6tudq\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package rest\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"io\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"net/url\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192\\tmetainternalversion \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    30\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\\\"\\n    32\u2192)\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192//TODO:\\n    35\u2192// Storage interfaces need to be separated into two groups; those that operate\\n    36\u2192// on collections and those that operate on individually named items.\\n    37\u2192// Collection interfaces:\\n    38\u2192// (Method: Current -> Proposed)\\n    39\u2192//    GET: Lister -> CollectionGetter\\n    40\u2192//    WATCH: Watcher -> CollectionWatcher\\n    41\u2192//    CREATE: Creater -> CollectionCreater\\n    42\u2192//    DELETE: (n/a) -> CollectionDeleter\\n    43\u2192//    UPDATE: (n/a) -> CollectionUpdater\\n    44\u2192//\\n    45\u2192// Single item interfaces:\\n    46\u2192// (Method: Current -> Proposed)\\n    47\u2192//    GET: Getter -> NamedGetter\\n    48\u2192//    WATCH: (n/a) -> NamedWatcher\\n    49\u2192//    CREATE: (n/a) -> NamedCreater\\n    50\u2192//    DELETE: Deleter -> NamedDeleter\\n    51\u2192//    UPDATE: Update -> NamedUpdater\\n    52\u2192\\n    53\u2192// Storage is a generic interface for RESTful storage services.\\n    54\u2192// Resources which are exported to the RESTful API of apiserver need to implement this interface. It is expected\\n    55\u2192// that objects may implement any of the below interfaces.\\n    56\u2192//\\n    57\u2192// Consider using StorageWithReadiness whenever possible.\\n    58\u2192type Storage interface {\\n    59\u2192\\t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create and Update after request data has been put into it.\\n    60\u2192\\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\\n    61\u2192\\tNew() runtime.Object\\n    62\u2192\\n    63\u2192\\t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\\n    64\u2192\\t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\\n    65\u2192\\t// for being called more than once.\\n    66\u2192\\tDestroy()\\n    67\u2192}\\n    68\u2192\\n    69\u2192// StorageWithReadiness extends Storage interface with the readiness check.\\n    70\u2192type StorageWithReadiness interface {\\n    71\u2192\\tStorage\\n    72\u2192\\n    73\u2192\\t// ReadinessCheck allows for checking storage readiness.\\n    74\u2192\\tReadinessCheck() error\\n    75\u2192}\\n    76\u2192\\n    77\u2192// Scoper indicates what scope the resource is at. It must be specified.\\n    78\u2192// It is usually provided automatically based on your strategy.\\n    79\u2192type Scoper interface {\\n    80\u2192\\t// NamespaceScoped returns true if the storage is namespaced\\n    81\u2192\\tNamespaceScoped() bool\\n    82\u2192}\\n    83\u2192\\n    84\u2192// KindProvider specifies a different kind for its API than for its internal storage.  This is necessary for external\\n    85\u2192// objects that are not compiled into the api server.  For such objects, there is no in-memory representation for\\n    86\u2192// the object, so they must be represented as generic objects (e.g. runtime.Unknown), but when we present the object as part of\\n    87\u2192// API discovery we want to present the specific kind, not the generic internal representation.\\n    88\u2192type KindProvider interface {\\n    89\u2192\\tKind() string\\n    90\u2192}\\n    91\u2192\\n    92\u2192// ShortNamesProvider is an interface for RESTful storage services. Delivers a list of short names for a resource. The list is used by kubectl to have short names representation of resources.\\n    93\u2192type ShortNamesProvider interface {\\n    94\u2192\\tShortNames() []string\\n    95\u2192}\\n    96\u2192\\n    97\u2192// CategoriesProvider allows a resource to specify which groups of resources (categories) it's part of. Categories can\\n    98\u2192// be used by API clients to refer to a batch of resources by using a single name (e.g. \\\"all\\\" could translate to \\\"pod,rc,svc,...\\\").\\n    99\u2192type CategoriesProvider interface {\\n   100\u2192\\tCategories() []string\\n   101\u2192}\\n   102\u2192\\n   103\u2192// SingularNameProvider returns singular name of resources. This is used by kubectl discovery to have singular\\n   104\u2192// name representation of resources. In case of shortcut conflicts(with CRD shortcuts) singular name should always map to this resource.\\n   105\u2192type SingularNameProvider interface {\\n   106\u2192\\tGetSingularName() string\\n   107\u2192}\\n   108\u2192\\n   109\u2192// GroupVersionKindProvider is used to specify a particular GroupVersionKind to discovery.  This is used for polymorphic endpoints\\n   110\u2192// which generally point to foreign versions.  Scale refers to Scale.v1beta1.extensions for instance.\\n   111\u2192// This trumps KindProvider since it is capable of providing the information required.\\n   112\u2192// TODO KindProvider (only used by federation) should be removed and replaced with this, but that presents greater risk late in 1.8.\\n   113\u2192type GroupVersionKindProvider interface {\\n   114\u2192\\tGroupVersionKind(containingGV schema.GroupVersion) schema.GroupVersionKind\\n   115\u2192}\\n   116\u2192\\n   117\u2192// GroupVersionAcceptor is used to determine if a particular GroupVersion is acceptable to send to an endpoint.\\n   118\u2192// This is used for endpoints which accept multiple versions (which is extremely rare).\\n   119\u2192// The only known instance is pods/evictions which accepts policy/v1, but also policy/v1beta1 for backwards compatibility.\\n   120\u2192type GroupVersionAcceptor interface {\\n   121\u2192\\tAcceptsGroupVersion(gv schema.GroupVersion) bool\\n   122\u2192}\\n   123\u2192\\n   124\u2192// Lister is an object that can retrieve resources that match the provided field and label criteria.\\n   125\u2192type Lister interface {\\n   126\u2192\\t// NewList returns an empty object that can be used with the List call.\\n   127\u2192\\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\\n   128\u2192\\tNewList() runtime.Object\\n   129\u2192\\t// List selects resources in the storage which match to the selector. 'options' can be nil.\\n   130\u2192\\tList(ctx context.Context, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\\n   131\u2192\\t// TableConvertor ensures all list implementers also implement table conversion\\n   132\u2192\\tTableConvertor\\n   133\u2192}\\n   134\u2192\\n   135\u2192// Getter is an object that can retrieve a named RESTful resource.\\n   136\u2192type Getter interface {\\n   137\u2192\\t// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.\\n   138\u2192\\t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\\n   139\u2192\\t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\\n   140\u2192\\tGet(ctx context.Context, name string, options *metav1.GetOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\\n   141\u2192}\\n   142\u2192\\n   143\u2192// GetterWithOptions is an object that retrieve a named RESTful resource and takes\\n   144\u2192// additional options on the get request. It allows a caller to also receive the\\n   145\u2192// subpath of the GET request.\\n   146\u2192type GetterWithOptions interface {\\n   147\u2192\\t// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.\\n   148\u2192\\t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\\n   149\u2192\\t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\\n   150\u2192\\t// The options object passed to it is of the same type returned by the NewGetOptions\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"bc31372e-9791-4816-baf5-df8273b6fc97\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:49.878Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"21a2cae1-b2d1-4693-b752-02a545e27254\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"bc31372e-9791-4816-baf5-df8273b6fc97\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_013D54sCU5Uqc961yZkNbFvq\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01229CLL256YGX1Zp8i94pN9\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/applyconfigurations/meta/v1/objectmeta.go\",\"limit\":100},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":8220,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":19256,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":8220},\"output_tokens\":3,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG56UUUsDS6mVqaw8uUo\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"84a16487-ad21-48f2-9d8d-d67d82385053\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:50.295Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"84a16487-ad21-48f2-9d8d-d67d82385053\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01229CLL256YGX1Zp8i94pN9\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01229CLL256YGX1Zp8i94pN9\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:50.299Z\",\"uuid\":\"5d082055-c466-4fa5-8d4a-9178 CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192// Code generated by applyconfiguration-gen. DO NOT EDIT.\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192package v1\\n    20\u2192\\n    21\u2192import (\\n    22\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\ttypes \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\\\"\\n    24\u2192)\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192// ObjectMetaApplyConfiguration represents a declarative configuration of the ObjectMeta type for use\\n    27\u2192// with apply.\\n    28\u2192//\\n    29\u2192// ObjectMeta is metadata that all persisted resources must have, which includes all objects\\n    30\u2192// users must create.\\n    31\u2192type ObjectMetaApplyConfiguration struct {\\n    32\u2192\\t// Name must be unique within a namespace. Is required when creating resources, although\\n    33\u2192\\t// some resources may allow a client to request the generation of an appropriate name\\n    34\u2192\\t// automatically. Name is primarily intended for creation idempotence and configuration\\n    35\u2192\\t// definition.\\n    36\u2192\\t// Cannot be updated.\\n    37\u2192\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names#names\\n    38\u2192\\tName *string `json:\\\"name,omitempty\\\"`\\n    39\u2192\\t// GenerateName is an optional prefix, used by the server, to generate a unique\\n    40\u2192\\t// name ONLY IF the Name field has not been provided.\\n    41\u2192\\t// If this field is used, the name returned to the client will be different\\n    42\u2192\\t// than the name passed. This value will also be combined with a unique suffix.\\n    43\u2192\\t// The provided value has the same validation rules as the Name field,\\n    44\u2192\\t// and may be truncated by the length of the suffix required to make the value\\n    45\u2192\\t// unique on the server.\\n    46\u2192\\t//\\n    47\u2192\\t// If this field is specified and the generated name exists, the server will return a 409.\\n    48\u2192\\t//\\n    49\u2192\\t// Applied only if Name is not specified.\\n    50\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#idempotency\\n    51\u2192\\tGenerateName *string `json:\\\"generateName,omitempty\\\"`\\n    52\u2192\\t// Namespace defines the space within which each name must be unique. An empty namespace is\\n    53\u2192\\t// equivalent to the \\\"default\\\" namespace, but \\\"default\\\" is the canonical representation.\\n    54\u2192\\t// Not all objects are required to be scoped to a namespace - the value of this field for\\n    55\u2192\\t// those objects will be empty.\\n    56\u2192\\t//\\n    57\u2192\\t// Must be a DNS_LABEL.\\n    58\u2192\\t// Cannot be updated.\\n    59\u2192\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/namespaces\\n    60\u2192\\tNamespace *string `json:\\\"namespace,omitempty\\\"`\\n    61\u2192\\t// UID is the unique in time and space value for this object. It is typically generated by\\n    62\u2192\\t// the server on successful creation of a resource and is not allowed to change on PUT\\n    63\u2192\\t// operations.\\n    64\u2192\\t//\\n    65\u2192\\t// Populated by the system.\\n    66\u2192\\t// Read-only.\\n    67\u2192\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names#uids\\n    68\u2192\\tUID *types.UID `json:\\\"uid,omitempty\\\"`\\n    69\u2192\\t// An opaque value that represents the internal version of this object that can\\n    70\u2192\\t// be used by clients to determine when objects have changed. May be used for optimistic\\n    71\u2192\\t// concurrency, change detection, and the watch operation on a resource or set of resources.\\n    72\u2192\\t// Clients must treat these values as opaque and passed unmodified back to the server.\\n    73\u2192\\t// They may only be valid for a particular resource or set of resources.\\n    74\u2192\\t//\\n    75\u2192\\t// Populated by the system.\\n    76\u2192\\t// Read-only.\\n    77\u2192\\t// Value must be treated as opaque by clients and .\\n    78\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#concurrency-control-and-consistency\\n    79\u2192\\tResourceVersion *string `json:\\\"resourceVersion,omitempty\\\"`\\n    80\u2192\\t// A sequence number representing a specific generation of the desired state.\\n    81\u2192\\t// Populated by the system. Read-only.\\n    82\u2192\\tGeneration *int64 `json:\\\"generation,omitempty\\\"`\\n    83\u2192\\t// CreationTimestamp is a timestamp representing the server time when this object was\\n    84\u2192\\t// created. It is not guaranteed to be set in happens-before order across separate operations.\\n    85\u2192\\t// Clients may not set this value. It is represented in RFC3339 form and is in UTC.\\n    86\u2192\\t//\\n    87\u2192\\t// Populated by the system.\\n    88\u2192\\t// Read-only.\\n    89\u2192\\t// Null for lists.\\n    90\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\\n    91\u2192\\tCreationTimestamp *metav1.Time `json:\\\"creationTimestamp,omitempty\\\"`\\n    92\u2192\\t// DeletionTimestamp is RFC 3339 date and time at which this resource will be deleted. This\\n    93\u2192\\t// field is set by the server when a graceful deletion is requested by the user, and is not\\n    94\u2192\\t// directly settable by a client. The resource is expected to be deleted (no longer visible\\n    95\u2192\\t// from resource lists, and not reachable by name) after the time in this field, once the\\n    96\u2192\\t// finalizers list is empty. As long as the finalizers list contains items, deletion is blocked.\\n    97\u2192\\t// Once the deletionTimestamp is set, this value may not be unset or be set further into the\\n    98\u2192\\t// future, although it may be shortened or the resource may be deleted prior to this time.\\n    99\u2192\\t// For example, a user may request that a pod is deleted in 30 seconds. The Kubelet will react\\n   100\u2192\\t// by sending a graceful termination signal to the containers in the pod. After that 30 seconds,\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing.
