    1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2021 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package managedfields\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"bytes\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    30\u2192)\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192// ExtractInto extracts the applied configuration state from object for fieldManager\\n    33\u2192// into applyConfiguration. If no managed fields are found for the given fieldManager,\\n    34\u2192// no error is returned, but applyConfiguration is left unpopulated. It is possible\\n    35\u2192// that no managed fields were found for the fieldManager because other field managers\\n    36\u2192// have taken ownership of all the fields previously owned by the fieldManager. It is\\n    37\u2192// also possible the fieldManager never owned fields.\\n    38\u2192//\\n    39\u2192// The provided object MUST bo a root resource object since subresource objects\\n    40\u2192// do not contain their own managed fields. For example, an autoscaling.Scale\\n    41\u2192// object read from a \\\"scale\\\" subresource does not have any managed fields and so\\n    42\u2192// cannot be used as the object.\\n    43\u2192//\\n    44\u2192// If the fields of a subresource are a subset of the fields of the root object,\\n    45\u2192// and their field paths and types are exactly the same, then ExtractInto can be\\n    46\u2192// called with the root resource as the object and the subresource as the\\n    47\u2192// applyConfiguration. This works for \\\"status\\\", obviously, because status is\\n    48\u2192// represented by the exact same object as the root resource. This does NOT\\n    49\u2192// work, for example, with the \\\"scale\\\" subresources of Deployment, ReplicaSet and\\n    50\u2192// StatefulSet. While the spec.replicas, status.replicas fields are in the same\\n    51\u2192// exact field path locations as they are in autoscaling.Scale, the selector\\n    52\u2192// fields are in different locations, and are a different type.\\n    53\u2192func ExtractInto(object runtime.Object, objectType typed.ParseableType, fieldManager string, applyConfiguration interface{}, subresource string) error {\\n    54\u2192\\ttypedObj, err := toTyped(object, objectType)\\n    55\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    56\u2192\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"error converting obj to typed: %w\\\", err)\\n    57\u2192\\t}\\n    58\u2192\\n    59\u2192\\taccessor, err := meta.Accessor(object)\\n    60\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    61\u2192\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"error accessing metadata: %w\\\", err)\\n    62\u2192\\t}\\n    63\u2192\\tfieldsEntry, ok := findManagedFields(accessor, fieldManager, subresource)\\n    64\u2192\\tif !ok {\\n    65\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n    66\u2192\\t}\\n    67\u2192\\tfieldset := &fieldpath.Set{}\\n    68\u2192\\terr = fieldset.FromJSON(bytes.NewReader(fieldsEntry.FieldsV1.Raw))\\n    69\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    70\u2192\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"error marshalling FieldsV1 to JSON: %w\\\", err)\\n    71\u2192\\t}\\n    72\u2192\\n    73\u2192\\tu := typedObj.ExtractItems(fieldset.Leaves()).AsValue().Unstructured()\\n    74\u2192\\tm, ok := u.(map[string]interface{})\\n    75\u2192\\tif !ok {\\n    76\u2192\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"unable to convert managed fields for %s to unstructured, expected map, got %T\\\", fieldManager, u)\\n    77\u2192\\t}\\n    78\u2192\\n    79\u2192\\t// set the type meta manually if it doesn't exist to avoid missing kind errors\\n    80\u2192\\t// when decoding from unstructured JSON\\n    81\u2192\\tif _, ok := m[\\\"kind\\\"]; !ok && object.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind().Kind != \\\"\\\" {\\n    82\u2192\\t\\tm[\\\"kind\\\"] = object.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind().Kind\\n    83\u2192\\t\\tm[\\\"apiVersion\\\"] = object.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind().GroupVersion().String()\\n    84\u2192\\t}\\n    85\u2192\\tif err := runtime.DefaultUnstructuredConverter.FromUnstructured(m, applyConfiguration); err != nil {\\n    86\u2192\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"error extracting into obj from unstructured: %w\\\", err)\\n    87\u2192\\t}\\n    88\u2192\\treturn nil\\n    89\u2192}\\n    90\u2192\\n    91\u2192func findManagedFields(accessor metav1.Object, fieldManager string, subresource string) (metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry, bool) {\\n    92\u2192\\tobjManagedFields := accessor.GetManagedFields()\\n    93\u2192\\tfor _, mf := range objManagedFields {\\n    94\u2192\\t\\tif mf.Manager == fieldManager && mf.Operation == metav1.ManagedFieldsOperationApply && mf.Subresource == subresource {\\n    95\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn mf, true\\n    96\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    97\u2192\\t}\\n    98\u2192\\treturn metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry{}, false\\n    99\u2192}\\n   100\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"d705aeb5-075c-4d1c-95cf-b136878629fd\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:12.219Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"4dc24c0b-0c94-4702-8f81-a9e4742eac0f\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d705aeb5-075c-4d1c-95cf-b136878629fd\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_016rjj3ZtQabAvdXo9NgcvrU\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01EhpxbQ1QNd5xK214vuwgFb\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"find /workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal -name \\\"*.go\\\" -exec grep -l \\\"Extract\\\\|extract\\\" {} \\\\;\",\"description\":\"Find extract-related files\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":3216,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":55787,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":3216},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG584XsYxQemyiThi44h\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"012036f4-0838-4825-bf55-7bdea56ecffc\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:12.718Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"012036f4-0838-4825-bf55-7bdea56ecffc\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_016rjj3ZtQabAvdXo9NgcvrU\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01WqjavD7CPCEr1UD4vmLoPK\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/fieldmanager/equality.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":3216,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":55787,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":3216},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG584XsYxQemyiThi44h\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"c4df50f3-0836-4823-938c-3bbb8f446f6f\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:13.013Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"012036f4-0838-4825-bf55-7bdea56ecffc\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01EhpxbQ1QNd5xK214vuwgFb\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"dc177e36-0049-40c3-82eb-fe59b5e14e81\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:13.912Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"012036f4-0838-4825-bf55-7bdea56ecffc\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"dc177e36-0049-40c3-82eb-fe59b5e14e81\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01WqjavD7CPCEr1UD4vmLoPK\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01WqjavD7CPCEr1UD4vmLoPK\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:13.917Z\",\"uuid\":\"c9f79579-79f0-4c4f-846c-083fdb6c0737\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"c4df50f3-0836-4823-938c-3bbb8f446f6f\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01WqjavD7CPCEr1UD4vmLoPK\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2021 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package fieldmanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"os\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"reflect\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/equality\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n    30\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\\\"\\n    33\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\\\"\\n    35\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    36\u2192)\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192var (\\n    39\u2192\\tavoidTimestampEqualities     conversion.Equalities\\n    40\u2192\\tinitAvoidTimestampEqualities sync.Once\\n    41\u2192)\\n    42\u2192\\n    43\u2192func getAvoidTimestampEqualities() conversion.Equalities {\\n    44\u2192\\tinitAvoidTimestampEqualities.Do(func() {\\n    45\u2192\\t\\tif avoidNoopTimestampUpdatesString, exists := os.LookupEnv(\\\"KUBE_APISERVER_AVOID_NOOP_SSA_TIMESTAMP_UPDATES\\\"); exists {\\n    46\u2192\\t\\t\\tif ret, err := strconv.ParseBool(avoidNoopTimestampUpdatesString); err == nil && !ret {\\n    47\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// leave avoidTimestampEqualities empty.\\n    48\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n    49\u2192\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n    50\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tklog.Errorf(\\\"failed to parse envar KUBE_APISERVER_AVOID_NOOP_SSA_TIMESTAMP_UPDATES: %v\\\", err)\\n    51\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n    52\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    53\u2192\\n    54\u2192\\t\\tvar eqs = equality.Semantic.Copy()\\n    55\u2192\\t\\terr := eqs.AddFuncs(\\n    56\u2192\\t\\t\\tfunc(a, b metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry) bool {\\n    57\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// Two objects' managed fields are equivalent if, ignoring timestamp,\\n    58\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t//\\tthe objects are deeply equal.\\n    59\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\ta.Time = nil\\n    60\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tb.Time = nil\\n    61\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn reflect.DeepEqual(a, b)\\n    62\u2192\\t\\t\\t},\\n    63\u2192\\t\\t\\tfunc(a, b unstructured.Unstructured) bool {\\n    64\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// Check if the managed fields are equal by converting to structured types and leveraging the above\\n    65\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// function, then, ignoring the managed fields, equality check the rest of the unstructured data.\\n    66\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tif !avoidTimestampEqualities.DeepEqual(a.GetManagedFields(), b.GetManagedFields()) {\\n    67\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n    68\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n    69\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn equalIgnoringValueAtPath(a.Object, b.Object, []string{\\\"metadata\\\", \\\"managedFields\\\"})\\n    70\u2192\\t\\t\\t},\\n    71\u2192\\t\\t)\\n    72\u2192\\n    73\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n    74\u2192\\t\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to instantiate semantic equalities: %w\\\", err))\\n    75\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    76\u2192\\n    77\u2192\\t\\tavoidTimestampEqualities = eqs\\n    78\u2192\\t})\\n    79\u2192\\treturn avoidTimestampEqualities\\n    80\u2192}\\n    81\u2192\\n    82\u2192func equalIgnoringValueAtPath(a, b any, path []string) bool {\\n    83\u2192\\tif len(path) == 0 { // found the value to ignore\\n    84\u2192\\t\\treturn true\\n    85\u2192\\t}\\n    86\u2192\\taMap, aOk := a.(map[string]any)\\n    87\u2192\\tbMap, bOk := b.(map[string]any)\\n    88\u2192\\tif !aOk || !bOk {\\n    89\u2192\\t\\t// Can't traverse into non-maps, ignore\\n    90\u2192\\t\\treturn true\\n    91\u2192\\t}\\n    92\u2192\\tif len(aMap) != len(bMap) {\\n    93\u2192\\t\\treturn false\\n    94\u2192\\t}\\n    95\u2192\\tpathHead := path[0]\\n    96\u2192\\tfor k, aVal := range aMap {\\n    97\u2192\\t\\tbVal, ok := bMap[k]\\n    98\u2192\\t\\tif !ok {\\n    99\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n   100\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   101\u2192\\t\\tif k == pathHead {\\n   102\u2192\\t\\t\\tif !equalIgnoringValueAtPath(aVal, bVal, path[1:]) {\\n   103\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n   104\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   105\u2192\\t\\t} else if !avoidTimestampEqualities.DeepEqual(aVal, bVal) {\\n   106\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n   107\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   108\u2192\\t}\\n   109\u2192\\treturn true\\n   110\u2192}\\n   111\u2192\\n   112\u2192// IgnoreManagedFieldsTimestampsTransformer reverts timestamp updates\\n   113\u2192// if the non-managed parts of the object are equivalent\\n   114\u2192func IgnoreManagedFieldsTimestampsTransformer(\\n   115\u2192\\t_ context.Context,\\n   116\u2192\\tnewObj runtime.Object,\\n   117\u2192\\toldObj runtime.Object,\\n   118\u2192) (res runtime.Object, err error) {\\n   119\u2192\\tequalities := getAvoidTimestampEqualities()\\n   120\u2192\\tif len(equalities.Equalities) == 0 {\\n   121\u2192\\t\\treturn newObj, nil\\n   122\u2192\\t}\\n   123\u2192\\n   124\u2192\\toutcome := \\\"unequal_objects_fast\\\"\\n   125\u2192\\tstart := time.Now()\\n   126\u2192\\terr = nil\\n   127\u2192\\tres = nil\\n   128\u2192\\n   129\u2192\\tdefer func() {\\n   130\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   131\u2192\\t\\t\\toutcome = \\\"error\\\"\\n   132\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   133\u2192\\n   134\u2192\\t\\tmetrics.RecordTimestampComparisonLatency(outcome, time.Since(start))\\n   135\u2192\\t}()\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192\\t// If managedFields modulo timestamps are unchanged\\n   138\u2192\\t//\\t\\tand\\n   139\u2192\\t//\\trest of object is unchanged\\n   140\u2192\\t//\\t\\tthen\\n   141\u2192\\t//\\trevert any changes to timestamps in managed fields\\n   142\u2192\\t//\\t\\t(to prevent spurious ResourceVersion bump)\\n   143\u2192\\t//\\n   144\u2192\\t// Procecure:\\n   145\u2192\\t// Do a quicker check to see if just managed fields modulo timestamps are\\n   146\u2192\\t//\\tunchanged. If so, then do the full, slower check.\\n   147\u2192\\t//\\n   148\u2192\\t// In most cases which actually update the object, the managed fields modulo\\n   149\u2192\\t//\\ttimestamp check will fail, and we will be able to return early.\\n   150\u2192\\t//\\n   151\u2192\\t// In other cases, the managed fields may be exactly the same,\\n   152\u2192\\t// \\texcept for timestamp, but the objects are the different. This is the\\n   153\u2192\\t//\\tslow path which checks the full object.\\n   154\u2192\\toldAccessor, err := meta.Accessor(oldObj)\\n   155\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   156\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to acquire accessor for oldObj: %v\\\", err)\\n   157\u2192\\t}\\n   158\u2192\\n   159\u2192\\taccessor, err := meta.Accessor(newObj)\\n   160\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   161\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to acquire accessor for newObj: %v\\\", err)\\n   162\u2192\\t}\\n   163\u2192\\n   164\u2192\\toldManagedFields := oldAccessor.GetManagedFields()\\n   165\u2192\\tnewManagedFields := accessor.GetManagedFields()\\n   166\u2192\\n   167\u2192\\tif len(oldManagedFields) != len(newManagedFields) {\\n   168\u2192\\t\\t// Return early if any managed fields entry was added/removed.\\n   169\u2192\\t\\t// We want to retain user expectation that even if they write to a field\\n   170\u2192\\t\\t// whose value did not change, they will still result as the field\\n   171\u2192\\t\\t// manager at the end.\\n   172\u2192\\t\\treturn newObj, nil\\n   173\u2192\\t} else if len(newManagedFields) == 0 {\\n   174\u2192\\t\\t// This transformation only makes sense when managedFields are\\n   175\u2192\\t\\t// non-empty\\n   176\u2192\\t\\treturn newObj, nil\\n   177\u2192\\t}\\n   178\u2192\\n   179\u2192\\t// This transformation only makes sense if the managed fields has at least one\\n   180\u2192\\t// changed timestamp; and are otherwise equal. Return early if there are no\\n   181\u2192\\t// changed timestamps.\\n   182\u2192\\tallTimesUnchanged := true\\n   183\u2192\\tfor i, e := range newManagedFields {\\n   184\u2192\\t\\tif !e.Time.Equal(oldManagedFields[i].Time) {\\n   185\u2192\\t\\t\\tallTimesUnchanged = false\\n   186\u2192\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n   187\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   188\u2192\\t}\\n   189\u2192\\n   190\u2192\\tif allTimesUnchanged {\\n   191\u2192\\t\\treturn newObj, nil\\n   192\u2192\\t}\\n   193\u2192\\n   194\u2192\\teqFn := equalities.DeepEqual\\n   195\u2192\\tif _, ok := newObj.(*unstructured.Unstructured); ok {\\n   196\u2192\\t\\t// Use strict equality with unstructured\\n   197\u2192\\t\\teqFn = equalities.DeepEqualWithNilDifferentFromEmpty\\n   198\u2192\\t}\\n   199\u2192\\n   200\u2192\\t// This condition ensures the managed fields are always compared first. If\\n   201\u2192\\t//\\tthis check fails, the if statement will short circuit. If the check\\n   202\u2192\\t// \\tsucceeds the slow path is taken which compares entire objects.\\n   203\u2192\\tif !eqFn(oldManagedFields, newManagedFields) {\\n   204\u2192\\t\\treturn newObj, nil\\n   205\u2192\\t}\\n   206\u2192\\n   207\u2192\\tif eqFn(newObj, oldObj) {\\n   208\u2192\\t\\t// Remove any changed timestamps, so that timestamp is not the only\\n   209\u2192\\t\\t// change seen by etcd.\\n   210\u2192\\t\\t//\\n   211\u2192\\t\\t// newManagedFields is known to be exactly pairwise equal to\\n   212\u2192\\t\\t// oldManagedFields except for timestamps.\\n   213\u2192\\t\\t//\\n   214\u2192\\t\\t// Simply replace possibly changed new timestamps with their old values.\\n   215\u2192\\t\\tfor idx := 0; idx < len(oldManagedFields); idx++ {\\n   216\u2192\\t\\t\\tnewManagedFields[idx].Time = oldManagedFields[idx].Time\\n   217\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   218\u2192\\n   219\u2192\\t\\taccessor.SetManagedFields(newManagedFields)\\n   220\u2192\\t\\toutcome = \\\"equal_objects\\\"\\n   221\u2192\\t\\treturn newObj, nil\\n   222\u2192\\t}\\n   223\u2192\\n   224\u2192\\toutcome = \\\"unequal_objects_slow\\\"\\n   225\u2192\\treturn newObj, nil\\n   226\u2192}\\n   227\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code nil, nil, err\\n   159\u2192\\t}\\n   160\u2192\\tmanaged = NewManaged(managedFields, managed.Times())\\n   161\u2192\\n   162\u2192\\tif newObjTyped == nil {\\n   163\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, managed, nil\\n   164\u2192\\t}\\n   165\u2192\\n   166\u2192\\tnewObj, err := f.typeConverter.TypedToObject(newObjTyped)\\n   167\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   168\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert new typed object (%v) to object: %v\\\", objectGVKNN(patchObj), err)\\n   169\u2192\\t}\\n   170\u2192\\n   171\u2192\\tnewObjVersioned, err := f.toVersioned(newObj)\\n   172\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   173\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert new object (%v) to proper version: %v\\\", objectGVKNN(patchObj), err)\\n   174\u2192\\t}\\n   175\u2192\\tf.objectDefaulter.Default(newObjVersioned)\\n   176\u2192\\n   177\u2192\\tnewObjUnversioned, err := f.toUnversioned(newObjVersioned)\\n   178\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   179\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert to unversioned (%v): %v\\\", objectGVKNN(patchObj), err)\\n   180\u2192\\t}\\n   181\u2192\\treturn newObjUnversioned, managed, nil\\n   182\u2192}\\n   183\u2192\\n   184\u2192func (f *structuredMergeManager) toVersioned(obj runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   185\u2192\\treturn f.objectConverter.ConvertToVersion(obj, f.groupVersion)\\n   186\u2192}\\n   187\u2192\\n   188\u2192func (f *structuredMergeManager) toUnversioned(obj runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   189\u2192\\treturn f.objectConverter.ConvertToVersion(obj, f.hubVersion)\\n   190\u2192}\\n   191\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be   400\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   401\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   402\u2192\\n   403\u2192\\t\\tpatchObj, err := jsonpatch.DecodePatch(p.patchBytes)\\n   404\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   405\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\\n   406\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   407\u2192\\t\\tif len(patchObj) > maxJSONPatchOperations {\\n   408\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewRequestEntityTooLargeError(\\n   409\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"The allowed maximum operations in a JSON patch is %d, got %d\\\",\\n   410\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmaxJSONPatchOperations, len(patchObj)))\\n   411\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   412\u2192\\t\\tpatchedJS, err := patchObj.Apply(versionedJS)\\n   413\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   414\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewGenericServerResponse(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, \\\"\\\", schema.GroupResource{}, \\\"\\\", err.Error(), 0, false)\\n   415\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   416\u2192\\t\\treturn patchedJS, strictErrors, nil\\n   417\u2192\\tcase types.MergePatchType:\\n   418\u2192\\t\\tif p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationStrict || p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn {\\n   419\u2192\\t\\t\\tv := map[string]interface{}{}\\n   420\u2192\\t\\t\\tvar err error\\n   421\u2192\\t\\t\\tstrictErrors, err = kjson.UnmarshalStrict(p.patchBytes, &v)\\n   422\u2192\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   423\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"error decoding patch: %v\\\", err))\\n   424\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   425\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   426\u2192\\n   427\u2192\\t\\tpatchedJS, retErr = jsonpatch.MergePatch(versionedJS, p.patchBytes)\\n   428\u2192\\t\\tif retErr == jsonpatch.ErrBadJSONPatch {\\n   429\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewBadRequest(retErr.Error())\\n   430\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   431\u2192\\t\\treturn patchedJS, strictErrors, retErr\\n   432\u2192\\tdefault:\\n   433\u2192\\t\\t// only here as a safety net - go-restful filters content-type\\n   434\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"unknown Content-Type header for patch: %v\\\", p.patchType)\\n   435\u2192\\t}\\n   436\u2192}\\n   437\u2192\\n   438\u2192type smpPatcher struct {\\n   439\u2192\\t*patcher\\n   440\u2192\\n   441\u2192\\t// Schema\\n   442\u2192\\tschemaReferenceObj runtime.Object\\n   443\u2192\\tfieldManager       *managedfields.FieldManager\\n   444\u2192}\\n   445\u2192\\n   446\u2192func (p *smpPatcher) applyPatchToCurrentObject(requestContext context.Context, currentObject runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   447\u2192\\t// Since the patch is applied on versioned objects, we need to convert the\\n   448\u2192\\t// current object to versioned representation first.\\n   449\u2192\\tcurrentVersionedObject, err := p.unsafeConvertor.ConvertToVersion(currentObject, p.kind.GroupVersion())\\n   450\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   451\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   452\u2192\\t}\\n   453\u2192\\tversionedObjToUpdate, err := p.creater.New(p.kind)\\n   454\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   455\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   456\u2192\\t}\\n   457\u2192\\tif err := strategicPatchObject(requestContext, p.defaulter, currentVersionedObject, p.patchBytes, versionedObjToUpdate, p.schemaReferenceObj, p.validationDirective); err != nil {\\n   458\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   459\u2192\\t}\\n   460\u2192\\t// Convert the object back to the hub version\\n   461\u2192\\tnewObj, err := p.unsafeConvertor.ConvertToVersion(versionedObjToUpdate, p.hubGroupVersion)\\n   462\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   463\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   464\u2192\\t}\\n   465\u2192\\n   466\u2192\\tnewObj = p.fieldManager.UpdateNoErrors(currentObject, newObj, managerOrUserAgent(p.options.FieldManager, p.userAgent))\\n   467\u2192\\treturn newObj, nil\\n   468\u2192}\\n   469\u2192\\n   470\u2192func (p *smpPatcher) createNewObject(_ context.Context) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   471\u2192\\treturn nil, errors.NewNotFound(p.resource.GroupResource(), p.name)\\n   472\u2192}\\n   473\u2192\\n   474\u2192func newApplyPatcher(p *patcher, fieldManager *managedfields.FieldManager, unmarshalFn, unmarshalStrictFn func([]byte, interface{}) error) *applyPatcher {\\n   475\u2192\\treturn &applyPatcher{\\n   476\u2192\\t\\tfieldManager:        fieldManager,\\n   477\u2192\\t\\tpatch:               p.patchBytes,\\n   478\u2192\\t\\toptions:             p.options,\\n   479\u2192\\t\\tcreater:             p.creater,\\n   480\u2192\\t\\tkind:                p.kind,\\n   481\u2192\\t\\tuserAgent:           p.userAgent,\\n   482\u2192\\t\\tvalidationDirective: p.validationDirective,\\n   483\u2192\\t\\tunmarshalFn:         unmarshalFn,\\n   484\u2192\\t\\tunmarshalStrictFn:   unmarshalStrictFn,\\n   485\u2192\\t}\\n   486\u2192}\\n   487\u2192\\n   488\u2192type applyPatcher struct {\\n   489\u2192\\tpatch               []byte\\n   490\u2192\\toptions             *metav1.PatchOptions\\n   491\u2192\\tcreater             runtime.ObjectCreater\\n   492\u2192\\tkind                schema.GroupVersionKind\\n   493\u2192\\tfieldManager        *managedfields.FieldManager\\n   494\u2192\\tuserAgent           string\\n   495\u2192\\tvalidationDirective string\\n   496\u2192\\tunmarshalFn         func(data []byte, v interface{}) error\\n   497\u2192\\tunmarshalStrictFn   func(data []byte, v interface{}) error\\n   498\u2192}\\n   499\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should 472\u2192}\\n   473\u2192\\n   474\u2192func newApplyPatcher(p *patcher, fieldManager *managedfields.FieldManager, unmarshalFn, unmarshalStrictFn func([]byte, interface{}) error) *applyPatcher {\\n   475\u2192\\treturn &applyPatcher{\\n   476\u2192\\t\\tfieldManager:        fieldManager,\\n   477\u2192\\t\\tpatch:               p.patchBytes,\\n   478\u2192\\t\\toptions:             p.options,\\n   479\u2192\\t\\tcreater:             p.creater,\\n   480\u2192\\t\\tkind:                p.kind,\\n   481\u2192\\t\\tuserAgent:           p.userAgent,\\n   482\u2192\\t\\tvalidationDirective: p.validationDirective,\\n   483\u2192\\t\\tunmarshalFn:         unmarshalFn,\\n   484\u2192\\t\\tunmarshalStrictFn:   unmarshalStrictFn,\\n   485\u2192\\t}\\n   486\u2192}\\n   487\u2192\\n   488\u2192type applyPatcher struct {\\n   489\u2192\\tpatch               []byte\\n   490\u2192\\toptions             *metav1.PatchOptions\\n   491\u2192\\tcreater             runtime.ObjectCreater\\n   492\u2192\\tkind                schema.GroupVersionKind\\n   493\u2192\\tfieldManager        *managedfields.FieldManager\\n   494\u2192\\tuserAgent           string\\n   495\u2192\\tvalidationDirective string\\n   496\u2192\\tunmarshalFn         func(data []byte, v interface{}) error\\n   497\u2192\\tunmarshalStrictFn   func(data []byte, v interface{}) error\\n   498\u2192}\\n   499\u2192\\n   500\u2192func (p *applyPatcher) applyPatchToCurrentObject(requestContext context.Context, obj runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   501\u2192\\tforce := false\\n   502\u2192\\tif p.options.Force != nil {\\n   503\u2192\\t\\tforce = *p.options.Force\\n   504\u2192\\t}\\n   505\u2192\\tif p.fieldManager == nil {\\n   506\u2192\\t\\tpanic(\\\"FieldManager must be installed to run apply\\\")\\n   507\u2192\\t}\\n   508\u2192\\n   509\u2192\\tpatchObj := &unstructured.Unstructured{Object: map[string]interface{}{}}\\n   510\u2192\\tif err := p.unmarshalFn(p.patch, &patchObj.Object); err != nil {\\n   511\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"error decoding YAML: %v\\\", err))\\n   512\u2192\\t}\\n   513\u2192\\n   514\u2192\\tobj, err := p.fieldManager.Apply(obj, patchObj, p.options.FieldManager, force)\\n   515\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   516\u2192\\t\\treturn obj, err\\n   517\u2192\\t}\\n   518\u2192\\n   519\u2192\\t// TODO: spawn something to track deciding whether a fieldValidation=Strict\\n   520\u2192\\t// fatal error should return before an error from the apply operation\\n   521\u2192\\tif p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationStrict || p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn {\\n   522\u2192\\t\\tif err := p.unmarshalStrictFn(p.patch, &map[string]interface{}{}); err != nil {\\n   523\u2192\\t\\t\\tif p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationStrict {\\n   524\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"error strict decoding YAML: %v\\\", err))\\n   525\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   526\u2192\\t\\t\\taddStrictDecodingWarnings(requestContext, []error{err})\\n   527\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   528\u2192\\t}\\n   529\u2192\\treturn obj, nil\\n   530\u2192}\\n   531\u2192\\n   532\u2192func (p *applyPatcher) createNewObject(requestContext context.Context) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   533\u2192\\tobj, err := p.creater.New(p.kind)\\n   534\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   535\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create new object: %v\\\", err)\\n   536\u2192\\t}\\n   537\u2192\\treturn p.applyPatchToCurrentObject(requestContext, obj)\\n   538\u2192}\\n   539\u2192\\n   540\u2192// strategicPatchObject applies a strategic merge patch of `patchBytes` to\\n   541\u2192// `originalObject` and stores the result in `objToUpdate`.\\n   542\u2192// It additionally returns the map[string]interface{} representation of the\\n   543\u2192// `originalObject` and `patchBytes`.\\n   544\u2192// NOTE: Both `originalObject` and `objToUpdate` are supposed to be versioned.\\n   545\u2192func strategicPatchObject(\\n   546\u2192\\trequestContext context.Context,\\n   547\u2192\\tdefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter,\\n   548\u2192\\toriginalObject runtime.Object,\\n   549\u2192\\tpatchBytes []byte,\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"19de80b7-fcbb-41a9-81c6-7f2cd3d03e34\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:16.871Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"81ae2cd3-c47b-436b-b4e0-c60571b0dbc3\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"19de80b7-fcbb-41a9-81c6-7f2cd3d03e34\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01HEnbXCMVPaKmfLH3Gy8EkZ\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Perfect! details:\"}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":4761,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":64489,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":4761},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG58bWT9dJY3s8DacU9o\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"ee828fd1-35d1-499a-91be-877e20a9191e\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:18.890Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"ee828fd1-35d1-499a-91be-877e20a9191e\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01HEnbXCMVPaKmfLH3Gy8EkZ\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01W48HvbJgfh1CNsJyYWzwnG\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go\",\"limit\":100,\"offset\":650},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":4761,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":64489,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":4761},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG58bWT9dJY3s8DacU9o\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"32161d98-2abe-4d04-bbca-aa1f804b58c7\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:19.504Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"32161d98-2abe-4d04-bbca-aa1f804b58c7\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01W48HvbJgfh1CNsJyYWzwnG\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01W48HvbJgfh1CNsJyYWzwnG\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:19.507Z\",\"uuid\":\"f84f3bdd-4d4c-42f4-a4eb-f1521daf8210\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"32161d98-2abe-4d04-bbca-aa1f804b58c7\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01W48HvbJgfh1CNsJyYWzwnG\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"   650\u2192}\\n   651\u2192\\n   652\u2192// patchResource divides PatchResource for easier unit testing\\n   653\u2192func (p *patcher) patchResource(ctx context.Context, scope *RequestScope) (runtime.Object, bool, error) {\\n   654\u2192\\tp.namespace = request.NamespaceValue(ctx)\\n   655\u2192\\tswitch p.patchType {\\n   656\u2192\\tcase types.JSONPatchType, types.MergePatchType:\\n   657\u2192\\t\\tp.mechanism = &jsonPatcher{\\n   658\u2192\\t\\t\\tpatcher:      p,\\n   659\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldManager: scope.FieldManager,\\n   660\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   661\u2192\\tcase types.StrategicMergePatchType:\\n   662\u2192\\t\\tschemaReferenceObj, err := p.unsafeConvertor.ConvertToVersion(p.restPatcher.New(), p.kind.GroupVersion())\\n   663\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   664\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, false, err\\n   665\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   666\u2192\\t\\tp.mechanism = &smpPatcher{\\n   667\u2192\\t\\t\\tpatcher:            p,\\n   668\u2192\\t\\t\\tschemaReferenceObj: schemaReferenceObj,\\n   669\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldManager:       scope.FieldManager,\\n   670\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   671\u2192\\t// this case is unreachable if ServerSideApply is not enabled because we will have already rejected the content type\\n   672\u2192\\tcase types.ApplyYAMLPatchType:\\n   673\u2192\\t\\tp.mechanism = newApplyPatcher(p, scope.FieldManager, yaml.Unmarshal, yaml.UnmarshalStrict)\\n   674\u2192\\t\\tp.forceAllowCreate = true\\n   675\u2192\\tcase types.ApplyCBORPatchType:\\n   676\u2192\\t\\tif !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.CBORServingAndStorage) {\\n   677\u2192\\t\\t\\tutilruntime.HandleErrorWithContext(context.TODO(), nil, \\\"CBOR apply requests should be rejected before reaching this point unless the feature gate is enabled.\\\")\\n   678\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"%v: unimplemented patch type\\\", p.patchType)\\n   679\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   680\u2192\\n   681\u2192\\t\\t// The strict and non-strict funcs are the same here because any CBOR map with\\n   682\u2192\\t\\t// duplicate keys is invalid and always rejected outright regardless of strictness\\n   683\u2192\\t\\t// mode, and unknown field errors can't occur in practice because the type of the\\n   684\u2192\\t\\t// destination value for unmarshaling an apply configuration is always\\n   685\u2192\\t\\t// \\\"unstructured\\\".\\n   686\u2192\\t\\tp.mechanism = newApplyPatcher(p, scope.FieldManager, cbor.Unmarshal, cbor.Unmarshal)\\n   687\u2192\\t\\tp.forceAllowCreate = true\\n   688\u2192\\tdefault:\\n   689\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"%v: unimplemented patch type\\\", p.patchType)\\n   690\u2192\\t}\\n   691\u2192\\tdedupOwnerReferencesTransformer := func(_ context.Context, obj, _ runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   692\u2192\\t\\t// Dedup owner references after mutating admission happens\\n   693\u2192\\t\\tdedupOwnerReferencesAndAddWarning(obj, ctx, true)\\n   694\u2192\\t\\treturn obj, nil\\n   695\u2192\\t}\\n   696\u2192\\n   697\u2192\\ttransformers := []rest.TransformFunc{p.applyPatch, p.applyAdmission, dedupOwnerReferencesTransformer}\\n   698\u2192\\n   699\u2192\\twasCreated := false\\n   700\u2192\\tp.updatedObjectInfo = rest.DefaultUpdatedObjectInfo(nil, transformers...)\\n   701\u2192\\trequestFunc := func() (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   702\u2192\\t\\t// Pass in UpdateOptions to override UpdateStrategy.AllowUpdateOnCreate\\n   703\u2192\\t\\toptions := patchToUpdateOptions(p.options)\\n   704\u2192\\t\\tupdateObject, created, updateErr := p.restPatcher.Update(ctx, p.name, p.updatedObjectInfo, p.createValidation, p.updateValidation, p.forceAllowCreate, options)\\n   705\u2192\\t\\twasCreated = created\\n   706\u2192\\t\\treturn updateObject, updateErr\\n   707\u2192\\t}\\n   708\u2192\\tresult, err := finisher.FinishRequest(ctx, func() (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   709\u2192\\n   710\u2192\\t\\tresult, err := requestFunc()\\n   711\u2192\\t\\t// If the object wasn't committed to storage because it's serialized size was too large,\\n   712\u2192\\t\\t// it is safe to remove managedFields (which can be large) and try again.\\n   713\u2192\\t\\tif isTooLargeError(err) && p.patchType != types.ApplyYAMLPatchType && p.patchType != types.ApplyCBORPatchType {\\n   714\u2192\\t\\t\\tif _, accessorErr := meta.Accessor(p.restPatcher.New()); accessorErr == nil {\\n   715\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tp.updatedObjectInfo = rest.DefaultUpdatedObjectInfo(nil,\\n   716\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tp.applyPatch,\\n   717\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tp.applyAdmission,\\n   718\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdedupOwnerReferencesTransformer,\\n   719\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfunc(_ context.Context, obj, _ runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   720\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\taccessor, _ := meta.Accessor(obj)\\n   721\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\taccessor.SetManagedFields(nil)\\n   722\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn obj, nil\\n   723\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n   724\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tresult, err = requestFunc()\\n   725\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   726\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   727\u2192\\t\\treturn result, err\\n   728\u2192\\t})\\n   729\u2192\\n   730\u2192\\t// In case of a timeout error, the goroutine handling the request is still running.\\n   731\u2192\\t// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/d2c12afa4593e50a187075157d38748292b02733/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/finisher/finisher.go#L127-L146\\n   732\u2192\\t// We cannot reliably read the variable (data race!) and have to assume that\\n   733\u2192\\t// the object was not created.\\n   734\u2192\\tif errors.IsTimeout(err) {\\n   735\u2192\\t\\treturn result, false, err\\n   736\u2192\\t}\\n   737\u2192\\treturn result, wasCreated, err\\n   738\u2192}\\n   739\u2192\\n   740\u2192// applyPatchToObject applies a strategic merge patch of <patchMap> to\\n   741\u2192// <originalMap> and stores the result in <objToUpdate>.\\n   742\u2192// NOTE: <objToUpdate> must be a versioned object.\\n   743\u2192func applyPatchToObject(\\n   744\u2192\\trequestContext context.Context,\\n   745\u2192\\tdefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter,\\n   746\u2192\\toriginalMap map[string]interface{},\\n   747\u2192\\tpatchMap map[string]interface{},\\n   748\u2192\\tobjToUpdate runtime.Object,\\n   749\u2192\\tschemaReferenceObj runtime.Object,\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"fcc840be-2c28-4698-9373-b998d55fe465\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:19.506Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"32161d98-2abe-4d04-bbca-aa1f804b58c7\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"fcc840be-2c28-4698-9373-b998d55fe465\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01HEnbXCMVPaKmfLH3Gy8EkZ\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01DWHUUE8PLmJ5fpHSjgYSUc\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"grep (p *patcher) patchResource(ctx context.Context, scope *RequestScope) (runtime.Object, bool, error) {\\n\\tp.namespace = request.NamespaceValue(ctx)\\n\\tswitch p.patchType {\\n\\tcase types.JSONPatchType, types.MergePatchType:\\n\\t\\tp.mechanism = &jsonPatcher{\\n\\t\\t\\tpatcher:      p,\\n\\t\\t\\tfieldManager: scope.FieldManager,\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\tcase types.StrategicMergePatchType:\\n\\t\\tschemaReferenceObj, err := p.unsafeConvertor.ConvertToVersion(p.restPatcher.New(), p.kind.GroupVersion())\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, false, err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tp.mechanism = &smpPatcher{\\n\\t\\t\\tpatcher:            p,\\n\\t\\t\\tschemaReferenceObj: schemaReferenceObj,\\n\\t\\t\\tfieldManager:       scope.FieldManager,\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t// this case is unreachable if ServerSideApply is not enabled because we will have already rejected the content type\\n\\tcase types.ApplyYAMLPatchType:\\n\\t\\tp.mechanism = newApplyPatcher(p, scope.FieldManager, yaml.Unmarshal, yaml.UnmarshalStrict)\\n\\t\\tp.forceAllowCreate = true\\n\\tcase types.ApplyCBORPatchType:\\n\\t\\tif !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.CBORServingAndStorage) {\\n\\t\\t\\tutilruntime.HandleErrorWithContext(context.TODO(), nil, \\\"CBOR apply requests should be rejected before reaching this point unless the feature gate is enabled.\\\")\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"%v: unimplemented patch type\\\", p.patchType)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\t// The strict and non-strict funcs are the same here because any CBOR map with\\n\\t\\t// duplicate keys is invalid and always rejected outright regardless of strictness\\n\\t\\t// mode, and unknown field errors can't occur in practice because the type of the\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"db386112-4316-4fc9-a8c1-093965ebe7e0\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:20.089Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"bcec1db9-6a72-4a61-8ad5-3d1becb73f10\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"db386112-4316-4fc9-a8c1-093965ebe7e0\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01HEnbXCMVPaKmfLH3Gy8EkZ\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01EaRppq828Pjy7gEa18kCvv\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/capmanagers.go\",\"limit\":80},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":4761,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":64489,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":4761},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG58bWT9dJY3s8DacU9o\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"d3832c2c-aa58-45d7-ae27-f718cf2220a8\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:20.501Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d3832c2c-aa58-45d7-ae27-f718cf2220a8\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01EaRppq828Pjy7gEa18kCvv\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01EaRppq828Pjy7gEa18kCvv\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:20.504Z\",\"uuid\":\"a7f0c8cd-8eec-472a-91e2-cbfb0f7b CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package internal\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    26\u2192)\\n    27\u2192\\n    28\u2192type capManagersManager struct {\\n    29\u2192\\tfieldManager          Manager\\n    30\u2192\\tmaxUpdateManagers     int\\n    31\u2192\\toldUpdatesManagerName string\\n    32\u2192}\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192var _ Manager = &capManagersManager{}\\n    35\u2192\\n    36\u2192// NewCapManagersManager creates a new wrapped FieldManager which ensures that the number of managers from updates\\n    37\u2192// does not exceed maxUpdateManagers, by merging some of the oldest entries on each update.\\n    38\u2192func NewCapManagersManager(fieldManager Manager, maxUpdateManagers int) Manager {\\n    39\u2192\\treturn &capManagersManager{\\n    40\u2192\\t\\tfieldManager:          fieldManager,\\n    41\u2192\\t\\tmaxUpdateManagers:     maxUpdateManagers,\\n    42\u2192\\t\\toldUpdatesManagerName: \\\"ancient-changes\\\",\\n    43\u2192\\t}\\n    44\u2192}\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192// Update implements Manager.\\n    47\u2192func (f *capManagersManager) Update(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string) (runtime.Object, Managed, error) {\\n    48\u2192\\tobject, managed, err := f.fieldManager.Update(liveObj, newObj, managed, manager)\\n    49\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    50\u2192\\t\\treturn object, managed, err\\n    51\u2192\\t}\\n    52\u2192\\tif managed, err = f.capUpdateManagers(managed); err != nil {\\n    53\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to cap update managers: %v\\\", err)\\n    54\u2192\\t}\\n    55\u2192\\treturn object, managed, nil\\n    56\u2192}\\n    57\u2192\\n    58\u2192// Apply implements Manager.\\n    59\u2192func (f *capManagersManager) Apply(liveObj, appliedObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, fieldManager string, force bool) (runtime.Object, Managed, error) {\\n    60\u2192\\treturn f.fieldManager.Apply(liveObj, appliedObj, managed, fieldManager, force)\\n    61\u2192}\\n    62\u2192\\n    63\u2192// capUpdateManagers merges a number of the oldest update entries into versioned buckets,\\n    64\u2192// such that the number of entries from updates does not exceed f.maxUpdateManagers.\\n    65\u2192func (f *capManagersManager) capUpdateManagers(managed Managed) (newManaged Managed, err error) {\\n    66\u2192\\t// Gather all entries from updates\\n    67\u2192\\tupdaters := []string{}\\n    68\u2192\\tfor manager, fields := range managed.Fields() {\\n    69\u2192\\t\\tif !fields.Applied() {\\n    70\u2192\\t\\t\\tupdaters = append(updaters, manager)\\n    71\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    72\u2192\\t}\\n    73\u2192\\tif len(updaters) <= f.maxUpdateManagers {\\n    74\u2192\\t\\treturn managed, nil\\n    75\u2192\\t}\\n    76\u2192\\n    77\u2192\\t// If we have more than the maximum, sort the update entries by time, oldest first.\\n    78\u2192\\tsort.Slice(updaters, func(i, j int) bool {\\n    79\u2192\\t\\tiTime, jTime, iSeconds, jSeconds := managed.Times()[updaters[i]], managed.Times()[updaters[j]], int64(0), int64(0)\\n    80\u2192\\t\\tif iTime != nil {\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"57153959-1435-4a90-9147-209f4da29a80\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:20.504Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"d3832c2c-aa58-45d7-ae27-f718cf2220a8\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"57153959-1435-4a90-9147-209f4da29a80\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01HEnbXCMVPaKmfLH3Gy8EkZ\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01XcFAx7TjiofQRXw9QATF49\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/lastappliedmanager.go\",\"limit\":80},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":4761,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":64489,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":4761},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG58bWT9dJY3s8DacU9o\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"98c36aa8-f9fa-42c9-839f-c24696c2e007\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:20.898Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"98c36aa8-f9fa-42c9-839f-c24696c2e007\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01XcFAx7TjiofQRXw9QATF49\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01XcFAx7TjiofQRXw9QATF49\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:20.900Z\",\"uuid\":\"c88955c0-8ae3-     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2020 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package internal\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"encoding/json\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge\\\"\\n    29\u2192)\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192type lastAppliedManager struct {\\n    32\u2192\\tfieldManager    Manager\\n    33\u2192\\ttypeConverter   TypeConverter\\n    34\u2192\\tobjectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor\\n    35\u2192\\tgroupVersion    schema.GroupVersion\\n    36\u2192}\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192var _ Manager = &lastAppliedManager{}\\n    39\u2192\\n    40\u2192// NewLastAppliedManager converts the client-side apply annotation to\\n    41\u2192// server-side apply managed fields\\n    42\u2192func NewLastAppliedManager(fieldManager Manager, typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, groupVersion schema.GroupVersion) Manager {\\n    43\u2192\\treturn &lastAppliedManager{\\n    44\u2192\\t\\tfieldManager:    fieldManager,\\n    45\u2192\\t\\ttypeConverter:   typeConverter,\\n    46\u2192\\t\\tobjectConverter: objectConverter,\\n    47\u2192\\t\\tgroupVersion:    groupVersion,\\n    48\u2192\\t}\\n    49\u2192}\\n    50\u2192\\n    51\u2192// Update implements Manager.\\n    52\u2192func (f *lastAppliedManager) Update(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string) (runtime.Object, Managed, error) {\\n    53\u2192\\treturn f.fieldManager.Update(liveObj, newObj, managed, manager)\\n    54\u2192}\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192// Apply will consider the last-applied annotation\\n    57\u2192// for upgrading an object managed by client-side apply to server-side apply\\n    58\u2192// without conflicts.\\n    59\u2192func (f *lastAppliedManager) Apply(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string, force bool) (runtime.Object, Managed, error) {\\n    60\u2192\\tnewLiveObj, newManaged, newErr := f.fieldManager.Apply(liveObj, newObj, managed, manager, force)\\n    61\u2192\\t// Upgrade the client-side apply annotation only from kubectl server-side-apply.\\n    62\u2192\\t// To opt-out of this behavior, users may specify a different field manager.\\n    63\u2192\\tif manager != \\\"kubectl\\\" {\\n    64\u2192\\t\\treturn newLiveObj, newManaged, newErr\\n    65\u2192\\t}\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192\\t// Check if we have conflicts\\n    68\u2192\\tif newErr == nil {\\n    69\u2192\\t\\treturn newLiveObj, newManaged, newErr\\n    70\u2192\\t}\\n    71\u2192\\tconflicts, ok := newErr.(merge.Conflicts)\\n    72\u2192\\tif !ok {\\n    73\u2192\\t\\treturn newLiveObj, newManaged, newErr\\n    74\u2192\\t}\\n    75\u2192\\tconflictSet := conflictsToSet(conflicts)\\n    76\u2192\\n    77\u2192\\t// Check if conflicts are allowed due to client-side apply,\\n    78\u2192\\t// and if so, then force apply\\n    79\u2192\\tallowedConflictSet, err := f.allowedConflictsFromLastApplied(liveObj)\\n    80\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"62a303fc-071f-4019-ac1c-b06908483610\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:20.900Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"98c36aa8-f9fa-42c9-839f-c24696c2e007\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"62a303fc-071f-4019-ac1c-b06908483610\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01PZq8bQWYVkifPbdpZtaAQY\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Excellent! files\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":5183,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":69250,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":5183},\"output_tokens\":4,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG58tfpdiVDRJ7xNougT\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"b024caea-94d1-4adc-bf26-493260c87221\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:22.972Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"b024caea-94d1-4adc-bf26-493260c87221\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01NKpCjjNVhWTJQMxN1FNKpT\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge/update.go\\n/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge/conflict.go\\n/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed/merge.go\\n/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed/remove.go\\n/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed/tofieldset.go\\n/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed/parser.go\\n/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed/typed.go\\n/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed/compare.go\\n/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed/doc.go\\n/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed/validate.go\\n/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed/helpers.go\\n/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed/reconcile_schema.go\\n/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/schema/schemaschema.go\\n/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/
