    1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2018 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package merge\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    25\u2192)\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192// Conflict is a conflict on a specific field with the current manager of\\n    28\u2192// that field. It does implement the error interface so that it can be\\n    29\u2192// used as an error.\\n    30\u2192type Conflict struct {\\n    31\u2192\\tManager string\\n    32\u2192\\tPath    fieldpath.Path\\n    33\u2192}\\n    34\u2192\\n    35\u2192// Conflict is an error.\\n    36\u2192var _ error = Conflict{}\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192// Error formats the conflict as an error.\\n    39\u2192func (c Conflict) Error() string {\\n    40\u2192\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"conflict with %q: %v\\\", c.Manager, c.Path)\\n    41\u2192}\\n    42\u2192\\n    43\u2192// Equals returns true if c == c2\\n    44\u2192func (c Conflict) Equals(c2 Conflict) bool {\\n    45\u2192\\tif c.Manager != c2.Manager {\\n    46\u2192\\t\\treturn false\\n    47\u2192\\t}\\n    48\u2192\\treturn c.Path.Equals(c2.Path)\\n    49\u2192}\\n    50\u2192\\n    51\u2192// Conflicts accumulates multiple conflicts and aggregates them by managers.\\n    52\u2192type Conflicts []Conflict\\n    53\u2192\\n    54\u2192var _ error = Conflicts{}\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192// Error prints the list of conflicts, grouped by sorted managers.\\n    57\u2192func (conflicts Conflicts) Error() string {\\n    58\u2192\\tif len(conflicts) == 1 {\\n    59\u2192\\t\\treturn conflicts[0].Error()\\n    60\u2192\\t}\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192\\tm := map[string][]fieldpath.Path{}\\n    63\u2192\\tfor _, conflict := range conflicts {\\n    64\u2192\\t\\tm[conflict.Manager] = append(m[conflict.Manager], conflict.Path)\\n    65\u2192\\t}\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192\\tmanagers := []string{}\\n    68\u2192\\tfor manager := range m {\\n    69\u2192\\t\\tmanagers = append(managers, manager)\\n    70\u2192\\t}\\n    71\u2192\\n    72\u2192\\t// Print conflicts by sorted managers.\\n    73\u2192\\tsort.Strings(managers)\\n    74\u2192\\n    75\u2192\\tmessages := []string{}\\n    76\u2192\\tfor _, manager := range managers {\\n    77\u2192\\t\\tmessages = append(messages, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"conflicts with %q:\\\", manager))\\n    78\u2192\\t\\tfor _, path := range m[manager] {\\n    79\u2192\\t\\t\\tmessages = append(messages, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"- %v\\\", path))\\n    80\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    81\u2192\\t}\\n    82\u2192\\treturn strings.Join(messages, \\\"\\\\n\\\")\\n    83\u2192}\\n    84\u2192\\n    85\u2192// Equals returns true if the lists of conflicts are the same.\\n    86\u2192func (c Conflicts) Equals(c2 Conflicts) bool {\\n    87\u2192\\tif len(c) != len(c2) {\\n    88\u2192\\t\\treturn false\\n    89\u2192\\t}\\n    90\u2192\\tfor i := range c {\\n    91\u2192\\t\\tif !c[i].Equals(c2[i]) {\\n    92\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n    93\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    94\u2192\\t}\\n    95\u2192\\treturn true\\n    96\u2192}\\n    97\u2192\\n    98\u2192// ToSet aggregates conflicts for all managers into a single Set.\\n    99\u2192func (c Conflicts) ToSet() *fieldpath.Set {\\n   100\u2192\\tset := fieldpath.NewSet()\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"d4242f16-b4c3-4c9e-9643-e06e5d5f69d5\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:52.142Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"8de43383-4303-4003-add3-5126de387282\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d4242f16-b4c3-4c9e-9643-e06e5d5f69d5\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_017pDoaWaGF5ptRSZ4jCoDfo\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01WavCd4YqqCM3d1uckDqD6R\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge/update.go\",\"limit\":100},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":8924,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":27476,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":8924},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG56f6ChJqKbzk346UgF\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"7b937f4c-72f4-4520-9eee-07f4ca5b3943\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:52.479Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"7b937f4c-72f4-4520-9eee-07f4ca5b3943\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01WavCd4YqqCM3d1uckDqD6R\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01WavCd4YqqCM3d1uckDqD6R\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:52.482Z\",\"uuid\":\"dec7f880-2bce-4873-baa9-3e7f2e1500e2\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"7b937f4c-72f4-4520-9eee-07f4ca5b3943\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01WavCd4YqqCM3d1uckDqD6R\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2018 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     4\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     5\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     6\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     7\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n     8\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n     9\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    10\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    11\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    12\u2192*/\\n    13\u2192\\n    14\u2192package merge\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192import (\\n    17\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/value\\\"\\n    22\u2192)\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192// Converter is an interface to the conversion logic. The converter\\n    25\u2192// needs to be able to convert objects from one version to another.\\n    26\u2192type Converter interface {\\n    27\u2192\\tConvert(object *typed.TypedValue, version fieldpath.APIVersion) (*typed.TypedValue, error)\\n    28\u2192\\tIsMissingVersionError(error) bool\\n    29\u2192}\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192// UpdateBuilder allows you to create a new Updater by exposing all of\\n    32\u2192// the options and setting them once.\\n    33\u2192type UpdaterBuilder struct {\\n    34\u2192\\tConverter    Converter\\n    35\u2192\\tIgnoreFilter map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter\\n    36\u2192\\n    37\u2192\\t// IgnoredFields provides a set of fields to ignore for each\\n    38\u2192\\tIgnoredFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set\\n    39\u2192\\n    40\u2192\\t// Stop comparing the new object with old object after applying.\\n    41\u2192\\t// This was initially used to avoid spurious etcd update, but\\n    42\u2192\\t// since that's vastly inefficient, we've come-up with a better\\n    43\u2192\\t// way of doing that. Create this flag to stop it.\\n    44\u2192\\t// Comparing has become more expensive too now that we're not using\\n    45\u2192\\t// `Compare` but `value.Equals` so this gives an option to avoid it.\\n    46\u2192\\tReturnInputOnNoop bool\\n    47\u2192}\\n    48\u2192\\n    49\u2192func (u *UpdaterBuilder) BuildUpdater() *Updater {\\n    50\u2192\\treturn &Updater{\\n    51\u2192\\t\\tConverter:         u.Converter,\\n    52\u2192\\t\\tIgnoreFilter:      u.IgnoreFilter,\\n    53\u2192\\t\\tIgnoredFields:     u.IgnoredFields,\\n    54\u2192\\t\\treturnInputOnNoop: u.ReturnInputOnNoop,\\n    55\u2192\\t}\\n    56\u2192}\\n    57\u2192\\n    58\u2192// Updater is the object used to compute updated FieldSets and also\\n    59\u2192// merge the object on Apply.\\n    60\u2192type Updater struct {\\n    61\u2192\\t// Deprecated: This will eventually become private.\\n    62\u2192\\tConverter Converter\\n    63\u2192\\n    64\u2192\\t// Deprecated: This will eventually become private.\\n    65\u2192\\tIgnoredFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192\\t// Deprecated: This will eventually become private.\\n    68\u2192\\tIgnoreFilter map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192\\treturnInputOnNoop bool\\n    71\u2192}\\n    72\u2192\\n    73\u2192func (s *Updater) update(oldObject, newObject *typed.TypedValue, version fieldpath.APIVersion, managers fieldpath.ManagedFields, workflow string, force bool) (fieldpath.ManagedFields, *typed.Comparison, error) {\\n    74\u2192\\tconflicts := fieldpath.ManagedFields{}\\n    75\u2192\\tremoved := fieldpath.ManagedFields{}\\n    76\u2192\\tcompare, err := oldObject.Compare(newObject)\\n    77\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    78\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to compare objects: %v\\\", err)\\n    79\u2192\\t}\\n    80\u2192\\n    81\u2192\\tvar versions map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*typed.Comparison\\n    82\u2192\\n    83\u2192\\tif s.IgnoredFields != nil && s.IgnoreFilter != nil {\\n    84\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"IgnoreFilter and IgnoreFilter may not both be set\\\")\\n    85\u2192\\t}\\n    86\u2192\\tif s.IgnoredFields != nil {\\n    87\u2192\\t\\tversions = map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*typed.Comparison{\\n    88\u2192\\t\\t\\tversion: compare.ExcludeFields(s.IgnoredFields[version]),\\n    89\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    90\u2192\\t} else {\\n    91\u2192\\t\\tversions = map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*typed.Comparison{\\n    92\u2192\\t\\t\\tversion: compare.FilterFields(s.IgnoreFilter[version]),\\n    93\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    94\u2192\\t}\\n    95\u2192\\n    96\u2192\\tfor manager, managerSet := range managers {\\n    97\u2192\\t\\tif manager == workflow {\\n    98\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n    99\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   100\u2192\\t\\tcompare, ok := versions[managerSet.APIVersion()]\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"2e92acb1-a189-4291-b62f-fc901b3f9df4\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:52.482Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"7b937f4c-72f4-4520-9eee-07f4ca5b3943\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"2e92acb1-a189-4291-b62f-fc901b3f9df4\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_017pDoaWaGF5ptRSZ4jCoDfo\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_018kCkYPLUYZm5RYruzsks5Q\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"find     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2018 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package internal\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"encoding/json\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    28\u2192)\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192// ManagedInterface groups a fieldpath.ManagedFields together with the timestamps associated with each operation.\\n    31\u2192type ManagedInterface interface {\\n    32\u2192\\t// Fields gets the fieldpath.ManagedFields.\\n    33\u2192\\tFields() fieldpath.ManagedFields\\n    34\u2192\\n    35\u2192\\t// Times gets the timestamps associated with each operation.\\n    36\u2192\\tTimes() map[string]*metav1.Time\\n    37\u2192}\\n    38\u2192\\n    39\u2192type managedStruct struct {\\n    40\u2192\\tfields fieldpath.ManagedFields\\n    41\u2192\\ttimes  map[string]*metav1.Time\\n    42\u2192}\\n    43\u2192\\n    44\u2192var _ ManagedInterface = &managedStruct{}\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192// Fields implements ManagedInterface.\\n    47\u2192func (m *managedStruct) Fields() fieldpath.ManagedFields {\\n    48\u2192\\treturn m.fields\\n    49\u2192}\\n    50\u2192\\n    51\u2192// Times implements ManagedInterface.\\n    52\u2192func (m *managedStruct) Times() map[string]*metav1.Time {\\n    53\u2192\\treturn m.times\\n    54\u2192}\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192// NewEmptyManaged creates an empty ManagedInterface.\\n    57\u2192func NewEmptyManaged() ManagedInterface {\\n    58\u2192\\treturn NewManaged(fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, map[string]*metav1.Time{})\\n    59\u2192}\\n    60\u2192\\n    61\u2192// NewManaged creates a ManagedInterface from a fieldpath.ManagedFields and the timestamps associated with each operation.\\n    62\u2192func NewManaged(f fieldpath.ManagedFields, t map[string]*metav1.Time) ManagedInterface {\\n    63\u2192\\treturn &managedStruct{\\n    64\u2192\\t\\tfields: f,\\n    65\u2192\\t\\ttimes:  t,\\n    66\u2192\\t}\\n    67\u2192}\\n    68\u2192\\n    69\u2192// RemoveObjectManagedFields removes the ManagedFields from the object\\n    70\u2192// before we merge so that it doesn't appear in the ManagedFields\\n    71\u2192// recursively.\\n    72\u2192func RemoveObjectManagedFields(obj runtime.Object) {\\n    73\u2192\\taccessor, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\\n    74\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    75\u2192\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"couldn't get accessor: %v\\\", err))\\n    76\u2192\\t}\\n    77\u2192\\taccessor.SetManagedFields(nil)\\n    78\u2192}\\n    79\u2192\\n    80\u2192// EncodeObjectManagedFields converts and stores the fieldpathManagedFields into the objects ManagedFields\\n    81\u2192func EncodeObjectManagedFields(obj runtime.Object, managed ManagedInterface) error {\\n    82\u2192\\taccessor, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\\n    83\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    84\u2192\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"couldn't get accessor: %v\\\", err))\\n    85\u2192\\t}\\n    86\u2192\\n    87\u2192\\tencodedManagedFields, err := encodeManagedFields(managed)\\n    88\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    89\u2192\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert back managed fields to API: %v\\\", err)\\n    90\u2192\\t}\\n    91\u2192\\taccessor.SetManagedFields(encodedManagedFields)\\n    92\u2192\\n    93\u2192\\treturn nil\\n    94\u2192}\\n    95\u2192\\n    96\u2192// DecodeManagedFields converts ManagedFields from the wire format (api format)\\n    97\u2192// to the format used by sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff\\n    98\u2192func DecodeManagedFields(encodedManagedFields []metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry) (ManagedInterface, error) {\\n    99\u2192\\tmanaged := managedStruct{}\\n   100\u2192\\tmanaged.fields = make(fieldpath.ManagedFields, len(encodedManagedFields))\\n   101\u2192\\tmanaged.times = make(map[string]*metav1.Time, len(encodedManagedFields))\\n   102\u2192\\n   103\u2192\\tfor i, encodedVersionedSet := range encodedManagedFields {\\n   104\u2192\\t\\tswitch encodedVersionedSet.Operation {\\n   105\u2192\\t\\tcase metav1.ManagedFieldsOperationApply, metav1.ManagedFieldsOperationUpdate:\\n   106\u2192\\t\\tdefault:\\n   107\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"operation must be `Apply` or `Update`\\\")\\n   108\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   109\u2192\\t\\tif len(encodedVersionedSet.APIVersion) < 1 {\\n   110\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"apiVersion must not be empty\\\")\\n   111\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   112\u2192\\t\\tswitch encodedVersionedSet.FieldsType {\\n   113\u2192\\t\\tcase \\\"FieldsV1\\\":\\n   114\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Valid case.\\n   115\u2192\\t\\tcase \\\"\\\":\\n   116\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"missing fieldsType in managed fields entry %d\\\", i)\\n   117\u2192\\t\\tdefault:\\n   118\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid fieldsType %q in managed fields entry %d\\\", encodedVersionedSet.FieldsType, i)\\n   119\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   120\u2192\\t\\tmanager, err := BuildManagerIdentifier(&encodedVersionedSet)\\n   121\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   122\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"error decoding manager from %v: %v\\\", encodedVersionedSet, err)\\n   123\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   124\u2192\\t\\tmanaged.fields[manager], err = decodeVersionedSet(&encodedVersionedSet)\\n   125\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   126\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"error decoding versioned set from %v: %v\\\", encodedVersionedSet, err)\\n   127\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   128\u2192\\t\\tmanaged.times[manager] = encodedVersionedSet.Time\\n   129\u2192\\t}\\n   130\u2192\\treturn &managed, nil\\n   131\u2192}\\n   132\u2192\\n   133\u2192// BuildManagerIdentifier creates a manager identifier string from a ManagedFieldsEntry\\n   134\u2192func BuildManagerIdentifier(encodedManager *metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry) (manager string, err error) {\\n   135\u2192\\tencodedManagerCopy := *encodedManager\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192\\t// Never include fields type in the manager identifier\\n   138\u2192\\tencodedManagerCopy.FieldsType = \\\"\\\"\\n   139\u2192\\n   140\u2192\\t// Never include the fields in the manager identifier\\n   141\u2192\\tencodedManagerCopy.FieldsV1 = nil\\n   142\u2192\\n   143\u2192\\t// Never include the time in the manager identifier\\n   144\u2192\\tencodedManagerCopy.Time = nil\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192\\t// For appliers, don't include the APIVersion in the manager identifier,\\n   147\u2192\\t// so it will always have the same manager identifier each time it applied.\\n   148\u2192\\tif encodedManager.Operation == metav1.ManagedFieldsOperationApply {\\n   149\u2192\\t\\tencodedManagerCopy.APIVersion = \\\"\\\"\\n   150\u2192\\t}\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"fdaada34-4f38-4442-a868-bb5b32c5787a\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:55.148Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"3d1049ce-b184-46f5-a217-0ace8c885c45\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"fdaada34-4f38-4442-a868-bb5b32c5787a\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01H1r4ByVcJANYNfjModVGQC\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01TPDiSKEbNTFf8mS8Y4LG4C\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/structuredmerge.go\",\"limit\":150},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":3851,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":36400,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":3851},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG56rV444X31wwkjjQzi\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"c3973f8c-f1b9-4fbd-b400-fe745536425e\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:55.457Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"c3973f8c-f1b9-4fbd-b400-fe745536425e\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01TPDiSKEbNTFf8mS8Y4LG4C\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01TPDiSKEbNTFf8mS8Y4LG4C\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:55.460Z\",\"uuid\":\"1e080d15-2055-4f 16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package internal\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n    30\u2192)\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192type structuredMergeManager struct {\\n    33\u2192\\ttypeConverter   TypeConverter\\n    34\u2192\\tobjectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor\\n    35\u2192\\tobjectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter\\n    36\u2192\\tgroupVersion    schema.GroupVersion\\n    37\u2192\\thubVersion      schema.GroupVersion\\n    38\u2192\\tupdater         merge.Updater\\n    39\u2192}\\n    40\u2192\\n    41\u2192var _ Manager = &structuredMergeManager{}\\n    42\u2192\\n    43\u2192// NewStructuredMergeManager creates a new Manager that merges apply requests\\n    44\u2192// and update managed fields for other types of requests.\\n    45\u2192func NewStructuredMergeManager(typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter, gv schema.GroupVersion, hub schema.GroupVersion, resetFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter) (Manager, error) {\\n    46\u2192\\tif typeConverter == nil {\\n    47\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"typeconverter must not be nil\\\")\\n    48\u2192\\t}\\n    49\u2192\\treturn &structuredMergeManager{\\n    50\u2192\\t\\ttypeConverter:   typeConverter,\\n    51\u2192\\t\\tobjectConverter: objectConverter,\\n    52\u2192\\t\\tobjectDefaulter: objectDefaulter,\\n    53\u2192\\t\\tgroupVersion:    gv,\\n    54\u2192\\t\\thubVersion:      hub,\\n    55\u2192\\t\\tupdater: merge.Updater{\\n    56\u2192\\t\\t\\tConverter:    newVersionConverter(typeConverter, objectConverter, hub), // This is the converter provided to SMD from k8s\\n    57\u2192\\t\\t\\tIgnoreFilter: resetFields,\\n    58\u2192\\t\\t},\\n    59\u2192\\t}, nil\\n    60\u2192}\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192// NewCRDStructuredMergeManager creates a new Manager specifically for\\n    63\u2192// CRDs. This allows for the possibility of fields which are not defined\\n    64\u2192// in models, as well as having no models defined at all.\\n    65\u2192func NewCRDStructuredMergeManager(typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter, gv schema.GroupVersion, hub schema.GroupVersion, resetFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter) (_ Manager, err error) {\\n    66\u2192\\treturn &structuredMergeManager{\\n    67\u2192\\t\\ttypeConverter:   typeConverter,\\n    68\u2192\\t\\tobjectConverter: objectConverter,\\n    69\u2192\\t\\tobjectDefaulter: objectDefaulter,\\n    70\u2192\\t\\tgroupVersion:    gv,\\n    71\u2192\\t\\thubVersion:      hub,\\n    72\u2192\\t\\tupdater: merge.Updater{\\n    73\u2192\\t\\t\\tConverter:    newCRDVersionConverter(typeConverter, objectConverter, hub),\\n    74\u2192\\t\\t\\tIgnoreFilter: resetFields,\\n    75\u2192\\t\\t},\\n    76\u2192\\t}, nil\\n    77\u2192}\\n    78\u2192\\n    79\u2192func objectGVKNN(obj runtime.Object) string {\\n    80\u2192\\tname := \\\"<unknown>\\\"\\n    81\u2192\\tnamespace := \\\"<unknown>\\\"\\n    82\u2192\\tif accessor, err := meta.Accessor(obj); err == nil {\\n    83\u2192\\t\\tname = accessor.GetName()\\n    84\u2192\\t\\tnamespace = accessor.GetNamespace()\\n    85\u2192\\t}\\n    86\u2192\\n    87\u2192\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%v/%v; %v\\\", namespace, name, obj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind())\\n    88\u2192}\\n    89\u2192\\n    90\u2192// Update implements Manager.\\n    91\u2192func (f *structuredMergeManager) Update(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string) (runtime.Object, Managed, error) {\\n    92\u2192\\tnewObjVersioned, err := f.toVersioned(newObj)\\n    93\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    94\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert new object (%v) to proper version (%v): %v\\\", objectGVKNN(newObj), f.groupVersion, err)\\n    95\u2192\\t}\\n    96\u2192\\tliveObjVersioned, err := f.toVersioned(liveObj)\\n    97\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    98\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert live object (%v) to proper version: %v\\\", objectGVKNN(liveObj), err)\\n    99\u2192\\t}\\n   100\u2192\\tnewObjTyped, err := f.typeConverter.ObjectToTyped(newObjVersioned, typed.AllowDuplicates)\\n   101\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   102\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert new object (%v) to smd typed: %v\\\", objectGVKNN(newObjVersioned), err)\\n   103\u2192\\t}\\n   104\u2192\\tliveObjTyped, err := f.typeConverter.ObjectToTyped(liveObjVersioned, typed.AllowDuplicates)\\n   105\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   106\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert live object (%v) to smd typed: %v\\\", objectGVKNN(liveObjVersioned), err)\\n   107\u2192\\t}\\n   108\u2192\\tapiVersion := fieldpath.APIVersion(f.groupVersion.String())\\n   109\u2192\\n   110\u2192\\t// TODO(apelisse) use the first return value when unions are implemented\\n   111\u2192\\t_, managedFields, err := f.updater.Update(liveObjTyped, newObjTyped, apiVersion, managed.Fields(), manager)\\n   112\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   113\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to update ManagedFields (%v): %v\\\", objectGVKNN(newObjVersioned), err)\\n   114\u2192\\t}\\n   115\u2192\\tmanaged = NewManaged(managedFields, managed.Times())\\n   116\u2192\\n   117\u2192\\treturn newObj, managed, nil\\n   118\u2192}\\n   119\u2192\\n   120\u2192// Apply implements Manager.\\n   121\u2192func (f *structuredMergeManager) Apply(liveObj, patchObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string, force bool) (runtime.Object, Managed, error) {\\n   122\u2192\\t// Check that the patch object has the same version as the live object\\n   123\u2192\\tif patchVersion := patchObj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind().GroupVersion(); patchVersion != f.groupVersion {\\n   124\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil,\\n   125\u2192\\t\\t\\terrors.NewBadRequest(\\n   126\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"Incorrect version specified in apply patch. \\\"+\\n   127\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Specified patch version: %s, expected: %s\\\",\\n   128\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpatchVersion, f.groupVersion))\\n   129\u2192\\t}\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192\\tpatchObjMeta, err := meta.Accessor(patchObj)\\n   132\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   133\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"couldn't get accessor: %v\\\", err)\\n   134\u2192\\t}\\n   135\u2192\\tif patchObjMeta.GetManagedFields() != nil {\\n   136\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewBadRequest(\\\"metadata.managedFields must be nil\\\")\\n   137\u2192\\t}\\n   138\u2192\\n   139\u2192\\tliveObjVersioned, err := f.toVersioned(liveObj)\\n   140\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   141\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert live object (%v) to proper version: %v\\\", objectGVKNN(liveObj), err)\\n   142\u2192\\t}\\n   143\u2192\\n   144\u2192\\t// Don't allow duplicates in the applied object.\\n   145\u2192\\tpatchObjTyped, err := f.typeConverter.ObjectToTyped(patchObj)\\n   146\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   147\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create typed patch object (%v): %v\\\", objectGVKNN(patchObj), err)\\n   148\u2192\\t}\\n   149\u2192\\n   150\u2192\\tliveObjTyped, err := f.typeConverter.ObjectToTyped(liveObjVersioned, typed.AllowDuplicates)\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code serializer defined for %v\\\", baseContentType), w, req)\\n   158\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   159\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   160\u2192\\t\\tgv := scope.Kind.GroupVersion()\\n   161\u2192\\n metav1.FieldValidationWarn || validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationStrict {\\n   165\u2192\\t\\t\\tdecodeSerializer = s.StrictSerializer\\n   166\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   167\u2192\\n scope.HubGroupVersion),\\n   171\u2192\\t\\t)\\n   172\u2192\\n   173\u2192\\t\\tuserInfo, _ := request.UserFrom(ctx)\\n   174\u2192\\t\\tstaticCreateAttributes := admission.NewAttributesRecord(\\n 185\u2192\\t\\t\\tuserInfo)\\n   186\u2192\\t\\tstaticUpdateAttributes := admission.NewAttributesRecord(\\n   187\u2192\\t\\t\\tnil,\\n   188\u2192\\t\\t\\tnil,\\n   189\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.Kind,\\n   190\u2192\\t\\t\\tnamespace,\\n   191\u2192\\t\\t\\tname,\\n   192\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.Resource,\\n   193\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.Subresource,\\n   194\u2192\\t\\t\\tadmission.Update,\\n   195\u2192\\t\\t\\tpatchToUpdateOptions(options),\\n   196\u2192\\t\\t\\tdryrun.IsDryRun(options.DryRun),\\n   197\u2192\\t\\t\\tuserInfo,\\n   198\u2192\\t\\t)\\n   199\u2192\\n   200\u2192\\t\\tadmit = fieldmanager.NewManagedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController(admit)\\n   201\u2192\\n   202\u2192\\t\\tmutatingAdmission, _ := admit.(admission.MutationInterface)\\n   203\u2192\\t\\tcreateAuthorizerAttributes := authorizer.AttributesRecord{\\n   204\u2192\\t\\t\\tUser:            userInfo,\\n   205\u2192\\t\\t\\tResourceRequest: true,\\n   206\u2192\\t\\t\\tPath:            req.URL.Path,\\n   207\u2192\\t\\t\\tVerb:            \\\"create\\\",\\n   208\u2192\\t\\t\\tAPIGroup:        scope.Resource.Group,\\n   209\u2192\\t\\t\\tAPIVersion:      scope.Resource.Version,\\n   210\u2192\\t\\t\\tResource:        scope.Resource.Resource,\\n   211\u2192\\t\\t\\tSubresource:     scope.Subresource,\\n   212\u2192\\t\\t\\tNamespace:       namespace,\\n   213\u2192\\t\\t\\tName:            name,\\n   214\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   215\u2192\\n   216\u2192\\t\\tp := patcher{\\n   217\u2192\\t\\t\\tnamer:               scope.Namer,\\n   218\u2192\\t\\t\\tcreater:             scope.Creater,\\n   219\u2192\\t\\t\\tdefaulter:           scope.Defaulter,\\n   220\u2192\\t\\t\\ttyper:               scope.Typer,\\n   221\u2192\\t\\t\\tunsafeConvertor:     scope.UnsafeConvertor,\\n   222\u2192\\t\\t\\tkind:                scope.Kind,\\n   223\u2192\\t\\t\\tresource:            scope.Resource,\\n   224\u2192\\t\\t\\tsubresource:         scope.Subresource,\\n   225\u2192\\t\\t\\tdryRun:              dryrun.IsDryRun(options.DryRun),\\n   226\u2192\\t\\t\\tvalidationDirective: validationDirective,\\n   227\u2192\\n   228\u2192\\t\\t\\tobjectInterfaces: scope,\\n   229\u2192\\n   230\u2192\\t\\t\\thubGroupVersion: scope.HubGroupVersion,\\n   231\u2192\\n   232\u2192\\t\\t\\tcreateValidation: withAuthorization(rest.AdmissionToValidateObjectFunc(admit, staticCreateAttributes, scope), scope.Authorizer, createAuthorizerAttributes),\\n   233\u2192\\t\\t\\tupdateValidation: rest.AdmissionToValidateObjectUpdateFunc(admit, staticUpdateAttributes, scope),\\n   234\u2192\\t\\t\\tadmissionCheck:   mutatingAdmission,\\n   235\u2192\\n   236\u2192\\t\\t\\tcodec: codec,\\n   237\u2192\\n   238\u2192\\t\\t\\toptions: options,\\n   239\u2192\\n   240\u2192\\t\\t\\trestPatcher: r,\\n   241\u2192\\t\\t\\tname:        name,\\n   242\u2192\\t\\t\\tpatchType:   patchType,\\n   243\u2192\\t\\t\\tpatchBytes:  patchBytes,\\n   244\u2192\\t\\t\\tuserAgent:   req.UserAgent(),\\n   245\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   246\u2192\\n   247\u2192\\t\\tresult, wasCreated, err := p.patchResource(ctx, scope)\\n   248\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   249\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n   250\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   251\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   252\u2192\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"Object stored in database\\\")\\n   253\u2192\\n   254\u2192\\t\\tstatus := http.StatusOK\\n   255\u2192\\t\\tif wasCreated {\\n   256\u2192\\t\\t\\tstatus = http.StatusCreated\\n   257\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   258\u2192\\n   259\u2192\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"About to write a response\\\")\\n   260\u2192\\t\\tdefer span.AddEvent(\\\"Writing http response done\\\")\\n   261\u2192\\t\\ttransformResponseObject(ctx, scope, req, w, status, outputMediaType, result)\\n   262\u2192\\t}\\n   263\u2192}\\n   264\u2192\\n   265\u2192type mutateObjectUpdateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object) error\\n   266\u2192\\n   267\u2192// patcher breaks the process of patch application and retries into smaller\\n   268\u2192// pieces of functionality.\\n   269\u2192// TODO: Use builder pattern to construct this object?\\n   270\u2192// TODO: As part of that effort, some aspects of PatchResource above could be\\n   271\u2192// moved into this type.\\n   272\u2192type patcher struct {\\n   273\u2192\\t// Pieces of RequestScope\\n   274\u2192\\tnamer               ScopeNamer\\n   275\u2192\\tcreater             runtime.ObjectCreater\\n   276\u2192\\tdefaulter           runtime.ObjectDefaulter\\n   277\u2192\\ttyper               runtime.ObjectTyper\\n   278\u2192\\tunsafeConvertor     runtime.ObjectConvertor\\n   279\u2192\\tresource            schema.GroupVersionResource\\n   280\u2192\\tkind                schema.GroupVersionKind\\n   281\u2192\\tsubresource         string\\n   282\u2192\\tdryRun              bool\\n   283\u2192\\tvalidationDirective string\\n   284\u2192\\n   285\u2192\\tobjectInterfaces admission.ObjectInterfaces\\n   286\u2192\\n   287\u2192\\thubGroupVersion schema.GroupVersion\\n   288\u2192\\n   289\u2192\\t// Validation functions\\n   290\u2192\\tcreateValidation rest.ValidateObjectFunc\\n   291\u2192\\tupdateValidation rest.ValidateObjectUpdateFunc\\n   292\u2192\\tadmissionCheck   admission.MutationInterface\\n   293\u2192\\n   294\u2192\\tcodec runtime.Codec\\n   295\u2192\\n   296\u2192\\toptions *metav1.PatchOptions\\n   297\u2192\\n   298\u2192\\t// Operation information\\n   299\u2192\\trestPatcher rest.Patcher\\n   300\u2192\\tname        string\\n   301\u2192\\tpatchType   types.PatchType\\n   302\u2192\\tpatchBytes  []byte\\n   303\u2192\\tuserAgent   string\\n   304\u2192\\n   305\u2192\\t// Set at invocation-time (by applyPatch) and immutable thereafter\\n   306\u2192\\tnamespace         string\\n   307\u2192\\tupdatedObjectInfo rest.UpdatedObjectInfo\\n   308\u2192\\tmechanism         patchMechanism\\n   309\u2192\\tforceAllowCreate  bool\\n   310\u2192}\\n   311\u2192\\n   312\u2192type patchMechanism interface {\\n   313\u2192\\tapplyPatchToCurrentObject(requextContext context.Context, currentObject runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error)\\n   314\u2192\\tcreateNewObject(requestContext context.Context) (runtime.Object, error)\\n   315\u2192}\\n   316\u2192\\n   317\u2192type jsonPatcher struct {\\n   318\u2192\\t*patcher\\n   319\u2192\\n   320\u2192\\tfieldManager *managedfields.FieldManager\\n   321\u2192}\\n   322\u2192\\n   323\u2192func (p *jsonPatcher) applyPatchToCurrentObject(requestContext context.Context, currentObject runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   324\u2192\\t// Encode will convert & return a versioned object in JSON.\\n   325\u2192\\tcurrentObjJS, err := runtime.Encode(p.codec, currentObject)\\n   326\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   327\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   328\u2192\\t}\\n   329\u2192\\n   330\u2192\\t// Apply the patch.\\n   331\u2192\\tpatchedObjJS, appliedStrictErrs, err := p.applyJSPatch(currentObjJS)\\n   332\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   333\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   334\u2192\\t}\\n   335\u2192\\n   336\u2192\\t// Construct the resulting typed, unversioned object.\\n   337\u2192\\tobjToUpdate := p.restPatcher.New()\\n   338\u2192\\tif err := runtime.DecodeInto(p.codec, patchedObjJS, objToUpdate); err != nil {\\n   339\u2192\\t\\tstrictError, isStrictError := runtime.AsStrictDecodingError(err)\\n   340\u2192\\t\\tswitch {\\n   341\u2192\\t\\tcase !isStrictError:\\n   342\u2192\\t\\t\\t// disregard any appliedStrictErrs, because it's an incomplete\\n   343\u2192\\t\\t\\t// list of strict errors given that we don't know what fields were\\n   344\u2192\\t\\t\\t// unknown because DecodeInto failed. Non-strict errors trump in this case.\\n   345\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{}, \\\"\\\", field.ErrorList{\\n   346\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfield.Invalid(field.NewPath(\\\"patch\\\"), string(patchedObjJS), err.Error()),\\n   347\u2192\\t\\t\\t})\\n   348\u2192\\t\\tcase p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn:\\n   349\u2192\\t\\t\\taddStrictDecodingWarnings(requestContext, append(appliedStrictErrs, strictError.Errors()...))\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code 16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package internal\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"bytes\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\n    22\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    25\u2192)\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192// EmptyFields represents a set with no paths\\n    28\u2192// It looks like metav1.Fields{Raw: []byte(\\\"{}\\\")}\\n    29\u2192var EmptyFields = func() metav1.FieldsV1 {\\n    30\u2192\\tf, err := SetToFields(*fieldpath.NewSet())\\n    31\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    32\u2192\\t\\tpanic(\\\"should never happen\\\")\\n    33\u2192\\t}\\n    34\u2192\\treturn f\\n    35\u2192}()\\n    36\u2192\\n    37\u2192// FieldsToSet creates a set paths from an input trie of fields\\n    38\u2192func FieldsToSet(f metav1.FieldsV1) (s fieldpath.Set, err error) {\\n    39\u2192\\terr = s.FromJSON(bytes.NewReader(f.Raw))\\n    40\u2192\\treturn s, err\\n    41\u2192}\\n    42\u2192\\n    43\u2192// SetToFields creates a trie of fields from an input set of paths\\n    44\u2192func SetToFields(s fieldpath.Set) (f metav1.FieldsV1, err error) {\\n    45\u2192\\tf.Raw, err = s.ToJSON()\\n    46\u2192\\treturn f, err\\n    47\u2192}\\n    48\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"ca695293-1f45-43e6-91cc-fb99b46c53ae\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:59.459Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"e4c84d02-eb68-46cc-a448-db9611c2f952\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"ca695293-1f45-43e6-91cc-fb99b46c53ae\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01QTwsxaZRJVyihuUmwxdNca\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_012KuSxQAEnMfXUPwcRAGQ92\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go\",\"limit\":100,\"offset\":320},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":8091,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":40251,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":8091},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG576pWrREKVZ8gk9xi6\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"4c339a66-d6c7-440d-9566-a0414e548321\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:59.922Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"4c339a66-d6c7-440d-9566-a0414e548321\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_012KuSxQAEnMfXUPwcRAGQ92\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_012KuSxQAEnMfXUPwcRAGQ92\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:59.925Z\",\"uuid\":\"64ae6215-c4c8-4831-bb75-8f8cd6d233dc\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"4c339a66-d6c7-440d-9566-a0414e548321\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_012KuSxQAEnMfXUPwcRAGQ92\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"   320\u2192\\tfieldManager *managedfields.FieldManager\\n   321\u2192}\\n   322\u2192\\n   323\u2192func (p *jsonPatcher) applyPatchToCurrentObject(requestContext context.Context, currentObject runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   324\u2192\\t// Encode will convert & return a versioned object in JSON.\\n   325\u2192\\tcurrentObjJS, err := runtime.Encode(p.codec, currentObject)\\n   326\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   327\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   328\u2192\\t}\\n   329\u2192\\n   330\u2192\\t// Apply the patch.\\n   331\u2192\\tpatchedObjJS, appliedStrictErrs, err := p.applyJSPatch(currentObjJS)\\n   332\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   333\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   334\u2192\\t}\\n   335\u2192\\n   336\u2192\\t// Construct the resulting typed, unversioned object.\\n   337\u2192\\tobjToUpdate := p.restPatcher.New()\\n   338\u2192\\tif err := runtime.DecodeInto(p.codec, patchedObjJS, objToUpdate); err != nil {\\n   339\u2192\\t\\tstrictError, isStrictError := runtime.AsStrictDecodingError(err)\\n   340\u2192\\t\\tswitch {\\n   341\u2192\\t\\tcase !isStrictError:\\n   342\u2192\\t\\t\\t// disregard any appliedStrictErrs, because it's an incomplete\\n   343\u2192\\t\\t\\t// list of strict errors given that we don't know what fields were\\n   344\u2192\\t\\t\\t// unknown because DecodeInto failed. Non-strict errors trump in this case.\\n   345\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{}, \\\"\\\", field.ErrorList{\\n   346\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfield.Invalid(field.NewPath(\\\"patch\\\"), string(patchedObjJS), err.Error()),\\n   347\u2192\\t\\t\\t})\\n   348\u2192\\t\\tcase p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn:\\n   349\u2192\\t\\t\\taddStrictDecodingWarnings(requestContext, append(appliedStrictErrs, strictError.Errors()...))\\n   350\u2192\\t\\tdefault:\\n   351\u2192\\t\\t\\tstrictDecodingError := runtime.NewStrictDecodingError(append(appliedStrictErrs, strictError.Errors()...))\\n   352\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{}, \\\"\\\", field.ErrorList{\\n   353\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfield.Invalid(field.NewPath(\\\"patch\\\"), string(patchedObjJS), strictDecodingError.Error()),\\n   354\u2192\\t\\t\\t})\\n   355\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   356\u2192\\t} else if len(appliedStrictErrs) > 0 {\\n   357\u2192\\t\\tswitch {\\n   358\u2192\\t\\tcase p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn:\\n   359\u2192\\t\\t\\taddStrictDecodingWarnings(requestContext, appliedStrictErrs)\\n   360\u2192\\t\\tdefault:\\n   361\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{}, \\\"\\\", field.ErrorList{\\n   362\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfield.Invalid(field.NewPath(\\\"patch\\\"), string(patchedObjJS), runtime.NewStrictDecodingError(appliedStrictErrs).Error()),\\n   363\u2192\\t\\t\\t})\\n   364\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   365\u2192\\t}\\n   366\u2192\\n   367\u2192\\tif p.options == nil {\\n   368\u2192\\t\\t// Provide a more informative error for the crash that would\\n   369\u2192\\t\\t// happen on the next line\\n   370\u2192\\t\\tpanic(\\\"PatchOptions required but not provided\\\")\\n   371\u2192\\t}\\n   372\u2192\\tobjToUpdate = p.fieldManager.UpdateNoErrors(currentObject, objToUpdate, managerOrUserAgent(p.options.FieldManager, p.userAgent))\\n   373\u2192\\treturn objToUpdate, nil\\n   374\u2192}\\n   375\u2192\\n   376\u2192func (p *jsonPatcher) createNewObject(_ context.Context) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   377\u2192\\treturn nil, errors.NewNotFound(p.resource.GroupResource(), p.name)\\n   378\u2192}\\n   379\u2192\\n   380\u2192type jsonPatchOp struct {\\n   381\u2192\\tOp    string      `json:\\\"op\\\"`\\n   382\u2192\\tPath  string      `json:\\\"path\\\"`\\n   383\u2192\\tFrom  string      `json:\\\"from\\\"`\\n   384\u2192\\tValue interface{} `json:\\\"value\\\"`\\n   385\u2192}\\n   386\u2192\\n   387\u2192// applyJSPatch applies the patch. Input and output objects must both have\\n   388\u2192// the external version, since that is what the patch must have been constructed against.\\n   389\u2192func (p *jsonPatcher) applyJSPatch(versionedJS []byte) (patchedJS []byte, strictErrors []error, retErr error) {\\n   390\u2192\\tswitch p.patchType {\\n   391\u2192\\tcase types.JSONPatchType:\\n   392\u2192\\t\\tif p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationStrict || p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn {\\n   393\u2192\\t\\t\\tvar v []jsonPatchOp\\n   394\u2192\\t\\t\\tvar err error\\n   395\u2192\\t\\t\\tif strictErrors, err = kjson.UnmarshalStrict(p.patchBytes, &v); err != nil {\\n   396\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"error decoding patch: %v\\\", err))\\n   397\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   398\u2192\\t\\t\\tfor i, e := range strictErrors {\\n   399\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tstrictErrors[i] = fmt.Errorf(\\\"json patch %v\\\", e)\\n   400\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   401\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   402\u2192\\n   403\u2192\\t\\tpatchObj, err := jsonpatch.DecodePatch(p.patchBytes)\\n   404\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   405\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\\n   406\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   407\u2192\\t\\tif len(patchObj) > maxJSONPatchOperations {\\n   408\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewRequestEntityTooLargeError(\\n   409\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"The allowed maximum operations in a JSON patch is %d, got %d\\\",\\n   410\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmaxJSONPatchOperations, len(patchObj)))\\n   411\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   412\u2192\\t\\tpatchedJS, err := patchObj.Apply(versionedJS)\\n   413\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   414\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewGenericServerResponse(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, \\\"\\\", schema.GroupResource{}, \\\"\\\", err.Error(), 0, false)\\n   415\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   416\u2192\\t\\treturn patchedJS, strictErrors, nil\\n   417\u2192\\tcase types.MergePatchType:\\n   418\u2192\\t\\tif p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationStrict || p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn {\\n   419\u2192\\t\\t\\tv := map[string]interface{}{}\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"f0deeff8-86fd-4975-bac8-d189d62bf46b\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:59.925Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"4c339a66-d6c7-440d-9566-a0414e548321\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"f0deeff8-86fd-4975-bac8-d189d62bf46b\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01QTwsxaZRJVyihuUmwxdNca\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01DXRQruzDtXbxpRXV4waGh2\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"grep ":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01RgnHkxhhe3LQwDVMGSGkhh\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01RgnHkxhhe3LQwDVMGSGkhh\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:02.804Z\",\"uuid\":\"425f199e-f65e-4411-b79f-205e40cf5508\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"6710f088-8114-4fc2-9fbe-77c370e7fbfc\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01RgnHkxhhe3LQwDVMGSGkhh\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"   286\u2192type Patcher interface {\\n   287\u2192\\tGetter\\n   288\u2192\\tUpdater\\n   289\u2192}\\n   290\u2192\\n   291\u2192// Watcher should be implemented by all Storage objects that\\n   292\u2192// want to offer the ability to watch for changes through the watch api.\\n   293\u2192type Watcher interface {\\n   294\u2192\\t// 'label' selects on labels; 'field' selects on the object's fields. Not all fields\\n   295\u2192\\t// are supported; an error should be returned if 'field' tries to select on a field that\\n   296\u2192\\t// isn't supported. 'resourceVersion' allows for continuing/starting a watch at a\\n   297\u2192\\t// particular version.\\n   298\u2192\\tWatch(ctx context.Context, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)\\n   299\u2192}\\n   300\u2192\\n   301\u2192// StandardStorage is an interface covering the common verbs. Provided for testing whether a\\n   302\u2192// resource satisfies the normal storage methods. Use Storage when passing opaque storage objects.\\n   303\u2192type StandardStorage interface {\\n   304\u2192\\tGetter\\n   305\u2192\\tLister\\n   306\u2192\\tCreaterUpdater\\n   307\u2192\\tGracefulDeleter\\n   308\u2192\\tCollectionDeleter\\n   309\u2192\\tWatcher\\n   310\u2192\\n   311\u2192\\t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\\n   312\u2192\\t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\\n   313\u2192\\t// for being called more than once.\\n   314\u2192\\tDestroy()\\n   315\u2192}\\n   316\u2192\\n   317\u2192// Redirector know how to return a remote resource's location.\\n   318\u2192type Redirector interface {\\n   319\u2192\\t// ResourceLocation should return the remote location of the given resource, and an optional transport to use to request it, or an error.\\n   320\u2192\\tResourceLocation(ctx context.Context, id string) (remoteLocation *url.URL, transport http.RoundTripper, err error)\\n   321\u2192}\\n   322\u2192\\n   323\u2192// Responder abstracts the normal response behavior for a REST method and is passed to callers that\\n   324\u2192// may wish to handle the response directly in some cases, but delegate to the normal error or object\\n   325\u2192// behavior in other cases.\\n   326\u2192type Responder interface {\\n   327\u2192\\t// Object writes the provided object to the response. Invoking this method multiple times is undefined.\\n   328\u2192\\tObject(statusCode int, obj runtime.Object)\\n   329\u2192\\t// Error writes the provided error to the response. This method may only be invoked once.\\n   330\u2192\\tError(err error)\\n   331\u2192}\\n   332\u2192\\n   333\u2192// Connecter is a storage object that responds to a connection request.\\n   334\u2192type Connecter interface {\\n   335\u2192\\t// Connect returns an http.Handler that will handle the request/response for a given API invocation.\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"0fdd07e6-ca8b-44dd-88d8-0b07ab80f7ca\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:02.804Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"6710f088-8114-4fc2-9fbe-77c370e7fbfc\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"0fdd07e6-ca8b-44dd-88d8-0b07ab80f7ca\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_017bwKKtM5JaiGiK9joogs2x\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01MsGQTCMAhnhAZc4rZnDgDb\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/manager.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":3002,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":48342,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":3002},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG57PSPajEqRSNb3FGCa\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"3e813423-fe7b-402c-bf83-3be18eaa5a6b\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:03.202Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"3e813423-fe7b-402c-bf83-3be18eaa5a6b\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01MsGQTCMAhnhAZc4rZnDgDb\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01MsGQTCMAhnhAZc4rZnDgDb\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:03.205Z\",\"uuid\":\"ee1ee75f-3fe9-4512-85fa-8999f4f45e58\" 16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package internal\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    23\u2192)\\n    24\u2192\\n    25\u2192// Managed groups a fieldpath.ManagedFields together with the timestamps associated with each operation.\\n    26\u2192type Managed interface {\\n    27\u2192\\t// Fields gets the fieldpath.ManagedFields.\\n    28\u2192\\tFields() fieldpath.ManagedFields\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192\\t// Times gets the timestamps associated with each operation.\\n    31\u2192\\tTimes() map[string]*metav1.Time\\n    32\u2192}\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192// Manager updates the managed fields and merges applied configurations.\\n    35\u2192type Manager interface {\\n    36\u2192\\t// Update is used when the object has already been merged (non-apply\\n    37\u2192\\t// use-case), and simply updates the managed fields in the output\\n    38\u2192\\t// object.\\n    39\u2192\\t//  * `liveObj` is not mutated by this function\\n    40\u2192\\t//  * `newObj` may be mutated by this function\\n    41\u2192\\t// Returns the new object with managedFields removed, and the object's new\\n    42\u2192\\t// proposed managedFields separately.\\n    43\u2192\\tUpdate(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string) (runtime.Object, Managed, error)\\n    44\u2192\\n    45\u2192\\t// Apply is used when server-side apply is called, as it merges the\\n    46\u2192\\t// object and updates the managed fields.\\n    47\u2192\\t//  * `liveObj` is not mutated by this function\\n    48\u2192\\t//  * `newObj` may be mutated by this function\\n    49\u2192\\t// Returns the new object with managedFields removed, and the object's new\\n    50\u2192\\t// proposed managedFields separately.\\n    51\u2192\\tApply(liveObj, appliedObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, fieldManager string, force bool) (runtime.Object, Managed, error)\\n    52\u2192}\\n    53\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"b18e7bf8-1664-4637-9a86-2f1f75385eea\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:03.205Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"3e813423-fe7b-402c-bf83-3be18eaa5a6b\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"b18e7bf8-1664-4637-9a86-2f1f75385eea\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_017bwKKtM5JaiGiK9joogs2x\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01Y9SftPAXmENPCLvQG3nGwA\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"find License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage internal\\n\\nimport (\\n\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// Managed groups a fieldpath.ManagedFields together with the timestamps associated with each operation.\\ntype Managed interface {\\n\\t// Fields gets the fieldpath.ManagedFields.\\n\\tFields() fieldpath.ManagedFields\\n\\n\\t// Times gets the timestamps associated with each operation.\\n\\tTimes() map[string]*metav1.Time\\n}\\n\\n// Manager updates the managed fields and merges applied configurations.\\ntype Manager interface {\\n\\t// Update is used when the object has already been merged (non-apply\\n\\t// use-case), and simply updates the managed fields in the output\\n\\t// object.\\n\\t//  * `liveObj` is not mutated by this function\\n\\t//  * `newObj` may be mutated by this function\\n\\t// Returns the new object with managedFields removed, and the object's new\\n\\t// proposed managedFields separately.\\n\\tUpdate(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string) (runtime.Object, Managed, error)\\n\\n\\t// Apply is used when server-side apply is called, as it merges the\\n\\t// object and updates the managed fields.\\n\\t//  * `liveObj` is not mutated by this function\\n\\t//  * `newObj` may be mutated by this function\\n\\t// Returns the new object with managedFields removed, and the object's new\\n\\t// proposed managedFields separately.\\n\\tApply(liveObj, appliedObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, fieldManager string, force bool) (runtime.Object, Managed, error)\\n}\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"8c4bf91e-d8a4-49fb-8be1-7838afd5d654\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:04.246Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"0472fd1e-f5fa-4b83-a628-6bfa9b4b7b1b\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"8c4bf91e-d8a4-49fb-8be1-7838afd5d654\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01QXJFfa82oNws8uxbaiVHja\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package internal\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\tapimachineryvalidation \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/validation\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    25\u2192)\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192// LastAppliedConfigAnnotation is the annotation used to store the previous\\n    28\u2192// configuration of a resource for use in a three way diff by UpdateApplyAnnotation.\\n    29\u2192//\\n    30\u2192// This is a copy of the corev1 annotation since we don't want to depend on the whole package.\\n    31\u2192const LastAppliedConfigAnnotation = \\\"kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192// SetLastApplied sets the last-applied annotation the given value in\\n    34\u2192// the object.\\n    35\u2192func SetLastApplied(obj runtime.Object, value string) error {\\n    36\u2192\\taccessor, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\\n    37\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    38\u2192\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"couldn't get accessor: %v\\\", err))\\n    39\u2192\\t}\\n    40\u2192\\tvar annotations = accessor.GetAnnotations()\\n    41\u2192\\tif annotations == nil {\\n    42\u2192\\t\\tannotations = map[string]string{}\\n    43\u2192\\t}\\n    44\u2192\\tannotations[LastAppliedConfigAnnotation] = value\\n    45\u2192\\tif err := apimachineryvalidation.ValidateAnnotationsSize(annotations); err != nil {\\n    46\u2192\\t\\tdelete(annotations, LastAppliedConfigAnnotation)\\n    47\u2192\\t}\\n    48\u2192\\taccessor.SetAnnotations(annotations)\\n    49\u2192\\treturn nil\\n    50\u2192}\\n    51\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"1fa99c88-c478-4751-aaa8-0586fc49aa2f\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:07.198Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"3d6bda27-10d8-4831-b0fc-ab8921103dd3\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"1fa99c88-c478-4751-aaa8-0586fc49aa2f\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaebe61\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01195uRMLBM9RY1pLEDjvfeK\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Let     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2021 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192// applyconfiguration-gen is a tool for auto-generating apply builder functions.\\n    18\u2192package main\\n    19\u2192\\n    20\u2192import (\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"flag\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/spf13/pflag\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/code-generator/cmd/applyconfiguration-gen/args\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/code-generator/cmd/applyconfiguration-gen/generators\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/gengo/v2\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/gengo/v2/generator\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    29\u2192)\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192func main() {\\n    32\u2192\\tklog.InitFlags(nil)\\n    33\u2192\\targs := args.New()\\n    34\u2192\\targs.AddFlags(pflag.CommandLine, \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis\\\") // TODO: move this input path out of applyconfiguration-gen\\n    35\u2192\\tif err := flag.Set(\\\"logtostderr\\\", \\\"true\\\"); err != nil {\\n    36\u2192\\t\\tklog.Fatalf(\\\"Error: %v\\\", err)\\n    37\u2192\\t}\\n    38\u2192\\tpflag.CommandLine.AddGoFlagSet(flag.CommandLine)\\n    39\u2192\\tpflag.Parse()\\n    40\u2192\\n    41\u2192\\tif err := args.Validate(); err != nil {\\n    42\u2192\\t\\tklog.Fatalf(\\\"Error: %v\\\", err)\\n    43\u2192\\t}\\n    44\u2192\\n    45\u2192\\tmyTargets := func(context *generator.Context) []generator.Target {\\n    46\u2192\\t\\treturn generators.GetTargets(context, args)\\n    47\u2192\\t}\\n    48\u2192\\n    49\u2192\\t// Run it.\\n    50\u2192\\tif err := gengo.Execute(\\n    51\u2192\\t\\tgenerators.NameSystems(),\\n    52\u2192\\t\\tgenerators.DefaultNameSystem(),\\n    53\u2192\\t\\tmyTargets,\\n    54\u2192\\t\\tgengo.StdBuildTag,\\n    55\u2192\\t\\tpflag.Args(),\\n    56\u2192\\t); err != nil {\\n    57\u2192\\t\\tklog.Fatalf(\\\"Error: %v\\\", err)\\n    58\u2192\\t}\\n    59\u2192\\tklog.V(2).Info(\\\"Completed successfully.\\\")\\n    60\u2192}\\n    61\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. 36\u2192\\tclientgentypes \\\"k8s.io/code-generator/cmd/client-gen/types\\\"\\n    37\u2192)\\n    38\u2192\\n    39\u2192// applyConfigurationGenerator produces apply configurations for a given GroupVersion and type.\\n    40\u2192type applyConfigurationGenerator struct {\\n    41\u2192\\tgenerator.GoGenerator\\n    42\u2192\\t// outPkgBase is the base package, under which the \\\"internal\\\" and GV-specific subdirs live\\n    43\u2192\\toutPkgBase   string // must be a Go import-path\\n    44\u2192\\tlocalPkg     string\\n    45\u2192\\tgroupVersion clientgentypes.GroupVersion\\n    46\u2192\\tapplyConfig  applyConfig\\n    47\u2192\\timports      namer.ImportTracker\\n    48\u2192\\trefGraph     refGraph\\n    49\u2192\\topenAPIType  *string // if absent, extraction function cannot be generated\\n    50\u2192}\\n    51\u2192\\n    52\u2192var _ generator.Generator = &applyConfigurationGenerator{}\\n    53\u2192\\n    54\u2192func (g *applyConfigurationGenerator) Filter(_ *generator.Context, t *types.Type) bool {\\n    55\u2192\\treturn t == g.applyConfig.Type\\n    56\u2192}\\n    57\u2192\\n    58\u2192func (g *applyConfigurationGenerator) Namers(*generator.Context) namer.NameSystems {\\n    59\u2192\\treturn namer.NameSystems{\\n    60\u2192\\t\\t\\\"raw\\\":          namer.NewRawNamer(g.localPkg, g.imports),\\n    61\u2192\\t\\t\\\"singularKind\\\": namer.NewPublicNamer(0),\\n    62\u2192\\t}\\n    63\u2192}\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192func (g *applyConfigurationGenerator) Imports(*generator.Context) (imports []string) {\\n    66\u2192\\treturn g.imports.ImportLines()\\n    67\u2192}\\n    68\u2192\\n    69\u2192// TypeParams provides a struct that an apply configuration\\n    70\u2192// is generated for as well as the apply configuration details\\n    71\u2192// and types referenced by the struct.\\n    72\u2192type TypeParams struct {\\n    73\u2192\\tStruct      *types.Type\\n    74\u2192\\tApplyConfig applyConfig\\n    75\u2192\\tTags        util.Tags\\n    76\u2192\\tAPIVersion  string\\n    77\u2192\\tExtractInto *types.Type\\n    78\u2192\\tParserFunc  *types.Type\\n    79\u2192\\tOpenAPIType *string\\n    80\u2192}\\n    81\u2192\\n    82\u2192type memberParams struct {\\n    83\u2192\\tTypeParams\\n    84\u2192\\tMember     types.Member\\n    85\u2192\\tMemberType *types.Type\\n    86\u2192\\tJSONTags   JSONTags\\n    87\u2192\\tArgType    *types.Type   // only set for maps and slices\\n    88\u2192\\tEmbeddedIn *memberParams // parent embedded member, if any\\n    89\u2192}\\n    90\u2192\\n    91\u2192func (g *applyConfigurationGenerator) GenerateType(c *generator.Context, t *types.Type, w io.Writer) error {\\n    92\u2192\\tsw := generator.NewSnippetWriter(w, c, \\\"$\\\", \\\"$\\\")\\n    93\u2192\\n    94\u2192\\tklog.V(5).Infof(\\\"processing type %v\\\", t)\\n    95\u2192\\ttypeParams := TypeParams{\\n    96\u2192\\t\\tStruct:      t,\\n    97\u2192\\t\\tApplyConfig: g.applyConfig,\\n    98\u2192\\t\\tTags:        genclientTags(t),\\n    99\u2192\\t\\tAPIVersion:  g.groupVersion.ToAPIVersion(),\\n   100\u2192\\t\\tExtractInto: extractInto,\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n39:\\t*metav1.ObjectMetaApplyConfiguration `json:\\\"metadata,omitempty\\\"`\\n42:\\tSpec *PodSpecApplyConfiguration `json:\\\"spec,omitempty\\\"`\\n48:\\tStatus *PodStatusApplyConfiguration `json:\\\"status,omitempty\\\"`\\n53:func Pod(name, namespace string) *PodApplyConfiguration {\\n54:\\tb := &PodApplyConfiguration{}\\n55:\\tb.WithName(name)\\n56:\\tb.WithNamespace(namespace)\\n57:\\tb.WithKind(\\\"Pod\\\")\\n58:\\tb.WithAPIVersion(\\\"v1\\\")\\n62:// ExtractPodFrom extracts the applied configuration owned by fieldManager from\\n66:// ExtractPodFrom provides a way to perform a extract/modify-in-place/apply workflow.\\n69:func ExtractPodFrom(pod *corev1.Pod, fieldManager string, subresource string) (*PodApplyConfiguration, error) {\\n70:\\tb := &PodApplyConfiguration{}\\n71:\\terr := managedfields.ExtractInto(pod, internal.Parser().Type(\\\"io.k8s.api.core.v1.Pod\\\"), fieldManager, b, subresource)\\n75:\\tb.WithName(pod.Name)\\n76:\\tb.WithNamespace(pod.Namespace)\\n78:\\tb.WithKind(\\\"Pod\\\")\\n79:\\tb.WithAPIVersion(\\\"v1\\\")\\n83:// ExtractPod extracts the applied configuration owned by fieldManager from\\n85:// PodApplyConfiguration is returned with only the Name, Namespace (if applicable),\\n90:// ExtractPod provides a way to perform a extract/modify-in-place/apply workflow.\\n93:func ExtractPod(pod *corev1.Pod, fieldManager string) (*PodApplyConfiguration, error) {\\n94:\\treturn ExtractPodFrom(pod, fieldManager, \\\"\\\")\\n97://
