Now I have all the information needed. Let me provide you with a comprehensive analysis:\\n\\n## Kubernetes Server-Side Apply & ApplyConfigurations Subsystem - Thorough Analysis\\n\\n### 1. APPLYCONFIGURATIONS DIRECTORY STRUCTURE\\n\\n**Location:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/applyconfigurations/`\\n\\n**Purpose:** Auto-generated builder/declarative configuration types for Server-Side Apply (SSA)\\n\\n**Structure:**\\n- **Root files:**\\n  - `utils.go` (132KB) - Utility functions and helpers, includes imports for all API versions\\n  - `OWNERS` - Ownership information\\n\\n- **Organization by API Group:**\\n  - `admissionregistration/` - Admission registration APIs\\n  - `apps/` - Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, etc.\\n  - `batch/` - Job, CronJob APIs\\n  - `core/` - Pod, Service, ConfigMap, Secret, etc.\\n  - `meta/v1/` - ObjectMeta, TypeMeta (metadata types)\\n  - `storage/`, `rbac/`, `policy/`, `networking/`, etc. - Other API groups\\n\\n**Key Files for Core Resources:**\\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/applyconfigurations/core/v1/pod.go`\\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/applyconfigurations/meta/v1/objectmeta.go`\\n- Each file is auto-generated with `// Code generated by applyconfiguration-gen. DO NOT EDIT.`\\n\\n### 2. APPLYCONFIGURATIONS - STRUCTURE & PATTERNS\\n\\n**Type Structure Example (`PodApplyConfiguration`):**\\n```go\\ntype PodApplyConfiguration struct {\\n    metav1.TypeMetaApplyConfiguration `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n    *metav1.ObjectMetaApplyConfiguration `json:\\\"metadata,omitempty\\\"`\\n    Spec *PodSpecApplyConfiguration `json:\\\"spec,omitempty\\\"`\\n    Status *PodStatusApplyConfiguration `json:\\\"status,omitempty\\\"`\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Key Methods Generated:**\\n1. **Constructor Functions:**\\n   - `Pod(name, namespace string) *PodApplyConfiguration` - Creates a new PodApplyConfiguration\\n   - Returns initialized object with metadata set\\n\\n2. **Extraction Functions:**\\n   - `ExtractPodFrom(pod *corev1.Pod, fieldManager string, subresource string) (*PodApplyConfiguration, error)` - Extracts what was previously applied by a specific field manager\\n   - `ExtractPod(pod *corev1.Pod, fieldManager string)` - Shorthand for main resource\\n   - `ExtractPodEphemeralcontainers()`, `ExtractPodResize()` - Subresource variants\\n\\n3. **Builder Methods:**\\n   - `WithName(name)`, `WithNamespace(namespace)`, `WithKind(\\\"Pod\\\")`, `WithAPIVersion(\\\"v1\\\")`\\n   - Chainable methods for building configurations declaratively\\n\\n**Key Types:**\\n- All pointers (`*string`, `*int64`, `*metav1.Time`) to track which fields were set\\n- Omitempty JSON tags for optional fields\\n- Embedded types for composition (ObjectMeta, TypeMeta)\\n\\n### 3. FIELD MANAGER & MANAGEDFIELDS IMPLEMENTATION\\n\\n**Location:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/`\\n\\n**Core Interfaces:**\\n\\n```go\\n// File: fieldmanager.go\\ntype FieldManager = internal.FieldManager\\n\\n// Factory functions\\nfunc NewDefaultFieldManager(typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, \\n    objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter, objectCreater runtime.ObjectCreater, \\n    kind schema.GroupVersionKind, hub schema.GroupVersion, subresource string, \\n    resetFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter) (*FieldManager, error)\\n\\nfunc NewDefaultCRDFieldManager(...) (_ *FieldManager, err error)\\nfunc ValidateManagedFields(encodedManagedFields []metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry) error\\n```\\n\\n**Manager Interface:**\\n```go\\n// File: internal/manager.go\\ntype Manager interface {\\n    Update(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string) \\n        (runtime.Object, Managed, error)\\n    \\n    Apply(liveObj, appliedObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, fieldManager string, force bool) \\n        (runtime.Object, Managed, error)\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Managed Interface:**\\n```go\\ntype Managed interface {\\n    Fields() fieldpath.ManagedFields  // The actual field ownership tracking\\n    Times() map[string]*metav1.Time   // Timestamps per manager\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**FieldManager Wrapper Chain** (internal/fieldmanager.go):\\nThe FieldManager is a layered wrapper system:\\n```go\\nNewDefaultFieldManager wraps:\\n  \u251c\u2500 VersionCheckManager (validates version compatibility)\\n  \u251c\u2500 LastAppliedUpdater (handles kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration)\\n  \u251c\u2500 LastAppliedManager (upgrades client-side apply to server-side)\\n  \u251c\u2500 ProbabilisticSkipNonAppliedManager (probabilistic tracking from creation)\\n  \u251c\u2500 CapManagersManager (limits # of update managers to avoid bloat)\\n  \u251c\u2500 BuildManagerInfoManager (augments manager identifier with type info)\\n  \u251c\u2500 ManagedFieldsUpdater (updates encoded managed fields)\\n  \u251c\u2500 StripMetaManager (removes managedFields before processing)\\n  \u2514\u2500 structuredMergeManager (core merge logic)\\n```\\n\\n### 4. MANAGED FIELDS ENCODING/DECODING\\n\\n**File:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/managedfields.go`\\n\\n**Key Types:**\\n```go\\ntype ManagedFieldsEntry struct {\\n    Manager     string                 // e.g., \\\"kubectl\\\", \\\"my-controller\\\"\\n    Operation   ManagedFieldsOperation // Apply or Update\\n    APIVersion  string                 // e.g., \\\"v1\\\", \\\"apps/v1\\\"\\n    Time        *metav1.Time           // When the operation happened\\n    FieldsType  string                 // Always \\\"FieldsV1\\\"\\n    FieldsV1    *FieldsV1             // Raw JSON representation of field paths\\n    Subresource string                 // Empty string for main resource, \\\"status\\\" for status subresource\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Conversion Functions:**\\n```go\\n// DecodeManagedFields converts from wire format (ManagedFieldsEntry) to fieldpath.ManagedFields\\nfunc DecodeManagedFields(encodedManagedFields []metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry) (ManagedInterface, error)\\n\\n// EncodeObjectManagedFields converts back to API format\\nfunc EncodeObjectManagedFields(obj runtime.Object, managed ManagedInterface) error\\n\\n// BuildManagerIdentifier creates manager key (ignores FieldsType, FieldsV1, Time, APIVersion for appliers)\\nfunc BuildManagerIdentifier(encodedManager *metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry) (manager string, err error)\\n```\\n\\n**FieldsV1 Format:**\\n- Stored as `metav1.FieldsV1.Raw` - JSON bytes\\n- Represents a trie structure of field paths\\n- Parsed using: `fieldpath.Set.FromJSON()` and `fieldpath.Set.ToJSON()`\\n- Each field path is a sequence of path elements (for maps, arrays, etc.)\\n\\n### 5. STRUCTURED MERGE DIFF (SMD) INTEGRATION\\n\\n**Location:** `/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/`\\n\\n**Core Concepts:**\\n- Used for semantic merging during apply operations\\n- Understands Kubernetes schema (merge strategies, list handling)\\n- Manages field ownership at a structural level\\n\\n**Key Types:**\\n```go\\n// From vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge/conflict.go\\ntype Conflict struct {\\n    Manager string         // Which field manager owns the field\\n    Path    fieldpath.Path // Path to the conflicted field\\n}\\n\\ntype Conflicts []Conflict  // Multiple conflicts aggregated\\n\\n// From vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge/update.go\\ntype Updater struct {\\n    Converter         Converter                                  // Converts between API versions\\n    IgnoredFields     map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set  // Fields to ignore in merge\\n    IgnoreFilter      map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter\\n    returnInputOnNoop bool\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Manager Implementation:**\\n```go\\n// File: internal/structuredmerge.go\\ntype structuredMergeManager struct {\\n    typeConverter   TypeConverter\\n    objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor\\n    objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter\\n    groupVersion    schema.GroupVersion\\n    hubVersion      schema.GroupVersion\\n    updater         merge.Updater  // The SMD updater\\n}\\n\\n// Implementations\\nfunc NewStructuredMergeManager(...) Manager\\nfunc NewCRDStructuredMergeManager(...) Manager  // For CRDs without schema constraints\\n```\\n\\n**Apply Flow:**\\n```\\nFieldManager.Apply()\\n  \u2192 structuredMergeManager.Apply()\\n    \u2192 Convert to typed representation (typeConverter.ObjectToTyped)\\n    \u2192 Call updater.Apply (SMD merge)\\n    \u2192 Returns merged TypedValue and managed fields\\n    \u2192 Convert back to runtime.Object (typeConverter.TypedToObject)\\n    \u2192 Apply defaults (objectDefaulter.Default)\\n```\\n\\n### 6. CONFLICT DETECTION & HANDLING\\n\\n**File:** `/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge/conflict.go`\\n\\n**Conflict Structure:**\\n```go\\ntype Conflict struct {\\n    Manager string         // \\\"other-manager\\\" - who owns this field\\n    Path    fieldpath.Path // The specific field path\\n}\\n\\nfunc (c Conflict) Error() string {\\n    return fmt.Sprintf(\\\"conflict with %q: %v\\\", c.Manager, c.Path)\\n}\\n\\ntype Conflicts []Conflict\\n// Aggregates conflicts by manager with nice formatting\\nfunc (conflicts Conflicts) Error() string {\\n    // Returns something like:\\n    // \\\"conflicts with 'other-manager':\\n    //    - .spec.replicas\\n    //    - .spec.image\\\"\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Flow in FieldManager:**\\n```go\\n// File: internal/fieldmanager.go\\nfunc (f *FieldManager) Apply(...) (object runtime.Object, err error) {\\n    // ...\\n    object, managed, err = f.fieldManager.Apply(liveObj, appliedObj, managed, manager, force)\\n    if err != nil {\\n        if conflicts, ok := err.(merge.Conflicts); ok {\\n            return nil, NewConflictError(conflicts)\\n        }\\n    }\\n    // ...\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Force Flag:**\\nThe `force` parameter in Apply allows overriding conflicts:\\n- When `force=true`, the field manager takes ownership of conflicting fields\\n- Used when user explicitly wants to override others' changes\\n- In kubectl: `kubectl apply --force` or similar\\n\\n### 7. APPLYCONFIGURATIONS CODE GENERATION\\n\\n**Generator Location:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/code-generator/cmd/applyconfiguration-gen/`\\n\\n**Key Components:**\\n\\n```\\napplyconfiguration-gen/\\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 main.go              # Entry point\\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 args/\\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 args.go         # Command-line arguments parsing\\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 externaltypes.go # External type handling\\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 generators/\\n    \u251c\u2500\u2500 applyconfiguration.go  # Main generator logic\\n    \u251c\u2500\u2500 types.go               # Type parameter handling\\n    \u251c\u2500\u2500 jsontagutil.go         # JSON tag parsing\\n    \u251c\u2500\u2500 refgraph.go           # Reference graph building\\n    \u251c\u2500\u2500 openapi.go            # OpenAPI type extraction\\n    \u251c\u2500\u2500 targets.go            # Build targets\\n    \u251c\u2500\u2500 util.go               # Utilities\\n    \u2514\u2500\u2500 internal.go           # Internal type generation\\n```\\n\\n**Generation Process:**\\n1. Read Kubernetes API types from Go source\\n2. For each type, generate an ApplyConfiguration struct\\n3. Generate constructor functions\\n4. Generate extraction functions using managedfields.ExtractInto\\n5. Generate builder methods (With*)\\n6. Output to `staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/applyconfigurations/`\\n\\n**Invocation:**\\n```bash\\n# From hack/update-codegen.sh\\napplyconfiguration-gen \\\\\\n    --output-base=\\\"$(dirname \\\"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\\\")/..\\\" \\\\\\n    --input-dirs=\\\"k8s.io/api/...\\\" \\\\\\n    --output-pkg=\\\"k8s.io/client-go/applyconfigurations\\\"\\n```\\n\\n### 8. EXTRACTION MECHANISM\\n\\n**File:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/extract.go`\\n\\n**ExtractInto Function:**\\n```go\\nfunc ExtractInto(object runtime.Object, objectType typed.ParseableType, \\n    fieldManager string, applyConfiguration interface{}, subresource string) error\\n{\\n    // 1. Convert object to typed representation\\n    typedObj, err := toTyped(object, objectType)\\n    \\n    // 2. Find managed fields entry for this fieldManager\\n    fieldsEntry, ok := findManagedFields(accessor, fieldManager, subresource)\\n    \\n    // 3. Parse FieldsV1 to fieldpath.Set\\n    fieldset := &fieldpath.Set{}\\n    fieldset.FromJSON(bytes.NewReader(fieldsEntry.FieldsV1.Raw))\\n    \\n    // 4. Extract only the fields owned by this manager\\n    extractedFields := typedObj.ExtractItems(fieldset.Leaves()).AsValue().Unstructured()\\n    \\n    // 5. Convert to unstructured and deserialize into applyConfiguration\\n    runtime.DefaultUnstructuredConverter.FromUnstructured(m, applyConfiguration)\\n}\\n\\nfunc findManagedFields(accessor metav1.Object, fieldManager string, subresource string) \\n    (metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry, bool) {\\n    // Finds entry where:\\n    //   mf.Manager == fieldManager\\n    //   mf.Operation == ManagedFieldsOperationApply\\n    //   mf.Subresource == subresource\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Usage in PodApplyConfiguration:**\\n```go\\nfunc ExtractPodFrom(pod *corev1.Pod, fieldManager string, subresource string) \\n    (*PodApplyConfiguration, error) {\\n    b := &PodApplyConfiguration{}\\n    err := managedfields.ExtractInto(pod, internal.Parser().Type(\\\"io.k8s.api.core.v1.Pod\\\"), \\n        fieldManager, b, subresource)\\n    // ...\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Extract/Modify/Apply Workflow:**\\n1. User does: `kubectl get pod -o yaml > pod.yaml`\\n2. Extract: `ExtractPodFrom(pod, \\\"kubectl\\\")` - gets what kubectl previously applied\\n3. Modify: Update the returned PodApplyConfiguration\\n4. Apply: Use it to apply changes server-side\\n\\n### 9. SERVER-SIDE APPLY REQUEST HANDLING\\n\\n**File:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go`\\n\\n**PatchResource Handler:**\\n```go\\nfunc PatchResource(r rest.Patcher, scope *RequestScope, admit admission.Interface, \\n    patchTypes []string) http.HandlerFunc {\\n    return func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n        // 1. Validate patch type (ApplyYAMLPatchType, ApplyCBORPatchType, etc.)\\n        // 2. Decode patch options from query parameters\\n        // 3. Create appropriate patcher mechanism\\n        // 4. Apply transformers: applyPatch \u2192 applyAdmission \u2192 dedup\\n        // 5. Call rest.Patcher.Update() to persist\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Patch Type Handling:**\\n```go\\nconst (\\n    JSONPatchType           types.PatchType = \\\"application/json-patch+json\\\"\\n    MergePatchType          types.PatchType = \\\"application/merge-patch+json\\\"\\n    StrategicMergePatchType types.PatchType = \\\"application/strategic-merge-patch+json\\\"\\n    ApplyYAMLPatchType      types.PatchType = \\\"application/apply+yaml\\\"\\n    ApplyCBORPatchType      types.PatchType = \\\"application/apply+cbor\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// In patchResource, routing:\\nswitch p.patchType {\\ncase types.JSONPatchType, types.MergePatchType:\\n    p.mechanism = &jsonPatcher{...}\\ncase types.StrategicMergePatchType:\\n    p.mechanism = &smpPatcher{...}\\ncase types.ApplyYAMLPatchType:\\n    p.mechanism = newApplyPatcher(p, scope.FieldManager, yaml.Unmarshal, yaml.UnmarshalStrict)\\n    p.forceAllowCreate = true\\ncase types.ApplyCBORPatchType:\\n    p.mechanism = newApplyPatcher(p, scope.FieldManager, cbor.Unmarshal, cbor.Unmarshal)\\n    p.forceAllowCreate = true\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Patcher Mechanisms:**\\n\\n1. **jsonPatcher** (for JSON Patch, Merge Patch):\\n   ```go\\n   type jsonPatcher struct {\\n       *patcher\\n       fieldManager *managedfields.FieldManager\\n   }\\n   \\n   func (p *jsonPatcher) applyPatchToCurrentObject(currentObject runtime.Object) \\n       (runtime.Object, error) {\\n       // Encode, apply JSON operations, decode\\n       // Update managed fields\\n       objToUpdate = p.fieldManager.UpdateNoErrors(currentObject, objToUpdate, manager)\\n   }\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **smpPatcher** (for Strategic Merge Patch):\\n   ```go\\n   type smpPatcher struct {\\n       *patcher\\n       schemaReferenceObj runtime.Object\\n       fieldManager       *managedfields.FieldManager\\n   }\\n   \\n   func (p *smpPatcher) applyPatchToCurrentObject(currentObject runtime.Object) \\n       (runtime.Object, error) {\\n       // Use schema-aware merge (lists with merge keys, etc.)\\n       // Update managed fields\\n       newObj = p.fieldManager.UpdateNoErrors(currentObject, newObj, manager)\\n   }\\n   ```\\n\\n3. **applyPatcher** (for Server-Side Apply YAML/CBOR):\\n   ```go\\n   type applyPatcher struct {\\n       patch               []byte\\n       fieldManager        *managedfields.FieldManager\\n       // ...\\n   }\\n   \\n   func (p *applyPatcher) applyPatchToCurrentObject(obj runtime.Object) \\n       (runtime.Object, error) {\\n       // Unmarshal YAML/CBOR to unstructured object\\n       patchObj := &unstructured.Unstructured{Object: map[string]interface{}{}}\\n       p.unmarshalFn(p.patch, &patchObj.Object)\\n       \\n       // Call FieldManager.Apply (not Update!)\\n       obj, err := p.fieldManager.Apply(obj, patchObj, p.options.FieldManager, force)\\n   }\\n   ```\\n\\n### 10. FIELD MANAGER IN ENDPOINT HANDLERS\\n\\n**File:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/fieldmanager/`\\n\\n**ManagedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController:**\\n```go\\n// admission.go\\nfunc NewManagedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController(wrap admission.Interface) admission.Interface\\n// Wraps admission to catch invalid managedFields modifications\\n// If admission modifies managedFields in invalid way, reverts and logs warning\\n\\ntype managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController struct {\\n    wrap admission.Interface\\n}\\n\\nfunc (admit *managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController) Admit(ctx context.Context, \\n    a admission.Attributes, o admission.ObjectInterfaces) error {\\n    managedFieldsBeforeAdmission := objectMeta.GetManagedFields()\\n    if err := mutationInterface.Admit(ctx, a, o); err != nil {\\n        return err\\n    }\\n    managedFieldsAfterAdmission := objectMeta.GetManagedFields()\\n    if err := managedfields.ValidateManagedFields(managedFieldsAfterAdmission); err != nil {\\n        objectMeta.SetManagedFields(managedFieldsBeforeAdmission)\\n        warning.AddWarning(ctx, \\\"\\\", InvalidManagedFieldsAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat)\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**IgnoreManagedFieldsTimestampsTransformer:**\\n```go\\n// equality.go\\nfunc IgnoreManagedFieldsTimestampsTransformer(ctx context.Context, \\n    newObj runtime.Object, oldObj runtime.Object) (res runtime.Object, err error)\\n    \\n// If managed fields are identical except for timestamps, \\n// and the rest of the object is unchanged,\\n// revert timestamp changes to prevent spurious ResourceVersion bumps\\n// (empty apply with no actual changes should be a no-op)\\n\\n// Optimization: Check quickly if managedFields modulo timestamps are unchanged\\n// If so, do full object comparison\\n// Most updates will fail fast in the first check\\n```\\n\\n### 11. KEY TYPES & INTERFACES SUMMARY\\n\\n**REST Storage Layer:**\\n```go\\n// File: staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go\\ntype Patcher interface {\\n    Getter\\n    Updater\\n}\\n\\ntype Updater interface {\\n    Update(ctx context.Context, name string, objInfo UpdatedObjectInfo, \\n        createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, \\n        updateValidation ValidateObjectUpdateFunc, \\n        forceAllowCreate bool, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**RequestScope:**\\nHas critical fields for field management:\\n```go\\nFieldManager *managedfields.FieldManager\\nKind         schema.GroupVersionKind\\nSubresource  string\\nDefaulter    runtime.ObjectDefaulter\\n// ...\\n```\\n\\n**MetaV1 Types:**\\n```go\\ntype ManagedFieldsEntry struct {\\n    Manager     string\\n    Operation   ManagedFieldsOperation  // \\\"Apply\\\" or \\\"Update\\\"\\n    APIVersion  string\\n    Time        *metav1.Time\\n    FieldsType  string  // \\\"FieldsV1\\\"\\n    FieldsV1    *FieldsV1\\n    Subresource string\\n}\\n\\ntype FieldsV1 struct {\\n    Raw []byte  // JSON representation of fieldpath.Set\\n}\\n\\ntype PatchOptions struct {\\n    TypeMeta      TypeMeta\\n    DryRun        []string                    // \\\"All\\\" for dry-run\\n    FieldManager  string                      // \\\"kubectl\\\", \\\"my-controller\\\", etc.\\n    FieldValidation FieldValidationStrategy  // \\\"Ignore\\\", \\\"Warn\\\", \\\"Strict\\\"\\n    Force         *bool                      // Override conflicts\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### 12. INITIALIZATION & SETUP\\n\\n**Example Initialization** (from fieldmanager.go):\\n```go\\nfunc NewDefaultFieldManager(typeConverter TypeConverter, \\n    objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, \\n    objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter, \\n    objectCreater runtime.ObjectCreater, \\n    kind schema.GroupVersionKind, \\n    hub schema.GroupVersion, \\n    subresource string, \\n    resetFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter) (*FieldManager, error) {\\n    \\n    f, err := internal.NewStructuredMergeManager(\\n        typeConverter, objectConverter, objectDefaulter, \\n        kind.GroupVersion(), hub, resetFields)\\n    \\n    return internal.NewDefaultFieldManager(\\n        f, typeConverter, objectConverter, objectCreater, kind, subresource)\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe DefaultFieldManager wraps layers as shown in section 3.\\n\\n### 13. CRITICAL FILE PATHS\\n\\n**ApplyConfigurations Generation:**\\n- Generator: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/code-generator/cmd/applyconfiguration-gen/main.go`\\n- Generated files: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/applyconfigurations/`\\n- Update script: `/workspace/hack/update-codegen.sh`\\n\\n**Field Management Core:**\\n- Public API: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/fieldmanager.go`\\n- Internal impl: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/`\\n  - `fieldmanager.go` - Main FieldManager\\n  - `manager.go` - Manager interface\\n  - `managedfields.go` - Encoding/decoding\\n  - `structuredmerge.go` - SMD integration\\n  - `capmanagers.go` - Manager cap logic\\n  - `lastappliedmanager.go` - Client-side apply upgrade\\n  - `fields.go` - FieldsV1 conversion\\n\\n**Server-Side Apply Handler:**\\n- Patch handler: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go`\\n- Field manager admission: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/fieldmanager/admission.go`\\n- Equality/timestamp: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/fieldmanager/equality.go`\\n\\n**Structured Merge Diff:**\\n- Vendored at: `/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/`\\n- Key files:\\n  - `merge/conflict.go` - Conflict types\\n  - `merge/update.go` - Updater\\n  - `typed/merge.go` - Typed merge logic\\n\\n**Extract Mechanism:**\\n- Extract API: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/extract.go`\\n\\n---\\n\\nThis is a comprehensive picture of the Kubernetes Server-Side Apply subsystem. The key insight is that it's a three-layer architecture:\\n\\n1. **Client-side:** ApplyConfiguration types (generated declarative builders)\\n2. **Server-side request handling:** patch.go routes requests to appropriate patcher\\n3. **Core merge logic:** FieldManager uses SMD library for semantic merging and conflict detection, tracking ownership in ManagedFieldsEntry entries with FieldsV1 field paths\"}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":5,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":629,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":74433,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":629},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG593gZMpCoQu7nmaQMk\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"43012a5d-84b4-49e9-85c5-2ad0c961bf84\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:18:17.243Z\"}\n",
