\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01AkS4ehxJwgKuAfrx9P7Was\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01AkS4ehxJwgKuAfrx9P7Was\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:49.926Z\",\"uuid\":\"ea26610d-7aea-4e31-acec-00e18779fdf3\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"4d5f0a6e-a18f-458b-88a1-f25a97d19f23\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01AkS4ehxJwgKuAfrx9P7Was\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192// Package v1 contains API types that are common to all versions.\\n    18\u2192//\\n    19\u2192// The package contains two categories of types:\\n    20\u2192//   - external (serialized) types that lack their own version (e.g TypeMeta)\\n    21\u2192//   - internal (never-serialized) types that are needed by several different\\n    22\u2192//     api groups, and so live here, to avoid duplication and/or import loops\\n    23\u2192//     (e.g. LabelSelector).\\n    24\u2192//\\n    25\u2192// In the future, we will probably move these categories of objects into\\n    26\u2192// separate packages.\\n    27\u2192package v1\\n    28\u2192\\n    29\u2192import (\\n    30\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\\\"\\n    35\u2192)\\n    36\u2192\\n    37\u2192// TypeMeta describes an individual object in an API response or request\\n    38\u2192// with strings representing the type of the object and its API schema version.\\n    39\u2192// Structures that are versioned or persisted should inline TypeMeta.\\n    40\u2192//\\n    41\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen=false\\n    42\u2192type TypeMeta struct {\\n    43\u2192\\t// Kind is a string value representing the REST resource this object represents.\\n    44\u2192\\t// Servers may infer this from the endpoint the client submits requests to.\\n    45\u2192\\t// Cannot be updated.\\n    46\u2192\\t// In CamelCase.\\n    47\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds\\n    48\u2192\\t// +optional\\n    49\u2192\\tKind string `json:\\\"kind,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=kind\\\"`\\n    50\u2192\\n    51\u2192\\t// APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.\\n    52\u2192\\t// Servers should convert recognized schemas to the latest internal value, and\\n    53\u2192\\t// may reject unrecognized values.\\n    54\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#resources\\n    55\u2192\\t// +optional\\n    56\u2192\\tAPIVersion string `json:\\\"apiVersion,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=apiVersion\\\"`\\n    57\u2192}\\n    58\u2192\\n    59\u2192// ListMeta describes metadata that synthetic resources must have, including lists and\\n    60\u2192// various status objects. A resource may have only one of {ObjectMeta, ListMeta}.\\n    61\u2192type ListMeta struct {\\n    62\u2192\\t// Deprecated: selfLink is a legacy read-only field that is no longer populated by the system.\\n    63\u2192\\t// +optional\\n    64\u2192\\tSelfLink string `json:\\\"selfLink,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=selfLink\\\"`\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192\\t// String that identifies the server's internal version of this object that\\n    67\u2192\\t// can be used by clients to determine when objects have changed.\\n    68\u2192\\t// Value must be treated as opaque by clients and passed unmodified back to the server.\\n    69\u2192\\t// Populated by the system.\\n    70\u2192\\t// Read-only.\\n    71\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#concurrency-control-and-consistency\\n    72\u2192\\t// +optional\\n    73\u2192\\tResourceVersion string `json:\\\"resourceVersion,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=resourceVersion\\\"`\\n    74\u2192\\n    75\u2192\\t// continue may be set if the user set a limit on the number of items returned, and indicates that\\n    76\u2192\\t// the server has more data available. The value is opaque and may be used to issue another request\\n    77\u2192\\t// to the endpoint that served this list to retrieve the next set of available objects. Continuing a\\n    78\u2192\\t// consistent list may not be possible if the server configuration has changed or more than a few\\n    79\u2192\\t// minutes have passed. The resourceVersion field returned when using this continue value will be\\n    80\u2192\\t// identical to the value in the first response, unless you have received this token from an error\\n    81\u2192\\t// message.\\n    82\u2192\\tContinue string `json:\\\"continue,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=continue\\\"`\\n    83\u2192\\n    84\u2192\\t// remainingItemCount is the number of subsequent items in the list which are not included in this\\n    85\u2192\\t// list response. If the list request contained label or field selectors, then the number of\\n    86\u2192\\t// remaining items is unknown and the field will be left unset and omitted during serialization.\\n    87\u2192\\t// If the list is complete (either because it is not chunking or because this is the last chunk),\\n    88\u2192\\t// then there are no more remaining items and this field will be left unset and omitted during\\n    89\u2192\\t// serialization.\\n    90\u2192\\t// Servers older than v1.15 do not set this field.\\n    91\u2192\\t// The intended use of the remainingItemCount is *estimating* the size of a collection. Clients\\n    92\u2192\\t// should not rely on the remainingItemCount to be set or to be exact.\\n    93\u2192\\t// +optional\\n    94\u2192\\tRemainingItemCount *int64 `json:\\\"remainingItemCount,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=remainingItemCount\\\"`\\n    95\u2192}\\n    96\u2192\\n    97\u2192// Field path constants that are specific to the internal API\\n    98\u2192// representation.\\n    99\u2192const (\\n   100\u2192\\tObjectNameField = \\\"metadata.name\\\"\\n   101\u2192)\\n   102\u2192\\n   103\u2192// These are internal finalizer values for Kubernetes-like APIs, must be qualified name unless defined here\\n   104\u2192const (\\n   105\u2192\\tFinalizerOrphanDependents = \\\"orphan\\\"\\n   106\u2192\\tFinalizerDeleteDependents = \\\"foregroundDeletion\\\"\\n   107\u2192)\\n   108\u2192\\n   109\u2192// ObjectMeta is metadata that all persisted resources must have, which includes all objects\\n   110\u2192// users must create.\\n   111\u2192type ObjectMeta struct {\\n   112\u2192\\t// Name must be unique within a namespace. Is required when creating resources, although\\n   113\u2192\\t// some resources may allow a client to request the generation of an appropriate name\\n   114\u2192\\t// automatically. Name is primarily intended for creation idempotence and configuration\\n   115\u2192\\t// definition.\\n   116\u2192\\t// Cannot be updated.\\n   117\u2192\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names#names\\n   118\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   119\u2192\\tName string `json:\\\"name,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=name\\\"`\\n   120\u2192\\n   121\u2192\\t// GenerateName is an optional prefix, used by the server, to generate a unique\\n   122\u2192\\t// name ONLY IF the Name field has not been provided.\\n   123\u2192\\t// If this field is used, the name returned to the client will be different\\n   124\u2192\\t// than the name passed. This value will also be combined with a unique suffix.\\n   125\u2192\\t// The provided value has the same validation rules as the Name field,\\n   126\u2192\\t// and may be truncated by the length of the suffix required to make the value\\n   127\u2192\\t// unique on the server.\\n   128\u2192\\t//\\n   129\u2192\\t// If this field is specified and the generated name exists, the server will return a 409.\\n   130\u2192\\t//\\n   131\u2192\\t// Applied only if Name is not specified.\\n   132\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#idempotency\\n   133\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   134\u2192\\tGenerateName string `json:\\\"generateName,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=generateName\\\"`\\n   135\u2192\\n   136\u2192\\t// Namespace defines the space within which each name must be unique. An empty namespace is\\n   137\u2192\\t// equivalent to the \\\"default\\\" namespace, but \\\"default\\\" is the canonical representation.\\n   138\u2192\\t// Not all objects are required to be scoped to a namespace - the value of this field for\\n   139\u2192\\t// those objects will be empty.\\n   140\u2192\\t//\\n   141\u2192\\t// Must be a DNS_LABEL.\\n   142\u2192\\t// Cannot be updated.\\n   143\u2192\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/namespaces\\n   144\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   145\u2192\\tNamespace string `json:\\\"namespace,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=namespace\\\"`\\n   146\u2192\\n   147\u2192\\t// Deprecated: selfLink is a legacy read-only field that is no longer populated by the system.\\n   148\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   149\u2192\\tSelfLink string `json:\\\"selfLink,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=selfLink\\\"`\\n   150\u2192\\n   151\u2192\\t// UID is the unique in time and space value for this object. It is typically generated by\\n   152\u2192\\t// the server on successful creation of a resource and is not allowed to change on PUT\\n   153\u2192\\t// operations.\\n   154\u2192\\t//\\n   155\u2192\\t// Populated by the system.\\n   156\u2192\\t// Read-only.\\n   157\u2192\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names#uids\\n   158\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   159\u2192\\tUID types.UID `json:\\\"uid,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=uid,casttype=k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/types.UID\\\"`\\n   160\u2192\\n   161\u2192\\t// An opaque value that represents the internal version of this object that can\\n   162\u2192\\t// be used by clients to determine when objects have changed. May be used for optimistic\\n   163\u2192\\t// concurrency, change detection, and the watch operation on a resource or set of resources.\\n   164\u2192\\t// Clients must treat these values as opaque and passed unmodified back to the server.\\n   165\u2192\\t// They may only be valid for a particular resource or set of resources.\\n   166\u2192\\t//\\n   167\u2192\\t// Populated by the system.\\n   168\u2192\\t// Read-only.\\n   169\u2192\\t// Value must be treated as opaque by clients and .\\n   170\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#concurrency-control-and-consistency\\n   171\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   172\u2192\\tResourceVersion string `json:\\\"resourceVersion,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=resourceVersion\\\"`\\n   173\u2192\\n   174\u2192\\t// A sequence number representing a specific generation of the desired state.\\n   175\u2192\\t// Populated by the system. Read-only.\\n   176\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   177\u2192\\tGeneration int64 `json:\\\"generation,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,7,opt,name=generation\\\"`\\n   178\u2192\\n   179\u2192\\t// CreationTimestamp is a timestamp representing the server time when this object was\\n   180\u2192\\t// created. It is not guaranteed to be set in happens-before order across separate operations.\\n   181\u2192\\t// Clients may not set this value. It is represented in RFC3339 form and is in UTC.\\n   182\u2192\\t//\\n   183\u2192\\t// Populated by the system.\\n   184\u2192\\t// Read-only.\\n   185\u2192\\t// Null for lists.\\n   186\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\\n   187\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   188\u2192\\tCreationTimestamp Time `json:\\\"creationTimestamp,omitempty,omitzero\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,8,opt,name=creationTimestamp\\\"`\\n   189\u2192\\n   190\u2192\\t// DeletionTimestamp is RFC 3339 date and time at which this resource will be deleted. This\\n   191\u2192\\t// field is set by the server when a graceful deletion is requested by the user, and is not\\n   192\u2192\\t// directly settable by a client. The resource is expected to be deleted (no longer visible\\n   193\u2192\\t// from resource lists, and not reachable by name) after the time in this field, once the\\n   194\u2192\\t// finalizers list is empty. As long as the finalizers list contains items, deletion is blocked.\\n   195\u2192\\t// Once the deletionTimestamp is set, this value may not be unset or be set further into the\\n   196\u2192\\t// future, although it may be shortened or the resource may be deleted prior to this time.\\n   197\u2192\\t// For example, a user may request that a pod is deleted in 30 seconds. The Kubelet will react\\n   198\u2192\\t// by sending a graceful termination signal to the containers in the pod. After that 30 seconds,\\n   199\u2192\\t// the Kubelet will send a hard termination signal (SIGKILL) to the container and after cleanup,\\n   200\u2192\\t// remove the pod from the API. In the presence of network partitions, this object may still\\n   201\u2192\\t// exist after this timestamp, until an administrator or automated process can determine the\\n   202\u2192\\t// resource is fully terminated.\\n   203\u2192\\t// If not set, graceful deletion of the object has not been requested.\\n   204\u2192\\t//\\n   205\u2192\\t// Populated by the system when a graceful deletion is requested.\\n   206\u2192\\t// Read-only.\\n   207\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\\n   208\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   209\u2192\\tDeletionTimestamp *Time `json:\\\"deletionTimestamp,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,9,opt,name=deletionTimestamp\\\"`\\n   210\u2192\\n   211\u2192\\t// Number of seconds allowed for this object to gracefully terminate before\\n   212\u2192\\t// it will be removed from the system. Only set when deletionTimestamp is also set.\\n   213\u2192\\t// May only be shortened.\\n   214\u2192\\t// Read-only.\\n   215\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   216\u2192\\tDeletionGracePeriodSeconds *int64 `json:\\\"deletionGracePeriodSeconds,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,10,opt,name=deletionGracePeriodSeconds\\\"`\\n   217\u2192\\n   218\u2192\\t// Map of string keys and values that can be used to organize and categorize\\n   219\u2192\\t// (scope and select) objects. May match selectors of replication controllers\\n   220\u2192\\t// and services.\\n   221\u2192\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels\\n   222\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   223\u2192\\tLabels map[string]string `json:\\\"labels,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,11,rep,name=labels\\\"`\\n   224\u2192\\n   225\u2192\\t// Annotations is an unstructured key value map stored with a resource that may be\\n   226\u2192\\t// set by external tools to store and retrieve arbitrary metadata. They are not\\n   227\u2192\\t// queryable and should be preserved when modifying objects.\\n   228\u2192\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations\\n   229\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   230\u2192\\tAnnotations map[string]string `json:\\\"annotations,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,12,rep,name=annotations\\\"`\\n   231\u2192\\n   232\u2192\\t// List of objects depended by this object. If ALL objects in the list have\\n   233\u2192\\t// been deleted, this object will be garbage collected. If this object is managed by a controller,\\n   234\u2192\\t// then an entry in this list will point to this controller, with the controller field set to true.\\n   235\u2192\\t// There cannot be more than one managing controller.\\n   236\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   237\u2192\\t// +patchMergeKey=uid\\n   238\u2192\\t// +patchStrategy=merge\\n   239\u2192\\t// +listType=map\\n   240\u2192\\t// +listMapKey=uid\\n   241\u2192\\tOwnerReferences []OwnerReference `json:\\\"ownerReferences,omitempty\\\" patchStrategy:\\\"merge\\\" patchMergeKey:\\\"uid\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,13,rep,name=ownerReferences\\\"`\\n   242\u2192\\n   243\u2192\\t// Must be empty before the object is deleted from the registry. Each entry\\n   244\u2192\\t// is an identifier for the responsible component that will remove the entry\\n   245\u2192\\t// from the list. If the deletionTimestamp of the object is non-nil, entries\\n   246\u2192\\t// in this list can only be removed.\\n   247\u2192\\t// Finalizers may be processed and removed in any order.  Order is NOT enforced\\n   248\u2192\\t// because it introduces significant risk of stuck finalizers.\\n   249\u2192\\t// finalizers is a shared field, any actor with permission can reorder it.\\n   250\u2192\\t// If the finalizer list is processed in order, then this can lead to a situation\\n   251\u2192\\t// in which the component responsible for the first finalizer in the list is\\n   252\u2192\\t// waiting for a signal (field value, external system, or other) produced by a\\n   253\u2192\\t// component responsible for a finalizer later in the list, resulting in a deadlock.\\n   254\u2192\\t// Without enforced ordering finalizers are free to order amongst themselves and\\n   255\u2192\\t// are not vulnerable to ordering changes in the list.\\n   256\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   257\u2192\\t// +patchStrategy=merge\\n   258\u2192\\t// +listType=set\\n   259\u2192\\tFinalizers []string `json:\\\"finalizers,omitempty\\\" patchStrategy:\\\"merge\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,14,rep,name=finalizers\\\"`\\n   260\u2192\\n   261\u2192\\t// Tombstone: ClusterName was a legacy field that was always cleared by\\n   262\u2192\\t// the system and never used.\\n   263\u2192\\t// ClusterName string `json:\\\"clusterName,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,15,opt,name=clusterName\\\"`\\n   264\u2192\\n   265\u2192\\t// ManagedFields maps workflow-id and version to the set of fields\\n   266\u2192\\t// that are managed by that workflow. This is mostly for internal\\n   267\u2192\\t// housekeeping, and users typically shouldn't need to set or\\n   268\u2192\\t// understand this field. A workflow can be the user's name, a\\n   269\u2192\\t// controller's name, or the name of a specific apply path like\\n   270\u2192\\t// \\\"ci-cd\\\". The set of fields is always in the version that the\\n   271\u2192\\t// workflow used when modifying the object.\\n   272\u2192\\t//\\n   273\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   274\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n   275\u2192\\tManagedFields []ManagedFieldsEntry `json:\\\"managedFields,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,17,rep,name=managedFields\\\"`\\n   276\u2192}\\n   277\u2192\\n   278\u2192const (\\n   279\u2192\\t// NamespaceDefault means the object is in the default namespace which is applied when not specified by clients\\n   280\u2192\\tNamespaceDefault = \\\"default\\\"\\n   281\u2192\\t// NamespaceAll is the default argument to specify on a context when you want to list or filter resources across all namespaces\\n   282\u2192\\tNamespaceAll = \\\"\\\"\\n   283\u2192\\t// NamespaceNone is the argument for a context when there is no namespace.\\n   284\u2192\\tNamespaceNone = \\\"\\\"\\n   285\u2192\\t// NamespaceSystem is the system namespace where we place system components.\\n   286\u2192\\tNamespaceSystem = \\\"kube-system\\\"\\n   287\u2192\\t// NamespacePublic is the namespace where we place public info (ConfigMaps)\\n   288\u2192\\tNamespacePublic = \\\"kube-public\\\"\\n   289\u2192)\\n   290\u2192\\n   291\u2192// OwnerReference contains enough information to let you identify an owning\\n   292\u2192// object. An owning object must be in the same namespace as the dependent, or\\n   293\u2192// be cluster-scoped, so there is no namespace field.\\n   294\u2192// +structType=atomic\\n   295\u2192type OwnerReference struct {\\n   296\u2192\\t// API version of the referent.\\n   297\u2192\\tAPIVersion string `json:\\\"apiVersion\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=apiVersion\\\"`\\n   298\u2192\\t// Kind of the referent.\\n   299\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds\\n   300\u2192\\tKind string `json:\\\"kind\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=kind\\\"`\\n   301\u2192\\t// Name of the referent.\\n   302\u2192\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names#names\\n   303\u2192\\tName string `json:\\\"name\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=name\\\"`\\n   304\u2192\\t// UID of the referent.\\n   305\u2192\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names#uids\\n   306\u2192\\tUID types.UID `json:\\\"uid\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=uid,casttype=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types.UID\\\"`\\n   307\u2192\\t// If true, this reference points to the managing controller.\\n   308\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   309\u2192\\tController *bool `json:\\\"controller,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,6,opt,name=controller\\\"`\\n   310\u2192\\t// If true, AND if the owner has the \\\"foregroundDeletion\\\" finalizer, then\\n   311\u2192\\t// the owner cannot be deleted from the key-value store until this\\n   312\u2192\\t// reference is removed.\\n   313\u2192\\t// See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/architecture/garbage-collection/#foreground-deletion\\n   314\u2192\\t// for how the garbage collector interacts with this field and enforces the foreground deletion.\\n   315\u2192\\t// Defaults to false.\\n   316\u2192\\t// To set this field, a user needs \\\"delete\\\" permission of the owner,\\n   317\u2192\\t// otherwise 422 (Unprocessable Entity) will be returned.\\n   318\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   319\u2192\\tBlockOwnerDeletion *bool `json:\\\"blockOwnerDeletion,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,7,opt,name=blockOwnerDeletion\\\"`\\n   320\u2192}\\n   321\u2192\\n   322\u2192// +k8s:conversion-gen:explicit-from=net/url.Values\\n   323\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n   324\u2192\\n   325\u2192// ListOptions is the query options to a standard REST list call.\\n   326\u2192type ListOptions struct {\\n   327\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n   328\u2192\\n   329\u2192\\t// A selector to restrict the list of returned objects by their labels.\\n   330\u2192\\t// Defaults to everything.\\n   331\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   332\u2192\\tLabelSelector string `json:\\\"labelSelector,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=labelSelector\\\"`\\n   333\u2192\\t// A selector to restrict the list of returned objects by their fields.\\n   334\u2192\\t// Defaults to everything.\\n   335\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   336\u2192\\tFieldSelector string `json:\\\"fieldSelector,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=fieldSelector\\\"`\\n   337\u2192\\n   338\u2192\\t// +k8s:deprecated=includeUninitialized,protobuf=6\\n   339\u2192\\n   340\u2192\\t// Watch for changes to the described resources and return them as a stream of\\n   341\u2192\\t// add, update, and remove notifications. Specify resourceVersion.\\n   342\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   343\u2192\\tWatch bool `json:\\\"watch,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,3,opt,name=watch\\\"`\\n   344\u2192\\t// allowWatchBookmarks requests watch events with type \\\"BOOKMARK\\\".\\n   345\u2192\\t// Servers that do not implement bookmarks may ignore this flag and\\n   346\u2192\\t// bookmarks are sent at the server's discretion. Clients should not\\n   347\u2192\\t// assume bookmarks are returned at any specific interval, nor may they\\n   348\u2192\\t// assume the server will send any BOOKMARK event during a session.\\n   349\u2192\\t// If this is not a watch, this field is ignored.\\n   350\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   351\u2192\\tAllowWatchBookmarks bool `json:\\\"allowWatchBookmarks,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,9,opt,name=allowWatchBookmarks\\\"`\\n   352\u2192\\n   353\u2192\\t// resourceVersion sets a constraint on what resource versions a request may be served from.\\n   354\u2192\\t// See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/using-api/api-concepts/#resource-versions for\\n   355\u2192\\t// details.\\n   356\u2192\\t//\\n   357\u2192\\t// Defaults to unset\\n   358\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   359\u2192\\tResourceVersion string `json:\\\"resourceVersion,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=resourceVersion\\\"`\\n   360\u2192\\n   361\u2192\\t// resourceVersionMatch determines how resourceVersion is applied to list calls.\\n   362\u2192\\t// It is highly recommended that resourceVersionMatch be set for list calls where\\n   363\u2192\\t// resourceVersion is set\\n   364\u2192\\t// See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/using-api/api-concepts/#resource-versions for\\n   365\u2192\\t// details.\\n   366\u2192\\t//\\n   367\u2192\\t// Defaults to unset\\n   368\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   369\u2192\\tResourceVersionMatch ResourceVersionMatch `json:\\\"resourceVersionMatch,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,10,opt,name=resourceVersionMatch,casttype=ResourceVersionMatch\\\"`\\n   370\u2192\\t// Timeout for the list/watch call.\\n   371\u2192\\t// This limits the duration of the call, regardless of any activity or inactivity.\\n   372\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   373\u2192\\tTimeoutSeconds *int64 `json:\\\"timeoutSeconds,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,5,opt,name=timeoutSeconds\\\"`\\n   374\u2192\\n   375\u2192\\t// limit is a maximum number of responses to return for a list call. If more items exist, the\\n   376\u2192\\t// server will set the `continue` field on the list metadata to a value that can be used with the\\n   377\u2192\\t// same initial query to retrieve the next set of results. Setting a limit may return fewer than\\n   378\u2192\\t// the requested amount of items (up to zero items) in the event all requested objects are\\n   379\u2192\\t// filtered out and clients should only use the presence of the continue field to determine whether\\n   380\u2192\\t// more results are available. Servers may choose not to support the limit argument and will return\\n   381\u2192\\t// all of the available results. If limit is specified and the continue field is empty, clients may\\n   382\u2192\\t// assume that no more results are available. This field is not supported if watch is true.\\n   383\u2192\\t//\\n   384\u2192\\t// The server guarantees that the objects returned when using continue will be identical to issuing\\n   385\u2192\\t// a single list call without a limit - that is, no objects created, modified, or deleted after the\\n   386\u2192\\t// first request is issued will be included in any subsequent continued requests. This is sometimes\\n   387\u2192\\t// referred to as a consistent snapshot, and ensures that a client that is using limit to receive\\n   388\u2192\\t// smaller chunks of a very large result can ensure they see all possible objects. If objects are\\n   389\u2192\\t// updated during a chunked list the version of the object that was present at the time the first list\\n   390\u2192\\t// result was calculated is returned.\\n   391\u2192\\tLimit int64 `json:\\\"limit,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,7,opt,name=limit\\\"`\\n   392\u2192\\t// The continue option should be set when retrieving more results from the server. Since this value is\\n   393\u2192\\t// server defined, clients may only use the continue value from a previous query result with identical\\n   394\u2192\\t// query parameters (except for the value of continue) and the server may reject a continue value it\\n   395\u2192\\t// does not recognize. If the specified continue value is no longer valid whether due to expiration\\n   396\u2192\\t// (generally five to fifteen minutes) or a configuration change on the server, the server will\\n   397\u2192\\t// respond with a 410 ResourceExpired error together with a continue token. If the client needs a\\n   398\u2192\\t// consistent list, it must restart their list without the continue field. Otherwise, the client may\\n   399\u2192\\t// send another list request with the token received with the 410 error, the server will respond with\\n   400\u2192\\t// a list starting from the next key, but from the latest snapshot, which is inconsistent from the\\n   401\u2192\\t// previous list results - objects that are created, modified, or deleted after the first list request\\n   402\u2192\\t// will be included in the response, as long as their keys are after the \\\"next key\\\".\\n   403\u2192\\t//\\n   404\u2192\\t// This field is not supported when watch is true. Clients may start a watch from the last\\n   405\u2192\\t// resourceVersion value returned by the server and not miss any modifications.\\n   406\u2192\\tContinue string `json:\\\"continue,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,8,opt,name=continue\\\"`\\n   407\u2192\\n   408\u2192\\t// `sendInitialEvents=true` may be set together with `watch=true`.\\n   409\u2192\\t// In that case, the watch stream will begin with synthetic events to\\n   410\u2192\\t// produce the current state of objects in the collection. Once all such\\n   411\u2192\\t// events have been sent, a synthetic \\\"Bookmark\\\" event  will be sent.\\n   412\u2192\\t// The bookmark will report the ResourceVersion (RV) corresponding to the\\n   413\u2192\\t// set of objects, and be marked with `\\\"k8s.io/initial-events-end\\\": \\\"true\\\"` annotation.\\n   414\u2192\\t// Afterwards, the watch stream will proceed as usual, sending watch events\\n   415\u2192\\t// corresponding to changes (subsequent to the RV) to objects watched.\\n   416\u2192\\t//\\n   417\u2192\\t// When `sendInitialEvents` option is set, we require `resourceVersionMatch`\\n   418\u2192\\t// option to also be set. The semantic of the watch request is as following:\\n   419\u2192\\t// - `resourceVersionMatch` = NotOlderThan\\n   420\u2192\\t//   is interpreted as \\\"data at least as new as the provided `resourceVersion`\\\"\\n   421\u2192\\t//   and the bookmark event is send when the state is synced\\n   422\u2192\\t//   to a `resourceVersion` at least as fresh as the one provided by the ListOptions.\\n   423\u2192\\t//   If `resourceVersion` is unset, this is interpreted as \\\"consistent read\\\" and the\\n   424\u2192\\t//   bookmark event is send when the state is synced at least to the moment\\n   425\u2192\\t//   when request started being processed.\\n   426\u2192\\t// - `resourceVersionMatch` set to any other value or unset\\n   427\u2192\\t//   Invalid error is returned.\\n   428\u2192\\t//\\n   429\u2192\\t// Defaults to true if `resourceVersion=\\\"\\\"` or `resourceVersion=\\\"0\\\"` (for backward\\n   430\u2192\\t// compatibility reasons) and to false otherwise.\\n   431\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   432\u2192\\tSendInitialEvents *bool `json:\\\"sendInitialEvents,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,11,opt,name=sendInitialEvents\\\"`\\n   433\u2192}\\n   434\u2192\\n   435\u2192const (\\n   436\u2192\\t// InitialEventsAnnotationKey the name of the key\\n   437\u2192\\t// under which an annotation marking the end of\\n   438\u2192\\t// a watchlist stream is stored.\\n   439\u2192\\t//\\n   440\u2192\\t// The annotation is added to a \\\"Bookmark\\\" event.\\n   441\u2192\\tInitialEventsAnnotationKey = \\\"k8s.io/initial-events-end\\\"\\n   442\u2192)\\n   443\u2192\\n   444\u2192// resourceVersionMatch specifies how the resourceVersion parameter is applied. resourceVersionMatch\\n   445\u2192// may only be set if resourceVersion is also set.\\n   446\u2192//\\n   447\u2192// \\\"NotOlderThan\\\" matches data at least as new as the provided resourceVersion.\\n   448\u2192// \\\"Exact\\\" matches data at the exact resourceVersion provided.\\n   449\u2192//\\n   450\u2192// See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/using-api/api-concepts/#resource-versions for\\n   451\u2192// details.\\n   452\u2192type ResourceVersionMatch string\\n   453\u2192\\n   454\u2192const (\\n   455\u2192\\t// ResourceVersionMatchNotOlderThan matches data at least as new as the provided\\n   456\u2192\\t// resourceVersion.\\n   457\u2192\\tResourceVersionMatchNotOlderThan ResourceVersionMatch = \\\"NotOlderThan\\\"\\n   458\u2192\\t// ResourceVersionMatchExact matches data at the exact resourceVersion\\n   459\u2192\\t// provided.\\n   460\u2192\\tResourceVersionMatchExact ResourceVersionMatch = \\\"Exact\\\"\\n   461\u2192)\\n   462\u2192\\n   463\u2192// +k8s:conversion-gen:explicit-from=net/url.Values\\n   464\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n   465\u2192\\n   466\u2192// GetOptions is the standard query options to the standard REST get call.\\n   467\u2192type GetOptions struct {\\n   468\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n   469\u2192\\t// resourceVersion sets a constraint on what resource versions a request may be served from.\\n   470\u2192\\t// See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/using-api/api-concepts/#resource-versions for\\n   471\u2192\\t// details.\\n   472\u2192\\t//\\n   473\u2192\\t// Defaults to unset\\n   474\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   475\u2192\\tResourceVersion string `json:\\\"resourceVersion,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=resourceVersion\\\"`\\n   476\u2192\\t// +k8s:deprecated=includeUninitialized,protobuf=2\\n   477\u2192}\\n   478\u2192\\n   479\u2192// DeletionPropagation decides if a deletion will propagate to the dependents of\\n   480\u2192// the object, and how the garbage collector will handle the propagation.\\n   481\u2192type DeletionPropagation string\\n   482\u2192\\n   483\u2192const (\\n   484\u2192\\t// Orphans the dependents.\\n   485\u2192\\tDeletePropagationOrphan DeletionPropagation = \\\"Orphan\\\"\\n   486\u2192\\t// Deletes the object from the key-value store, the garbage collector will\\n   487\u2192\\t// delete the dependents in the background.\\n   488\u2192\\tDeletePropagationBackground DeletionPropagation = \\\"Background\\\"\\n   489\u2192\\t// The object exists in the key-value store until the garbage collector\\n   490\u2192\\t// deletes all the dependents whose ownerReference.blockOwnerDeletion=true\\n   491\u2192\\t// from the key-value store.  API sever will put the \\\"foregroundDeletion\\\"\\n   492\u2192\\t// finalizer on the object, and sets its deletionTimestamp.  This policy is\\n   493\u2192\\t// cascading, i.e., the dependents will be deleted with Foreground.\\n   494\u2192\\tDeletePropagationForeground DeletionPropagation = \\\"Foreground\\\"\\n   495\u2192)\\n   496\u2192\\n   497\u2192const (\\n   498\u2192\\t// DryRunAll means to complete all processing stages, but don't\\n   499\u2192\\t// persist changes to storage.\\n   500\u2192\\tDryRunAll = \\\"All\\\"\\n   501\u2192)\\n   502\u2192\\n   503\u2192// +k8s:conversion-gen:explicit-from=net/url.Values\\n   504\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n   505\u2192\\n   506\u2192// DeleteOptions may be provided when deleting an API object.\\n   507\u2192type DeleteOptions struct {\\n   508\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n   509\u2192\\n   510\u2192\\t// The duration in seconds before the object should be deleted. Value must be non-negative integer.\\n   511\u2192\\t// The value zero indicates delete immediately. If this value is nil, the default grace period for the\\n   512\u2192\\t// specified type will be used.\\n   513\u2192\\t// Defaults to a per object value if not specified. zero means delete immediately.\\n   514\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   515\u2192\\tGracePeriodSeconds *int64 `json:\\\"gracePeriodSeconds,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,1,opt,name=gracePeriodSeconds\\\"`\\n   516\u2192\\n   517\u2192\\t// Must be fulfilled before a deletion is carried out. If not possible, a 409 Conflict status will be\\n   518\u2192\\t// returned.\\n   519\u2192\\t// +k8s:conversion-gen=false\\n   520\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   521\u2192\\tPreconditions *Preconditions `json:\\\"preconditions,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=preconditions\\\"`\\n   522\u2192\\n   523\u2192\\t// Deprecated: please use the PropagationPolicy, this field will be deprecated in 1.7.\\n   524\u2192\\t// Should the dependent objects be orphaned. If true/false, the \\\"orphan\\\"\\n   525\u2192\\t// finalizer will be added to/removed from the object's finalizers list.\\n   526\u2192\\t// Either this field or PropagationPolicy may be set, but not both.\\n   527\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   528\u2192\\tOrphanDependents *bool `json:\\\"orphanDependents,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,3,opt,name=orphanDependents\\\"`\\n   529\u2192\\n   530\u2192\\t// Whether and how garbage collection will be performed.\\n   531\u2192\\t// Either this field or OrphanDependents may be set, but not both.\\n   532\u2192\\t// The default policy is decided by the existing finalizer set in the\\n   533\u2192\\t// metadata.finalizers and the resource-specific default policy.\\n   534\u2192\\t// Acceptable values are: 'Orphan' - orphan the dependents; 'Background' -\\n   535\u2192\\t// allow the garbage collector to delete the dependents in the background;\\n   536\u2192\\t// 'Foreground' - a cascading policy that deletes all dependents in the\\n   537\u2192\\t// foreground.\\n   538\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   539\u2192\\tPropagationPolicy *DeletionPropagation `json:\\\"propagationPolicy,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,4,opt,name=propagationPolicy\\\"`\\n   540\u2192\\n   541\u2192\\t// When present, indicates that modifications should not be\\n   542\u2192\\t// persisted. An invalid or unrecognized dryRun directive will\\n   543\u2192\\t// result in an error response and no further processing of the\\n   544\u2192\\t// request. Valid values are:\\n   545\u2192\\t// - All: all dry run stages will be processed\\n   546\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   547\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n   548\u2192\\tDryRun []string `json:\\\"dryRun,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,5,rep,name=dryRun\\\"`\\n   549\u2192\\n   550\u2192\\t// if set to true, it will trigger an unsafe deletion of the resource in\\n   551\u2192\\t// case the normal deletion flow fails with a corrupt object error.\\n   552\u2192\\t// A resource is considered corrupt if it can not be retrieved from\\n   553\u2192\\t// the underlying storage successfully because of a) its data can\\n   554\u2192\\t// not be transformed e.g. decryption failure, or b) it fails\\n   555\u2192\\t// to decode into an object.\\n   556\u2192\\t// NOTE: unsafe deletion ignores finalizer constraints, skips\\n   557\u2192\\t// precondition checks, and removes the object from the storage.\\n   558\u2192\\t// WARNING: This may potentially break the cluster if the workload\\n   559\u2192\\t// associated with the resource being unsafe-deleted relies on normal\\n   560\u2192\\t// deletion flow. Use only if you REALLY know what you are doing.\\n   561\u2192\\t// The default value is false, and the user must opt in to enable it\\n   562\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   563\u2192\\tIgnoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential *bool `json:\\\"ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,6,opt,name=ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential\\\"`\\n   564\u2192}\\n   565\u2192\\n   566\u2192const (\\n   567\u2192\\t// FieldValidationIgnore ignores unknown/duplicate fields\\n   568\u2192\\tFieldValidationIgnore = \\\"Ignore\\\"\\n   569\u2192\\t// FieldValidationWarn responds with a warning, but successfully serve the request\\n   570\u2192\\tFieldValidationWarn = \\\"Warn\\\"\\n   571\u2192\\t// FieldValidationStrict fails the request on unknown/duplicate fields\\n   572\u2192\\tFieldValidationStrict = \\\"Strict\\\"\\n   573\u2192)\\n   574\u2192\\n   575\u2192// +k8s:conversion-gen:explicit-from=net/url.Values\\n   576\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n   577\u2192\\n   578\u2192// CreateOptions may be provided when creating an API object.\\n   579\u2192type CreateOptions struct {\\n   580\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n   581\u2192\\n   582\u2192\\t// When present, indicates that modifications should not be\\n   583\u2192\\t// persisted. An invalid or unrecognized dryRun directive will\\n   584\u2192\\t// result in an error response and no further processing of the\\n   585\u2192\\t// request. Valid values are:\\n   586\u2192\\t// - All: all dry run stages will be processed\\n   587\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   588\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n   589\u2192\\tDryRun []string `json:\\\"dryRun,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,rep,name=dryRun\\\"`\\n   590\u2192\\t// +k8s:deprecated=includeUninitialized,protobuf=2\\n   591\u2192\\n   592\u2192\\t// fieldManager is a name associated with the actor or entity\\n   593\u2192\\t// that is making these changes. The value must be less than or\\n   594\u2192\\t// 128 characters long, and only contain printable characters,\\n   595\u2192\\t// as defined by https://golang.org/pkg/unicode/#IsPrint.\\n   596\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   597\u2192\\tFieldManager string `json:\\\"fieldManager,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,name=fieldManager\\\"`\\n   598\u2192\\n   599\u2192\\t// fieldValidation instructs the server on how to handle\\n   600\u2192\\t// objects in the request (POST/PUT/PATCH) containing unknown\\n   601\u2192\\t// or duplicate fields. Valid values are:\\n   602\u2192\\t// - Ignore: This will ignore any unknown fields that are silently\\n   603\u2192\\t// dropped from the object, and will ignore all but the last duplicate\\n   604\u2192\\t// field that the decoder encounters. This is the default behavior\\n   605\u2192\\t// prior to v1.23.\\n   606\u2192\\t// - Warn: This will send a warning via the standard warning response\\n   607\u2192\\t// header for each unknown field that is dropped from the object, and\\n   608\u2192\\t// for each duplicate field that is encountered. The request will\\n   609\u2192\\t// still succeed if there are no other errors, and will only persist\\n   610\u2192\\t// the last of any duplicate fields. This is the default in v1.23+\\n   611\u2192\\t// - Strict: This will fail the request with a BadRequest error if\\n   612\u2192\\t// any unknown fields would be dropped from the object, or if any\\n   613\u2192\\t// duplicate fields are present. The error returned from the server\\n   614\u2192\\t// will contain all unknown and duplicate fields encountered.\\n   615\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   616\u2192\\tFieldValidation string `json:\\\"fieldValidation,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,name=fieldValidation\\\"`\\n   617\u2192}\\n   618\u2192\\n   619\u2192// +k8s:conversion-gen:explicit-from=net/url.Values\\n   620\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n   621\u2192\\n   622\u2192// PatchOptions may be provided when patching an API object.\\n   623\u2192// PatchOptions is meant to be a superset of UpdateOptions.\\n   624\u2192type PatchOptions struct {\\n   625\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n   626\u2192\\n   627\u2192\\t// When present, indicates that modifications should not be\\n   628\u2192\\t// persisted. An invalid or unrecognized dryRun directive will\\n   629\u2192\\t// result in an error response and no further processing of the\\n   630\u2192\\t// request. Valid values are:\\n   631\u2192\\t// - All: all dry run stages will be processed\\n   632\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   633\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n   634\u2192\\tDryRun []string `json:\\\"dryRun,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,rep,name=dryRun\\\"`\\n   635\u2192\\n   636\u2192\\t// Force is going to \\\"force\\\" Apply requests. It means user will\\n   637\u2192\\t// re-acquire conflicting fields owned by other people. Force\\n   638\u2192\\t// flag must be unset for non-apply patch requests.\\n   639\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   640\u2192\\tForce *bool `json:\\\"force,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,2,opt,name=force\\\"`\\n   641\u2192\\n   642\u2192\\t// fieldManager is a name associated with the actor or entity\\n   643\u2192\\t// that is making these changes. The value must be less than or\\n   644\u2192\\t// 128 characters long, and only contain printable characters,\\n   645\u2192\\t// as defined by https://golang.org/pkg/unicode/#IsPrint. This\\n   646\u2192\\t// field is required for apply requests\\n   647\u2192\\t// (application/apply-patch) but optional for non-apply patch\\n   648\u2192\\t// types (JsonPatch, MergePatch, StrategicMergePatch).\\n   649\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   650\u2192\\tFieldManager string `json:\\\"fieldManager,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,name=fieldManager\\\"`\\n   651\u2192\\n   652\u2192\\t// fieldValidation instructs the server on how to handle\\n   653\u2192\\t// objects in the request (POST/PUT/PATCH) containing unknown\\n   654\u2192\\t// or duplicate fields. Valid values are:\\n   655\u2192\\t// - Ignore: This will ignore any unknown fields that are silently\\n   656\u2192\\t// dropped from the object, and will ignore all but the last duplicate\\n   657\u2192\\t// field that the decoder encounters. This is the default behavior\\n   658\u2192\\t// prior to v1.23.\\n   659\u2192\\t// - Warn: This will send a warning via the standard warning response\\n   660\u2192\\t// header for each unknown field that is dropped from the object, and\\n   661\u2192\\t// for each duplicate field that is encountered. The request will\\n   662\u2192\\t// still succeed if there are no other errors, and will only persist\\n   663\u2192\\t// the last of any duplicate fields. This is the default in v1.23+\\n   664\u2192\\t// - Strict: This will fail the request with a BadRequest error if\\n   665\u2192\\t// any unknown fields would be dropped from the object, or if any\\n   666\u2192\\t// duplicate fields are present. The error returned from the server\\n   667\u2192\\t// will contain all unknown and duplicate fields encountered.\\n   668\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   669\u2192\\tFieldValidation string `json:\\\"fieldValidation,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,name=fieldValidation\\\"`\\n   670\u2192}\\n   671\u2192\\n   672\u2192// ApplyOptions may be provided when applying an API object.\\n   673\u2192// FieldManager is required for apply requests.\\n   674\u2192// ApplyOptions is equivalent to PatchOptions. It is provided as a convenience with documentation\\n   675\u2192// that speaks specifically to how the options fields relate to apply.\\n   676\u2192type ApplyOptions struct {\\n   677\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n   678\u2192\\n   679\u2192\\t// When present, indicates that modifications should not be\\n   680\u2192\\t// persisted. An invalid or unrecognized dryRun directive will\\n   681\u2192\\t// result in an error response and no further processing of the\\n   682\u2192\\t// request. Valid values are:\\n   683\u2192\\t// - All: all dry run stages will be processed\\n   684\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   685\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n   686\u2192\\tDryRun []string `json:\\\"dryRun,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,rep,name=dryRun\\\"`\\n   687\u2192\\n   688\u2192\\t// Force is going to \\\"force\\\" Apply requests. It means user will\\n   689\u2192\\t// re-acquire conflicting fields owned by other people.\\n   690\u2192\\tForce bool `json:\\\"force\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,2,opt,name=force\\\"`\\n   691\u2192\\n   692\u2192\\t// fieldManager is a name associated with the actor or entity\\n   693\u2192\\t// that is making these changes. The value must be less than or\\n   694\u2192\\t// 128 characters long, and only contain printable characters,\\n   695\u2192\\t// as defined by https://golang.org/pkg/unicode/#IsPrint. This\\n   696\u2192\\t// field is required.\\n   697\u2192\\tFieldManager string `json:\\\"fieldManager\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,name=fieldManager\\\"`\\n   698\u2192}\\n   699\u2192\\n   700\u2192func (o ApplyOptions) ToPatchOptions() PatchOptions {\\n   701\u2192\\treturn PatchOptions{DryRun: o.DryRun, Force: &o.Force, FieldManager: o.FieldManager}\\n   702\u2192}\\n   703\u2192\\n   704\u2192// +k8s:conversion-gen:explicit-from=net/url.Values\\n   705\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n   706\u2192\\n   707\u2192// UpdateOptions may be provided when updating an API object.\\n   708\u2192// All fields in UpdateOptions should also be present in PatchOptions.\\n   709\u2192type UpdateOptions struct {\\n   710\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n   711\u2192\\n   712\u2192\\t// When present, indicates that modifications should not be\\n   713\u2192\\t// persisted. An invalid or unrecognized dryRun directive will\\n   714\u2192\\t// result in an error response and no further processing of the\\n   715\u2192\\t// request. Valid values are:\\n   716\u2192\\t// - All: all dry run stages will be processed\\n   717\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   718\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n   719\u2192\\tDryRun []string `json:\\\"dryRun,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,rep,name=dryRun\\\"`\\n   720\u2192\\n   721\u2192\\t// fieldManager is a name associated with the actor or entity\\n   722\u2192\\t// that is making these changes. The value must be less than or\\n   723\u2192\\t// 128 characters long, and only contain printable characters,\\n   724\u2192\\t// as defined by https://golang.org/pkg/unicode/#IsPrint.\\n   725\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   726\u2192\\tFieldManager string `json:\\\"fieldManager,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,name=fieldManager\\\"`\\n   727\u2192\\n   728\u2192\\t// fieldValidation instructs the server on how to handle\\n   729\u2192\\t// objects in the request (POST/PUT/PATCH) containing unknown\\n   730\u2192\\t// or duplicate fields. Valid values are:\\n   731\u2192\\t// - Ignore: This will ignore any unknown fields that are silently\\n   732\u2192\\t// dropped from the object, and will ignore all but the last duplicate\\n   733\u2192\\t// field that the decoder encounters. This is the default behavior\\n   734\u2192\\t// prior to v1.23.\\n   735\u2192\\t// - Warn: This will send a warning via the standard warning response\\n   736\u2192\\t// header for each unknown field that is dropped from the object, and\\n   737\u2192\\t// for each duplicate field that is encountered. The request will\\n   738\u2192\\t// still succeed if there are no other errors, and will only persist\\n   739\u2192\\t// the last of any duplicate fields. This is the default in v1.23+\\n   740\u2192\\t// - Strict: This will fail the request with a BadRequest error if\\n   741\u2192\\t// any unknown fields would be dropped from the object, or if any\\n   742\u2192\\t// duplicate fields are present. The error returned from the server\\n   743\u2192\\t// will contain all unknown and duplicate fields encountered.\\n   744\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   745\u2192\\tFieldValidation string `json:\\\"fieldValidation,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,name=fieldValidation\\\"`\\n   746\u2192}\\n   747\u2192\\n   748\u2192// Preconditions must be fulfilled before an operation (update, delete, etc.) is carried out.\\n   749\u2192type Preconditions struct {\\n   750\u2192\\t// Specifies the target UID.\\n   751\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   752\u2192\\tUID *types.UID `json:\\\"uid,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=uid,casttype=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types.UID\\\"`\\n   753\u2192\\t// Specifies the target ResourceVersion\\n   754\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   755\u2192\\tResourceVersion *string `json:\\\"resourceVersion,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=resourceVersion\\\"`\\n   756\u2192}\\n   757\u2192\\n   758\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n   759\u2192\\n   760\u2192// Status is a return value for calls that don't return other objects.\\n   761\u2192type Status struct {\\n   762\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n   763\u2192\\t// Standard list metadata.\\n   764\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds\\n   765\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   766\u2192\\tListMeta `json:\\\"metadata,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\\\"`\\n   767\u2192\\n   768\u2192\\t// Status of the operation.\\n   769\u2192\\t// One of: \\\"Success\\\" or \\\"Failure\\\".\\n   770\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#spec-and-status\\n   771\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   772\u2192\\tStatus string `json:\\\"status,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=status\\\"`\\n   773\u2192\\t// A human-readable description of the status of this operation.\\n   774\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   775\u2192\\tMessage string `json:\\\"message,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=message\\\"`\\n   776\u2192\\t// A machine-readable description of why this operation is in the\\n   777\u2192\\t// \\\"Failure\\\" status. If this value is empty there\\n   778\u2192\\t// is no information available. A Reason clarifies an HTTP status\\n   779\u2192\\t// code but does not override it.\\n   780\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   781\u2192\\tReason StatusReason `json:\\\"reason,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=reason,casttype=StatusReason\\\"`\\n   782\u2192\\t// Extended data associated with the reason.  Each reason may define its\\n   783\u2192\\t// own extended details. This field is optional and the data returned\\n   784\u2192\\t// is not guaranteed to conform to any schema except that defined by\\n   785\u2192\\t// the reason type.\\n   786\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   787\u2192\\tDetails *StatusDetails `json:\\\"details,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=details\\\"`\\n   788\u2192\\t// Suggested HTTP return code for this status, 0 if not set.\\n   789\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   790\u2192\\tCode int32 `json:\\\"code,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,6,opt,name=code\\\"`\\n   791\u2192}\\n   792\u2192\\n   793\u2192// StatusDetails is a set of additional properties that MAY be set by the\\n   794\u2192// server to provide additional information about a response. The Reason\\n   795\u2192// field of a Status object defines what attributes will be set. Clients\\n   796\u2192// must ignore fields that do not match the defined type of each attribute,\\n   797\u2192// and should assume that any attribute may be empty, invalid, or under\\n   798\u2192// defined.\\n   799\u2192type StatusDetails struct {\\n   800\u2192\\t// The name attribute of the resource associated with the status StatusReason\\n   801\u2192\\t// (when there is a single name which can be described).\\n   802\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   803\u2192\\tName string `json:\\\"name,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=name\\\"`\\n   804\u2192\\t// The group attribute of the resource associated with the status StatusReason.\\n   805\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   806\u2192\\tGroup string `json:\\\"group,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=group\\\"`\\n   807\u2192\\t// The kind attribute of the resource associated with the status StatusReason.\\n   808\u2192\\t// On some operations may differ from the requested resource Kind.\\n   809\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds\\n   810\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   811\u2192\\tKind string `json:\\\"kind,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=kind\\\"`\\n   812\u2192\\t// UID of the resource.\\n   813\u2192\\t// (when there is a single resource which can be described).\\n   814\u2192\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names#uids\\n   815\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   816\u2192\\tUID types.UID `json:\\\"uid,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=uid,casttype=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types.UID\\\"`\\n   817\u2192\\t// The Causes array includes more details associated with the StatusReason\\n   818\u2192\\t// failure. Not all StatusReasons may provide detailed causes.\\n   819\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   820\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n   821\u2192\\tCauses []StatusCause `json:\\\"causes,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,rep,name=causes\\\"`\\n   822\u2192\\t// If specified, the time in seconds before the operation should be retried. Some errors may indicate\\n   823\u2192\\t// the client must take an alternate action - for those errors this field may indicate how long to wait\\n   824\u2192\\t// before taking the alternate action.\\n   825\u2192\\t// +optional\\n   826\u2192\\tRetryAfterSeconds int32 `json:\\\"retryAfterSeconds,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,5,opt,name=retryAfterSeconds\\\"`\\n   827\u2192}\\n   828\u2192\\n   829\u2192// Values of Status.Status\\n   830\u2192const (\\n   831\u2192\\tStatusSuccess = \\\"Success\\\"\\n   832\u2192\\tStatusFailure = \\\"Failure\\\"\\n   833\u2192)\\n   834\u2192\\n   835\u2192// StatusReason is an enumeration of possible failure causes.  Each StatusReason\\n   836\u2192// must map to a single HTTP status code, but multiple reasons may map\\n   837\u2192// to the same HTTP status code.\\n   838\u2192// TODO: move to apiserver\\n   839\u2192type StatusReason string\\n   840\u2192\\n   841\u2192const (\\n   842\u2192\\t// StatusReasonUnknown means the server has declined to indicate a specific reason.\\n   843\u2192\\t// The details field may contain other information about this error.\\n   844\u2192\\t// Status code 500.\\n   845\u2192\\tStatusReasonUnknown StatusReason = \\\"\\\"\\n   846\u2192\\n   847\u2192\\t// StatusReasonUnauthorized means the server can be reached and understood the request, but requires\\n   848\u2192\\t// the user to present appropriate authorization credentials (identified by the WWW-Authenticate header)\\n   849\u2192\\t// in order for the action to be completed. If the user has specified credentials on the request, the\\n   850\u2192\\t// server considers them insufficient.\\n   851\u2192\\t// Status code 401\\n   852\u2192\\tStatusReasonUnauthorized StatusReason = \\\"Unauthorized\\\"\\n   853\u2192\\n   854\u2192\\t// StatusReasonForbidden means the server can be reached and understood the request, but refuses\\n   855\u2192\\t// to take any further action.  It is the result of the server being configured to deny access for some reason\\n   856\u2192\\t// to the requested resource by the client.\\n   857\u2192\\t// Details (optional):\\n   858\u2192\\t//   \\\"kind\\\" string - the kind attribute of the forbidden resource\\n   859\u2192\\t//                   on some operations may differ from the requested\\n   860\u2192\\t//                   resource.\\n   861\u2192\\t//   \\\"id\\\"   string - the identifier of the forbidden resource\\n   862\u2192\\t// Status code 403\\n   863\u2192\\tStatusReasonForbidden StatusReason = \\\"Forbidden\\\"\\n   864\u2192\\n   865\u2192\\t// StatusReasonNotFound means one or more resources required for this operation\\n   866\u2192\\t// could not be found.\\n   867\u2192\\t// Details (optional):\\n   868\u2192\\t//   \\\"kind\\\" string - the kind attribute of the missing resource\\n   869\u2192\\t//                   on some operations may differ from the requested\\n   870\u2192\\t//                   resource.\\n   871\u2192\\t//   \\\"id\\\"   string - the identifier of the missing resource\\n   872\u2192\\t// Status code 404\\n   873\u2192\\tStatusReasonNotFound StatusReason = \\\"NotFound\\\"\\n   874\u2192\\n   875\u2192\\t// StatusReasonAlreadyExists means the resource you are creating already exists.\\n   876\u2192\\t// Details (optional):\\n   877\u2192\\t//   \\\"kind\\\" string - the kind attribute of the conflicting resource\\n   878\u2192\\t//   \\\"id\\\"   string - the identifier of the conflicting resource\\n   879\u2192\\t// Status code 409\\n   880\u2192\\tStatusReasonAlreadyExists StatusReason = \\\"AlreadyExists\\\"\\n   881\u2192\\n   882\u2192\\t// StatusReasonConflict means the requested operation cannot be completed\\n   883\u2192\\t// due to a conflict in the operation. The client may need to alter the\\n   884\u2192\\t// request. Each resource may define custom details that indicate the\\n   885\u2192\\t// nature of the conflict.\\n   886\u2192\\t// Status code 409\\n   887\u2192\\tStatusReasonConflict StatusReason = \\\"Conflict\\\"\\n   888\u2192\\n   889\u2192\\t// StatusReasonGone means the item is no longer available at the server and no\\n   890\u2192\\t// forwarding address is known.\\n   891\u2192\\t// Status code 410\\n   892\u2192\\tStatusReasonGone StatusReason = \\\"Gone\\\"\\n   893\u2192\\n   894\u2192\\t// StatusReasonInvalid means the requested create or update operation cannot be\\n   895\u2192\\t// completed due to invalid data provided as part of the request. The client may\\n   896\u2192\\t// need to alter the request. When set, the client may use the StatusDetails\\n   897\u2192\\t// message field as a summary of the issues encountered.\\n   898\u2192\\t// Details (optional):\\n   899\u2192\\t//   \\\"kind\\\" string - the kind attribute of the invalid resource\\n   900\u2192\\t//   \\\"id\\\"   string - the identifier of the invalid resource\\n   901\u2192\\t//   \\\"causes\\\"      - one or more StatusCause entries indicating the data in the\\n   902\u2192\\t//                   provided resource that was invalid.  The code, message, and\\n   903\u2192\\t//                   field attributes will be set.\\n   904\u2192\\t// Status code 422\\n   905\u2192\\tStatusReasonInvalid StatusReason = \\\"Invalid\\\"\\n   906\u2192\\n   907\u2192\\t// StatusReasonServerTimeout means the server can be reached and understood the request,\\n   908\u2192\\t// but cannot complete the action in a reasonable time. The client should retry the request.\\n   909\u2192\\t// This is may be due to temporary server load or a transient communication issue with\\n   910\u2192\\t// another server. Status code 500 is used because the HTTP spec provides no suitable\\n   911\u2192\\t// server-requested client retry and the 5xx class represents actionable errors.\\n   912\u2192\\t// Details (optional):\\n   913\u2192\\t//   \\\"kind\\\" string - the kind attribute of the resource being acted on.\\n   914\u2192\\t//   \\\"id\\\"   string - the operation that is being attempted.\\n   915\u2192\\t//   \\\"retryAfterSeconds\\\" int32 - the number of seconds before the operation should be retried\\n   916\u2192\\t// Status code 500\\n   917\u2192\\tStatusReasonServerTimeout StatusReason = \\\"ServerTimeout\\\"\\n   918\u2192\\n   919\u2192\\t// StatusReasonStoreReadError means that the server encountered an error while\\n   920\u2192\\t// retrieving resources from the backend object store.\\n   921\u2192\\t// This may be due to backend database error, or because processing of the read\\n   922\u2192\\t// resource failed.\\n   923\u2192\\t// Details:\\n   924\u2192\\t//   \\\"kind\\\" string - the kind attribute of the resource being acted on.\\n   925\u2192\\t//   \\\"name\\\" string - the prefix where the reading error(s) occurred\\n   926\u2192\\t//   \\\"causes\\\" []StatusCause\\n   927\u2192\\t//      - (optional):\\n   928\u2192\\t//        - \\\"type\\\" CauseType - CauseTypeUnexpectedServerResponse\\n   929\u2192\\t//        - \\\"message\\\" string - the error message from the store backend\\n   930\u2192\\t//        - \\\"field\\\" string - the full path with the key of the resource that failed reading\\n   931\u2192\\t//\\n   932\u2192\\t// Status code 500\\n   933\u2192\\tStatusReasonStoreReadError StatusReason = \\\"StorageReadError\\\"\\n   934\u2192\\n   935\u2192\\t// StatusReasonTimeout means that the request could not be completed within the given time.\\n   936\u2192\\t// Clients can get this response only when they specified a timeout param in the request,\\n   937\u2192\\t// or if the server cannot complete the operation within a reasonable amount of time.\\n   938\u2192\\t// The request might succeed with an increased value of timeout param. The client *should*\\n   939\u2192\\t// wait at least the number of seconds specified by the retryAfterSeconds field.\\n   940\u2192\\t// Details (optional):\\n   941\u2192\\t//   \\\"retryAfterSeconds\\\" int32 - the number of seconds before the operation should be retried\\n   942\u2192\\t// Status code 504\\n   943\u2192\\tStatusReasonTimeout StatusReason = \\\"Timeout\\\"\\n   944\u2192\\n   945\u2192\\t// StatusReasonTooManyRequests means the server experienced too many requests within a\\n   946\u2192\\t// given window and that the client must wait to perform the action again. A client may\\n   947\u2192\\t// always retry the request that led to this error, although the client should wait at least\\n   948\u2192\\t// the number of seconds specified by the retryAfterSeconds field.\\n   949\u2192\\t// Details (optional):\\n   950\u2192\\t//   \\\"retryAfterSeconds\\\" int32 - the number of seconds before the operation should be retried\\n   951\u2192\\t// Status code 429\\n   952\u2192\\tStatusReasonTooManyRequests StatusReason = \\\"TooManyRequests\\\"\\n   953\u2192\\n   954\u2192\\t// StatusReasonBadRequest means that the request itself was invalid, because the request\\n   955\u2192\\t// doesn't make any sense, for example deleting a read-only object.  This is different than\\n   956\u2192\\t// StatusReasonInvalid above which indicates that the API call could possibly succeed, but the\\n   957\u2192\\t// data was invalid.  API calls that return BadRequest can never succeed.\\n   958\u2192\\t// Status code 400\\n   959\u2192\\tStatusReasonBadRequest StatusReason = \\\"BadRequest\\\"\\n   960\u2192\\n   961\u2192\\t// StatusReasonMethodNotAllowed means that the action the client attempted to perform on the\\n   962\u2192\\t// resource was not supported by the code - for instance, attempting to delete a resource that\\n   963\u2192\\t// can only be created. API calls that return MethodNotAllowed can never succeed.\\n   964\u2192\\t// Status code 405\\n   965\u2192\\tStatusReasonMethodNotAllowed StatusReason = \\\"MethodNotAllowed\\\"\\n   966\u2192\\n   967\u2192\\t// StatusReasonNotAcceptable means that the accept types indicated by the client were not acceptable\\n   968\u2192\\t// to the server - for instance, attempting to receive protobuf for a resource that supports only json and yaml.\\n   969\u2192\\t// API calls that return NotAcceptable can never succeed.\\n   970\u2192\\t// Status code 406\\n   971\u2192\\tStatusReasonNotAcceptable StatusReason = \\\"NotAcceptable\\\"\\n   972\u2192\\n   973\u2192\\t// StatusReasonRequestEntityTooLarge means that the request entity is too large.\\n   974\u2192\\t// Status code 413\\n   975\u2192\\tStatusReasonRequestEntityTooLarge StatusReason = \\\"RequestEntityTooLarge\\\"\\n   976\u2192\\n   977\u2192\\t// StatusReasonUnsupportedMediaType means that the content type sent by the client is not acceptable\\n   978\u2192\\t// to the server - for instance, attempting to send protobuf for a resource that supports only json and yaml.\\n   979\u2192\\t// API calls that return UnsupportedMediaType can never succeed.\\n   980\u2192\\t// Status code 415\\n   981\u2192\\tStatusReasonUnsupportedMediaType StatusReason = \\\"UnsupportedMediaType\\\"\\n   982\u2192\\n   983\u2192\\t// StatusReasonInternalError indicates that an internal error occurred, it is unexpected\\n   984\u2192\\t// and the outcome of the call is unknown.\\n   985\u2192\\t// Details (optional):\\n   986\u2192\\t//   \\\"causes\\\" - The original error\\n   987\u2192\\t// Status code 500\\n   988\u2192\\tStatusReasonInternalError StatusReason = \\\"InternalError\\\"\\n   989\u2192\\n   990\u2192\\t// StatusReasonExpired indicates that the request is invalid because the content you are requesting\\n   991\u2192\\t// has expired and is no longer available. It is typically associated with watches that can't be\\n   992\u2192\\t// serviced.\\n   993\u2192\\t// Status code 410 (gone)\\n   994\u2192\\tStatusReasonExpired StatusReason = \\\"Expired\\\"\\n   995\u2192\\n   996\u2192\\t// StatusReasonServiceUnavailable means that the request itself was valid,\\n   997\u2192\\t// but the requested service is unavailable at this time.\\n   998\u2192\\t// Retrying the request after some time might succeed.\\n   999\u2192\\t// Status code 503\\n  1000\u2192\\tStatusReasonServiceUnavailable StatusReason = \\\"ServiceUnavailable\\\"\\n  1001\u2192)\\n  1002\u2192\\n  1003\u2192// StatusCause provides more information about an api.Status failure, including\\n  1004\u2192// cases when multiple errors are encountered.\\n  1005\u2192type StatusCause struct {\\n  1006\u2192\\t// A machine-readable description of the cause of the error. If this value is\\n  1007\u2192\\t// empty there is no information available.\\n  1008\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1009\u2192\\tType CauseType `json:\\\"reason,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=reason,casttype=CauseType\\\"`\\n  1010\u2192\\t// A human-readable description of the cause of the error.  This field may be\\n  1011\u2192\\t// presented as-is to a reader.\\n  1012\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1013\u2192\\tMessage string `json:\\\"message,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=message\\\"`\\n  1014\u2192\\t// The field of the resource that has caused this error, as named by its JSON\\n  1015\u2192\\t// serialization. May include dot and postfix notation for nested attributes.\\n  1016\u2192\\t// Arrays are zero-indexed.  Fields may appear more than once in an array of\\n  1017\u2192\\t// causes due to fields having multiple errors.\\n  1018\u2192\\t// Optional.\\n  1019\u2192\\t//\\n  1020\u2192\\t// Examples:\\n  1021\u2192\\t//   \\\"name\\\" - the field \\\"name\\\" on the current resource\\n  1022\u2192\\t//   \\\"items[0].name\\\" - the field \\\"name\\\" on the first array entry in \\\"items\\\"\\n  1023\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1024\u2192\\tField string `json:\\\"field,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=field\\\"`\\n  1025\u2192}\\n  1026\u2192\\n  1027\u2192// CauseType is a machine readable value providing more detail about what\\n  1028\u2192// occurred in a status response. An operation may have multiple causes for a\\n  1029\u2192// status (whether Failure or Success).\\n  1030\u2192type CauseType string\\n  1031\u2192\\n  1032\u2192const (\\n  1033\u2192\\t// CauseTypeFieldValueNotFound is used to report failure to find a requested value\\n  1034\u2192\\t// (e.g. looking up an ID).\\n  1035\u2192\\tCauseTypeFieldValueNotFound CauseType = \\\"FieldValueNotFound\\\"\\n  1036\u2192\\t// CauseTypeFieldValueRequired is used to report required values that are not\\n  1037\u2192\\t// provided (e.g. empty strings, null values, or empty arrays).\\n  1038\u2192\\tCauseTypeFieldValueRequired CauseType = \\\"FieldValueRequired\\\"\\n  1039\u2192\\t// CauseTypeFieldValueDuplicate is used to report collisions of values that must be\\n  1040\u2192\\t// unique (e.g. unique IDs).\\n  1041\u2192\\tCauseTypeFieldValueDuplicate CauseType = \\\"FieldValueDuplicate\\\"\\n  1042\u2192\\t// CauseTypeFieldValueInvalid is used to report malformed values (e.g. failed regex\\n  1043\u2192\\t// match).\\n  1044\u2192\\tCauseTypeFieldValueInvalid CauseType = \\\"FieldValueInvalid\\\"\\n  1045\u2192\\t// CauseTypeFieldValueNotSupported is used to report valid (as per formatting rules)\\n  1046\u2192\\t// values that can not be handled (e.g. an enumerated string).\\n  1047\u2192\\tCauseTypeFieldValueNotSupported CauseType = \\\"FieldValueNotSupported\\\"\\n  1048\u2192\\t// CauseTypeForbidden is used to report valid (as per formatting rules)\\n  1049\u2192\\t// values which would be accepted under some conditions, but which are not\\n  1050\u2192\\t// permitted by the current conditions (such as security policy).  See\\n  1051\u2192\\t// Forbidden().\\n  1052\u2192\\tCauseTypeForbidden CauseType = \\\"FieldValueForbidden\\\"\\n  1053\u2192\\t// CauseTypeTooLong is used to report that the given value is too long.\\n  1054\u2192\\t// This is similar to ErrorTypeInvalid, but the error will not include the\\n  1055\u2192\\t// too-long value.  See TooLong().\\n  1056\u2192\\tCauseTypeTooLong CauseType = \\\"FieldValueTooLong\\\"\\n  1057\u2192\\t// CauseTypeTooMany is used to report \\\"too many\\\". This is used to\\n  1058\u2192\\t// report that a given list has too many items. This is similar to FieldValueTooLong,\\n  1059\u2192\\t// but the error indicates quantity instead of length.\\n  1060\u2192\\tCauseTypeTooMany CauseType = \\\"FieldValueTooMany\\\"\\n  1061\u2192\\t// CauseTypeInternal is used to report other errors that are not related\\n  1062\u2192\\t// to user input.  See InternalError().\\n  1063\u2192\\tCauseTypeInternal CauseType = \\\"InternalError\\\"\\n  1064\u2192\\t// CauseTypeTypeInvalid is for the value did not match the schema type for that field\\n  1065\u2192\\tCauseTypeTypeInvalid CauseType = \\\"FieldValueTypeInvalid\\\"\\n  1066\u2192\\t// CauseTypeUnexpectedServerResponse is used to report when the server responded to the client\\n  1067\u2192\\t// without the expected return type. The presence of this cause indicates the error may be\\n  1068\u2192\\t// due to an intervening proxy or the server software malfunctioning.\\n  1069\u2192\\tCauseTypeUnexpectedServerResponse CauseType = \\\"UnexpectedServerResponse\\\"\\n  1070\u2192\\t// FieldManagerConflict is used to report when another client claims to manage this field,\\n  1071\u2192\\t// It should only be returned for a request using server-side apply.\\n  1072\u2192\\tCauseTypeFieldManagerConflict CauseType = \\\"FieldManagerConflict\\\"\\n  1073\u2192\\t// CauseTypeResourceVersionTooLarge is used to report that the requested resource version\\n  1074\u2192\\t// is newer than the data observed by the API server, so the request cannot be served.\\n  1075\u2192\\tCauseTypeResourceVersionTooLarge CauseType = \\\"ResourceVersionTooLarge\\\"\\n  1076\u2192)\\n  1077\u2192\\n  1078\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n  1079\u2192\\n  1080\u2192// List holds a list of objects, which may not be known by the server.\\n  1081\u2192type List struct {\\n  1082\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n  1083\u2192\\t// Standard list metadata.\\n  1084\u2192\\t// More
