protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\\\"`\\n  1087\u2192\\n  1088\u2192\\t// List of objects\\n  1089\u2192\\tItems []runtime.RawExtension `json:\\\"items\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\\\"`\\n  1090\u2192}\\n  1091\u2192\\n  1092\u2192// APIVersions lists the versions that are available, to allow clients to\\n  1093\u2192// discover the API at /api, which is the root path of the legacy v1 API.\\n  1094\u2192//\\n  1095\u2192// +protobuf.options.(gogoproto.goproto_stringer)=false\\n  1096\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n  1097\u2192type APIVersions struct {\\n  1098\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n  1099\u2192\\t// versions are the api versions that are available.\\n  1100\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n  1101\u2192\\tVersions []string `json:\\\"versions\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,rep,name=versions\\\"`\\n  1102\u2192\\t// a map of client CIDR to server address that is serving this group.\\n  1103\u2192\\t// This is to help clients reach servers in the most network-efficient way possible.\\n  1104\u2192\\t// Clients can use the appropriate server address as per the CIDR that they match.\\n  1105\u2192\\t// In case of multiple matches, clients should use the longest matching CIDR.\\n  1106\u2192\\t// The server returns only those CIDRs that it thinks that the client can match.\\n  1107\u2192\\t// For example: the master will return an internal IP CIDR only, if the client reaches the server using an internal IP.\\n  1108\u2192\\t// Server looks at X-Forwarded-For header or X-Real-Ip header or request.RemoteAddr (in that order) to get the client IP.\\n  1109\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n  1110\u2192\\tServerAddressByClientCIDRs []ServerAddressByClientCIDR `json:\\\"serverAddressByClientCIDRs\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,rep,name=serverAddressByClientCIDRs\\\"`\\n  1111\u2192}\\n  1112\u2192\\n  1113\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n  1114\u2192\\n  1115\u2192// APIGroupList is a list of APIGroup, to allow clients to discover the API at\\n  1116\u2192// /apis.\\n  1117\u2192type APIGroupList struct {\\n  1118\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n  1119\u2192\\t// groups is a list of APIGroup.\\n  1120\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n  1121\u2192\\tGroups []APIGroup `json:\\\"groups\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,rep,name=groups\\\"`\\n  1122\u2192}\\n  1123\u2192\\n  1124\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n  1125\u2192\\n  1126\u2192// APIGroup contains the name, the supported versions, and the preferred version\\n  1127\u2192// of a group.\\n  1128\u2192type APIGroup struct {\\n  1129\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n  1130\u2192\\t// name is the name of the group.\\n  1131\u2192\\tName string `json:\\\"name\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=name\\\"`\\n  1132\u2192\\t// versions are the versions supported in this group.\\n  1133\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n  1134\u2192\\tVersions []GroupVersionForDiscovery `json:\\\"versions\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,rep,name=versions\\\"`\\n  1135\u2192\\t// preferredVersion is the version preferred by the API server, which\\n  1136\u2192\\t// probably is the storage version.\\n  1137\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1138\u2192\\tPreferredVersion GroupVersionForDiscovery `json:\\\"preferredVersion,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=preferredVersion\\\"`\\n  1139\u2192\\t// a map of client CIDR to server address that is serving this group.\\n  1140\u2192\\t// This is to help clients reach servers in the most network-efficient way possible.\\n  1141\u2192\\t// Clients can use the appropriate server address as per the CIDR that they match.\\n  1142\u2192\\t// In case of multiple matches, clients should use the longest matching CIDR.\\n  1143\u2192\\t// The server returns only those CIDRs that it thinks that the client can match.\\n  1144\u2192\\t// For example: the master will return an internal IP CIDR only, if the client reaches the server using an internal IP.\\n  1145\u2192\\t// Server looks at X-Forwarded-For header or X-Real-Ip header or request.RemoteAddr (in that order) to get the client IP.\\n  1146\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1147\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n  1148\u2192\\tServerAddressByClientCIDRs []ServerAddressByClientCIDR `json:\\\"serverAddressByClientCIDRs,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,rep,name=serverAddressByClientCIDRs\\\"`\\n  1149\u2192}\\n  1150\u2192\\n  1151\u2192// ServerAddressByClientCIDR helps the client to determine the server address that they should use, depending on the clientCIDR that they match.\\n  1152\u2192type ServerAddressByClientCIDR struct {\\n  1153\u2192\\t// The CIDR with which clients can match their IP to figure out the server address that they should use.\\n  1154\u2192\\tClientCIDR string `json:\\\"clientCIDR\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=clientCIDR\\\"`\\n  1155\u2192\\t// Address of this server, suitable for a client that matches the above CIDR.\\n  1156\u2192\\t// This can be a hostname, hostname:port, IP or IP:port.\\n  1157\u2192\\tServerAddress string `json:\\\"serverAddress\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=serverAddress\\\"`\\n  1158\u2192}\\n  1159\u2192\\n  1160\u2192// GroupVersion contains the \\\"group/version\\\" and \\\"version\\\" string of a version.\\n  1161\u2192// It is made a struct to keep extensibility.\\n  1162\u2192type GroupVersionForDiscovery struct {\\n  1163\u2192\\t// groupVersion specifies the API group and version in the form \\\"group/version\\\"\\n  1164\u2192\\tGroupVersion string `json:\\\"groupVersion\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=groupVersion\\\"`\\n  1165\u2192\\t// version specifies the version in the form of \\\"version\\\". This is to save\\n  1166\u2192\\t// the clients the trouble of splitting the GroupVersion.\\n  1167\u2192\\tVersion string `json:\\\"version\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=version\\\"`\\n  1168\u2192}\\n  1169\u2192\\n  1170\u2192// APIResource specifies the name of a resource and whether it is namespaced.\\n  1171\u2192type APIResource struct {\\n  1172\u2192\\t// name is the plural name of the resource.\\n  1173\u2192\\tName string `json:\\\"name\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=name\\\"`\\n  1174\u2192\\t// singularName is the singular name of the resource.  This allows clients to handle plural and singular opaquely.\\n  1175\u2192\\t// The singularName is more correct for reporting status on a single item and both singular and plural are allowed\\n  1176\u2192\\t// from the kubectl CLI interface.\\n  1177\u2192\\tSingularName string `json:\\\"singularName\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=singularName\\\"`\\n  1178\u2192\\t// namespaced indicates if a resource is namespaced or not.\\n  1179\u2192\\tNamespaced bool `json:\\\"namespaced\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,2,opt,name=namespaced\\\"`\\n  1180\u2192\\t// group is the preferred group of the resource. Only equality comparison on the value is valid.\\n  1200\u2192\\t// This is an alpha feature and may change or be removed in the future.\\n  1201\u2192\\t// The field is populated by the apiserver only if the\\n  1202\u2192\\t// StorageVersionHash feature gate is enabled.\\n  1203\u2192\\t// This field will remain optional even if it graduates.\\n  1204\u2192\\t// +optional\\n +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n  1219\u2192\\n  1220\u2192// APIResourceList is a list of APIResource, it is used to expose the name of the\\n  1221\u2192// resources supported in a specific group and version, and if the resource\\n  1222\u2192// is namespaced.\\n  1223\u2192type APIResourceList struct {\\n  1224\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n {\\n  1247\u2192\\treturn \\\"fieldSelector\\\"\\n  1248\u2192}\\n  1249\u2192\\n  1250\u2192// String returns available api versions as a human-friendly version string.\\n  1251\u2192func string {\\n  1256\u2192\\treturn apiVersions.String()\\n  1257\u2192}\\n  1258\u2192\\n  1259\u2192// Patch is provided to give a concrete name and type to the Kubernetes PATCH request body.\\n  1260\u2192type Patch struct{}\\n  1261\u2192\\n  1262\u2192// Note:\\n  1263\u2192// There are two different styles of label selectors used in versioned types:\\n  1264\u2192// an older style which is represented as just a string in versioned types, and a\\n  1265\u2192// newer style that is structured.  LabelSelector is an internal representation for the\\n  1266\u2192// latter style.\\n  1267\u2192\\n  1268\u2192// A label selector is a label query over a set of resources. The result of matchLabels and\\n  1269\u2192// matchExpressions are ANDed. An empty label selector matches all objects. A null\\n  1270\u2192// label selector matches no objects.\\n  1271\u2192// +structType=atomic\\n  1272\u2192type LabelSelector struct {\\n  1273\u2192\\t// matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels\\n  1274\u2192\\t// map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is \\\"key\\\", the\\n  1275\u2192\\t// operator is \\\"In\\\", and the values array contains only \\\"value\\\". The requirements are ANDed.\\n  1276\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1277\u2192\\tMatchLabels map[string]string `json:\\\"matchLabels,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,rep,name=matchLabels\\\"`\\n  1278\u2192\\t// matchExpressions is a list of label selector requirements. The requirements are ANDed.\\n  1279\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1280\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n  1281\u2192\\tMatchExpressions []LabelSelectorRequirement `json:\\\"matchExpressions,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,rep,name=matchExpressions\\\"`\\n  1282\u2192}\\n  1283\u2192\\n  1284\u2192// A label selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that\\n  1285\u2192// relates the key and values.\\n  1286\u2192type LabelSelectorRequirement struct {\\n  1287\u2192\\t// key is the label key that the selector applies to.\\n  1288\u2192\\tKey string `json:\\\"key\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=key\\\"`\\n  1289\u2192\\t// operator represents a key's relationship to a set of values.\\n  1290\u2192\\t// Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.\\n  1291\u2192\\tOperator LabelSelectorOperator `json:\\\"operator\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=operator,casttype=LabelSelectorOperator\\\"`\\n  1292\u2192\\t// values is an array of string values. If the operator is In or NotIn,\\n  1293\u2192\\t// the values array must be non-empty. If the operator is Exists or DoesNotExist,\\n  1294\u2192\\t// the values array must be empty. This array is replaced during a strategic\\n  1295\u2192\\t// merge patch.\\n  1296\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1297\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n  1298\u2192\\tValues []string `json:\\\"values,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,rep,name=values\\\"`\\n  1299\u2192}\\n  1300\u2192\\n  1301\u2192// A label selector operator is the set of operators that can be used in a selector requirement.\\n  1302\u2192type LabelSelectorOperator string\\n  1303\u2192\\n  1304\u2192const (\\n  1305\u2192\\tLabelSelectorOpIn           LabelSelectorOperator = \\\"In\\\"\\n  1306\u2192\\tLabelSelectorOpNotIn        LabelSelectorOperator = \\\"NotIn\\\"\\n  1307\u2192\\tLabelSelectorOpExists       LabelSelectorOperator = \\\"Exists\\\"\\n  1308\u2192\\tLabelSelectorOpDoesNotExist LabelSelectorOperator = \\\"DoesNotExist\\\"\\n  1309\u2192)\\n  1310\u2192\\n  1311\u2192// FieldSelectorRequirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that\\n  1312\u2192// relates the key and values.\\n  1313\u2192type FieldSelectorRequirement struct {\\n  1314\u2192\\t// key is the field selector key that the requirement applies to.\\n  1315\u2192\\tKey string `json:\\\"key\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=key\\\"`\\n  1316\u2192\\t// operator represents a key's relationship to a set of values.\\n  1317\u2192\\t// Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist.\\n  1318\u2192\\t// The list of operators may grow in the future.\\n  1319\u2192\\tOperator FieldSelectorOperator `json:\\\"operator\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=operator,casttype=FieldSelectorOperator\\\"`\\n  1320\u2192\\t// values is an array of string values.\\n  1321\u2192\\t// If the operator is In or NotIn, the values array must be non-empty.\\n  1322\u2192\\t// If the operator is Exists or DoesNotExist, the values array must be empty.\\n  1323\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1324\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n  1325\u2192\\tValues []string `json:\\\"values,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,rep,name=values\\\"`\\n  1326\u2192}\\n  1327\u2192\\n  1328\u2192// A field selector operator is the set of operators that can be used in a selector requirement.\\n  1329\u2192type FieldSelectorOperator string\\n  1330\u2192\\n  1331\u2192const (\\n  1332\u2192\\tFieldSelectorOpIn           FieldSelectorOperator = \\\"In\\\"\\n  1333\u2192\\tFieldSelectorOpNotIn        FieldSelectorOperator = \\\"NotIn\\\"\\n  1334\u2192\\tFieldSelectorOpExists       FieldSelectorOperator = \\\"Exists\\\"\\n  1335\u2192\\tFieldSelectorOpDoesNotExist FieldSelectorOperator = \\\"DoesNotExist\\\"\\n  1336\u2192)\\n  1337\u2192\\n  1338\u2192// ManagedFieldsEntry is a workflow-id, a FieldSet and the group version of the resource\\n  1339\u2192// that the fieldset applies to.\\n  1340\u2192type ManagedFieldsEntry struct {\\n  1341\u2192\\t// Manager is an identifier of the workflow managing these fields.\\n  1342\u2192\\tManager string `json:\\\"manager,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=manager\\\"`\\n  1343\u2192\\t// Operation is the type of operation which lead to this ManagedFieldsEntry being created.\\n  1344\u2192\\t// The only valid values for this field are 'Apply' and 'Update'.\\n  1345\u2192\\tOperation ManagedFieldsOperationType `json:\\\"operation,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=operation,casttype=ManagedFieldsOperationType\\\"`\\n  1346\u2192\\t// APIVersion defines the version of this resource that this field set\\n  1347\u2192\\t// applies to. The format is \\\"group/version\\\" just like the top-level\\n  1348\u2192\\t// APIVersion field. It is necessary to track the version of a field\\n  1349\u2192\\t// set because it cannot be automatically converted.\\n  1350\u2192\\tAPIVersion string `json:\\\"apiVersion,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=apiVersion\\\"`\\n  1351\u2192\\t// Time is the timestamp of when the ManagedFields entry was added. The\\n  1352\u2192\\t// timestamp will also be updated if a field is added, the manager\\n  1353\u2192\\t// changes any of the owned fields value or removes a field. The\\n  1354\u2192\\t// timestamp does not update when a field is removed from the entry\\n  1355\u2192\\t// because another manager took it over.\\n  1356\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1357\u2192\\tTime *Time `json:\\\"time,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=time\\\"`\\n  1358\u2192\\n  1359\u2192\\t// Fields is tombstoned to show why 5 is a reserved protobuf tag.\\n  1360\u2192\\t//Fields *Fields `json:\\\"fields,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=fields,casttype=Fields\\\"`\\n  1361\u2192\\n  1362\u2192\\t// FieldsType is the discriminator for the different fields format and version.\\n  1363\u2192\\t// There is currently only one possible value: \\\"FieldsV1\\\"\\n  1364\u2192\\tFieldsType string `json:\\\"fieldsType,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=fieldsType\\\"`\\n  1365\u2192\\t// FieldsV1 holds the first JSON version format as described in the \\\"FieldsV1\\\" type.\\n  1366\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1367\u2192\\tFieldsV1 *FieldsV1 `json:\\\"fieldsV1,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,7,opt,name=fieldsV1\\\"`\\n  1368\u2192\\n  1369\u2192\\t// Subresource is the name of the subresource used to update that object, or\\n  1370\u2192\\t// empty string if the object was updated through the main resource. The\\n  1371\u2192\\t// value of this field is used to distinguish between managers, even if they\\n  1372\u2192\\t// share the same name. For example, a status update will be distinct from a\\n  1373\u2192\\t// regular update using the same manager name.\\n  1374\u2192\\t// Note that the APIVersion field is not related to the Subresource field and\\n  1375\u2192\\t// it always corresponds to the version of the main resource.\\n  1376\u2192\\tSubresource string `json:\\\"subresource,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,8,opt,name=subresource\\\"`\\n  1377\u2192}\\n  1378\u2192\\n  1379\u2192// ManagedFieldsOperationType is the type of operation which lead to a ManagedFieldsEntry being created.\\n  1380\u2192type ManagedFieldsOperationType string\\n  1381\u2192\\n  1382\u2192const (\\n  1383\u2192\\tManagedFieldsOperationApply  ManagedFieldsOperationType = \\\"Apply\\\"\\n  1384\u2192\\tManagedFieldsOperationUpdate ManagedFieldsOperationType = \\\"Update\\\"\\n  1385\u2192)\\n  1386\u2192\\n  1387\u2192// FieldsV1 stores a set of fields in a data structure like a Trie, in JSON format.\\n  1388\u2192//\\n  1389\u2192// Each key is either a '.' representing the field itself, and will always map to an empty set,\\n  1390\u2192// or a string representing a sub-field or item. The string will follow one of these four formats:\\n  1391\u2192// 'f:<name>', where <name> is the name of a field in a struct, or key in a map\\n  1392\u2192// 'v:<value>', where <value> is the exact json formatted value of a list item\\n  1393\u2192// 'i:<index>', where <index> is position of a item in a list\\n  1394\u2192// 'k:<keys>', where <keys> is a map of  a list item's key fields to their unique values\\n  1395\u2192// If a key maps to an empty Fields value, the field that key represents is part of the set.\\n  1396\u2192//\\n  1397\u2192// The exact format is defined in sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff\\n  1398\u2192// +protobuf.options.(gogoproto.goproto_stringer)=false\\n  1399\u2192type FieldsV1 struct {\\n  1400\u2192\\t// Raw is the underlying serialization of this object.\\n  1401\u2192\\tRaw []byte `json:\\\"-\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=Raw\\\"`\\n  1402\u2192}\\n  1403\u2192\\n  1404\u2192func (f FieldsV1) String() string {\\n  1405\u2192\\treturn string(f.Raw)\\n  1406\u2192}\\n  1407\u2192\\n  1408\u2192// TODO: Table does not generate to protobuf because of the interface{} - fix protobuf\\n  1409\u2192//   generation to support a meta type that can accept any valid JSON. This can be introduced\\n  1410\u2192//   in a v1 because clients a) receive an error if they try to access proto today, and b)\\n  1411\u2192//   once introduced they would be able to gracefully switch over to using it.\\n  1412\u2192\\n  1413\u2192// Table is a tabular representation of a set of API resources. The server transforms the\\n  1414\u2192// object into a set of preferred columns for quickly reviewing the objects.\\n  1415\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n  1416\u2192// +protobuf=false\\n  1417\u2192type Table struct {\\n  1418\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n  1419\u2192\\t// Standard list metadata.\\n  1420\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds\\n  1421\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1422\u2192\\tListMeta `json:\\\"metadata,omitempty\\\"`\\n  1423\u2192\\n  1424\u2192\\t// columnDefinitions describes each column in the returned items array. The number of cells per row\\n  1425\u2192\\t// will always match the number of column definitions.\\n  1426\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n  1427\u2192\\tColumnDefinitions []TableColumnDefinition `json:\\\"columnDefinitions\\\"`\\n  1428\u2192\\t// rows is the list of items in the table.\\n  1429\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n  1430\u2192\\tRows []TableRow `json:\\\"rows\\\"`\\n  1431\u2192}\\n  1432\u2192\\n  1433\u2192// TableColumnDefinition contains information about a column returned in the Table.\\n  1434\u2192// +protobuf=false\\n  1435\u2192type TableColumnDefinition struct {\\n  1436\u2192\\t// name is a human readable name for the column.\\n  1437\u2192\\tName string `json:\\\"name\\\"`\\n  1438\u2192\\t// type is an OpenAPI type definition for this column, such as number, integer, string, or\\n  1439\u2192\\t// array.\\n  1440\u2192\\t// See https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/2.0.md#data-types for more.\\n  1441\u2192\\tType string `json:\\\"type\\\"`\\n  1442\u2192\\t// format is an optional OpenAPI type modifier for this column. A format modifies the type and\\n  1443\u2192\\t// imposes additional rules, like date or time formatting for a string. The 'name' format is applied\\n  1444\u2192\\t// to the primary identifier column which has type 'string' to assist in clients identifying column\\n  1445\u2192\\t// is the resource name.\\n  1446\u2192\\t// See https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/2.0.md#data-types for more.\\n  1447\u2192\\tFormat string `json:\\\"format\\\"`\\n  1448\u2192\\t// description is a human readable description of this column.\\n  1449\u2192\\tDescription string `json:\\\"description\\\"`\\n  1450\u2192\\t// priority is an integer defining the relative importance of this column compared to others. Lower\\n  1451\u2192\\t// numbers are considered higher priority. Columns that may be omitted in limited space scenarios\\n  1452\u2192\\t// should be given a higher priority.\\n  1453\u2192\\tPriority int32 `json:\\\"priority\\\"`\\n  1454\u2192}\\n  1455\u2192\\n  1456\u2192// TableRow is an individual row in a table.\\n  1457\u2192// +protobuf=false\\n  1458\u2192type TableRow struct {\\n  1459\u2192\\t// cells will be as wide as the column definitions array and may contain strings, numbers (float64 or\\n  1460\u2192\\t// int64), booleans, simple maps, lists, or null. See the type field of the column definition for a\\n  1461\u2192\\t// more detailed description.\\n  1462\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n  1463\u2192\\tCells []interface{} `json:\\\"cells\\\"`\\n  1464\u2192\\t// conditions describe additional status of a row that are relevant for a human user. These conditions\\n  1465\u2192\\t// apply to the row, not to the object, and will be specific to table output. The only defined\\n  1466\u2192\\t// condition type is 'Completed', for a row that indicates a resource that has run to completion and\\n  1467\u2192\\t// can be given less visual priority.\\n  1468\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1469\u2192\\t// +listType=atomic\\n  1470\u2192\\tConditions []TableRowCondition `json:\\\"conditions,omitempty\\\"`\\n  1471\u2192\\t// This field contains the requested additional information about each object based on the includeObject\\n  1472\u2192\\t// policy when requesting the Table. If \\\"None\\\", this field is empty, if \\\"Object\\\" this will be the\\n  1473\u2192\\t// default serialization of the object for the current API version, and if \\\"Metadata\\\" (the default) will\\n  1474\u2192\\t// contain the object metadata. Check the returned kind and apiVersion of the object before parsing.\\n  1475\u2192\\t// The media type of the object will always match the enclosing list - if this as a JSON table, these\\n  1476\u2192\\t// will be JSON encoded objects.\\n  1477\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1478\u2192\\tObject runtime.RawExtension `json:\\\"object,omitempty\\\"`\\n  1479\u2192}\\n  1480\u2192\\n  1481\u2192// TableRowCondition allows a row to be marked with additional information.\\n  1482\u2192// +protobuf=false\\n  1483\u2192type TableRowCondition struct {\\n  1484\u2192\\t// Type of row condition. The only defined value is 'Completed' indicating that the\\n  1485\u2192\\t// object this row represents has reached a completed state and may be given less visual\\n  1486\u2192\\t// priority than other rows. Clients are not required to honor any conditions but should\\n  1487\u2192\\t// be consistent where possible about handling the conditions.\\n  1488\u2192\\tType RowConditionType `json:\\\"type\\\"`\\n  1489\u2192\\t// Status of the condition, one of True, False, Unknown.\\n  1490\u2192\\tStatus ConditionStatus `json:\\\"status\\\"`\\n  1491\u2192\\t// (brief) machine readable reason for the condition's last transition.\\n  1492\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1493\u2192\\tReason string `json:\\\"reason,omitempty\\\"`\\n  1494\u2192\\t// Human readable message indicating details about last transition.\\n  1495\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1496\u2192\\tMessage string `json:\\\"message,omitempty\\\"`\\n  1497\u2192}\\n  1498\u2192\\n  1499\u2192type RowConditionType string\\n  1500\u2192\\n  1501\u2192// These are valid conditions of a row. This list is not exhaustive and new conditions may be\\n  1502\u2192// included by other resources.\\n  1503\u2192const (\\n  1504\u2192\\t// RowCompleted means the underlying resource has reached completion and may be given less\\n  1505\u2192\\t// visual priority than other resources.\\n  1506\u2192\\tRowCompleted RowConditionType = \\\"Completed\\\"\\n  1507\u2192)\\n  1508\u2192\\n  1509\u2192type ConditionStatus string\\n  1510\u2192\\n  1511\u2192// These are valid condition statuses. \\\"ConditionTrue\\\" means a resource is in the condition.\\n  1512\u2192// \\\"ConditionFalse\\\" means a resource is not in the condition. \\\"ConditionUnknown\\\" means kubernetes\\n  1513\u2192// can't decide if a resource is in the condition or not. In the future, we could add other\\n  1514\u2192// intermediate conditions, e.g. ConditionDegraded.\\n  1515\u2192const (\\n  1516\u2192\\tConditionTrue    ConditionStatus = \\\"True\\\"\\n  1517\u2192\\tConditionFalse   ConditionStatus = \\\"False\\\"\\n  1518\u2192\\tConditionUnknown ConditionStatus = \\\"Unknown\\\"\\n  1519\u2192)\\n  1520\u2192\\n  1521\u2192// IncludeObjectPolicy controls which portion of the object is returned with a Table.\\n  1522\u2192type IncludeObjectPolicy string\\n  1523\u2192\\n  1524\u2192const (\\n  1525\u2192\\t// IncludeNone returns no object.\\n  1526\u2192\\tIncludeNone IncludeObjectPolicy = \\\"None\\\"\\n  1527\u2192\\t// IncludeMetadata serializes the object containing only its metadata field.\\n  1528\u2192\\tIncludeMetadata IncludeObjectPolicy = \\\"Metadata\\\"\\n  1529\u2192\\t// IncludeObject contains the full object.\\n  1530\u2192\\tIncludeObject IncludeObjectPolicy = \\\"Object\\\"\\n  1531\u2192)\\n  1532\u2192\\n  1533\u2192// TableOptions are used when a Table is requested by the caller.\\n  1534\u2192// +k8s:conversion-gen:explicit-from=net/url.Values\\n  1535\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n  1536\u2192type TableOptions struct {\\n  1537\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n  1538\u2192\\n  1539\u2192\\t// NoHeaders is only exposed for internal callers. It is not included in our OpenAPI definitions\\n  1540\u2192\\t// and may be removed as a field in a future release.\\n  1541\u2192\\tNoHeaders bool `json:\\\"-\\\"`\\n  1542\u2192\\n  1543\u2192\\t// includeObject decides whether to include each object along with its columnar information.\\n  1544\u2192\\t// Specifying \\\"None\\\" will return no object, specifying \\\"Object\\\" will return the full object contents, and\\n  1545\u2192\\t// specifying \\\"Metadata\\\" (the default) will return the object's metadata in the PartialObjectMetadata kind\\n  1546\u2192\\t// in version v1beta1 of the meta.k8s.io API group.\\n  1547\u2192\\tIncludeObject IncludeObjectPolicy `json:\\\"includeObject,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=includeObject,casttype=IncludeObjectPolicy\\\"`\\n  1548\u2192}\\n  1549\u2192\\n  1550\u2192// PartialObjectMetadata is a generic representation of any object with ObjectMeta. It allows clients\\n  1551\u2192// to get access to a particular ObjectMeta schema without knowing the details of the version.\\n  1552\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n  1553\u2192type PartialObjectMetadata struct {\\n  1554\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n  1555\u2192\\t// Standard object's metadata.\\n  1556\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\\n  1557\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1558\u2192\\tObjectMeta `json:\\\"metadata,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\\\"`\\n  1559\u2192}\\n  1560\u2192\\n  1561\u2192// PartialObjectMetadataList contains a list of objects containing only their metadata\\n  1562\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n  1563\u2192type PartialObjectMetadataList struct {\\n  1564\u2192\\tTypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n  1565\u2192\\t// Standard list metadata.\\n  1566\u2192\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds\\n  1567\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1568\u2192\\tListMeta `json:\\\"metadata,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\\\"`\\n  1569\u2192\\n  1570\u2192\\t// items contains each of the included items.\\n  1571\u2192\\tItems []PartialObjectMetadata `json:\\\"items\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\\\"`\\n  1572\u2192}\\n  1573\u2192\\n  1574\u2192// Condition contains details for one aspect of the current state of this API Resource.\\n  1575\u2192// ---\\n  1576\u2192// This struct is intended for direct use as an array at the field path .status.conditions.  For example,\\n  1577\u2192//\\n  1578\u2192//\\ttype FooStatus struct{\\n  1579\u2192//\\t    // Represents the observations of a foo's current state.\\n  1580\u2192//\\t    // Known .status.conditions.type are: \\\"Available\\\", \\\"Progressing\\\", and \\\"Degraded\\\"\\n  1581\u2192//\\t    // +patchMergeKey=type\\n  1582\u2192//\\t    // +patchStrategy=merge\\n  1583\u2192//\\t    // +listType=map\\n  1584\u2192//\\t    // +listMapKey=type\\n  1585\u2192//\\t    Conditions []metav1.Condition `json:\\\"conditions,omitempty\\\" patchStrategy:\\\"merge\\\" patchMergeKey:\\\"type\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,rep,name=conditions\\\"`\\n  1586\u2192//\\n  1587\u2192//\\t    // other fields\\n  1588\u2192//\\t}\\n  1589\u2192type Condition struct {\\n  1590\u2192\\t// type of condition in CamelCase or in foo.example.com/CamelCase.\\n  1591\u2192\\t// ---\\n  1592\u2192\\t// Many .condition.type values are consistent across resources like Available, but because arbitrary conditions can be\\n  1593\u2192\\t// useful (see .node.status.conditions), the ability to deconflict is important.\\n  1594\u2192\\t// The regex it matches is (dns1123SubdomainFmt/)?(qualifiedNameFmt)\\n  1595\u2192\\t// +required\\n  1596\u2192\\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\\n  1597\u2192\\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Pattern=`^([a-z0-9]([-a-z0-9]*[a-z0-9])?(\\\\.[a-z0-9]([-a-z0-9]*[a-z0-9])?)*/)?(([A-Za-z0-9][-A-Za-z0-9_.]*)?[A-Za-z0-9])$`\\n  1598\u2192\\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxLength=316\\n  1599\u2192\\tType string `json:\\\"type\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=type\\\"`\\n  1600\u2192\\t// status of the condition, one of True, False, Unknown.\\n  1601\u2192\\t// +required\\n  1602\u2192\\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\\n  1603\u2192\\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=True;False;Unknown\\n  1604\u2192\\tStatus ConditionStatus `json:\\\"status\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=status\\\"`\\n  1605\u2192\\t// observedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon.\\n  1606\u2192\\t// For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions[x].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date\\n  1607\u2192\\t// with respect to the current state of the instance.\\n  1608\u2192\\t// +optional\\n  1609\u2192\\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=0\\n  1610\u2192\\tObservedGeneration int64 `json:\\\"observedGeneration,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,3,opt,name=observedGeneration\\\"`\\n  1611\u2192\\t// lastTransitionTime is the last time the condition transitioned from one status to another.\\n  1612\u2192\\t// This should be when the underlying condition changed.  If that is not known, then using the time when the API field changed is acceptable.\\n  1613\u2192\\t// +required\\n  1614\u2192\\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\\n  1615\u2192\\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Type=string\\n  1616\u2192\\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Format=date-time\\n  1617\u2192\\tLastTransitionTime Time `json:\\\"lastTransitionTime\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=lastTransitionTime\\\"`\\n  1618\u2192\\t// reason contains a programmatic identifier indicating the reason for the condition's last transition.\\n  1619\u2192\\t// Producers of specific condition types may define expected values and meanings for this field,\\n  1620\u2192\\t// and whether the values are considered a guaranteed API.\\n  1621\u2192\\t// The value should be a CamelCase string.\\n  1622\u2192\\t// This field may not be empty.\\n  1623\u2192\\t// +required\\n  1624\u2192\\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\\n  1625\u2192\\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxLength=1024\\n  1626\u2192\\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinLength=1\\n  1627\u2192\\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Pattern=`^[A-Za-z]([A-Za-z0-9_,:]*[A-Za-z0-9_])?$`\\n  1628\u2192\\tReason string `json:\\\"reason\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=reason\\\"`\\n  1629\u2192\\t// message is a human readable message indicating details about the transition.\\n  1630\u2192\\t// This may be an empty string.\\n  1631\u2192\\t// +required\\n  1632\u2192\\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\\n  1633\u2192\\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxLength=32768\\n  1634\u2192\\tMessage string `json:\\\"message\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=message\\\"`\\n  1635\u2192}\\n  1636\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"099452a8-5efd-475a-96b1-ecc1ffe87ce6\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:49.925Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"4d5f0a6e-a18f-458b-88a1-f25a97d19f23\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"099452a8-5efd-475a-96b1-ecc1ffe87ce6\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01NeHGU46ccGfN3tKvTguMTa\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01YAyf18g4x5TTL25GjSinXB\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":7494,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":10906,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":7494},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG56VuJYufMxrcQaD6Zw\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"9c1ba6e3-3794-4c9b-949c-911de5e35b4a\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:49.997Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"9c1ba6e3-3794-4c9b-949c-911de5e35b4a\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01YAyf18g4x5TTL25GjSinXB\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01YAyf18g4x5TTL25GjSinXB\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:50.173Z\",\"uuid\":\"33fd903b-892b-4226-b5dc-8728ab072e8a\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"9c1ba6e3-3794-4c9b-949c-911de5e35b4a\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01YAyf18g4x5TTL25GjSinXB\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package handlers\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\tjsonpatch \\\"gopkg.in/evanphx/json-patch.v4\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\tkjson \\\"sigs.k8s.io/json\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\tmetainternalversionscheme \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion/scheme\\\"\\n    33\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured\\\"\\n    35\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/validation\\\"\\n    36\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    37\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n    38\u2192\\tcbor \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer/cbor/direct\\\"\\n    39\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\\\"\\n    40\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\\\"\\n    41\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/mergepatch\\\"\\n    42\u2192\\tutilruntime \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\\\"\\n    43\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n    44\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/strategicpatch\\\"\\n    45\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation/field\\\"\\n    46\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/yaml\\\"\\n    47\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\\\"\\n    48\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\\\"\\n    49\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\\\"\\n    50\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/fieldmanager\\\"\\n    51\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/finisher\\\"\\n    52\u2192\\trequestmetrics \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/metrics\\\"\\n    53\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/negotiation\\\"\\n    54\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\\\"\\n    55\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\\\"\\n    56\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\\\"\\n    57\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/dryrun\\\"\\n    58\u2192\\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n    59\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\\\"\\n    60\u2192)\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192const (\\n    63\u2192\\t// maximum number of operations a single json patch may contain.\\n    64\u2192\\tmaxJSONPatchOperations = 10000\\n    65\u2192)\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192// PatchResource returns a function that will handle a resource patch.\\n    68\u2192func PatchResource(r rest.Patcher, scope *RequestScope, admit admission.Interface, patchTypes []string) http.HandlerFunc {\\n    69\u2192\\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n    70\u2192\\t\\tctx := req.Context()\\n    71\u2192\\t\\t// For performance tracking purposes.\\n    72\u2192\\t\\tctx, span := tracing.Start(ctx, \\\"Patch\\\", traceFields(req)...)\\n    73\u2192\\t\\treq = req.WithContext(ctx)\\n    74\u2192\\t\\tdefer span.End(500 * time.Millisecond)\\n    75\u2192\\n    76\u2192\\t\\t// Do this first, otherwise name extraction can fail for unrecognized content types\\n    77\u2192\\t\\t// TODO: handle this in negotiation\\n    78\u2192\\t\\tcontentType := req.Header.Get(\\\"Content-Type\\\")\\n    79\u2192\\t\\t// Remove \\\"; charset=\\\" if included in header.\\n    80\u2192\\t\\tif idx := strings.Index(contentType, \\\";\\\"); idx > 0 {\\n    81\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontentType = contentType[:idx]\\n    82\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    83\u2192\\t\\tpatchType := types.PatchType(contentType)\\n    84\u2192\\n    85\u2192\\t\\t// Ensure the patchType is one we support\\n    86\u2192\\t\\tif !sets.NewString(patchTypes...).Has(contentType) {\\n    87\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.err(negotiation.NewUnsupportedMediaTypeError(patchTypes), w, req)\\n    88\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n    89\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    90\u2192\\n    91\u2192\\t\\tnamespace, name, err := scope.Namer.Name(req)\\n    92\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n    93\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n    94\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n    95\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    96\u2192\\n    97\u2192\\t\\t// enforce a timeout of at most requestTimeoutUpperBound (34s) or less if the user-provided\\n    98\u2192\\t\\t// timeout inside the parent context is lower than requestTimeoutUpperBound.\\n    99\u2192\\t\\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, requestTimeoutUpperBound)\\n   100\u2192\\t\\tdefer cancel()\\n   101\u2192\\n   102\u2192\\t\\tctx = request.WithNamespace(ctx, namespace)\\n   103\u2192\\n   104\u2192\\t\\toutputMediaType, _, err := negotiation.NegotiateOutputMediaType(req, scope.Serializer, scope)\\n   105\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   106\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n   107\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   108\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   109\u2192\\n   110\u2192\\t\\tpatchBytes, err := limitedReadBodyWithRecordMetric(ctx, req, scope.MaxRequestBodyBytes, scope.Resource.GroupResource(), requestmetrics.Patch)\\n   111\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   112\u2192\\t\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"limitedReadBody failed\\\", attribute.Int(\\\"len\\\", len(patchBytes)), attribute.String(\\\"err\\\", err.Error()))\\n   113\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n   114\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   115\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   116\u2192\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"limitedReadBody succeeded\\\", attribute.Int(\\\"len\\\", len(patchBytes)))\\n   117\u2192\\n   118\u2192\\t\\toptions := &metav1.PatchOptions{}\\n   119\u2192\\t\\tif err := metainternalversionscheme.ParameterCodec.DecodeParameters(req.URL.Query(), scope.MetaGroupVersion, options); err != nil {\\n   120\u2192\\t\\t\\terr = errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\\n   121\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n   122\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   123\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   124\u2192\\t\\tif errs := validation.ValidatePatchOptions(options, patchType); len(errs) > 0 {\\n   125\u2192\\t\\t\\terr := errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{Group: metav1.GroupName, Kind: \\\"PatchOptions\\\"}, \\\"\\\", errs)\\n   126\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n   127\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   128\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   129\u2192\\t\\toptions.TypeMeta.SetGroupVersionKind(metav1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind(\\\"PatchOptions\\\"))\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192\\t\\tadmit = admission.WithAudit(admit)\\n   132\u2192\\n   133\u2192\\t\\taudit.LogRequestPatch(req.Context(), patchBytes)\\n   134\u2192\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"Recorded the audit event\\\")\\n   135\u2192\\n   136\u2192\\t\\tvar baseContentType string\\n   137\u2192\\t\\tswitch patchType {\\n   138\u2192\\t\\tcase types.ApplyYAMLPatchType:\\n   139\u2192\\t\\t\\tbaseContentType = runtime.ContentTypeYAML\\n   140\u2192\\t\\tcase types.ApplyCBORPatchType:\\n   141\u2192\\t\\t\\tif !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.CBORServingAndStorage) {\\n   142\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// This request should have already been rejected by the\\n   143\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// Content-Type allowlist check. Return 500 because assumptions are\\n   144\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// already broken and the feature is not GA.\\n   145\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tutilruntime.HandleErrorWithContext(req.Context(), nil, \\\"The patch content-type allowlist check should have made this unreachable.\\\")\\n   146\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tscope.err(errors.NewInternalError(errors.NewInternalError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"unexpected patch type: %v\\\", patchType))), w, req)\\n   147\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   148\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   149\u2192\\n   150\u2192\\t\\t\\tbaseContentType = runtime.ContentTypeCBOR\\n   151\u2192\\t\\tdefault:\\n   152\u2192\\t\\t\\tbaseContentType = runtime.ContentTypeJSON\\n   153\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   154\u2192\\n   155\u2192\\t\\ts, ok := runtime.SerializerInfoForMediaType(scope.Serializer.SupportedMediaTypes(), baseContentType)\\n   156\u2192\\t\\tif !ok {\\n   157\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.err(fmt.Errorf(\\\"no serializer defined for %v\\\", baseContentType), w, req)\\n   158\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   159\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   160\u2192\\t\\tgv := scope.Kind.GroupVersion()\\n   161\u2192\\n   162\u2192\\t\\tvalidationDirective := fieldValidation(options.FieldValidation)\\n   163\u2192\\t\\tdecodeSerializer := s.Serializer\\n   164\u2192\\t\\tif validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn || validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationStrict {\\n   165\u2192\\t\\t\\tdecodeSerializer = s.StrictSerializer\\n   166\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   167\u2192\\n   168\u2192\\t\\tcodec := runtime.NewCodec(\\n   169\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.Serializer.EncoderForVersion(s.Serializer, gv),\\n   170\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.Serializer.DecoderToVersion(decodeSerializer, scope.HubGroupVersion),\\n   171\u2192\\t\\t)\\n   172\u2192\\n   173\u2192\\t\\tuserInfo, _ := request.UserFrom(ctx)\\n   174\u2192\\t\\tstaticCreateAttributes := admission.NewAttributesRecord(\\n   175\u2192\\t\\t\\tnil,\\n   176\u2192\\t\\t\\tnil,\\n   177\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.Kind,\\n   178\u2192\\t\\t\\tnamespace,\\n   179\u2192\\t\\t\\tname,\\n   180\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.Resource,\\n   181\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.Subresource,\\n   182\u2192\\t\\t\\tadmission.Create,\\n   183\u2192\\t\\t\\tpatchToCreateOptions(options),\\n   184\u2192\\t\\t\\tdryrun.IsDryRun(options.DryRun),\\n   185\u2192\\t\\t\\tuserInfo)\\n   186\u2192\\t\\tstaticUpdateAttributes := admission.NewAttributesRecord(\\n   187\u2192\\t\\t\\tnil,\\n   188\u2192\\t\\t\\tnil,\\n   189\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.Kind,\\n   190\u2192\\t\\t\\tnamespace,\\n   191\u2192\\t\\t\\tname,\\n   192\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.Resource,\\n   193\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.Subresource,\\n   194\u2192\\t\\t\\tadmission.Update,\\n   195\u2192\\t\\t\\tpatchToUpdateOptions(options),\\n   196\u2192\\t\\t\\tdryrun.IsDryRun(options.DryRun),\\n   197\u2192\\t\\t\\tuserInfo,\\n   198\u2192\\t\\t)\\n   199\u2192\\n   200\u2192\\t\\tadmit = fieldmanager.NewManagedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController(admit)\\n   201\u2192\\n   202\u2192\\t\\tmutatingAdmission, _ := admit.(admission.MutationInterface)\\n   203\u2192\\t\\tcreateAuthorizerAttributes := authorizer.AttributesRecord{\\n   204\u2192\\t\\t\\tUser:            userInfo,\\n   205\u2192\\t\\t\\tResourceRequest: true,\\n   206\u2192\\t\\t\\tPath:            req.URL.Path,\\n   207\u2192\\t\\t\\tVerb:            \\\"create\\\",\\n   208\u2192\\t\\t\\tAPIGroup:        scope.Resource.Group,\\n   209\u2192\\t\\t\\tAPIVersion:      scope.Resource.Version,\\n   210\u2192\\t\\t\\tResource:        scope.Resource.Resource,\\n   211\u2192\\t\\t\\tSubresource:     scope.Subresource,\\n   212\u2192\\t\\t\\tNamespace:       namespace,\\n   213\u2192\\t\\t\\tName:            name,\\n   214\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   215\u2192\\n   216\u2192\\t\\tp := patcher{\\n   217\u2192\\t\\t\\tnamer:               scope.Namer,\\n   218\u2192\\t\\t\\tcreater:             scope.Creater,\\n   219\u2192\\t\\t\\tdefaulter:           scope.Defaulter,\\n   220\u2192\\t\\t\\ttyper:               scope.Typer,\\n   221\u2192\\t\\t\\tunsafeConvertor:     scope.UnsafeConvertor,\\n   222\u2192\\t\\t\\tkind:                scope.Kind,\\n   223\u2192\\t\\t\\tresource:            scope.Resource,\\n   224\u2192\\t\\t\\tsubresource:         scope.Subresource,\\n   225\u2192\\t\\t\\tdryRun:              dryrun.IsDryRun(options.DryRun),\\n   226\u2192\\t\\t\\tvalidationDirective: validationDirective,\\n   227\u2192\\n   228\u2192\\t\\t\\tobjectInterfaces: scope,\\n   229\u2192\\n   230\u2192\\t\\t\\thubGroupVersion: scope.HubGroupVersion,\\n   231\u2192\\n   232\u2192\\t\\t\\tcreateValidation: withAuthorization(rest.AdmissionToValidateObjectFunc(admit, staticCreateAttributes, scope), scope.Authorizer, createAuthorizerAttributes),\\n   233\u2192\\t\\t\\tupdateValidation: rest.AdmissionToValidateObjectUpdateFunc(admit, staticUpdateAttributes, scope),\\n   234\u2192\\t\\t\\tadmissionCheck:   mutatingAdmission,\\n   235\u2192\\n   236\u2192\\t\\t\\tcodec: codec,\\n   237\u2192\\n   238\u2192\\t\\t\\toptions: options,\\n   239\u2192\\n   240\u2192\\t\\t\\trestPatcher: r,\\n   241\u2192\\t\\t\\tname:        name,\\n   242\u2192\\t\\t\\tpatchType:   patchType,\\n   243\u2192\\t\\t\\tpatchBytes:  patchBytes,\\n   244\u2192\\t\\t\\tuserAgent:   req.UserAgent(),\\n   245\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   246\u2192\\n   247\u2192\\t\\tresult, wasCreated, err := p.patchResource(ctx, scope)\\n   248\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   249\u2192\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n   250\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   251\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   252\u2192\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"Object stored in database\\\")\\n   253\u2192\\n   254\u2192\\t\\tstatus := http.StatusOK\\n   255\u2192\\t\\tif wasCreated {\\n   256\u2192\\t\\t\\tstatus = http.StatusCreated\\n   257\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   258\u2192\\n   259\u2192\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"About to write a response\\\")\\n   260\u2192\\t\\tdefer span.AddEvent(\\\"Writing http response done\\\")\\n   261\u2192\\t\\ttransformResponseObject(ctx, scope, req, w, status, outputMediaType, result)\\n   262\u2192\\t}\\n   263\u2192}\\n   264\u2192\\n   265\u2192type mutateObjectUpdateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object) error\\n   266\u2192\\n   267\u2192// patcher breaks the process of patch application and retries into smaller\\n   268\u2192// pieces of functionality.\\n   269\u2192// TODO: Use builder pattern to construct this object?\\n   270\u2192// TODO: As part of that effort, some aspects of PatchResource above could be\\n   271\u2192// moved into this type.\\n   272\u2192type patcher struct {\\n   273\u2192\\t// Pieces of RequestScope\\n   274\u2192\\tnamer               ScopeNamer\\n   275\u2192\\tcreater             runtime.ObjectCreater\\n   276\u2192\\tdefaulter           runtime.ObjectDefaulter\\n   277\u2192\\ttyper               runtime.ObjectTyper\\n   278\u2192\\tunsafeConvertor     runtime.ObjectConvertor\\n   279\u2192\\tresource            schema.GroupVersionResource\\n   280\u2192\\tkind                schema.GroupVersionKind\\n   281\u2192\\tsubresource         string\\n   282\u2192\\tdryRun              bool\\n   283\u2192\\tvalidationDirective string\\n   284\u2192\\n   285\u2192\\tobjectInterfaces admission.ObjectInterfaces\\n   286\u2192\\n   287\u2192\\thubGroupVersion schema.GroupVersion\\n   288\u2192\\n   289\u2192\\t// Validation functions\\n   290\u2192\\tcreateValidation rest.ValidateObjectFunc\\n   291\u2192\\tupdateValidation rest.ValidateObjectUpdateFunc\\n   292\u2192\\tadmissionCheck   admission.MutationInterface\\n   293\u2192\\n   294\u2192\\tcodec runtime.Codec\\n   295\u2192\\n   296\u2192\\toptions *metav1.PatchOptions\\n   297\u2192\\n   298\u2192\\t// Operation information\\n   299\u2192\\trestPatcher rest.Patcher\\n   300\u2192\\tname        string\\n   301\u2192\\tpatchType   types.PatchType\\n   302\u2192\\tpatchBytes  []byte\\n   303\u2192\\tuserAgent   string\\n   304\u2192\\n   305\u2192\\t// Set at invocation-time (by applyPatch) and immutable thereafter\\n   306\u2192\\tnamespace         string\\n   307\u2192\\tupdatedObjectInfo rest.UpdatedObjectInfo\\n   308\u2192\\tmechanism         patchMechanism\\n   309\u2192\\tforceAllowCreate  bool\\n   310\u2192}\\n   311\u2192\\n   312\u2192type patchMechanism interface {\\n   313\u2192\\tapplyPatchToCurrentObject(requextContext context.Context, currentObject runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error)\\n   314\u2192\\tcreateNewObject(requestContext context.Context) (runtime.Object, error)\\n   315\u2192}\\n   316\u2192\\n   317\u2192type jsonPatcher struct {\\n   318\u2192\\t*patcher\\n   319\u2192\\n   320\u2192\\tfieldManager *managedfields.FieldManager\\n   321\u2192}\\n   322\u2192\\n   323\u2192func (p *jsonPatcher) applyPatchToCurrentObject(requestContext context.Context, currentObject runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   324\u2192\\t// Encode will convert & return a versioned object in JSON.\\n   325\u2192\\tcurrentObjJS, err := runtime.Encode(p.codec, currentObject)\\n   326\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   327\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   328\u2192\\t}\\n   329\u2192\\n   330\u2192\\t// Apply the patch.\\n   331\u2192\\tpatchedObjJS, appliedStrictErrs, err := p.applyJSPatch(currentObjJS)\\n   332\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   333\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   334\u2192\\t}\\n   335\u2192\\n   336\u2192\\t// Construct the resulting typed, unversioned object.\\n   337\u2192\\tobjToUpdate := p.restPatcher.New()\\n   338\u2192\\tif err := runtime.DecodeInto(p.codec, patchedObjJS, objToUpdate); err != nil {\\n   339\u2192\\t\\tstrictError, isStrictError := runtime.AsStrictDecodingError(err)\\n   340\u2192\\t\\tswitch {\\n   341\u2192\\t\\tcase !isStrictError:\\n   342\u2192\\t\\t\\t// disregard any appliedStrictErrs, because it's an incomplete\\n   343\u2192\\t\\t\\t// list of strict errors given that we don't know what fields were\\n   344\u2192\\t\\t\\t// unknown because DecodeInto failed. Non-strict errors trump in this case.\\n   345\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{}, \\\"\\\", field.ErrorList{\\n   346\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfield.Invalid(field.NewPath(\\\"patch\\\"), string(patchedObjJS), err.Error()),\\n   347\u2192\\t\\t\\t})\\n   348\u2192\\t\\tcase p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn:\\n   349\u2192\\t\\t\\taddStrictDecodingWarnings(requestContext, append(appliedStrictErrs, strictError.Errors()...))\\n   350\u2192\\t\\tdefault:\\n   351\u2192\\t\\t\\tstrictDecodingError := runtime.NewStrictDecodingError(append(appliedStrictErrs, strictError.Errors()...))\\n   352\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{}, \\\"\\\", field.ErrorList{\\n   353\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfield.Invalid(field.NewPath(\\\"patch\\\"), string(patchedObjJS), strictDecodingError.Error()),\\n   354\u2192\\t\\t\\t})\\n   355\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   356\u2192\\t} else if len(appliedStrictErrs) > 0 {\\n   357\u2192\\t\\tswitch {\\n   358\u2192\\t\\tcase p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn:\\n   359\u2192\\t\\t\\taddStrictDecodingWarnings(requestContext, appliedStrictErrs)\\n   360\u2192\\t\\tdefault:\\n   361\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{}, \\\"\\\", field.ErrorList{\\n   362\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfield.Invalid(field.NewPath(\\\"patch\\\"), string(patchedObjJS), runtime.NewStrictDecodingError(appliedStrictErrs).Error()),\\n   363\u2192\\t\\t\\t})\\n   364\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   365\u2192\\t}\\n   366\u2192\\n   367\u2192\\tif p.options == nil {\\n   368\u2192\\t\\t// Provide a more informative error for the crash that would\\n   369\u2192\\t\\t// happen on the next line\\n   370\u2192\\t\\tpanic(\\\"PatchOptions required but not provided\\\")\\n   371\u2192\\t}\\n   372\u2192\\tobjToUpdate = p.fieldManager.UpdateNoErrors(currentObject, objToUpdate, managerOrUserAgent(p.options.FieldManager, p.userAgent))\\n   373\u2192\\treturn objToUpdate, nil\\n   374\u2192}\\n   375\u2192\\n   376\u2192func (p *jsonPatcher) createNewObject(_ context.Context) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   377\u2192\\treturn nil, errors.NewNotFound(p.resource.GroupResource(), p.name)\\n   378\u2192}\\n   379\u2192\\n   380\u2192type jsonPatchOp struct {\\n   381\u2192\\tOp    string      `json:\\\"op\\\"`\\n   382\u2192\\tPath  string      `json:\\\"path\\\"`\\n   383\u2192\\tFrom  string      `json:\\\"from\\\"`\\n   384\u2192\\tValue interface{} `json:\\\"value\\\"`\\n   385\u2192}\\n   386\u2192\\n   387\u2192// applyJSPatch applies the patch. Input and output objects must both have\\n   388\u2192// the external version, since that is what the patch must have been constructed against.\\n   389\u2192func (p *jsonPatcher) applyJSPatch(versionedJS []byte) (patchedJS []byte, strictErrors []error, retErr error) {\\n   390\u2192\\tswitch p.patchType {\\n   391\u2192\\tcase types.JSONPatchType:\\n   392\u2192\\t\\tif p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationStrict || p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn {\\n   393\u2192\\t\\t\\tvar v []jsonPatchOp\\n   394\u2192\\t\\t\\tvar err error\\n   395\u2192\\t\\t\\tif strictErrors, err = kjson.UnmarshalStrict(p.patchBytes, &v); err != nil {\\n   396\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"error decoding patch: %v\\\", err))\\n   397\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   398\u2192\\t\\t\\tfor i, e := range strictErrors {\\n   399\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tstrictErrors[i] = fmt.Errorf(\\\"json patch %v\\\", e)\\n   400\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   401\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   402\u2192\\n   403\u2192\\t\\tpatchObj, err := jsonpatch.DecodePatch(p.patchBytes)\\n   404\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   405\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\\n   406\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   407\u2192\\t\\tif len(patchObj) > maxJSONPatchOperations {\\n   408\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewRequestEntityTooLargeError(\\n   409\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"The allowed maximum operations in a JSON patch is %d, got %d\\\",\\n   410\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmaxJSONPatchOperations, len(patchObj)))\\n   411\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   412\u2192\\t\\tpatchedJS, err := patchObj.Apply(versionedJS)\\n   413\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   414\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewGenericServerResponse(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, \\\"\\\", schema.GroupResource{}, \\\"\\\", err.Error(), 0, false)\\n   415\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   416\u2192\\t\\treturn patchedJS, strictErrors, nil\\n   417\u2192\\tcase types.MergePatchType:\\n   418\u2192\\t\\tif p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationStrict || p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn {\\n   419\u2192\\t\\t\\tv := map[string]interface{}{}\\n   420\u2192\\t\\t\\tvar err error\\n   421\u2192\\t\\t\\tstrictErrors, err = kjson.UnmarshalStrict(p.patchBytes, &v)\\n   422\u2192\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   423\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"error decoding patch: %v\\\", err))\\n   424\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   425\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   426\u2192\\n   427\u2192\\t\\tpatchedJS, retErr = jsonpatch.MergePatch(versionedJS, p.patchBytes)\\n   428\u2192\\t\\tif retErr == jsonpatch.ErrBadJSONPatch {\\n   429\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewBadRequest(retErr.Error())\\n   430\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   431\u2192\\t\\treturn patchedJS, strictErrors, retErr\\n   432\u2192\\tdefault:\\n   433\u2192\\t\\t// only here as a safety net - go-restful filters content-type\\n   434\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"unknown Content-Type header for patch: %v\\\", p.patchType)\\n   435\u2192\\t}\\n   436\u2192}\\n   437\u2192\\n   438\u2192type smpPatcher struct {\\n   439\u2192\\t*patcher\\n   440\u2192\\n   441\u2192\\t// Schema\\n   442\u2192\\tschemaReferenceObj runtime.Object\\n   443\u2192\\tfieldManager       *managedfields.FieldManager\\n   444\u2192}\\n   445\u2192\\n   446\u2192func (p *smpPatcher) applyPatchToCurrentObject(requestContext context.Context, currentObject runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   447\u2192\\t// Since the patch is applied on versioned objects, we need to convert the\\n   448\u2192\\t// current object to versioned representation first.\\n   449\u2192\\tcurrentVersionedObject, err := p.unsafeConvertor.ConvertToVersion(currentObject, p.kind.GroupVersion())\\n   450\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   451\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   452\u2192\\t}\\n   453\u2192\\tversionedObjToUpdate, err := p.creater.New(p.kind)\\n   454\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   455\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   456\u2192\\t}\\n   457\u2192\\tif err := strategicPatchObject(requestContext, p.defaulter, currentVersionedObject, p.patchBytes, versionedObjToUpdate, p.schemaReferenceObj, p.validationDirective); err != nil {\\n   458\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   459\u2192\\t}\\n   460\u2192\\t// Convert the object back to the hub version\\n   461\u2192\\tnewObj, err := p.unsafeConvertor.ConvertToVersion(versionedObjToUpdate, p.hubGroupVersion)\\n   462\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   463\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   464\u2192\\t}\\n   465\u2192\\n   466\u2192\\tnewObj = p.fieldManager.UpdateNoErrors(currentObject, newObj, managerOrUserAgent(p.options.FieldManager, p.userAgent))\\n   467\u2192\\treturn newObj, nil\\n   468\u2192}\\n   469\u2192\\n   470\u2192func (p *smpPatcher) createNewObject(_ context.Context) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   471\u2192\\treturn nil, errors.NewNotFound(p.resource.GroupResource(), p.name)\\n   472\u2192}\\n   473\u2192\\n   474\u2192func newApplyPatcher(p *patcher, fieldManager *managedfields.FieldManager, unmarshalFn, unmarshalStrictFn func([]byte, interface{}) error) *applyPatcher {\\n   475\u2192\\treturn &applyPatcher{\\n   476\u2192\\t\\tfieldManager:        fieldManager,\\n   477\u2192\\t\\tpatch:               p.patchBytes,\\n   478\u2192\\t\\toptions:             p.options,\\n   479\u2192\\t\\tcreater:             p.creater,\\n   480\u2192\\t\\tkind:                p.kind,\\n   481\u2192\\t\\tuserAgent:           p.userAgent,\\n   482\u2192\\t\\tvalidationDirective: p.validationDirective,\\n   483\u2192\\t\\tunmarshalFn:         unmarshalFn,\\n   484\u2192\\t\\tunmarshalStrictFn:   unmarshalStrictFn,\\n   485\u2192\\t}\\n   486\u2192}\\n   487\u2192\\n   488\u2192type applyPatcher struct {\\n   489\u2192\\tpatch               []byte\\n   490\u2192\\toptions             *metav1.PatchOptions\\n   491\u2192\\tcreater             runtime.ObjectCreater\\n   492\u2192\\tkind                schema.GroupVersionKind\\n   493\u2192\\tfieldManager        *managedfields.FieldManager\\n   494\u2192\\tuserAgent           string\\n   495\u2192\\tvalidationDirective string\\n   496\u2192\\tunmarshalFn         func(data []byte, v interface{}) error\\n   497\u2192\\tunmarshalStrictFn   func(data []byte, v interface{}) error\\n   498\u2192}\\n   499\u2192\\n   500\u2192func (p *applyPatcher) applyPatchToCurrentObject(requestContext context.Context, obj runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   501\u2192\\tforce := false\\n   502\u2192\\tif p.options.Force != nil {\\n   503\u2192\\t\\tforce = *p.options.Force\\n   504\u2192\\t}\\n   505\u2192\\tif p.fieldManager == nil {\\n   506\u2192\\t\\tpanic(\\\"FieldManager must be installed to run apply\\\")\\n   507\u2192\\t}\\n   508\u2192\\n   509\u2192\\tpatchObj := &unstructured.Unstructured{Object: map[string]interface{}{}}\\n   510\u2192\\tif err := p.unmarshalFn(p.patch, &patchObj.Object); err != nil {\\n   511\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"error decoding YAML: %v\\\", err))\\n   512\u2192\\t}\\n   513\u2192\\n   514\u2192\\tobj, err := p.fieldManager.Apply(obj, patchObj, p.options.FieldManager, force)\\n   515\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   516\u2192\\t\\treturn obj, err\\n   517\u2192\\t}\\n   518\u2192\\n   519\u2192\\t// TODO: spawn something to track deciding whether a fieldValidation=Strict\\n   520\u2192\\t// fatal error should return before an error from the apply operation\\n   521\u2192\\tif p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationStrict || p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn {\\n   522\u2192\\t\\tif err := p.unmarshalStrictFn(p.patch, &map[string]interface{}{}); err != nil {\\n   523\u2192\\t\\t\\tif p.validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationStrict {\\n   524\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"error strict decoding YAML: %v\\\", err))\\n   525\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   526\u2192\\t\\t\\taddStrictDecodingWarnings(requestContext, []error{err})\\n   527\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   528\u2192\\t}\\n   529\u2192\\treturn obj, nil\\n   530\u2192}\\n   531\u2192\\n   532\u2192func (p *applyPatcher) createNewObject(requestContext context.Context) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   533\u2192\\tobj, err := p.creater.New(p.kind)\\n   534\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   535\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create new object: %v\\\", err)\\n   536\u2192\\t}\\n   537\u2192\\treturn p.applyPatchToCurrentObject(requestContext, obj)\\n   538\u2192}\\n   539\u2192\\n   540\u2192// strategicPatchObject applies a strategic merge patch of `patchBytes` to\\n   541\u2192// `originalObject` and stores the result in `objToUpdate`.\\n   542\u2192// It additionally returns the map[string]interface{} representation of the\\n   543\u2192// `originalObject` and `patchBytes`.\\n   544\u2192// NOTE: Both `originalObject` and `objToUpdate` are supposed to be versioned.\\n   545\u2192func strategicPatchObject(\\n   546\u2192\\trequestContext context.Context,\\n   547\u2192\\tdefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter,\\n   548\u2192\\toriginalObject runtime.Object,\\n   549\u2192\\tpatchBytes []byte,\\n   550\u2192\\tobjToUpdate runtime.Object,\\n   551\u2192\\tschemaReferenceObj runtime.Object,\\n   552\u2192\\tvalidationDirective string,\\n   553\u2192) error {\\n   554\u2192\\toriginalObjMap, err := runtime.DefaultUnstructuredConverter.ToUnstructured(originalObject)\\n   555\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   556\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   557\u2192\\t}\\n   558\u2192\\n   559\u2192\\tpatchMap := make(map[string]interface{})\\n   560\u2192\\tvar strictErrs []error\\n   561\u2192\\tif validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn || validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationStrict {\\n   562\u2192\\t\\tstrictErrs, err = kjson.UnmarshalStrict(patchBytes, &patchMap)\\n   563\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   564\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\\n   565\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   566\u2192\\t} else {\\n   567\u2192\\t\\tif err = kjson.UnmarshalCaseSensitivePreserveInts(patchBytes, &patchMap); err != nil {\\n   568\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\\n   569\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   570\u2192\\t}\\n   571\u2192\\n   572\u2192\\tif err := applyPatchToObject(requestContext, defaulter, originalObjMap, patchMap, objToUpdate, schemaReferenceObj, strictErrs, validationDirective); err != nil {\\n   573\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   574\u2192\\t}\\n   575\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   576\u2192}\\n   577\u2192\\n   578\u2192// applyPatch is called every time GuaranteedUpdate asks for the updated object,\\n   579\u2192// and is given the currently persisted object as input.\\n   580\u2192// TODO: rename this function because the name implies it is related to applyPatcher\\n   581\u2192func (p *patcher) applyPatch(ctx context.Context, _, currentObject runtime.Object) (objToUpdate runtime.Object, patchErr error) {\\n   582\u2192\\t// Make sure we actually have a persisted currentObject\\n   583\u2192\\ttracing.SpanFromContext(ctx).AddEvent(\\\"About to apply patch\\\")\\n   584\u2192\\tcurrentObjectHasUID, err := hasUID(currentObject)\\n   585\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   586\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   587\u2192\\t} else if !currentObjectHasUID {\\n   588\u2192\\t\\tobjToUpdate, patchErr = p.mechanism.createNewObject(ctx)\\n   589\u2192\\t} else {\\n   590\u2192\\t\\tobjToUpdate, patchErr = p.mechanism.applyPatchToCurrentObject(ctx, currentObject)\\n   591\u2192\\t}\\n   592\u2192\\n   593\u2192\\tif patchErr != nil {\\n   594\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, patchErr\\n   595\u2192\\t}\\n   596\u2192\\n   597\u2192\\tobjToUpdateHasUID, err := hasUID(objToUpdate)\\n   598\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   599\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   600\u2192\\t}\\n   601\u2192\\tif objToUpdateHasUID && !currentObjectHasUID {\\n   602\u2192\\t\\taccessor, err := meta.Accessor(objToUpdate)\\n   603\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   604\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   605\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   606\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, errors.NewConflict(p.resource.GroupResource(), p.name, fmt.Errorf(\\\"uid mismatch: the provided object specified uid %s, and no existing object was found\\\", accessor.GetUID()))\\n   607\u2192\\t}\\n   608\u2192\\n   609\u2192\\t// if this object supports namespace info\\n   610\u2192\\tif objectMeta, err := meta.Accessor(objToUpdate); err == nil {\\n   611\u2192\\t\\t// ensure namespace on the object is correct, or error if a conflicting namespace was set in the object\\n   612\u2192\\t\\tif err := rest.EnsureObjectNamespaceMatchesRequestNamespace(rest.ExpectedNamespaceForResource(p.namespace, p.resource), objectMeta); err != nil {\\n   613\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   614\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   615\u2192\\t}\\n   616\u2192\\n   617\u2192\\tif err := checkName(objToUpdate, p.name, p.namespace, p.namer); err != nil {\\n   618\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   619\u2192\\t}\\n   620\u2192\\treturn objToUpdate, nil\\n   621\u2192}\\n   622\u2192\\n   623\u2192func (p *patcher) admissionAttributes(ctx context.Context, updatedObject runtime.Object, currentObject runtime.Object, operation admission.Operation, operationOptions runtime.Object) admission.Attributes {\\n   624\u2192\\tuserInfo, _ := request.UserFrom(ctx)\\n   625\u2192\\treturn admission.NewAttributesRecord(updatedObject, currentObject, p.kind, p.namespace, p.name, p.resource, p.subresource, operation, operationOptions, p.dryRun, userInfo)\\n   626\u2192}\\n   627\u2192\\n   628\u2192// applyAdmission is called every time GuaranteedUpdate asks for the updated object,\\n   629\u2192// and is given the currently persisted object and the patched object as input.\\n   630\u2192// TODO: rename this function because the name implies it is related to applyPatcher\\n   631\u2192func (p *patcher) applyAdmission(ctx context.Context, patchedObject runtime.Object, currentObject runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   632\u2192\\ttracing.SpanFromContext(ctx).AddEvent(\\\"About to check admission control\\\")\\n   633\u2192\\tvar operation admission.Operation\\n   634\u2192\\tvar options runtime.Object\\n   635\u2192\\tif hasUID, err := hasUID(currentObject); err != nil {\\n   636\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   637\u2192\\t} else if !hasUID {\\n   638\u2192\\t\\toperation = admission.Create\\n   639\u2192\\t\\tcurrentObject = nil\\n   640\u2192\\t\\toptions = patchToCreateOptions(p.options)\\n   641\u2192\\t} else {\\n   642\u2192\\t\\toperation = admission.Update\\n   643\u2192\\t\\toptions = patchToUpdateOptions(p.options)\\n   644\u2192\\t}\\n   645\u2192\\tif p.admissionCheck != nil && p.admissionCheck.Handles(operation) {\\n   646\u2192\\t\\tattributes := p.admissionAttributes(ctx, patchedObject, currentObject, operation, options)\\n   647\u2192\\t\\treturn patchedObject, p.admissionCheck.Admit(ctx, attributes, p.objectInterfaces)\\n   648\u2192\\t}\\n   649\u2192\\treturn patchedObject, nil\\n   650\u2192}\\n   651\u2192\\n   652\u2192// patchResource divides PatchResource for easier unit testing\\n   653\u2192func (p *patcher) patchResource(ctx context.Context, scope *RequestScope) (runtime.Object, bool, error) {\\n   654\u2192\\tp.namespace = request.NamespaceValue(ctx)\\n   655\u2192\\tswitch p.patchType {\\n   656\u2192\\tcase types.JSONPatchType, types.MergePatchType:\\n   657\u2192\\t\\tp.mechanism = &jsonPatcher{\\n   658\u2192\\t\\t\\tpatcher:      p,\\n   659\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldManager: scope.FieldManager,\\n   660\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   661\u2192\\tcase types.StrategicMergePatchType:\\n   662\u2192\\t\\tschemaReferenceObj, err := p.unsafeConvertor.ConvertToVersion(p.restPatcher.New(), p.kind.GroupVersion())\\n   663\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   664\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, false, err\\n   665\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   666\u2192\\t\\tp.mechanism = &smpPatcher{\\n   667\u2192\\t\\t\\tpatcher:            p,\\n   668\u2192\\t\\t\\tschemaReferenceObj: schemaReferenceObj,\\n   669\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldManager:       scope.FieldManager,\\n   670\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   671\u2192\\t// this case is unreachable if ServerSideApply is not enabled because we will have already rejected the content type\\n   672\u2192\\tcase types.ApplyYAMLPatchType:\\n   673\u2192\\t\\tp.mechanism = newApplyPatcher(p, scope.FieldManager, yaml.Unmarshal, yaml.UnmarshalStrict)\\n   674\u2192\\t\\tp.forceAllowCreate = true\\n   675\u2192\\tcase types.ApplyCBORPatchType:\\n   676\u2192\\t\\tif !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.CBORServingAndStorage) {\\n   677\u2192\\t\\t\\tutilruntime.HandleErrorWithContext(context.TODO(), nil, \\\"CBOR apply requests should be rejected before reaching this point unless the feature gate is enabled.\\\")\\n   678\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"%v: unimplemented patch type\\\", p.patchType)\\n   679\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   680\u2192\\n   681\u2192\\t\\t// The strict and non-strict funcs are the same here because any CBOR map with\\n   682\u2192\\t\\t// duplicate keys is invalid and always rejected outright regardless of strictness\\n   683\u2192\\t\\t// mode, and unknown field errors can't occur in practice because the type of the\\n   684\u2192\\t\\t// destination value for unmarshaling an apply configuration is always\\n   685\u2192\\t\\t// \\\"unstructured\\\".\\n   686\u2192\\t\\tp.mechanism = newApplyPatcher(p, scope.FieldManager, cbor.Unmarshal, cbor.Unmarshal)\\n   687\u2192\\t\\tp.forceAllowCreate = true\\n   688\u2192\\tdefault:\\n   689\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"%v: unimplemented patch type\\\", p.patchType)\\n   690\u2192\\t}\\n   691\u2192\\tdedupOwnerReferencesTransformer := func(_ context.Context, obj, _ runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   692\u2192\\t\\t// Dedup owner references after mutating admission happens\\n   693\u2192\\t\\tdedupOwnerReferencesAndAddWarning(obj, ctx, true)\\n   694\u2192\\t\\treturn obj, nil\\n   695\u2192\\t}\\n   696\u2192\\n   697\u2192\\ttransformers := []rest.TransformFunc{p.applyPatch, p.applyAdmission, dedupOwnerReferencesTransformer}\\n   698\u2192\\n   699\u2192\\twasCreated := false\\n   700\u2192\\tp.updatedObjectInfo = rest.DefaultUpdatedObjectInfo(nil, transformers...)\\n   701\u2192\\trequestFunc := func() (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   702\u2192\\t\\t// Pass in UpdateOptions to override UpdateStrategy.AllowUpdateOnCreate\\n   703\u2192\\t\\toptions := patchToUpdateOptions(p.options)\\n   704\u2192\\t\\tupdateObject, created, updateErr := p.restPatcher.Update(ctx, p.name, p.updatedObjectInfo, p.createValidation, p.updateValidation, p.forceAllowCreate, options)\\n   705\u2192\\t\\twasCreated = created\\n   706\u2192\\t\\treturn updateObject, updateErr\\n   707\u2192\\t}\\n   708\u2192\\tresult, err := finisher.FinishRequest(ctx, func() (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   709\u2192\\n   710\u2192\\t\\tresult, err := requestFunc()\\n   711\u2192\\t\\t// If the object wasn't committed to storage because it's serialized size was too large,\\n   712\u2192\\t\\t// it is safe to remove managedFields (which can be large) and try again.\\n   713\u2192\\t\\tif isTooLargeError(err) && p.patchType != types.ApplyYAMLPatchType && p.patchType != types.ApplyCBORPatchType {\\n   714\u2192\\t\\t\\tif _, accessorErr := meta.Accessor(p.restPatcher.New()); accessorErr == nil {\\n   715\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tp.updatedObjectInfo = rest.DefaultUpdatedObjectInfo(nil,\\n   716\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tp.applyPatch,\\n   717\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tp.applyAdmission,\\n   718\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdedupOwnerReferencesTransformer,\\n   719\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfunc(_ context.Context, obj, _ runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   720\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\taccessor, _ := meta.Accessor(obj)\\n   721\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\taccessor.SetManagedFields(nil)\\n   722\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn obj, nil\\n   723\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n   724\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tresult, err = requestFunc()\\n   725\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   726\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   727\u2192\\t\\treturn result, err\\n   728\u2192\\t})\\n   729\u2192\\n   730\u2192\\t// In case of a timeout error, the goroutine handling the request is still running.\\n   731\u2192\\t// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/d2c12afa4593e50a187075157d38748292b02733/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/finisher/finisher.go#L127-L146\\n   732\u2192\\t// We cannot reliably read the variable (data race!) and have to assume that\\n   733\u2192\\t// the object was not created.\\n   734\u2192\\tif errors.IsTimeout(err) {\\n   735\u2192\\t\\treturn result, false, err\\n   736\u2192\\t}\\n   737\u2192\\treturn result, wasCreated, err\\n   738\u2192}\\n   739\u2192\\n   740\u2192// applyPatchToObject applies a strategic merge patch of <patchMap> to\\n   741\u2192// <originalMap> and stores the result in <objToUpdate>.\\n   742\u2192// NOTE: <objToUpdate> must be a versioned object.\\n   743\u2192func applyPatchToObject(\\n   744\u2192\\trequestContext context.Context,\\n   745\u2192\\tdefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter,\\n   746\u2192\\toriginalMap map[string]interface{},\\n   747\u2192\\tpatchMap map[string]interface{},\\n   748\u2192\\tobjToUpdate runtime.Object,\\n   749\u2192\\tschemaReferenceObj runtime.Object,\\n   750\u2192\\tstrictErrs []error,\\n   751\u2192\\tvalidationDirective string,\\n   752\u2192) error {\\n   753\u2192\\tpatchedObjMap, err := strategicpatch.StrategicMergeMapPatch(originalMap, patchMap, schemaReferenceObj)\\n   754\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   755\u2192\\t\\treturn interpretStrategicMergePatchError(err)\\n   756\u2192\\t}\\n   757\u2192\\n   758\u2192\\t// Rather than serialize the patched map to JSON, then decode it to an object, we go directly from a map to an object\\n   759\u2192\\tconverter := runtime.DefaultUnstructuredConverter\\n   760\u2192\\treturnUnknownFields := validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn || validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationStrict\\n   761\u2192\\tif err := converter.FromUnstructuredWithValidation(patchedObjMap, objToUpdate, returnUnknownFields); err != nil {\\n   762\u2192\\t\\tstrictError, isStrictError := runtime.AsStrictDecodingError(err)\\n   763\u2192\\t\\tswitch {\\n   764\u2192\\t\\tcase !isStrictError:\\n   765\u2192\\t\\t\\t// disregard any sttrictErrs, because it's an incomplete\\n   766\u2192\\t\\t\\t// list of strict errors given that we don't know what fields were\\n   767\u2192\\t\\t\\t// unknown because StrategicMergeMapPatch failed.\\n   768\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Non-strict errors trump in this case.\\n   769\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{}, \\\"\\\", field.ErrorList{\\n   770\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfield.Invalid(field.NewPath(\\\"patch\\\"), fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%+v\\\", patchMap), err.Error()),\\n   771\u2192\\t\\t\\t})\\n   772\u2192\\t\\tcase validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn:\\n   773\u2192\\t\\t\\taddStrictDecodingWarnings(requestContext, append(strictErrs, strictError.Errors()...))\\n   774\u2192\\t\\tdefault:\\n   775\u2192\\t\\t\\tstrictDecodingError := runtime.NewStrictDecodingError(append(strictErrs, strictError.Errors()...))\\n   776\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{}, \\\"\\\", field.ErrorList{\\n   777\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfield.Invalid(field.NewPath(\\\"patch\\\"), fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%+v\\\", patchMap), strictDecodingError.Error()),\\n   778\u2192\\t\\t\\t})\\n
