779\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   780\u2192\\t} else if len(strictErrs) > 0 {\\n   781\u2192\\t\\tswitch {\\n   782\u2192\\t\\tcase validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn:\\n   783\u2192\\t\\t\\taddStrictDecodingWarnings(requestContext, strictErrs)\\n   784\u2192\\t\\tdefault:\\n   785\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{}, \\\"\\\", field.ErrorList{\\n   786\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfield.Invalid(field.NewPath(\\\"patch\\\"), fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%+v\\\", patchMap), runtime.NewStrictDecodingError(strictErrs).Error()),\\n   787\u2192\\t\\t\\t})\\n   788\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   789\u2192\\t}\\n   790\u2192\\n   791\u2192\\t// Decoding from JSON to a versioned object would apply defaults, so we do the same here\\n   792\u2192\\tdefaulter.Default(objToUpdate)\\n   793\u2192\\n   794\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   795\u2192}\\n   796\u2192\\n   797\u2192// interpretStrategicMergePatchError interprets the error type and returns an error with appropriate HTTP code.\\n   798\u2192func interpretStrategicMergePatchError(err error) error {\\n   799\u2192\\tswitch err {\\n   800\u2192\\tcase mergepatch.ErrBadJSONDoc, mergepatch.ErrBadPatchFormatForPrimitiveList, mergepatch.ErrBadPatchFormatForRetainKeys, mergepatch.ErrBadPatchFormatForSetElementOrderList, mergepatch.ErrUnsupportedStrategicMergePatchFormat:\\n   801\u2192\\t\\treturn errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\\n   802\u2192\\tcase mergepatch.ErrNoListOfLists, mergepatch.ErrPatchContentNotMatchRetainKeys:\\n   803\u2192\\t\\treturn errors.NewGenericServerResponse(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, \\\"\\\", schema.GroupResource{}, \\\"\\\", err.Error(), 0, false)\\n   804\u2192\\tdefault:\\n   805\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   806\u2192\\t}\\n   807\u2192}\\n   808\u2192\\n   809\u2192// patchToUpdateOptions creates an UpdateOptions with the same field values as the provided PatchOptions.\\n   810\u2192func patchToUpdateOptions(po *metav1.PatchOptions) *metav1.UpdateOptions {\\n   811\u2192\\tif po == nil {\\n   812\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   813\u2192\\t}\\n   814\u2192\\tuo := &metav1.UpdateOptions{\\n   815\u2192\\t\\tDryRun:          po.DryRun,\\n   816\u2192\\t\\tFieldManager:    po.FieldManager,\\n   817\u2192\\t\\tFieldValidation: po.FieldValidation,\\n   818\u2192\\t}\\n   819\u2192\\tuo.TypeMeta.SetGroupVersionKind(metav1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind(\\\"UpdateOptions\\\"))\\n   820\u2192\\treturn uo\\n   821\u2192}\\n   822\u2192\\n   823\u2192// patchToCreateOptions creates an CreateOptions with the same field values as the provided PatchOptions.\\n   824\u2192func patchToCreateOptions(po *metav1.PatchOptions) *metav1.CreateOptions {\\n   825\u2192\\tif po == nil {\\n   826\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   827\u2192\\t}\\n   828\u2192\\tco := &metav1.CreateOptions{\\n   829\u2192\\t\\tDryRun:          po.DryRun,\\n   830\u2192\\t\\tFieldManager:    po.FieldManager,\\n   831\u2192\\t\\tFieldValidation: po.FieldValidation,\\n   832\u2192\\t}\\n   833\u2192\\tco.TypeMeta.SetGroupVersionKind(metav1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind(\\\"CreateOptions\\\"))\\n   834\u2192\\treturn co\\n   835\u2192}\\n   836\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"c2bfa00b-faab-4017-ac12-dff90553df61\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:50.173Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"9c1ba6e3-3794-4c9b-949c-911de5e35b4a\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"c2bfa00b-faab-4017-ac12-dff90553df61\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01NeHGU46ccGfN3tKvTguMTa\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01WyYniAp7UhrG8Bih1Q2xru\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/fieldmanager.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":7494,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":10906,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":7494},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG56VuJYufMxrcQaD6Zw\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"b0f5e3e9-a95a-4046-9436-941689b46cb8\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:50.355Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"b0f5e3e9-a95a-4046-9436-941689b46cb8\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01WyYniAp7UhrG8Bih1Q2xru\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01WyYniAp7UhrG8Bih1Q2xru\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:50.358Z\",\"uuid\":\"cb5d84ae-f03c-4b00-baaf-c8144857747f\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"b0f5e3e9-a95a-4046-9436-941689b46cb8\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01WyYniAp7UhrG8Bih1Q2xru\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2018 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package managedfields\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal\\\"\\n    28\u2192)\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192// FieldManager updates the managed fields and merges applied\\n    31\u2192// configurations.\\n    32\u2192type FieldManager = internal.FieldManager\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192// NewDefaultFieldManager creates a new FieldManager that merges apply requests\\n    35\u2192// and update managed fields for other types of requests.\\n    36\u2192func NewDefaultFieldManager(typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter, objectCreater runtime.ObjectCreater, kind schema.GroupVersionKind, hub schema.GroupVersion, subresource string, resetFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter) (*FieldManager, error) {\\n    37\u2192\\tf, err := internal.NewStructuredMergeManager(typeConverter, objectConverter, objectDefaulter, kind.GroupVersion(), hub, resetFields)\\n    38\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    39\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create field manager: %v\\\", err)\\n    40\u2192\\t}\\n    41\u2192\\treturn internal.NewDefaultFieldManager(f, typeConverter, objectConverter, objectCreater, kind, subresource), nil\\n    42\u2192}\\n    43\u2192\\n    44\u2192// NewDefaultCRDFieldManager creates a new FieldManager specifically for\\n    45\u2192// CRDs. This allows for the possibility of fields which are not defined\\n    46\u2192// in models, as well as having no models defined at all.\\n    47\u2192func NewDefaultCRDFieldManager(typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter, objectCreater runtime.ObjectCreater, kind schema.GroupVersionKind, hub schema.GroupVersion, subresource string, resetFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter) (_ *FieldManager, err error) {\\n    48\u2192\\tf, err := internal.NewCRDStructuredMergeManager(typeConverter, objectConverter, objectDefaulter, kind.GroupVersion(), hub, resetFields)\\n    49\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    50\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create field manager: %v\\\", err)\\n    51\u2192\\t}\\n    52\u2192\\treturn internal.NewDefaultFieldManager(f, typeConverter, objectConverter, objectCreater, kind, subresource), nil\\n    53\u2192}\\n    54\u2192\\n    55\u2192func ValidateManagedFields(encodedManagedFields []metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry) error {\\n    56\u2192\\t_, err := internal.DecodeManagedFields(encodedManagedFields)\\n    57\u2192\\treturn err\\n    58\u2192}\\n    59\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"8e7642ff-61bc-4cf2-a192-1de13f921968\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:50.358Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"b0f5e3e9-a95a-4046-9436-941689b46cb8\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"8e7642ff-61bc-4cf2-a192-1de13f921968\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01NeHGU46ccGfN3tKvTguMTa\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01825ngNNTSMA26XdPrEKqai\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/fieldmanager.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":7494,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":10906,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":7494},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG56VuJYufMxrcQaD6Zw\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"d540a010-718c-40a6-8858-58bdf53d0131\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:50.637Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d540a010-718c-40a6-8858-58bdf53d0131\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01825ngNNTSMA26XdPrEKqai\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01825ngNNTSMA26XdPrEKqai\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:50.639Z\",\"uuid\":\"fcd17e96-d72d-4eed-b5cf-b11d5721efec\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d540a010-718c-40a6-8858-58bdf53d0131\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01825ngNNTSMA26XdPrEKqai\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package internal\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"reflect\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge\\\"\\n    30\u2192)\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192// DefaultMaxUpdateManagers defines the default maximum retained number of managedFields entries from updates\\n    33\u2192// if the number of update managers exceeds this, the oldest entries will be merged until the number is below the maximum.\\n    34\u2192// TODO(jennybuckley): Determine if this is really the best value. Ideally we wouldn't unnecessarily merge too many entries.\\n    35\u2192const DefaultMaxUpdateManagers int = 10\\n    36\u2192\\n    37\u2192// DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability defines the default probability that the field management of an object\\n    38\u2192// starts being tracked from the object's creation, instead of from the first time the object is applied to.\\n    39\u2192const DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability float32 = 1\\n    40\u2192\\n    41\u2192var atMostEverySecond = NewAtMostEvery(time.Second)\\n    42\u2192\\n    43\u2192// FieldManager updates the managed fields and merges applied\\n    44\u2192// configurations.\\n    45\u2192type FieldManager struct {\\n    46\u2192\\tfieldManager Manager\\n    47\u2192\\tsubresource  string\\n    48\u2192}\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192// NewFieldManager creates a new FieldManager that decodes, manages, then re-encodes managedFields\\n    51\u2192// on update and apply requests.\\n    52\u2192func NewFieldManager(f Manager, subresource string) *FieldManager {\\n    53\u2192\\treturn &FieldManager{fieldManager: f, subresource: subresource}\\n    54\u2192}\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192// newDefaultFieldManager is a helper function which wraps a Manager with certain default logic.\\n    57\u2192func NewDefaultFieldManager(f Manager, typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, objectCreater runtime.ObjectCreater, kind schema.GroupVersionKind, subresource string) *FieldManager {\\n    58\u2192\\treturn NewFieldManager(\\n    59\u2192\\t\\tNewVersionCheckManager(\\n    60\u2192\\t\\t\\tNewLastAppliedUpdater(\\n    61\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tNewLastAppliedManager(\\n    62\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNewProbabilisticSkipNonAppliedManager(\\n    63\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNewCapManagersManager(\\n    64\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNewBuildManagerInfoManager(\\n    65\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNewManagedFieldsUpdater(\\n    66\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNewStripMetaManager(f),\\n    67\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t), kind.GroupVersion(), subresource,\\n    68\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t), DefaultMaxUpdateManagers,\\n    69\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t), objectCreater, DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability,\\n    70\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t), typeConverter, objectConverter, kind.GroupVersion(),\\n    71\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n    72\u2192\\t\\t\\t), kind,\\n    73\u2192\\t\\t), subresource,\\n    74\u2192\\t)\\n    75\u2192}\\n    76\u2192\\n    77\u2192func decodeLiveOrNew(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, ignoreManagedFieldsFromRequestObject bool) (Managed, error) {\\n    78\u2192\\tliveAccessor, err := meta.Accessor(liveObj)\\n    79\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    80\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n    81\u2192\\t}\\n    82\u2192\\n    83\u2192\\t// We take the managedFields of the live object in case the request tries to\\n    84\u2192\\t// manually set managedFields via a subresource.\\n    85\u2192\\tif ignoreManagedFieldsFromRequestObject {\\n    86\u2192\\t\\treturn emptyManagedFieldsOnErr(DecodeManagedFields(liveAccessor.GetManagedFields()))\\n    87\u2192\\t}\\n    88\u2192\\n    89\u2192\\t// If the object doesn't have metadata, we should just return without trying to\\n    90\u2192\\t// set the managedFields at all, so creates/updates/patches will work normally.\\n    91\u2192\\tnewAccessor, err := meta.Accessor(newObj)\\n    92\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    93\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n    94\u2192\\t}\\n    95\u2192\\n    96\u2192\\tif isResetManagedFields(newAccessor.GetManagedFields()) {\\n    97\u2192\\t\\treturn NewEmptyManaged(), nil\\n    98\u2192\\t}\\n    99\u2192\\n   100\u2192\\t// If the managed field is empty or we failed to decode it,\\n   101\u2192\\t// let's try the live object. This is to prevent clients who\\n   102\u2192\\t// don't understand managedFields from deleting it accidentally.\\n   103\u2192\\tmanaged, err := DecodeManagedFields(newAccessor.GetManagedFields())\\n   104\u2192\\tif err != nil || len(managed.Fields()) == 0 {\\n   105\u2192\\t\\treturn emptyManagedFieldsOnErr(DecodeManagedFields(liveAccessor.GetManagedFields()))\\n   106\u2192\\t}\\n   107\u2192\\treturn managed, nil\\n   108\u2192}\\n   109\u2192\\n   110\u2192func emptyManagedFieldsOnErr(managed Managed, err error) (Managed, error) {\\n   111\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   112\u2192\\t\\treturn NewEmptyManaged(), nil\\n   113\u2192\\t}\\n   114\u2192\\treturn managed, nil\\n   115\u2192}\\n   116\u2192\\n   117\u2192// Update is used when the object has already been merged (non-apply\\n   118\u2192// use-case), and simply updates the managed fields in the output\\n   119\u2192// object.\\n   120\u2192func (f *FieldManager) Update(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, manager string) (object runtime.Object, err error) {\\n   121\u2192\\t// First try to decode the managed fields provided in the update,\\n   122\u2192\\t// This is necessary to allow directly updating managed fields.\\n   123\u2192\\tisSubresource := f.subresource != \\\"\\\"\\n   124\u2192\\tmanaged, err := decodeLiveOrNew(liveObj, newObj, isSubresource)\\n   125\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   126\u2192\\t\\treturn newObj, nil\\n   127\u2192\\t}\\n   128\u2192\\n   129\u2192\\tRemoveObjectManagedFields(newObj)\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192\\tif object, managed, err = f.fieldManager.Update(liveObj, newObj, managed, manager); err != nil {\\n   132\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   133\u2192\\t}\\n   134\u2192\\n   135\u2192\\tif err = EncodeObjectManagedFields(object, managed); err != nil {\\n   136\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to encode managed fields: %v\\\", err)\\n   137\u2192\\t}\\n   138\u2192\\n   139\u2192\\treturn object, nil\\n   140\u2192}\\n   141\u2192\\n   142\u2192// UpdateNoErrors is the same as Update, but it will not return\\n   143\u2192// errors. If an error happens, the object is returned with\\n   144\u2192// managedFields cleared.\\n   145\u2192func (f *FieldManager) UpdateNoErrors(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, manager string) runtime.Object {\\n   146\u2192\\tobj, err := f.Update(liveObj, newObj, manager)\\n   147\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   148\u2192\\t\\tatMostEverySecond.Do(func() {\\n   149\u2192\\t\\t\\tns, name := \\\"unknown\\\", \\\"unknown\\\"\\n   150\u2192\\t\\t\\tif accessor, err := meta.Accessor(newObj); err == nil {\\n   151\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tns = accessor.GetNamespace()\\n   152\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tname = accessor.GetName()\\n   153\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   154\u2192\\n   155\u2192\\t\\t\\tklog.ErrorS(err, \\\"[SHOULD NOT HAPPEN] failed to update managedFields\\\", \\\"versionKind\\\",\\n   156\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tnewObj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind(), \\\"namespace\\\", ns, \\\"name\\\", name)\\n   157\u2192\\t\\t})\\n   158\u2192\\t\\t// Explicitly remove managedFields on failure, so that\\n   159\u2192\\t\\t// we can't have garbage in it.\\n   160\u2192\\t\\tRemoveObjectManagedFields(newObj)\\n   161\u2192\\t\\treturn newObj\\n   162\u2192\\t}\\n   163\u2192\\treturn obj\\n   164\u2192}\\n   165\u2192\\n   166\u2192// Returns true if the managedFields indicate that the user is trying to\\n   167\u2192// reset the managedFields, i.e. if the list is non-nil but empty, or if\\n   168\u2192// the list has one empty item.\\n   169\u2192func isResetManagedFields(managedFields []metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry) bool {\\n   170\u2192\\tif len(managedFields) == 0 {\\n   171\u2192\\t\\treturn managedFields != nil\\n   172\u2192\\t}\\n   173\u2192\\n   174\u2192\\tif len(managedFields) == 1 {\\n   175\u2192\\t\\treturn reflect.DeepEqual(managedFields[0], metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry{})\\n   176\u2192\\t}\\n   177\u2192\\n   178\u2192\\treturn false\\n   179\u2192}\\n   180\u2192\\n   181\u2192// Apply is used when server-side apply is called, as it merges the\\n   182\u2192// object and updates the managed fields.\\n   183\u2192func (f *FieldManager) Apply(liveObj, appliedObj runtime.Object, manager string, force bool) (object runtime.Object, err error) {\\n   184\u2192\\t// If the object doesn't have metadata, apply isn't allowed.\\n   185\u2192\\taccessor, err := meta.Accessor(liveObj)\\n   186\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   187\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"couldn't get accessor: %v\\\", err)\\n   188\u2192\\t}\\n   189\u2192\\n   190\u2192\\t// Decode the managed fields in the live object, since it isn't allowed in the patch.\\n   191\u2192\\tmanaged, err := DecodeManagedFields(accessor.GetManagedFields())\\n   192\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   193\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to decode managed fields: %v\\\", err)\\n   194\u2192\\t}\\n   195\u2192\\n   196\u2192\\tobject, managed, err = f.fieldManager.Apply(liveObj, appliedObj, managed, manager, force)\\n   197\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   198\u2192\\t\\tif conflicts, ok := err.(merge.Conflicts); ok {\\n   199\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, NewConflictError(conflicts)\\n   200\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   201\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   202\u2192\\t}\\n   203\u2192\\n   204\u2192\\tif err = EncodeObjectManagedFields(object, managed); err != nil {\\n   205\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to encode managed fields: %v\\\", err)\\n   206\u2192\\t}\\n   207\u2192\\n   208\u2192\\treturn object, nil\\n   209\u2192}\\n   210\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"912ca65a-77fa-4fb4-a177-98c84ba3f360\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:50.639Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"d540a010-718c-40a6-8858-58bdf53d0131\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"912ca65a-77fa-4fb4-a177-98c84ba3f360\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_013G3yjSWq5eTRnhdRJirLqQ\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Excellent! :\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01HXdzNbdL693YkBusS9Lwsh\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01HXdzNbdL693YkBusS9Lwsh\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:53.494Z\",\"uuid\":\"f173cfc8-3d9c-4497-9b23-da0dd8ac6f44\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"5dc64854-e96e-456b-b06b-bfe4efb81f19\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01HXdzNbdL693YkBusS9Lwsh\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package internal\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"encoding/json\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge\\\"\\n    30\u2192)\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192// NewConflictError returns an error including details on the requests apply conflicts\\n    33\u2192func NewConflictError(conflicts merge.Conflicts) *errors.StatusError {\\n    34\u2192\\tcauses := []metav1.StatusCause{}\\n    35\u2192\\tfor _, conflict := range conflicts {\\n    36\u2192\\t\\tcauses = append(causes, metav1.StatusCause{\\n    37\u2192\\t\\t\\tType:    metav1.CauseTypeFieldManagerConflict,\\n    38\u2192\\t\\t\\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(\\\"conflict with %v\\\", printManager(conflict.Manager)),\\n    39\u2192\\t\\t\\tField:   conflict.Path.String(),\\n    40\u2192\\t\\t})\\n    41\u2192\\t}\\n    42\u2192\\treturn errors.NewApplyConflict(causes, getConflictMessage(conflicts))\\n    43\u2192}\\n    44\u2192\\n    45\u2192func getConflictMessage(conflicts merge.Conflicts) string {\\n    46\u2192\\tif len(conflicts) == 1 {\\n    47\u2192\\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Apply failed with 1 conflict: conflict with %v: %v\\\", printManager(conflicts[0].Manager), conflicts[0].Path)\\n    48\u2192\\t}\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192\\tm := map[string][]fieldpath.Path{}\\n    51\u2192\\tfor _, conflict := range conflicts {\\n    52\u2192\\t\\tm[conflict.Manager] = append(m[conflict.Manager], conflict.Path)\\n    53\u2192\\t}\\n    54\u2192\\n    55\u2192\\tuniqueManagers := []string{}\\n    56\u2192\\tfor manager := range m {\\n    57\u2192\\t\\tuniqueManagers = append(uniqueManagers, manager)\\n    58\u2192\\t}\\n    59\u2192\\n    60\u2192\\t// Print conflicts by sorted managers.\\n    61\u2192\\tsort.Strings(uniqueManagers)\\n    62\u2192\\n    63\u2192\\tmessages := []string{}\\n    64\u2192\\tfor _, manager := range uniqueManagers {\\n    65\u2192\\t\\tmessages = append(messages, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"conflicts with %v:\\\", printManager(manager)))\\n    66\u2192\\t\\tfor _, path := range m[manager] {\\n    67\u2192\\t\\t\\tmessages = append(messages, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"- %v\\\", path))\\n    68\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    69\u2192\\t}\\n    70\u2192\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Apply failed with %d conflicts: %s\\\", len(conflicts), strings.Join(messages, \\\"\\\\n\\\"))\\n    71\u2192}\\n    72\u2192\\n    73\u2192func printManager(manager string) string {\\n    74\u2192\\tencodedManager := &metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry{}\\n    75\u2192\\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(manager), encodedManager); err != nil {\\n    76\u2192\\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%q\\\", manager)\\n    77\u2192\\t}\\n    78\u2192\\tmanagerStr := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%q\\\", encodedManager.Manager)\\n    79\u2192\\tif encodedManager.Subresource != \\\"\\\" {\\n    80\u2192\\t\\tmanagerStr = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s with subresource %q\\\", managerStr, encodedManager.Subresource)\\n    81\u2192\\t}\\n    82\u2192\\tif encodedManager.Operation == metav1.ManagedFieldsOperationUpdate {\\n    83\u2192\\t\\tif encodedManager.Time == nil {\\n    84\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s using %v\\\", managerStr, encodedManager.APIVersion)\\n    85\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    86\u2192\\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s using %v at %v\\\", managerStr, encodedManager.APIVersion, encodedManager.Time.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339))\\n    87\u2192\\t}\\n    88\u2192\\treturn managerStr\\n    89\u2192}\\n    90\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"eb2dd945-a05c-44d0-b234-8e767a05fa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:53.494Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"5dc64854-e96e-456b-b06b-bfe4efb81f19\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"eb2dd945-a05c-44d0-b234-8e767a05fa31\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_013G3yjSWq5eTRnhdRJirLqQ\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01Vrs5vuG845tDhibqaGvcD8\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/manager.go\",\"limit\":200},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":47068,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":18400,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":47068},\"output_tokens\":4,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG56fMpw2qiWrPXRLekb\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"b99a5bbc-df61-413a-a2ce-8c0eb893fd88\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:53.904Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"b99a5bbc-df61-413a-a2ce-8c0eb893fd88\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01Vrs5vuG845tDhibqaGvcD8\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01Vrs5vuG845tDhibqaGvcD8\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:53.908Z\",\"uuid\":\"860df832-a4df-4e7f-b10b-5bee769b1c50\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"b99a5bbc-df61-413a-a2ce-8c0eb893fd88\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01Vrs5vuG845tDhibqaGvcD8\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package internal\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    23\u2192)\\n    24\u2192\\n    25\u2192// Managed groups a fieldpath.ManagedFields together with the timestamps associated with each operation.\\n    26\u2192type Managed interface {\\n    27\u2192\\t// Fields gets the fieldpath.ManagedFields.\\n    28\u2192\\tFields() fieldpath.ManagedFields\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192\\t// Times gets the timestamps associated with each operation.\\n    31\u2192\\tTimes() map[string]*metav1.Time\\n    32\u2192}\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192// Manager updates the managed fields and merges applied configurations.\\n    35\u2192type Manager interface {\\n    36\u2192\\t// Update is used when the object has already been merged (non-apply\\n    37\u2192\\t// use-case), and simply updates the managed fields in the output\\n    38\u2192\\t// object.\\n    39\u2192\\t//  * `liveObj` is not mutated by this function\\n    40\u2192\\t//  * `newObj` may be mutated by this function\\n    41\u2192\\t// Returns the new object with managedFields removed, and the object's new\\n    42\u2192\\t// proposed managedFields separately.\\n    43\u2192\\tUpdate(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string) (runtime.Object, Managed, error)\\n    44\u2192\\n    45\u2192\\t// Apply is used when server-side apply is called, as it merges the\\n    46\u2192\\t// object and updates the managed fields.\\n    47\u2192\\t//  * `liveObj` is not mutated by this function\\n    48\u2192\\t//  * `newObj` may be mutated by this function\\n    49\u2192\\t// Returns the new object with managedFields removed, and the object's new\\n    50\u2192\\t// proposed managedFields separately.\\n    51\u2192\\tApply(liveObj, appliedObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, fieldManager string, force bool) (runtime.Object, Managed, error)\\n    52\u2192}\\n    53\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"a5f22752-7d71-4900-8a14-44a7c7ec157d\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:53.908Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"b99a5bbc-df61-413a-a2ce-8c0eb893fd88\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"a5f22752-7d71-4900-8a14-44a7c7ec157d\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_013G3yjSWq5eTRnhdRJirLqQ\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_015pyrCiJUPoZqsj2coLpT9R\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge/conflict.go\",\"limit\":150},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":47068,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":18400,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":47068},\"output_tokens\":4,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG56fMpw2qiWrPXRLekb\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"ff02d625-2ce9-4f90-bb6b-222892c86cfa\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:54.203Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"ff02d625-2ce9-4f90-bb6b-222892c86cfa\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_015pyrCiJUPoZqsj2coLpT9R\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_015pyrCiJUPoZqsj2coLpT9R\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:54.205Z\",\"uuid\":\"6eedbae3-88ce-4b4d-a039-a24aa9e8fc7     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2018 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package merge\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    25\u2192)\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192// Conflict is a conflict on a specific field with the current manager of\\n    28\u2192// that field. It does implement the error interface so that it can be\\n    29\u2192// used as an error.\\n    30\u2192type Conflict struct {\\n    31\u2192\\tManager string\\n    32\u2192\\tPath    fieldpath.Path\\n    33\u2192}\\n    34\u2192\\n    35\u2192// Conflict is an error.\\n    36\u2192var _ error = Conflict{}\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192// Error formats the conflict as an error.\\n    39\u2192func (c Conflict) Error() string {\\n    40\u2192\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"conflict with %q: %v\\\", c.Manager, c.Path)\\n    41\u2192}\\n    42\u2192\\n    43\u2192// Equals returns true if c == c2\\n    44\u2192func (c Conflict) Equals(c2 Conflict) bool {\\n    45\u2192\\tif c.Manager != c2.Manager {\\n    46\u2192\\t\\treturn false\\n    47\u2192\\t}\\n    48\u2192\\treturn c.Path.Equals(c2.Path)\\n    49\u2192}\\n    50\u2192\\n    51\u2192// Conflicts accumulates multiple conflicts and aggregates them by managers.\\n    52\u2192type Conflicts []Conflict\\n    53\u2192\\n    54\u2192var _ error = Conflicts{}\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192// Error prints the list of conflicts, grouped by sorted managers.\\n    57\u2192func (conflicts Conflicts) Error() string {\\n    58\u2192\\tif len(conflicts) == 1 {\\n    59\u2192\\t\\treturn conflicts[0].Error()\\n    60\u2192\\t}\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192\\tm := map[string][]fieldpath.Path{}\\n    63\u2192\\tfor _, conflict := range conflicts {\\n    64\u2192\\t\\tm[conflict.Manager] = append(m[conflict.Manager], conflict.Path)\\n    65\u2192\\t}\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192\\tmanagers := []string{}\\n    68\u2192\\tfor manager := range m {\\n    69\u2192\\t\\tmanagers = append(managers, manager)\\n    70\u2192\\t}\\n    71\u2192\\n    72\u2192\\t// Print conflicts by sorted managers.\\n    73\u2192\\tsort.Strings(managers)\\n    74\u2192\\n    75\u2192\\tmessages := []string{}\\n    76\u2192\\tfor _, manager := range managers {\\n    77\u2192\\t\\tmessages = append(messages, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"conflicts with %q:\\\", manager))\\n    78\u2192\\t\\tfor _, path := range m[manager] {\\n    79\u2192\\t\\t\\tmessages = append(messages, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"- %v\\\", path))\\n    80\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    81\u2192\\t}\\n    82\u2192\\treturn strings.Join(messages, \\\"\\\\n\\\")\\n    83\u2192}\\n    84\u2192\\n    85\u2192// Equals returns true if the lists of conflicts are the same.\\n    86\u2192func (c Conflicts) Equals(c2 Conflicts) bool {\\n    87\u2192\\tif len(c) != len(c2) {\\n    88\u2192\\t\\treturn false\\n    89\u2192\\t}\\n    90\u2192\\tfor i := range c {\\n    91\u2192\\t\\tif !c[i].Equals(c2[i]) {\\n    92\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n    93\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    94\u2192\\t}\\n    95\u2192\\treturn true\\n    96\u2192}\\n    97\u2192\\n    98\u2192// ToSet aggregates conflicts for all managers into a single Set.\\n    99\u2192func (c Conflicts) ToSet() *fieldpath.Set {\\n   100\u2192\\tset := fieldpath.NewSet()\\n   101\u2192\\tfor _, conflict := range []Conflict(c) {\\n   102\u2192\\t\\tset.Insert(conflict.Path)\\n   103\u2192\\t}\\n   104\u2192\\treturn set\\n   105\u2192}\\n   106\u2192\\n   107\u2192// ConflictsFromManagers creates a list of conflicts given Managers sets.\\n   108\u2192func ConflictsFromManagers(sets fieldpath.ManagedFields) Conflicts {\\n   109\u2192\\tconflicts := []Conflict{}\\n   110\u2192\\n   111\u2192\\tfor manager, set := range sets {\\n   112\u2192\\t\\tset.Set().Iterate(func(p fieldpath.Path) {\\n   113\u2192\\t\\t\\tconflicts = append(conflicts, Conflict{\\n   114\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tManager: manager,\\n   115\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tPath:    p.Copy(),\\n   116\u2192\\t\\t\\t})\\n   117\u2192\\t\\t})\\n   118\u2192\\t}\\n   119\u2192\\n   120\u2192\\treturn conflicts\\n   121\u2192}\\n   122\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"ef17febd-fa1f-4fc9-b40b-aceb50e20e68\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:54.205Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"ff02d625-2ce9-4f90-bb6b-222892c86cfa\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"ef17febd-fa1f-4fc9-b40b-aceb50e20e68\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01E6sfYLst6rDGWaupgPTb6T\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package internal\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n    30\u2192)\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192type structuredMergeManager struct {\\n    33\u2192\\ttypeConverter   TypeConverter\\n    34\u2192\\tobjectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor\\n    35\u2192\\tobjectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter\\n    36\u2192\\tgroupVersion    schema.GroupVersion\\n    37\u2192\\thubVersion      schema.GroupVersion\\n    38\u2192\\tupdater         merge.Updater\\n    39\u2192}\\n    40\u2192\\n    41\u2192var _ Manager = &structuredMergeManager{}\\n    42\u2192\\n    43\u2192// NewStructuredMergeManager creates a new Manager that merges apply requests\\n    44\u2192// and update managed fields for other types of requests.\\n    45\u2192func NewStructuredMergeManager(typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter, gv schema.GroupVersion, hub schema.GroupVersion, resetFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter) (Manager, error) {\\n    46\u2192\\tif typeConverter == nil {\\n    47\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"typeconverter must not be nil\\\")\\n    48\u2192\\t}\\n    49\u2192\\treturn &structuredMergeManager{\\n    50\u2192\\t\\ttypeConverter:   typeConverter,\\n    51\u2192\\t\\tobjectConverter: objectConverter,\\n    52\u2192\\t\\tobjectDefaulter: objectDefaulter,\\n    53\u2192\\t\\tgroupVersion:    gv,\\n    54\u2192\\t\\thubVersion:      hub,\\n    55\u2192\\t\\tupdater: merge.Updater{\\n    56\u2192\\t\\t\\tConverter:    newVersionConverter(typeConverter, objectConverter, hub), // This is the converter provided to SMD from k8s\\n    57\u2192\\t\\t\\tIgnoreFilter: resetFields,\\n    58\u2192\\t\\t},\\n    59\u2192\\t}, nil\\n    60\u2192}\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192// NewCRDStructuredMergeManager creates a new Manager specifically for\\n    63\u2192// CRDs. This allows for the possibility of fields which are not defined\\n    64\u2192// in models, as well as having no models defined at all.\\n    65\u2192func NewCRDStructuredMergeManager(typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter, gv schema.GroupVersion, hub schema.GroupVersion, resetFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter) (_ Manager, err error) {\\n    66\u2192\\treturn &structuredMergeManager{\\n    67\u2192\\t\\ttypeConverter:   typeConverter,\\n    68\u2192\\t\\tobjectConverter: objectConverter,\\n    69\u2192\\t\\tobjectDefaulter: objectDefaulter,\\n    70\u2192\\t\\tgroupVersion:    gv,\\n    71\u2192\\t\\thubVersion:      hub,\\n    72\u2192\\t\\tupdater: merge.Updater{\\n    73\u2192\\t\\t\\tConverter:    newCRDVersionConverter(typeConverter, objectConverter, hub),\\n    74\u2192\\t\\t\\tIgnoreFilter: resetFields,\\n    75\u2192\\t\\t},\\n    76\u2192\\t}, nil\\n    77\u2192}\\n    78\u2192\\n    79\u2192func objectGVKNN(obj runtime.Object) string {\\n    80\u2192\\tname := \\\"<unknown>\\\"\\n    81\u2192\\tnamespace := \\\"<unknown>\\\"\\n    82\u2192\\tif accessor, err := meta.Accessor(obj); err == nil {\\n    83\u2192\\t\\tname = accessor.GetName()\\n    84\u2192\\t\\tnamespace = accessor.GetNamespace()\\n    85\u2192\\t}\\n    86\u2192\\n    87\u2192\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%v/%v; %v\\\", namespace, name, obj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind())\\n    88\u2192}\\n    89\u2192\\n    90\u2192// Update implements Manager.\\n    91\u2192func (f *structuredMergeManager) Update(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string) (runtime.Object, Managed, error) {\\n    92\u2192\\tnewObjVersioned, err := f.toVersioned(newObj)\\n    93\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    94\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert new object (%v) to proper version (%v): %v\\\", objectGVKNN(newObj), f.groupVersion, err)\\n    95\u2192\\t}\\n    96\u2192\\tliveObjVersioned, err := f.toVersioned(liveObj)\\n    97\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    98\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert live object (%v) to proper version: %v\\\", objectGVKNN(liveObj), err)\\n    99\u2192\\t}\\n   100\u2192\\tnewObjTyped, err := f.typeConverter.ObjectToTyped(newObjVersioned, typed.AllowDuplicates)\\n   101\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   102\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert new object (%v) to smd typed: %v\\\", objectGVKNN(newObjVersioned), err)\\n   103\u2192\\t}\\n   104\u2192\\tliveObjTyped, err := f.typeConverter.ObjectToTyped(liveObjVersioned, typed.AllowDuplicates)\\n   105\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   106\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert live object (%v) to smd typed: %v\\\", objectGVKNN(liveObjVersioned), err)\\n   107\u2192\\t}\\n   108\u2192\\tapiVersion := fieldpath.APIVersion(f.groupVersion.String())\\n   109\u2192\\n   110\u2192\\t// TODO(apelisse) use the first return value when unions are implemented\\n   111\u2192\\t_, managedFields, err := f.updater.Update(liveObjTyped, newObjTyped, apiVersion, managed.Fields(), manager)\\n   112\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   113\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to update ManagedFields (%v): %v\\\", objectGVKNN(newObjVersioned), err)\\n   114\u2192\\t}\\n   115\u2192\\tmanaged = NewManaged(managedFields, managed.Times())\\n   116\u2192\\n   117\u2192\\treturn newObj, managed, nil\\n   118\u2192}\\n   119\u2192\\n   120\u2192// Apply implements Manager.\\n   121\u2192func (f *structuredMergeManager) Apply(liveObj, patchObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string, force bool) (runtime.Object, Managed, error) {\\n   122\u2192\\t// Check that the patch object has the same version as the live object\\n   123\u2192\\tif patchVersion := patchObj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind().GroupVersion(); patchVersion != f.groupVersion {\\n   124\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil,\\n   125\u2192\\t\\t\\terrors.NewBadRequest(\\n   126\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"Incorrect version specified in apply patch. \\\"+\\n   127\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Specified patch version: %s, expected: %s\\\",\\n   128\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpatchVersion, f.groupVersion))\\n   129\u2192\\t}\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192\\tpatchObjMeta, err := meta.Accessor(patchObj)\\n   132\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   133\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"couldn't get accessor: %v\\\", err)\\n   134\u2192\\t}\\n   135\u2192\\tif patchObjMeta.GetManagedFields() != nil {\\n   136\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errors.NewBadRequest(\\\"metadata.managedFields must be nil\\\")\\n   137\u2192\\t}\\n   138\u2192\\n   139\u2192\\tliveObjVersioned, err := f.toVersioned(liveObj)\\n   140\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   141\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert live object (%v) to proper version: %v\\\", objectGVKNN(liveObj), err)\\n   142\u2192\\t}\\n   143\u2192\\n   144\u2192\\t// Don't allow duplicates in the applied object.\\n   145\u2192\\tpatchObjTyped, err := f.typeConverter.ObjectToTyped(patchObj)\\n   146\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   147\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create typed patch object (%v): %v\\\", objectGVKNN(patchObj), err)\\n   148\u2192\\t}\\n   149\u2192\\n   150\u2192\\tliveObjTyped, err := f.typeConverter.ObjectToTyped(liveObjVersioned, typed.AllowDuplicates)\\n   151\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   152\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create typed live object (%v): %v\\\", objectGVKNN(liveObjVersioned), err)\\n   153\u2192\\t}\\n   154\u2192\\n   155\u2192\\tapiVersion := fieldpath.APIVersion(f.groupVersion.String())\\n   156\u2192\\tnewObjTyped, managedFields, err := f.updater.Apply(liveObjTyped, patchObjTyped, apiVersion, managed.Fields(), manager, force)\\n   157\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   158\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n   159\u2192\\t}\\n   160\u2192\\tmanaged = NewManaged(managedFields, managed.Times())\\n   161\u2192\\n   162\u2192\\tif newObjTyped == nil {\\n   163\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, managed, nil\\n   164\u2192\\t}\\n   165\u2192\\n   166\u2192\\tnewObj, err := f.typeConverter.TypedToObject(newObjTyped)\\n   167\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   168\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert new typed object (%v) to object: %v\\\", objectGVKNN(patchObj), err)\\n   169\u2192\\t}\\n   170\u2192\\n   171\u2192\\tnewObjVersioned, err := f.toVersioned(newObj)\\n   172\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   173\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert new object (%v) to proper version: %v\\\", objectGVKNN(patchObj), err)\\n   174\u2192\\t}\\n   175\u2192\\tf.objectDefaulter.Default(newObjVersioned)\\n   176\u2192\\n   177\u2192\\tnewObjUnversioned, err := f.toUnversioned(newObjVersioned)\\n   178\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   179\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert to unversioned (%v): %v\\\", objectGVKNN(patchObj), err)\\n   180\u2192\\t}\\n   181\u2192\\treturn newObjUnversioned, managed, nil\\n   182\u2192}\\n   183\u2192\\n   184\u2192func (f *structuredMergeManager) toVersioned(obj runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   185\u2192\\treturn f.objectConverter.ConvertToVersion(obj, f.groupVersion)\\n   186\u2192}\\n   187\u2192\\n   188\u2192func (f *structuredMergeManager) toUnversioned(obj runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n   189\u2192\\treturn f.objectConverter.ConvertToVersion(obj, f.hubVersion)\\n   190\u2192}\\n   191\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"44b5bef2-e101-4d32-823e-ba4cbb75cebf\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:57.376Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"873278d4-c3ca-46bd-bc7c-fd401b5ebfb0\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"44b5bef2-e101-4d32-823e-ba4cbb75cebf\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01E6sfYLst6rDGWaupgPTb6T\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01H8DEbaqXw6WW8x4Q6fb3hs\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/fieldmanager/admission.go\",\"limit\":150},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":3897,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":65468,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":3897},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG56vbrh9w6zkXFrKzQh\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"b592a1d4-430c-4632-855d-10a6a58c2d32\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:57.824Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"b592a1d4-430c-4632-855d-10a6a58c2d32\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01H8DEbaqXw6WW8x4Q6fb3hs\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01H8DEbaqXw6WW8x4Q6fb3hs\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:57.827Z\",\"uuid\":\"a29c4963-de7a-4827-b2     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2021 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package fieldmanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/warning\\\"\\n    27\u2192)\\n    28\u2192\\n    29\u2192// InvalidManagedFieldsAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat is the warning that a client receives\\n    30\u2192// when a create/update/patch request results in invalid managedFields after going through the admission chain.\\n    31\u2192const InvalidManagedFieldsAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat = \\\".metadata.managedFields was in an invalid state after admission; this could be caused by an outdated mutating admission controller; please fix your requests: %v\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192// NewManagedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController validates the managedFields after calling\\n    34\u2192// the provided admission and resets them to their original state if they got changed to an invalid value\\n    35\u2192func NewManagedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController(wrap admission.Interface) admission.Interface {\\n    36\u2192\\tif wrap == nil {\\n    37\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n    38\u2192\\t}\\n    39\u2192\\treturn &managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController{wrap: wrap}\\n    40\u2192}\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192type managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController struct {\\n    43\u2192\\twrap admission.Interface\\n    44\u2192}\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192var _ admission.Interface = &managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController{}\\n    47\u2192var _ admission.MutationInterface = &managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController{}\\n    48\u2192var _ admission.ValidationInterface = &managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController{}\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192// Handles calls the wrapped admission.Interface if applicable\\n    51\u2192func (admit *managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController) Handles(operation admission.Operation) bool {\\n    52\u2192\\treturn admit.wrap.Handles(operation)\\n    53\u2192}\\n    54\u2192\\n    55\u2192// Admit calls the wrapped admission.Interface if applicable and resets the managedFields to their state before admission if they\\n    56\u2192// got modified in an invalid way\\n    57\u2192func (admit *managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController) Admit(ctx context.Context, a admission.Attributes, o admission.ObjectInterfaces) (err error) {\\n    58\u2192\\tmutationInterface, isMutationInterface := admit.wrap.(admission.MutationInterface)\\n    59\u2192\\tif !isMutationInterface {\\n    60\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n    61\u2192\\t}\\n    62\u2192\\tobjectMeta, err := meta.Accessor(a.GetObject())\\n    63\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    64\u2192\\t\\t// the object we are dealing with doesn't have object metadata defined\\n    65\u2192\\t\\t// in that case we don't have to keep track of the managedField\\n    66\u2192\\t\\t// just call the wrapped admission\\n    67\u2192\\t\\treturn mutationInterface.Admit(ctx, a, o)\\n    68\u2192\\t}\\n    69\u2192\\tmanagedFieldsBeforeAdmission := objectMeta.GetManagedFields()\\n    70\u2192\\tif err := mutationInterface.Admit(ctx, a, o); err != nil {\\n    71\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n    72\u2192\\t}\\n    73\u2192\\tmanagedFieldsAfterAdmission := objectMeta.GetManagedFields()\\n    74\u2192\\tif err := managedfields.ValidateManagedFields(managedFieldsAfterAdmission); err != nil {\\n    75\u2192\\t\\tobjectMeta.SetManagedFields(managedFieldsBeforeAdmission)\\n    76\u2192\\t\\twarning.AddWarning(ctx, \\\"\\\",\\n    77\u2192\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(InvalidManagedFieldsAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat,\\n    78\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\terr.Error()),\\n    79\u2192\\t\\t)\\n    80\u2192\\t}\\n    81\u2192\\treturn nil\\n    82\u2192}\\n    83\u2192\\n    84\u2192// Validate calls the wrapped admission.Interface if aplicable\\n    85\u2192func (admit *managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController) Validate(ctx context.Context, a admission.Attributes, o admission.ObjectInterfaces) (err error) {\\n    86\u2192\\tif validationInterface, isValidationInterface := admit.wrap.(admission.ValidationInterface); isValidationInterface {\\n    87\u2192\\t\\treturn validationInterface.Validate(ctx, a, o)\\n    88\u2192\\t}\\n    89\u2192\\treturn nil\\n    90\u2192}\\n    91\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"32b435b6-7aac-4d9c-a387-f3e37e0d8997\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:16:57.827Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"b592a1d4-430c-4632-855d-10a6a58c2d32\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"32b435b6-7aac-4d9c-a387-f3e37e0d8997\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01E6sfYLst6rDGWaupgPTb6T\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01CogT6BKvo7gFgSUqsoC6ho\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"grep 16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package internal\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"encoding/json\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    28\u2192)\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192// ManagedInterface groups a fieldpath.ManagedFields together with the timestamps associated with each operation.\\n    31\u2192type ManagedInterface interface {\\n    32\u2192\\t// Fields gets the fieldpath.ManagedFields.\\n    33\u2192\\tFields() fieldpath.ManagedFields\\n    34\u2192\\n    35\u2192\\t// Times gets the timestamps associated with each operation.\\n    36\u2192\\tTimes() map[string]*metav1.Time\\n    37\u2192}\\n    38\u2192\\n    39\u2192type managedStruct struct {\\n    40\u2192\\tfields fieldpath.ManagedFields\\n    41\u2192\\ttimes  map[string]*metav1.Time\\n    42\u2192}\\n    43\u2192\\n    44\u2192var _ ManagedInterface = &managedStruct{}\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192// Fields implements ManagedInterface.\\n    47\u2192func (m *managedStruct) Fields() fieldpath.ManagedFields {\\n    48\u2192\\treturn m.fields\\n    49\u2192}\\n    50\u2192\\n    51\u2192// Times implements ManagedInterface.\\n    52\u2192func (m *managedStruct) Times() map[string]*metav1.Time {\\n    53\u2192\\treturn m.times\\n    54\u2192}\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192// NewEmptyManaged creates an empty ManagedInterface.\\n    57\u2192func NewEmptyManaged() ManagedInterface {\\n    58\u2192\\treturn NewManaged(fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, map[string]*metav1.Time{})\\n    59\u2192}\\n    60\u2192\\n    61\u2192// NewManaged creates a ManagedInterface from a fieldpath.ManagedFields and the timestamps associated with each operation.\\n    62\u2192func NewManaged(f fieldpath.ManagedFields, t map[string]*metav1.Time) ManagedInterface {\\n    63\u2192\\treturn &managedStruct{\\n    64\u2192\\t\\tfields: f,\\n    65\u2192\\t\\ttimes:  t,\\n    66\u2192\\t}\\n    67\u2192}\\n    68\u2192\\n    69\u2192// RemoveObjectManagedFields removes the ManagedFields from the object\\n    70\u2192// before we merge so that it doesn't appear in the ManagedFields\\n    71\u2192// recursively.\\n    72\u2192func RemoveObjectManagedFields(obj runtime.Object) {\\n    73\u2192\\taccessor, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\\n    74\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    75\u2192\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"couldn't get accessor: %v\\\", err))\\n    76\u2192\\t}\\n    77\u2192\\taccessor.SetManagedFields(nil)\\n    78\u2192}\\n    79\u2192\\n    80\u2192// EncodeObjectManagedFields converts and stores the fieldpathManagedFields into the objects ManagedFields\\n    81\u2192func EncodeObjectManagedFields(obj runtime.Object, managed ManagedInterface) error {\\n    82\u2192\\taccessor, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\\n    83\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    84\u2192\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"couldn't get accessor: %v\\\", err))\\n    85\u2192\\t}\\n    86\u2192\\n    87\u2192\\tencodedManagedFields, err := encodeManagedFields(managed)\\n    88\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    89\u2192\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert back managed fields to API: %v\\\", err)\\n    90\u2192\\t}\\n    91\u2192\\taccessor.SetManagedFields(encodedManagedFields)\\n    92\u2192\\n    93\u2192\\treturn nil\\n    94\u2192}\\n    95\u2192\\n    96\u2192// DecodeManagedFields converts ManagedFields from the wire format (api format)\\n    97\u2192// to the format used by sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff\\n    98\u2192func DecodeManagedFields(encodedManagedFields []metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry) (ManagedInterface, error) {\\n    99\u2192\\tmanaged := managedStruct{}\\n   100\u2192\\tmanaged.fields = make(fieldpath.ManagedFields, len(encodedManagedFields))\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.  15\u2192\\n    16\u2192import (\\n    17\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/value\\\"\\n    22\u2192)\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192// Converter is an interface to the conversion logic. The converter\\n    25\u2192// needs to be able to convert objects from one version to another.\\n    26\u2192type Converter interface {\\n    27\u2192\\tConvert(object *typed.TypedValue, version fieldpath.APIVersion) (*typed.TypedValue, error)\\n    28\u2192\\tIsMissingVersionError(error) bool\\n    29\u2192}\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192// UpdateBuilder allows you to create a new Updater by exposing all of\\n    32\u2192// the options and setting them once.\\n    33\u2192type UpdaterBuilder struct {\\n    34\u2192\\tConverter    Converter\\n    35\u2192\\tIgnoreFilter map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter\\n    36\u2192\\n    37\u2192\\t// IgnoredFields provides a set of fields to ignore for each\\n    38\u2192\\tIgnoredFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set\\n    39\u2192\\n    40\u2192\\t// Stop comparing the new object with old object after applying.\\n    41\u2192\\t// This was initially used to avoid spurious etcd update, but\\n    42\u2192\\t// since that's vastly inefficient, we've come-up with a better\\n    43\u2192\\t// way of doing that. Create this flag to stop it.\\n    44\u2192\\t// Comparing has become more expensive too now that we're not using\\n    45\u2192\\t// `Compare` but `value.Equals` so this gives an option to avoid it.\\n    46\u2192\\tReturnInputOnNoop bool\\n    47\u2192}\\n    48\u2192\\n    49\u2192func (u *UpdaterBuilder) BuildUpdater() *Updater {\\n    50\u2192\\treturn &Updater{\\n    51\u2192\\t\\tConverter:         u.Converter,\\n    52\u2192\\t\\tIgnoreFilter:      u.IgnoreFilter,\\n    53\u2192\\t\\tIgnoredFields:     u.IgnoredFields,\\n    54\u2192\\t\\treturnInputOnNoop: u.ReturnInputOnNoop,\\n    55\u2192\\t}\\n    56\u2192}\\n    57\u2192\\n    58\u2192// Updater is the object used to compute updated FieldSets and also\\n    59\u2192// merge the object on Apply.\\n    60\u2192type Updater struct {\\n    61\u2192\\t// Deprecated: This will eventually become private.\\n    62\u2192\\tConverter Converter\\n    63\u2192\\n    64\u2192\\t// Deprecated: This will eventually become private.\\n    65\u2192\\tIgnoredFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192\\t// Deprecated: This will eventually become private.\\n    68\u2192\\tIgnoreFilter map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192\\treturnInputOnNoop bool\\n    71\u2192}\\n    72\u2192\\n    73\u2192func (s *Updater) update(oldObject, newObject *typed.TypedValue, version fieldpath.APIVersion, managers fieldpath.ManagedFields, workflow string, force bool) (fieldpath.ManagedFields, *typed.Comparison, error) {\\n    74\u2192\\tconflicts := fieldpath.ManagedFields{}\\n    75\u2192\\tremoved := fieldpath.ManagedFields{}\\n    76\u2192\\tcompare, err := oldObject.Compare(newObject)\\n    77\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    78\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to compare objects: %v\\\", err)\\n    79\u2192\\t}\\n    80\u2192\\n    81\u2192\\tvar versions map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*typed.Comparison\\n    82\u2192\\n    83\u2192\\tif s.IgnoredFields != nil && s.IgnoreFilter != nil {\\n    84\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"IgnoreFilter and IgnoreFilter may not both be set\\\")\\n    85\u2192\\t}\\n    86\u2192\\tif s.IgnoredFields != nil {\\n    87\u2192\\t\\tversions = map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*typed.Comparison{\\n    88\u2192\\t\\t\\tversion: compare.ExcludeFields(s.IgnoredFields[version]),\\n    89\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    90\u2192\\t} else {\\n    91\u2192\\t\\tversions = map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*typed.Comparison{\\n    92\u2192\\t\\t\\tversion: compare.FilterFields(s.IgnoreFilter[version]),\\n    93\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    94\u2192\\t}\\n    95\u2192\\n    96\u2192\\tfor manager, managerSet := range managers {\\n    97\u2192\\t\\tif manager == workflow {\\n    98\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n    99\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   100\u2192\\t\\tcompare, ok := versions[managerSet.APIVersion()]\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"913b63e6-cce4-46c1-8218-0b601f5670af\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:01.952Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"2352c3e1-bf23-4f0f-b164-48f6b60c9609\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"913b63e6-cce4-46c1-8218-0b601f5670af\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01JSSEzqQ8NPqW8HtFxSDkMh\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01Tz4Hpybd92q7v4nhHdJtmT\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"grep (s *Updater) Apply(liveObject, configObject *typed.TypedValue, version fieldpath.APIVersion, managers fieldpath.ManagedFields, manager string, force bool) (*typed.TypedValue, fieldpath.ManagedFields, error) {\\n\\tvar err error\\n\\tmanagers, err = s.reconcileManagedFieldsWithSchemaChanges(liveObject, managers)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, err\\n\\t}\\n\\tnewObject, err := liveObject.Merge(configObject)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to merge config: %v\\\", err)\\n\\t}\\n\\tlastSet := managers[manager]\\n\\tset, err := configObject.ToFieldSet()\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to get field set: %v\\\", err)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tif s.IgnoredFields != nil && s.IgnoreFilter != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"IgnoreFilter and IgnoreFilter may not both be set\\\")\\n\\t}\\n\\tvar ignoreFilter fieldpath.Filter\\n\\tif s.IgnoredFields != nil {\\n\\t\\tignoreFilter = fieldpath.NewExcludeSetFilter(s.IgnoredFields[version])\\n\\t} else {\\n\\t\\tignoreFilter = s.IgnoreFilter[version]\\n\\t}\\n\\tif ignoreFilter != nil {\\n\\t\\tset = ignoreFilter.Filter(set)\\n\\t}\\n\\tmanagers[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(set, version, true)\\n\\tnewObject, err = s.prune(newObject, managers, manager, lastSet)\\n\\tif err != nil {\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"23bc641d-361d-4b5b-a04c-035ad5ec72a2\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:05.382Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"b5655bfe-1f35-4255-84e8-3167fa3f6588\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"23bc641d-361d-4b5b-a04c-035ad5ec72a2\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01QtgDZw1ChSWzB8dvAqjYVU\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_016XuyaXWJMr9YcnXug8e3XB\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"grep (s *Updater) Update(liveObject, newObject *typed.TypedValue, version fieldpath.APIVersion, managers fieldpath.ManagedFields, manager string) (*typed.TypedValue, fieldpath.ManagedFields, error) {\\n\\tvar err error\\n\\tmanagers, err = s.reconcileManagedFieldsWithSchemaChanges(liveObject, managers)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, err\\n\\t}\\n\\tmanagers, compare, err := s.update(liveObject, newObject, version, managers, manager, true)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, err\\n\\t}\\n\\tif _, ok := managers[manager]; !ok {\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"d790dd98-b3a6-4521-a93f-cc4e3f93c1c3\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:05.703Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"7e774bcc-4846-4213-8c9e-db92b2f82162\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d790dd98-b3a6-4521-a93f-cc4e3f93c1c3\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01QtgDZw1ChSWzB8dvAqjYVU\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01Y55QyXYHf2xxmbDps9LvJr\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/lastappliedmanager.go\",\"limit\":100},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":4186,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":75779,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":4186},\"output_tokens\":3,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG57abbx7iBJruYerdMK\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"6b85aa40-d459-46d0-9f34-76f390e7a014\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:06.222Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"6b85aa40-d459-46d0-9f34-76f390e7a014\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01Y55QyXYHf2xxmbDps9LvJr\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01Y55QyXYHf2xxmbDps9LvJr\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:06.225Z\",\"uuid\":\"17106f01-1d7f-4efd-b149-e1     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2020 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this
