16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package internal\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"encoding/json\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge\\\"\\n    29\u2192)\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192type lastAppliedManager struct {\\n    32\u2192\\tfieldManager    Manager\\n    33\u2192\\ttypeConverter   TypeConverter\\n    34\u2192\\tobjectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor\\n    35\u2192\\tgroupVersion    schema.GroupVersion\\n    36\u2192}\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192var _ Manager = &lastAppliedManager{}\\n    39\u2192\\n    40\u2192// NewLastAppliedManager converts the client-side apply annotation to\\n    41\u2192// server-side apply managed fields\\n    42\u2192func NewLastAppliedManager(fieldManager Manager, typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, groupVersion schema.GroupVersion) Manager {\\n    43\u2192\\treturn &lastAppliedManager{\\n    44\u2192\\t\\tfieldManager:    fieldManager,\\n    45\u2192\\t\\ttypeConverter:   typeConverter,\\n    46\u2192\\t\\tobjectConverter: objectConverter,\\n    47\u2192\\t\\tgroupVersion:    groupVersion,\\n    48\u2192\\t}\\n    49\u2192}\\n    50\u2192\\n    51\u2192// Update implements Manager.\\n    52\u2192func (f *lastAppliedManager) Update(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string) (runtime.Object, Managed, error) {\\n    53\u2192\\treturn f.fieldManager.Update(liveObj, newObj, managed, manager)\\n    54\u2192}\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192// Apply will consider the last-applied annotation\\n    57\u2192// for upgrading an object managed by client-side apply to server-side apply\\n    58\u2192// without conflicts.\\n    59\u2192func (f *lastAppliedManager) Apply(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string, force bool) (runtime.Object, Managed, error) {\\n    60\u2192\\tnewLiveObj, newManaged, newErr := f.fieldManager.Apply(liveObj, newObj, managed, manager, force)\\n    61\u2192\\t// Upgrade the client-side apply annotation only from kubectl server-side-apply.\\n    62\u2192\\t// To opt-out of this behavior, users may specify a different field manager.\\n    63\u2192\\tif manager != \\\"kubectl\\\" {\\n    64\u2192\\t\\treturn newLiveObj, newManaged, newErr\\n    65\u2192\\t}\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192\\t// Check if we have conflicts\\n    68\u2192\\tif newErr == nil {\\n    69\u2192\\t\\treturn newLiveObj, newManaged, newErr\\n    70\u2192\\t}\\n    71\u2192\\tconflicts, ok := newErr.(merge.Conflicts)\\n    72\u2192\\tif !ok {\\n    73\u2192\\t\\treturn newLiveObj, newManaged, newErr\\n    74\u2192\\t}\\n    75\u2192\\tconflictSet := conflictsToSet(conflicts)\\n    76\u2192\\n    77\u2192\\t// Check if conflicts are allowed due to client-side apply,\\n    78\u2192\\t// and if so, then force apply\\n    79\u2192\\tallowedConflictSet, err := f.allowedConflictsFromLastApplied(liveObj)\\n    80\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    81\u2192\\t\\treturn newLiveObj, newManaged, newErr\\n    82\u2192\\t}\\n    83\u2192\\tif !conflictSet.Difference(allowedConflictSet).Empty() {\\n    84\u2192\\t\\tnewConflicts := conflictsDifference(conflicts, allowedConflictSet)\\n    85\u2192\\t\\treturn newLiveObj, newManaged, newConflicts\\n    86\u2192\\t}\\n    87\u2192\\n    88\u2192\\treturn f.fieldManager.Apply(liveObj, newObj, managed, manager, true)\\n    89\u2192}\\n    90\u2192\\n    91\u2192func (f *lastAppliedManager) allowedConflictsFromLastApplied(liveObj runtime.Object) (*fieldpath.Set, error) {\\n    92\u2192\\tvar accessor, err = meta.Accessor(liveObj)\\n    93\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    94\u2192\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"couldn't get accessor: %v\\\", err))\\n    95\u2192\\t}\\n    96\u2192\\n    97\u2192\\t// If there is no client-side apply annotation, then there is nothing to do\\n    98\u2192\\tvar annotations = accessor.GetAnnotations()\\n    99\u2192\\tif annotations == nil {\\n   100\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"no last applied annotation\\\")\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"504170a7-28c8-425a-a02b-fe2817f1e111\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:06.225Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"6b85aa40-d459-46d0-9f34-76f390e7a014\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"504170a7-28c8-425a-a02b-fe2817f1e111\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01Dybk8G5yXoAPzpZ5zLKruT\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Excellent! behavior:\"}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":2503,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":79965,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":2503},\"output_tokens\":4,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG57oymeYzKgwCsTGr6B\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"0ed2bd1f-99c0-49ff-9cf6-ac27469a9992\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:08.193Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"0ed2bd1f-99c0-49ff-9cf6-ac27469a9992\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01Dybk8G5yXoAPzpZ5zLKruT\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01WzbXnR21oFgPE8dVycUuCF\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"grep -B 5 -A 40 \\\"func (s \\\\*Updater) prune\\\" /workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge/update.go | head -60\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":2503,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":79965,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":2503},\"output_tokens\":4,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG57oymeYzKgwCsTGr6B\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"93e5ac43-7abb-4406-9a06-1f972c4939fe\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:08.935Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"93e5ac43-7abb-4406-9a06-1f972c4939fe\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01WzbXnR21oFgPE8dVycUuCF\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"// prune will remove a field, list or map item, iff:\\n// * applyingManager applied it last time\\n// * applyingManager didn't apply it this time\\n// * no other applier claims to manage it\\nfunc (s *Updater) prune(merged *typed.TypedValue, managers fieldpath.ManagedFields, applyingManager string, lastSet fieldpath.VersionedSet) (*typed.TypedValue, error) {\\n\\tif lastSet == nil || lastSet.Set().Empty() {\\n\\t\\treturn merged, nil\\n\\t}\\n\\tversion := lastSet.APIVersion()\\n\\tconvertedMerged, err := s.Converter.Convert(merged, version)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\tif s.Converter.IsMissingVersionError(err) {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn merged, nil\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert merged object to last applied version: %v\\\", err)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tsc, tr := convertedMerged.Schema(), convertedMerged.TypeRef()\\n\\tpruned := convertedMerged.RemoveItems(lastSet.Set().EnsureNamedFieldsAreMembers(sc, tr))\\n\\tpruned, err = s.addBackOwnedItems(convertedMerged, pruned, version, managers, applyingManager)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed add back owned items: %v\\\", err)\\n\\t}\\n\\tpruned, err = s.addBackDanglingItems(convertedMerged, pruned, lastSet)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed add back dangling items: %v\\\", err)\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn s.Converter.Convert(pruned, managers[applyingManager].APIVersion())\\n}\\n\\n// addBackOwnedItems adds back any fields, list and map items that were removed by prune,\\n// but other appliers or updaters (or the current applier's new config) claim to own.\\nfunc (s *Updater) addBackOwnedItems(merged, pruned *typed.TypedValue, prunedVersion fieldpath.APIVersion, managedFields fieldpath.ManagedFields, applyingManager string) (*typed.TypedValue, error) {\\n\\tvar err error\\n\\tmanagedAtVersion := map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set{}\\n\\tfor _, managerSet := range managedFields {\\n\\t\\tif _, ok := managedAtVersion[managerSet.APIVersion()]; !ok {\\n\\t\\t\\tmanagedAtVersion[managerSet.APIVersion()] = fieldpath.NewSet()\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tmanagedAtVersion[managerSet.APIVersion()] = managedAtVersion[managerSet.APIVersion()].Union(managerSet.Set())\\n\\t}\\n\\t// Add back owned items at pruned version first to avoid conversion failure\\n\\t// caused by pruned fields which are required for conversion.\\n\\tif managed, ok := managedAtVersion[prunedVersion]; ok {\\n\\t\\tmerged, pruned, err = s.addBackOwnedItemsForVersion(merged, pruned, prunedVersion, managed)\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"091e62d4-4ef0-453e-bdc7-4a8976786e6c\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:08.950Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"93e5ac43-7abb-4406-9a06-1f972c4939fe\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"091e62d4-4ef0-453e-bdc7-4a8976786e6c\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01Dybk8G5yXoAPzpZ5zLKruT\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01EDJgG5KPWwh5yHWq6ex8rY\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"grep -B 5 -A 30 \\\"force bool\\\" /workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge/update.go | grep -A 30 \\\"if force\\\"\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":2503,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":79965,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":2503},\"output_tokens\":4,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG57oymeYzKgwCsTGr6B\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"cc4537d7-8951-450f-9c72-03bafe083725\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:09.340Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"cc4537d7-8951-450f-9c72-03bafe083725\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01EDJgG5KPWwh5yHWq6ex8rY\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"187d737d-d247-4487-b09c-4dc9a2a4c651\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:09.355Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"cc4537d7-8951-450f-9c72-03bafe083725\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"187d737d-d247-4487-b09c-4dc9a2a4c651\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01Dybk8G5yXoAPzpZ5zLKruT\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01MXt2Sw99cwWbmzo3xZwFUp\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/stripmeta.go\",\"limit\":150},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":2503,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":799 CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package internal\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    24\u2192)\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192type stripMetaManager struct {\\n    27\u2192\\tfieldManager Manager\\n    28\u2192\\n    29\u2192\\t// stripSet is the list of fields that should never be part of a mangedFields.\\n    30\u2192\\tstripSet *fieldpath.Set\\n    31\u2192}\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192var _ Manager = &stripMetaManager{}\\n    34\u2192\\n    35\u2192// NewStripMetaManager creates a new Manager that strips metadata and typemeta fields from the manager's fieldset.\\n    36\u2192func NewStripMetaManager(fieldManager Manager) Manager {\\n    37\u2192\\treturn &stripMetaManager{\\n    38\u2192\\t\\tfieldManager: fieldManager,\\n    39\u2192\\t\\tstripSet: fieldpath.NewSet(\\n    40\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldpath.MakePathOrDie(\\\"apiVersion\\\"),\\n    41\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldpath.MakePathOrDie(\\\"kind\\\"),\\n    42\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldpath.MakePathOrDie(\\\"metadata\\\"),\\n    43\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldpath.MakePathOrDie(\\\"metadata\\\", \\\"name\\\"),\\n    44\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldpath.MakePathOrDie(\\\"metadata\\\", \\\"namespace\\\"),\\n    45\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldpath.MakePathOrDie(\\\"metadata\\\", \\\"creationTimestamp\\\"),\\n    46\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldpath.MakePathOrDie(\\\"metadata\\\", \\\"selfLink\\\"),\\n    47\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldpath.MakePathOrDie(\\\"metadata\\\", \\\"uid\\\"),\\n    48\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldpath.MakePathOrDie(\\\"metadata\\\", \\\"clusterName\\\"),\\n    49\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldpath.MakePathOrDie(\\\"metadata\\\", \\\"generation\\\"),\\n    50\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldpath.MakePathOrDie(\\\"metadata\\\", \\\"managedFields\\\"),\\n    51\u2192\\t\\t\\tfieldpath.MakePathOrDie(\\\"metadata\\\", \\\"resourceVersion\\\"),\\n    52\u2192\\t\\t),\\n    53\u2192\\t}\\n    54\u2192}\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192// Update implements Manager.\\n    57\u2192func (f *stripMetaManager) Update(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string) (runtime.Object, Managed, error) {\\n    58\u2192\\tnewObj, managed, err := f.fieldManager.Update(liveObj, newObj, managed, manager)\\n    59\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    60\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n    61\u2192\\t}\\n    62\u2192\\tf.stripFields(managed.Fields(), manager)\\n    63\u2192\\treturn newObj, managed, nil\\n    64\u2192}\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192// Apply implements Manager.\\n    67\u2192func (f *stripMetaManager) Apply(liveObj, appliedObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string, force bool) (runtime.Object, Managed, error) {\\n    68\u2192\\tnewObj, managed, err := f.fieldManager.Apply(liveObj, appliedObj, managed, manager, force)\\n    69\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    70\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n    71\u2192\\t}\\n    72\u2192\\tf.stripFields(managed.Fields(), manager)\\n    73\u2192\\treturn newObj, managed, nil\\n    74\u2192}\\n    75\u2192\\n    76\u2192// stripFields removes a predefined set of paths found in typed from managed\\n    77\u2192func (f *stripMetaManager) stripFields(managed fieldpath.ManagedFields, manager string) {\\n    78\u2192\\tvs, ok := managed[manager]\\n    79\u2192\\tif ok {\\n    80\u2192\\t\\tif vs == nil {\\n    81\u2192\\t\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Found unexpected nil manager which should never happen: %s\\\", manager))\\n    82\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    83\u2192\\t\\tnewSet := vs.Set().Difference(f.stripSet)\\n    84\u2192\\t\\tif newSet.Empty() {\\n    85\u2192\\t\\t\\tdelete(managed, manager)\\n    86\u2192\\t\\t} else {\\n    87\u2192\\t\\t\\tmanaged[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(newSet, vs.APIVersion(), vs.Applied())\\n    88\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    89\u2192\\t}\\n    90\u2192}\\n    91\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"1634c48d-4117-4535-b4cf-2cae19e95689\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:09.765Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"16462406-8839-4e06-9418-86e380a83380\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"1634c48d-4117-4535-b4cf-2cae19e95689\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_011J613GGkrxcNF5sySGrta3\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Perfect! (s *Updater) update(oldObject, newObject *typed.TypedValue, version fieldpath.APIVersion, managers fieldpath.ManagedFields, workflow string, force bool) (fieldpath.ManagedFields, *typed.Comparison, error) {\\n\\tconflicts := fieldpath.ManagedFields{}\\n\\tremoved := fieldpath.ManagedFields{}\\n\\tcompare, err := oldObject.Compare(newObject)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to compare objects: %v\\\", err)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tvar versions map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*typed.Comparison\\n\\n\\tif s.IgnoredFields != nil && s.IgnoreFilter != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"IgnoreFilter and IgnoreFilter may not both be set\\\")\\n\\t}\\n\\tif s.IgnoredFields != nil {\\n\\t\\tversions = map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*typed.Comparison{\\n\\t\\t\\tversion: compare.ExcludeFields(s.IgnoredFields[version]),\\n--\\n\\t\\tif !compare.Removed.Empty() {\\n\\t\\t\\tremoved[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(compare.Removed, managerSet.APIVersion(), false)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tif !force && len(conflicts) != 0 {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, nil, ConflictsFromManagers(conflicts)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tfor manager, conflictSet := range conflicts {\\n\\t\\tmanagers[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(managers[manager].Set().Difference(conflictSet.Set()), managers[manager].APIVersion(), managers[manager].Applied())\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tfor manager, removedSet := range removed {\\n\\t\\tmanagers[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(managers[manager].Set().Difference(removedSet.Set()), managers[manager].APIVersion(), managers[manager].Applied())\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tfor manager := range managers {\\n\\t\\tif managers[manager].Set().Empty() {\\n\\t\\t\\tdelete(managers, manager)\\n\\t\\t}\\n--\\n}\\n\\n// Apply should be called when Apply is run, given the current object as\\n// well as the configuration that is applied. This will merge the object\\n// and return it.\\nfunc (s *Updater) Apply(liveObject, configObject *typed.TypedValue, version fieldpath.APIVersion, managers fieldpath.ManagedFields, manager string, force bool) (*typed.TypedValue, fieldpath.ManagedFields, error) {\\n\\tvar err error\\n\\tmanagers, err = s.reconcileManagedFieldsWithSchemaChanges(liveObject, managers)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, err\\n\\t}\\n\\tnewObject, err := liveObject.Merge(configObject)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to merge config: %v\\\", err)\\n\\t}\\n\\tlastSet := managers[manager]\\n\\tset, err := configObject.ToFieldSet()\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to get field set: %v\\\", err)\\n\\t}\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"208c522c-f4d8-4c79-9b65-50675e0befe2\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:14.705Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"14953e6b-9162-4b7a-b047-db35a111b3c1\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"208c522c-f4d8-4c79-9b65-50675e0befe2\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_012aVtJg1MSpaacG3brB71eq\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01NgzjDNTAQLFYVnU1NonPcs\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge/update.go\",\"limit\":250},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":424,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":84937,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":424},\"output_tokens\":3,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG58Hx1uzdB46VfEuChn\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"2291876e-c0ba-49c6-b736-fe9f8265405a\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:15.115Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"2291876e-c0ba-49c6-b736-fe9f8265405a\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01NgzjDNTAQLFYVnU1NonPcs\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01NgzjDNTAQLFYVnU1NonPcs\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:15.118Z\",\"uuid\":\"1d7e0a20-f7a9-42f4-ac59-8d5eb5cc4cee\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"2291876e-c0ba-49c6-b736-fe9f8265405a\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01NgzjDNTAQLFYVnU1NonPcs\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2018 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     4\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     5\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     6\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     7\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n     8\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n     9\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    10\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    11\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    12\u2192*/\\n    13\u2192\\n    14\u2192package merge\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192import (\\n    17\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/typed\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/value\\\"\\n    22\u2192)\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192// Converter is an interface to the conversion logic. The converter\\n    25\u2192// needs to be able to convert objects from one version to another.\\n    26\u2192type Converter interface {\\n    27\u2192\\tConvert(object *typed.TypedValue, version fieldpath.APIVersion) (*typed.TypedValue, error)\\n    28\u2192\\tIsMissingVersionError(error) bool\\n    29\u2192}\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192// UpdateBuilder allows you to create a new Updater by exposing all of\\n    32\u2192// the options and setting them once.\\n    33\u2192type UpdaterBuilder struct {\\n    34\u2192\\tConverter    Converter\\n    35\u2192\\tIgnoreFilter map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter\\n    36\u2192\\n    37\u2192\\t// IgnoredFields provides a set of fields to ignore for each\\n    38\u2192\\tIgnoredFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set\\n    39\u2192\\n    40\u2192\\t// Stop comparing the new object with old object after applying.\\n    41\u2192\\t// This was initially used to avoid spurious etcd update, but\\n    42\u2192\\t// since that's vastly inefficient, we've come-up with a better\\n    43\u2192\\t// way of doing that. Create this flag to stop it.\\n    44\u2192\\t// Comparing has become more expensive too now that we're not using\\n    45\u2192\\t// `Compare` but `value.Equals` so this gives an option to avoid it.\\n    46\u2192\\tReturnInputOnNoop bool\\n    47\u2192}\\n    48\u2192\\n    49\u2192func (u *UpdaterBuilder) BuildUpdater() *Updater {\\n    50\u2192\\treturn &Updater{\\n    51\u2192\\t\\tConverter:         u.Converter,\\n    52\u2192\\t\\tIgnoreFilter:      u.IgnoreFilter,\\n    53\u2192\\t\\tIgnoredFields:     u.IgnoredFields,\\n    54\u2192\\t\\treturnInputOnNoop: u.ReturnInputOnNoop,\\n    55\u2192\\t}\\n    56\u2192}\\n    57\u2192\\n    58\u2192// Updater is the object used to compute updated FieldSets and also\\n    59\u2192// merge the object on Apply.\\n    60\u2192type Updater struct {\\n    61\u2192\\t// Deprecated: This will eventually become private.\\n    62\u2192\\tConverter Converter\\n    63\u2192\\n    64\u2192\\t// Deprecated: This will eventually become private.\\n    65\u2192\\tIgnoredFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192\\t// Deprecated: This will eventually become private.\\n    68\u2192\\tIgnoreFilter map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192\\treturnInputOnNoop bool\\n    71\u2192}\\n    72\u2192\\n    73\u2192func (s *Updater) update(oldObject, newObject *typed.TypedValue, version fieldpath.APIVersion, managers fieldpath.ManagedFields, workflow string, force bool) (fieldpath.ManagedFields, *typed.Comparison, error) {\\n    74\u2192\\tconflicts := fieldpath.ManagedFields{}\\n    75\u2192\\tremoved := fieldpath.ManagedFields{}\\n    76\u2192\\tcompare, err := oldObject.Compare(newObject)\\n    77\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    78\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to compare objects: %v\\\", err)\\n    79\u2192\\t}\\n    80\u2192\\n    81\u2192\\tvar versions map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*typed.Comparison\\n    82\u2192\\n    83\u2192\\tif s.IgnoredFields != nil && s.IgnoreFilter != nil {\\n    84\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"IgnoreFilter and IgnoreFilter may not both be set\\\")\\n    85\u2192\\t}\\n    86\u2192\\tif s.IgnoredFields != nil {\\n    87\u2192\\t\\tversions = map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*typed.Comparison{\\n    88\u2192\\t\\t\\tversion: compare.ExcludeFields(s.IgnoredFields[version]),\\n    89\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    90\u2192\\t} else {\\n    91\u2192\\t\\tversions = map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*typed.Comparison{\\n    92\u2192\\t\\t\\tversion: compare.FilterFields(s.IgnoreFilter[version]),\\n    93\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    94\u2192\\t}\\n    95\u2192\\n    96\u2192\\tfor manager, managerSet := range managers {\\n    97\u2192\\t\\tif manager == workflow {\\n    98\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n    99\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   100\u2192\\t\\tcompare, ok := versions[managerSet.APIVersion()]\\n   101\u2192\\t\\tif !ok {\\n   102\u2192\\t\\t\\tvar err error\\n   103\u2192\\t\\t\\tversionedOldObject, err := s.Converter.Convert(oldObject, managerSet.APIVersion())\\n   104\u2192\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   105\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tif s.Converter.IsMissingVersionError(err) {\\n   106\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdelete(managers, manager)\\n   107\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   108\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n   109\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert old object: %v\\\", err)\\n   110\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   111\u2192\\t\\t\\tversionedNewObject, err := s.Converter.Convert(newObject, managerSet.APIVersion())\\n   112\u2192\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   113\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tif s.Converter.IsMissingVersionError(err) {\\n   114\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdelete(managers, manager)\\n   115\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   116\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n   117\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to convert new object: %v\\\", err)\\n   118\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   119\u2192\\t\\t\\tcompare, err = versionedOldObject.Compare(versionedNewObject)\\n   120\u2192\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   121\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to compare objects: %v\\\", err)\\n   122\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   123\u2192\\n   124\u2192\\t\\t\\tif s.IgnoredFields != nil {\\n   125\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tversions[managerSet.APIVersion()] = compare.ExcludeFields(s.IgnoredFields[managerSet.APIVersion()])\\n   126\u2192\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n   127\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tversions[managerSet.APIVersion()] = compare.FilterFields(s.IgnoreFilter[managerSet.APIVersion()])\\n   128\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   129\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192\\t\\tconflictSet := managerSet.Set().Intersection(compare.Modified.Union(compare.Added))\\n   132\u2192\\t\\tif !conflictSet.Empty() {\\n   133\u2192\\t\\t\\tconflicts[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(conflictSet, managerSet.APIVersion(), false)\\n   134\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   135\u2192\\n   136\u2192\\t\\tif !compare.Removed.Empty() {\\n   137\u2192\\t\\t\\tremoved[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(compare.Removed, managerSet.APIVersion(), false)\\n   138\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   139\u2192\\t}\\n   140\u2192\\n   141\u2192\\tif !force && len(conflicts) != 0 {\\n   142\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, ConflictsFromManagers(conflicts)\\n   143\u2192\\t}\\n   144\u2192\\n   145\u2192\\tfor manager, conflictSet := range conflicts {\\n   146\u2192\\t\\tmanagers[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(managers[manager].Set().Difference(conflictSet.Set()), managers[manager].APIVersion(), managers[manager].Applied())\\n   147\u2192\\t}\\n   148\u2192\\n   149\u2192\\tfor manager, removedSet := range removed {\\n   150\u2192\\t\\tmanagers[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(managers[manager].Set().Difference(removedSet.Set()), managers[manager].APIVersion(), managers[manager].Applied())\\n   151\u2192\\t}\\n   152\u2192\\n   153\u2192\\tfor manager := range managers {\\n   154\u2192\\t\\tif managers[manager].Set().Empty() {\\n   155\u2192\\t\\t\\tdelete(managers, manager)\\n   156\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   157\u2192\\t}\\n   158\u2192\\n   159\u2192\\treturn managers, compare, nil\\n   160\u2192}\\n   161\u2192\\n   162\u2192// Update is the method you should call once you've merged your final\\n   163\u2192// object on CREATE/UPDATE/PATCH verbs. newObject must be the object\\n   164\u2192// that you intend to persist (after applying the patch if this is for a\\n   165\u2192// PATCH call), and liveObject must be the original object (empty if\\n   166\u2192// this is a CREATE call).\\n   167\u2192func (s *Updater) Update(liveObject, newObject *typed.TypedValue, version fieldpath.APIVersion, managers fieldpath.ManagedFields, manager string) (*typed.TypedValue, fieldpath.ManagedFields, error) {\\n   168\u2192\\tvar err error\\n   169\u2192\\tmanagers, err = s.reconcileManagedFieldsWithSchemaChanges(liveObject, managers)\\n   170\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   171\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, err\\n   172\u2192\\t}\\n   173\u2192\\tmanagers, compare, err := s.update(liveObject, newObject, version, managers, manager, true)\\n   174\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   175\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, err\\n   176\u2192\\t}\\n   177\u2192\\tif _, ok := managers[manager]; !ok {\\n   178\u2192\\t\\tmanagers[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(fieldpath.NewSet(), version, false)\\n   179\u2192\\t}\\n   180\u2192\\tset := managers[manager].Set().Difference(compare.Removed).Union(compare.Modified).Union(compare.Added)\\n   181\u2192\\n   182\u2192\\tif s.IgnoredFields != nil && s.IgnoreFilter != nil {\\n   183\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"IgnoreFilter and IgnoreFilter may not both be set\\\")\\n   184\u2192\\t}\\n   185\u2192\\tvar ignoreFilter fieldpath.Filter\\n   186\u2192\\tif s.IgnoredFields != nil {\\n   187\u2192\\t\\tignoreFilter = fieldpath.NewExcludeSetFilter(s.IgnoredFields[version])\\n   188\u2192\\t} else {\\n   189\u2192\\t\\tignoreFilter = s.IgnoreFilter[version]\\n   190\u2192\\t}\\n   191\u2192\\tif ignoreFilter != nil {\\n   192\u2192\\t\\tset = ignoreFilter.Filter(set)\\n   193\u2192\\t}\\n   194\u2192\\n   195\u2192\\tmanagers[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(\\n   196\u2192\\t\\tset,\\n   197\u2192\\t\\tversion,\\n   198\u2192\\t\\tfalse,\\n   199\u2192\\t)\\n   200\u2192\\tif managers[manager].Set().Empty() {\\n   201\u2192\\t\\tdelete(managers, manager)\\n   202\u2192\\t}\\n   203\u2192\\treturn newObject, managers, nil\\n   204\u2192}\\n   205\u2192\\n   206\u2192// Apply should be called when Apply is run, given the current object as\\n   207\u2192// well as the configuration that is applied. This will merge the object\\n   208\u2192// and return it.\\n   209\u2192func (s *Updater) Apply(liveObject, configObject *typed.TypedValue, version fieldpath.APIVersion, managers fieldpath.ManagedFields, manager string, force bool) (*typed.TypedValue, fieldpath.ManagedFields, error) {\\n   210\u2192\\tvar err error\\n   211\u2192\\tmanagers, err = s.reconcileManagedFieldsWithSchemaChanges(liveObject, managers)\\n   212\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   213\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, err\\n   214\u2192\\t}\\n   215\u2192\\tnewObject, err := liveObject.Merge(configObject)\\n   216\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   217\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to merge config: %v\\\", err)\\n   218\u2192\\t}\\n   219\u2192\\tlastSet := managers[manager]\\n   220\u2192\\tset, err := configObject.ToFieldSet()\\n   221\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   222\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to get field set: %v\\\", err)\\n   223\u2192\\t}\\n   224\u2192\\n   225\u2192\\tif s.IgnoredFields != nil && s.IgnoreFilter != nil {\\n   226\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"IgnoreFilter and IgnoreFilter may not both be set\\\")\\n   227\u2192\\t}\\n   228\u2192\\tvar ignoreFilter fieldpath.Filter\\n   229\u2192\\tif s.IgnoredFields != nil {\\n   230\u2192\\t\\tignoreFilter = fieldpath.NewExcludeSetFilter(s.IgnoredFields[version])\\n   231\u2192\\t} else {\\n   232\u2192\\t\\tignoreFilter = s.IgnoreFilter[version]\\n   233\u2192\\t}\\n   234\u2192\\tif ignoreFilter != nil {\\n   235\u2192\\t\\tset = ignoreFilter.Filter(set)\\n   236\u2192\\t}\\n   237\u2192\\tmanagers[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(set, version, true)\\n   238\u2192\\tnewObject, err = s.prune(newObject, managers, manager, lastSet)\\n   239\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   240\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to prune fields: %v\\\", err)\\n   241\u2192\\t}\\n   242\u2192\\tmanagers, _, err = s.update(liveObject, newObject, version, managers, manager, force)\\n   243\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   244\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, err\\n   245\u2192\\t}\\n   246\u2192\\tif !s.returnInputOnNoop && value.EqualsUsing(value.NewFreelistAllocator(), liveObject.AsValue(), newObject.AsValue()) {\\n   247\u2192\\t\\tnewObject = nil\\n   248\u2192\\t}\\n   249\u2192\\treturn newObject, managers, nil\\n You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"d495a8e9-9ede-45a8-8467-63acd800d3d7\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:15.117Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"2291876e-c0ba-49c6-b736-fe9f8265405a\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d495a8e9-9ede-45a8-8467-63acd800d3d7\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_0118BgJennWHQ6kTDAhKZbmT\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Perfect! patchedObject, nil\\n}\\n\\n// patchResource divides PatchResource for easier unit testing\\nfunc (p *patcher) patchResource(ctx context.Context, scope *RequestScope) (runtime.Object, bool, error) {\\n\\tp.namespace = request.NamespaceValue(ctx)\\n\\tswitch p.patchType {\\n\\tcase types.JSONPatchType, types.MergePatchType:\\n\\t\\tp.mechanism = &jsonPatcher{\\n\\t\\t\\tpatcher:      p,\\n\\t\\t\\tfieldManager: scope.FieldManager,\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\tcase types.StrategicMergePatchType:\\n\\t\\tschemaReferenceObj, err := p.unsafeConvertor.ConvertToVersion(p.restPatcher.New(), p.kind.GroupVersion())\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, false, err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tp.mechanism = &smpPatcher{\\n\\t\\t\\tpatcher:            p,\\n\\t\\t\\tschemaReferenceObj: schemaReferenceObj,\\n\\t\\t\\tfieldManager:       scope.FieldManager,\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t// this case is unreachable if ServerSideApply is not enabled because we will have already rejected the content type\\n\\tcase types.ApplyYAMLPatchType:\\n\\t\\tp.mechanism = newApplyPatcher(p, scope.FieldManager, yaml.Unmarshal, yaml.UnmarshalStrict)\\n--\\n\\t\\tif !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.CBORServingAndStorage) {\\n\\t\\t\\tutilruntime.HandleErrorWithContext(context.TODO(), nil, \\\"CBOR apply requests should be rejected before reaching this point unless the feature gate is enabled.\\\")\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, false, fmt.Errorf(\\\"%v: unimplemented patch type\\\", p.patchType)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\t// The strict and non-strict funcs are the same here because any CBOR map with\\n\\t\\t// duplicate keys is invalid and always rejected outright regardless of strictness\\n\\t\\t// mode, and unknown field errors can't occur in practice because the type of the\\n\\t\\t// destination value for unmarshaling an apply configuration is always\\n\\t\\t// \\\"unstructured\\\".\\n\\t\\tp.mechanism = newApplyPatcher(p, scope.FieldManager, cbor.Unmarshal, cbor.Unmarshal)\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"4c8acb32-e3d9-4d47-95d4-5624c6c0fd86\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:17:17.814Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"1582506d-94a0-4562-8917-fdd67ba87fa1\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"4c8acb32-e3d9-4d47-95d4-5624c6c0fd86\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"4e92cd59-a71b-4f31-afff-2945541cc5d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ab120ab\",\"slug\":\"graceful-splashing-zephyr\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01NgVX1tMDs36Yfrnr8Euzqr\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Perfect! Now I have enough information to create a comprehensive report. Let me write my findings:\\n\\n## Comprehensive Analysis of Server-Side Apply Field Manager and Conflict Handling in Kubernetes\\n\\nBased on my thorough exploration of the Kubernetes repository at `/workspace`, here is a detailed understanding of the Server-Side Apply field manager and conflict handling system:\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. ManagedFields Storage and Structure\\n\\n### ObjectMeta Definition\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/types.go` (lines 265-276)\\n\\n```go\\ntype ObjectMeta struct {\\n    // ... other fields ...\\n    \\n    // ManagedFields maps workflow-id and version to the set of fields\\n    // that are managed by that workflow. This is mostly for internal\\n    // housekeeping, and users typically shouldn't need to set or\\n    // understand this field.\\n    ManagedFields []ManagedFieldsEntry `json:\\\"managedFields,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,17,rep,name=managedFields\\\"`\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### ManagedFieldsEntry Type Definition\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/types.go` (lines 1340-1377)\\n\\n```go\\ntype ManagedFieldsEntry struct {\\n    // Manager is an identifier of the workflow managing these fields.\\n    Manager string `json:\\\"manager,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=manager\\\"`\\n    \\n    // Operation is the type of operation which lead to this ManagedFieldsEntry being created.\\n    // The only valid values for this field are 'Apply' and 'Update'.\\n    Operation ManagedFieldsOperationType `json:\\\"operation,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=operation,casttype=ManagedFieldsOperationType\\\"`\\n    \\n    // APIVersion defines the version of this resource that this field set\\n    // applies to. The format is \\\"group/version\\\" just like the top-level APIVersion field.\\n    APIVersion string `json:\\\"apiVersion,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=apiVersion\\\"`\\n    \\n    // Time is the timestamp of when the ManagedFields entry was added.\\n    Time *Time `json:\\\"time,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=time\\\"`\\n    \\n    // FieldsType is the discriminator for the different fields format and version.\\n    // There is currently only one possible value: \\\"FieldsV1\\\"\\n    FieldsType string `json:\\\"fieldsType,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=fieldsType\\\"`\\n    \\n    // FieldsV1 holds the first JSON version format as described in the \\\"FieldsV1\\\" type.\\n    FieldsV1 *FieldsV1 `json:\\\"fieldsV1,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,7,opt,name=fieldsV1\\\"`\\n    \\n    // Subresource is the name of the subresource used to update that object, or\\n    // empty string if the object was updated through the main resource.\\n    Subresource string `json:\\\"subresource,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,8,opt,name=subresource\\\"`\\n}\\n\\ntype FieldsV1 struct {\\n    // Raw is the underlying serialization of this object.\\n    Raw []byte `json:\\\"-\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=Raw\\\"`\\n}\\n\\ntype ManagedFieldsOperationType string\\n\\nconst (\\n    ManagedFieldsOperationApply  ManagedFieldsOperationType = \\\"Apply\\\"\\n    ManagedFieldsOperationUpdate ManagedFieldsOperationType = \\\"Update\\\"\\n)\\n```\\n\\n**Key Insight**: Each manager (e.g., \\\"kubectl\\\", a controller, \\\"kube-scheduler\\\") maintains its own ManagedFieldsEntry that tracks:\\n- The set of fields it owns (stored as FieldsV1, a compressed trie-based representation)\\n- When it last modified those fields (Time)\\n- The API version it was operating at (APIVersion)\\n- The operation type (Apply or Update)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. The FieldManager Architecture\\n\\n### Public Interface\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/fieldmanager.go` (lines 34-42)\\n\\n```go\\n// FieldManager updates the managed fields and merges applied configurations.\\ntype FieldManager = internal.FieldManager\\n\\nfunc NewDefaultFieldManager(\\n    typeConverter TypeConverter,\\n    objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor,\\n    objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter,\\n    objectCreater runtime.ObjectCreater,\\n    kind schema.GroupVersionKind,\\n    hub schema.GroupVersion,\\n    subresource string,\\n    resetFields map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter,\\n) (*FieldManager, error)\\n```\\n\\n### Internal Manager Interface\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/manager.go` (lines 34-52)\\n\\n```go\\ntype Manager interface {\\n    // Update is used when the object has already been merged (non-apply use-case)\\n    // Returns the new object with managedFields removed, and the object's new\\n    // proposed managedFields separately.\\n    Update(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, manager string) (runtime.Object, Managed, error)\\n\\n    // Apply is used when server-side apply is called, as it merges the\\n    // object and updates the managed fields.\\n    Apply(liveObj, appliedObj runtime.Object, managed Managed, fieldManager string, force bool) (runtime.Object, Managed, error)\\n}\\n\\ntype Managed interface {\\n    Fields() fieldpath.ManagedFields\\n    Times() map[string]*metav1.Time\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Default FieldManager Implementation\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/fieldmanager.go` (lines 45-74)\\n\\nThe default FieldManager wraps a chain of managers (decorator pattern):\\n\\n```go\\nreturn NewFieldManager(\\n    NewVersionCheckManager(\\n        NewLastAppliedUpdater(\\n            NewLastAppliedManager(\\n                NewProbabilisticSkipNonAppliedManager(\\n                    NewCapManagersManager(\\n                        NewBuildManagerInfoManager(\\n                            NewManagedFieldsUpdater(\\n                                NewStripMetaManager(f),  // Core: structuredMergeManager\\n                            ), kind.GroupVersion(), subresource,\\n                        ), DefaultMaxUpdateManagers,  // Cap at 10 managers\\n                    ), objectCreater, DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability,\\n                ), typeConverter, objectConverter, kind.GroupVersion(),\\n            ),\\n        ), kind,\\n    ), subresource,\\n)\\n```\\n\\n**Each layer provides specific functionality:**\\n1. **StripMetaManager**: Removes metadata fields from managedFields tracking\\n2. **ManagedFieldsUpdater**: Updates the managedFields in the object\\n3. **BuildManagerInfoManager**: Adds manager metadata (APIVersion, subresource)\\n4. **CapManagersManager**: Limits managers to 10, merges oldest when exceeded\\n5. **SkipNonAppliedManager**: Probabilistically tracks from creation\\n6. **LastAppliedManager**: Migrates from client-side apply (kubectl annotation)\\n7. **LastAppliedUpdater**: Maintains last-applied-configuration annotation\\n8. **VersionCheckManager**: Validates API version consistency\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. Core Structured Merge Manager\\n\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/structuredmerge.go` (lines 121-182)\\n\\n### Apply Method Signature\\n```go\\nfunc (f *structuredMergeManager) Apply(\\n    liveObj, patchObj runtime.Object,\\n    managed Managed,\\n    manager string,\\n    force bool,\\n) (runtime.Object, Managed, error) {\\n    // Check version consistency\\n    if patchVersion := patchObj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind().GroupVersion(); \\n        patchVersion != f.groupVersion {\\n        return nil, nil, errors.NewBadRequest(...)\\n    }\\n    \\n    // Ensure managedFields is nil in the patch\\n    if patchObjMeta.GetManagedFields() != nil {\\n        return nil, nil, errors.NewBadRequest(\\\"metadata.managedFields must be nil\\\")\\n    }\\n    \\n    // Convert objects to typed representation for SMD library\\n    liveObjTyped, _ := f.typeConverter.ObjectToTyped(liveObjVersioned, typed.AllowDuplicates)\\n    patchObjTyped, _ := f.typeConverter.ObjectToTyped(patchObj)  // No duplicates\\n    \\n    // Delegate to structured-merge-diff library\\n    newObjTyped, managedFields, err := f.updater.Apply(\\n        liveObjTyped, patchObjTyped, apiVersion,\\n        managed.Fields(), manager, force,\\n    )\\n    if err != nil {\\n        return nil, nil, err\\n    }\\n    \\n    // Convert back to runtime object\\n    return newObjUnversioned, managed, nil\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Update Method Signature\\n```go\\nfunc (f *structuredMergeManager) Update(\\n    liveObj, newObj runtime.Object,\\n    managed Managed,\\n    manager string,\\n) (runtime.Object, Managed, error) {\\n    // For non-apply (regular Update), force=true means conflicts don't prevent update\\n    _, managedFields, err := f.updater.Update(\\n        liveObjTyped, newObjTyped, apiVersion,\\n        managed.Fields(), manager,\\n    )\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. Conflict Detection Algorithm\\n\\n### Location\\nFile: `/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge/update.go` (lines 73-159)\\n\\n### How Conflicts Are Detected\\n\\n```go\\nfunc (s *Updater) update(\\n    oldObject, newObject *typed.TypedValue,\\n    version fieldpath.APIVersion,\\n    managers fieldpath.ManagedFields,\\n    workflow string,  // The manager applying/updating\\n    force bool,\\n) (fieldpath.ManagedFields, *typed.Comparison, error) {\\n    \\n    // Step 1: Compare old and new object\\n    compare, _ := oldObject.Compare(newObject)\\n    \\n    // Step 2: For EACH other manager (not the applying manager)\\n    for manager, managerSet := range managers {\\n        if manager == workflow {  // Skip the applying manager\\n            continue\\n        }\\n        \\n        // Step 3: Determine if this manager's fields intersect with modified/added fields\\n        conflictSet := managerSet.Set().Intersection(\\n            compare.Modified.Union(compare.Added),\\n        )\\n        \\n        // Step 4: Record conflict if there's overlap\\n        if !conflictSet.Empty() {\\n            conflicts[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(\\n                conflictSet, managerSet.APIVersion(), false,\\n            )\\n        }\\n    }\\n    \\n    // Step 5: CRITICAL - Return ConflictError unless force=true\\n    if !force && len(conflicts) != 0 {\\n        return nil, nil, ConflictsFromManagers(conflicts)\\n    }\\n    \\n    // Step 6: If force=true, remove conflicting fields from OTHER managers\\n    for manager, conflictSet := range conflicts {\\n        managers[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(\\n            managers[manager].Set().Difference(conflictSet.Set()),\\n            managers[manager].APIVersion(),\\n            managers[manager].Applied(),\\n        )\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Conflict Definition**: A conflict occurs when:\\n- Another manager owns field(s) that the current manager is trying to modify or add\\n- Ownership is tracked in managedFields for each manager\\n- The intersection of [another manager's fields] \u2229 [newly modified/added fields] is non-empty\\n\\n### Error Representation\\nFile: `/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge/conflict.go` (lines 27-49)\\n\\n```go\\ntype Conflict struct {\\n    Manager string\\n    Path    fieldpath.Path\\n}\\n\\ntype Conflicts []Conflict\\n\\nfunc (conflicts Conflicts) Error() string {\\n    // Aggregates and formats conflicts by manager\\n    // Example: \\\"conflicts with \\\"other-manager\\\": spec.replicas\\\"\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Conflict Error Conversion\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/conflict.go` (lines 32-43)\\n\\n```go\\nfunc NewConflictError(conflicts merge.Conflicts) *errors.StatusError {\\n    causes := []metav1.StatusCause{}\\n    for _, conflict := range conflicts {\\n        causes = append(causes, metav1.StatusCause{\\n            Type:    metav1.CauseTypeFieldManagerConflict,\\n            Message: fmt.Sprintf(\\\"conflict with %v\\\", printManager(conflict.Manager)),\\n            Field:   conflict.Path.String(),\\n        })\\n    }\\n    return errors.NewApplyConflict(causes, getConflictMessage(conflicts))\\n}\\n```\\n\\nReturns HTTP 409 Conflict with detailed field-level conflict information.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. The \\\"Force\\\" Flag Behavior\\n\\n### PatchOptions Definition\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/types.go` (lines 624-670)\\n\\n```go\\ntype PatchOptions struct {\\n    // Force is going to \\\"force\\\" Apply requests. It means user will\\n    // re-acquire conflicting fields owned by other people. Force\\n    // flag must be unset for non-apply patch requests.\\n    Force *bool `json:\\\"force,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,2,opt,name=force\\\"`\\n    \\n    // FieldManager is required for apply requests\\n    FieldManager string `json:\\\"fieldManager,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,name=fieldManager\\\"`\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Force Behavior in Apply\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/structuredmerge.go` (line 156)\\n\\n```go\\nnewObjTyped, managedFields, err := f.updater.Apply(\\n    liveObjTyped, patchObjTyped, apiVersion,\\n    managed.Fields(), manager, force,\\n)\\n```\\n\\n### Structured-Merge-Diff Library Apply Logic\\nFile: `/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge/update.go` (lines 209-249)\\n\\n```go\\nfunc (s *Updater) Apply(\\n    liveObject, configObject *typed.TypedValue,\\n    version fieldpath.APIVersion,\\n    managers fieldpath.ManagedFields,\\n    manager string,\\n    force bool,  // <-- KEY PARAMETER\\n) (*typed.TypedValue, fieldpath.ManagedFields, error) {\\n    \\n    // Step 1: Merge config into live object\\n    newObject, _ := liveObject.Merge(configObject)\\n    \\n    // Step 2: Extract fields from the configuration\\n    set, _ := configObject.ToFieldSet()\\n    \\n    // Step 3: Update managers to mark these fields as owned by current manager\\n    managers[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(set, version, true)\\n    \\n    // Step 4: Prune fields that were removed from config\\n    newObject, _ = s.prune(newObject, managers, manager, lastSet)\\n    \\n    // Step 5: Check for conflicts with OTHER managers\\n    // This calls s.update() with force parameter\\n    managers, _, err := s.update(liveObject, newObject, version, managers, manager, force)\\n    \\n    if err != nil {\\n        return nil, fieldpath.ManagedFields{}, err\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Force Flag Semantics**:\\n- **force=false**: Return conflict error if any other manager owns the fields being modified\\n- **force=true**: Override other managers' ownership and take ownership of the fields (lines 145-147 in update.go)\\n\\n```go\\n// If force=true, steal ownership from other managers\\nfor manager, conflictSet := range conflicts {\\n    managers[manager] = fieldpath.NewVersionedSet(\\n        managers[manager].Set().Difference(conflictSet.Set()),  // Remove from other manager\\n        managers[manager].APIVersion(),\\n        managers[manager].Applied(),\\n    )\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. Structured-Merge-Diff Library Integration\\n\\n### Where SMD is Used\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/structuredmerge.go` (lines 32-59)\\n\\n```go\\ntype structuredMergeManager struct {\\n    typeConverter   TypeConverter\\n    objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor\\n    objectDefaulter runtime.ObjectDefaulter\\n    groupVersion    schema.GroupVersion\\n    hubVersion      schema.GroupVersion\\n    updater         merge.Updater  // <-- From sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge\\n}\\n\\nfunc NewStructuredMergeManager(...) (Manager, error) {\\n    return &structuredMergeManager{\\n        // ...\\n        updater: merge.Updater{\\n            Converter:    newVersionConverter(typeConverter, objectConverter, hub),\\n            IgnoreFilter: resetFields,\\n        },\\n    }, nil\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Key SMD Types Used\\n- **fieldpath.ManagedFields**: Map of manager name to VersionedSet of fields\\n- **fieldpath.APIVersion**: Version at which fields were tracked\\n- **typed.TypedValue**: Schema-aware representation of objects\\n- **fieldpath.Set**: Set of field paths in trie representation\\n- **merge.Updater**: The core merge engine\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 7. Comparison: Apply vs Update Semantics\\n\\nFile: `/workspace/vendor/sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge/update.go`\\n\\n### Update (Traditional PUT/PATCH)\\n```\\nUpdate(liveObject, newObject, managers, manager):\\n  1. Compare old vs new\\n  2. Check if new fields conflict with other managers\\n  3. force=true (no conflicts can occur)\\n  4. Track ALL modified/added fields as owned by manager\\n  5. Untrack removed fields\\n  \u2192 Always succeeds (overwrites other managers)\\n```\\n\\n### Apply (Server-Side Apply PATCH)\\n```\\nApply(liveObject, configObject, managers, manager, force):\\n  1. Merge configObject into liveObject\\n  2. Mark fields from configObject as owned by manager\\n  3. Prune fields removed from configObject\\n  4. Check for conflicts with other managers:\\n     - If force=false: \u2717 Return 409 Conflict\\n     - If force=true: Steal ownership from other managers\\n  5. Return merged object with updated managedFields\\n```\\n\\n**Key Differences**:\\n- Apply is **declarative** (fields in config = fields owned)\\n- Update is **imperative** (captures ALL changes made)\\n- Apply respects other managers by default (force controls this)\\n- Update always takes ownership (force=true by default)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 8. Manager Identity and FieldManager Name\\n\\n### Extraction from Request\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go` (lines 118-128)\\n\\n```go\\noptions := &metav1.PatchOptions{}\\nif err := metainternalversionscheme.ParameterCodec.DecodeParameters(\\n    req.URL.Query(), scope.MetaGroupVersion, options,\\n); err != nil {\\n    // ...\\n}\\n\\n// options.FieldManager comes from query parameter or body\\n// It must be < 128 characters and contain only printable characters\\n```\\n\\n### Usage in Apply\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/fieldmanager.go` (line 196)\\n\\n```go\\nobject, managed, err = f.fieldManager.Apply(\\n    liveObj, appliedObj, managed,\\n    manager,  // From options.FieldManager\\n    force,\\n)\\n```\\n\\n### Manager Identification\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/buildmanagerinfo.go`\\n\\nThe manager string is stored as a JSON-encoded ManagedFieldsEntry for later identification:\\n\\n```json\\n{\\n  \\\"manager\\\": \\\"kubectl\\\",\\n  \\\"operation\\\": \\\"Apply\\\",\\n  \\\"apiVersion\\\": \\\"v1\\\",\\n  \\\"subresource\\\": \\\"\\\"\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 9. Complete Apply Request Flow\\n\\n### HTTP Handler\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go` (lines 68-263)\\n\\n```\\nPatchResource(request):\\n  1. Extract content type \u2192 patchType (ApplyYAMLPatchType, etc.)\\n  2. Validate patchType is supported\\n  3. Decode PatchOptions from query/body\\n  4. Create applyPatcher mechanism\\n  5. Call patcher.patchResource()\\n```\\n\\n### Patch Processing\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go` (lines 653-738)\\n\\n```\\npatchResource(ctx, scope):\\n  1. Based on patchType, select mechanism:\\n     - ApplyYAMLPatchType \u2192 newApplyPatcher(yaml.Unmarshal)\\n     - ApplyCBORPatchType \u2192 newApplyPatcher(cbor.Unmarshal)\\n     - etc.\\n  \\n  2. Create transformers:\\n     - p.applyPatch: Apply patch \u2192 patchedObj\\n     - p.applyAdmission: Run admission controllers\\n     - dedupOwnerReferences: Clean up references\\n  \\n  3. Call restPatcher.Update() with DefaultUpdatedObjectInfo\\n     This performs optimistic concurrency control with retries\\n```\\n\\n### Apply Patch Mechanism\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go` (lines 500-530)\\n\\n```go\\nfunc (p *applyPatcher) applyPatchToCurrentObject(ctx, obj) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n    force := false\\n    if p.options.Force != nil {\\n        force = *p.options.Force\\n    }\\n    \\n    patchObj := &unstructured.Unstructured{Object: map[string]interface{}{}}\\n    if err := p.unmarshalFn(p.patch, &patchObj.Object); err != nil {\\n        return nil, errors.NewBadRequest(...)\\n    }\\n    \\n    // Core call to FieldManager.Apply()\\n    obj, err := p.fieldManager.Apply(\\n        obj,                          // liveObj\\n        patchObj,                     // appliedObj (config from YAML/CBOR)\\n        p.options.FieldManager,       // manager identity\\n        force,                        // force flag\\n    )\\n    if err != nil {\\n        return obj, err\\n    }\\n    \\n    // Handle field validation\\n    return obj, nil\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### FieldManager.Apply()\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/fieldmanager.go` (lines 183-209)\\n\\n```go\\nfunc (f *FieldManager) Apply(\\n    liveObj, appliedObj runtime.Object,\\n    manager string,\\n    force bool,\\n) (object runtime.Object, err error) {\\n    \\n    // Get accessor and decode managedFields\\n    accessor, _ := meta.Accessor(liveObj)\\n    managed, _ := DecodeManagedFields(accessor.GetManagedFields())\\n    \\n    // Delegate to underlying manager (through decorator chain)\\n    object, managed, err = f.fieldManager.Apply(\\n        liveObj, appliedObj, managed, manager, force,\\n    )\\n    if err != nil {\\n        if conflicts, ok := err.(merge.Conflicts); ok {\\n            return nil, NewConflictError(conflicts)  // 409 Conflict\\n        }\\n        return nil, err\\n    }\\n    \\n    // Encode updated managedFields back to object\\n    if err = EncodeObjectManagedFields(object, managed); err != nil {\\n        return nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to encode managed fields: %v\\\", err)\\n    }\\n    \\n    return object, nil\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Storage\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go` (line 704)\\n\\n```go\\n// restPatcher.Update() performs GuaranteedUpdate with optimistic concurrency\\nupdateObject, created, updateErr := p.restPatcher.Update(\\n    ctx, p.name, p.updatedObjectInfo,\\n    p.createValidation, p.updateValidation,\\n    p.forceAllowCreate, options,\\n)\\n```\\n\\nGuaranteedUpdate retries on conflict (resourceVersion mismatch) up to the retry count.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 10. Admission Controller Validation\\n\\nFile: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/fieldmanager/admission.go`\\n\\n```go\\n// NewManagedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController wraps admission\\n// controllers and validates managedFields remain valid after mutation\\nfunc NewManagedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController(wrap admission.Interface) admission.Interface {\\n    return &managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController{wrap: wrap}\\n}\\n\\n// Admit validates managedFields after admission controller mutations\\nfunc (admit *managedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController) Admit(ctx context.Context, a admission.Attributes, o admission.ObjectInterfaces) error {\\n    managedFieldsBeforeAdmission := objectMeta.GetManagedFields()\\n    \\n    if err := mutationInterface.Admit(ctx, a, o); err != nil {\\n        return err\\n    }\\n    \\n    managedFieldsAfterAdmission := objectMeta.GetManagedFields()\\n    \\n    // Validate managedFields still valid after mutation\\n    if err := managedfields.ValidateManagedFields(managedFieldsAfterAdmission); err != nil {\\n        // Restore original managedFields\\n        objectMeta.SetManagedFields(managedFieldsBeforeAdmission)\\n        warning.AddWarning(ctx, \\\"...\\\", fmt.Sprintf(InvalidManagedFieldsAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat, err.Error()))\\n    }\\n    return nil\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThis prevents admission controllers from corrupting managedFields.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Summary Table\\n\\n| Aspect | Details |\\n|--------|---------|\\n| **Storage** | ObjectMeta.ManagedFields: []ManagedFieldsEntry |\\n| **Entry Structure** | Manager (name), Operation (Apply/Update), APIVersion, Time, FieldsV1 (trie) |\\n| **Field Representation** | sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff fieldpath.Set (trie structure) |\\n| **Conflict Detection** | `intersection(other_manager_fields, newly_modified_fields) != empty` |\\n| **Force Behavior** | true = steal ownership; false = reject with 409 Conflict |\\n| **Apply vs Update** | Apply = declarative + respects other managers; Update = imperative + overwrites |\\n| **Manager Identity** | String from FieldManager query parameter or body |\\n| **HTTP Endpoint** | PATCH with Content-Type: application/apply+yaml or application/apply+cbor |\\n| **Error Code** | HTTP 409 Conflict with CauseTypeFieldManagerConflict details |\\n| **Library** | sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6 for merge algorithms |\\n| **Decorator Chain** | 9 layers: StripMeta \u2192 ManagedFieldsUpdater \u2192 BuildManagerInfo \u2192 CapManagers \u2192 etc. |\\n\\nThis architecture ensures safe, concurrent multi-manager object manipulation with clear ownership semantics and conflict resolution.\"}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":5,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":721,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":90155,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":721},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG58fbGRq8EtVeppW74Q\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"a7da62da-34d2-4bd8-9aa3-5675869e29b1\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:18:12.494Z\"}\n",
    "status": "completed",
    "task_id": "ab120ab",
    "task_type": "local_agent"
  }
}

  "file": {
    "content": "/*\nCopyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage internal\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"reflect\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/merge\"\n)\n\n// DefaultMaxUpdateManagers defines the default maximum retained number of managedFields entries from updates\n// if the number of update managers exceeds this, the oldest entries will be merged until the number is below the maximum.\n// TODO(jennybuckley): Determine if this is really the best value. Ideally we wouldn't unnecessarily merge too many entries.\nconst DefaultMaxUpdateManagers int = 10\n\n// DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability defines the default probability that the field management of an object\n// starts being tracked from the object's creation, instead of from the first time the object is applied to.\nconst DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability float32 = 1\n\nvar atMostEverySecond = NewAtMostEvery(time.Second)\n\n// FieldManager updates the managed fields and merges applied\n// configurations.\ntype FieldManager struct {\n\tfieldManager Manager\n\tsubresource  string\n}\n\n// NewFieldManager creates a new FieldManager that decodes, manages, then re-encodes managedFields\n// on update and apply requests.\nfunc NewFieldManager(f Manager, subresource string) *FieldManager {\n\treturn &FieldManager{fieldManager: f, subresource: subresource}\n}\n\n// newDefaultFieldManager is a helper function which wraps a Manager with certain default logic.\nfunc NewDefaultFieldManager(f Manager, typeConverter TypeConverter, objectConverter runtime.ObjectConvertor, objectCreater runtime.ObjectCreater, kind schema.GroupVersionKind, subresource string) *FieldManager {\n\treturn NewFieldManager(\n\t\tNewVersionCheckManager(\n\t\t\tNewLastAppliedUpdater(\n\t\t\t\tNewLastAppliedManager(\n\t\t\t\t\tNewProbabilisticSkipNonAppliedManager(\n\t\t\t\t\t\tNewCapManagersManager(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tNewBuildManagerInfoManager(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tNewManagedFieldsUpdater(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tNewStripMetaManager(f),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t), kind.GroupVersion(), subresource,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t), DefaultMaxUpdateManagers,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t), objectCreater, DefaultTrackOnCreateProbability,\n\t\t\t\t\t), typeConverter, objectConverter, kind.GroupVersion(),\n\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t), kind,\n\t\t), subresource,\n\t)\n}\n\nfunc decodeLiveOrNew(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, ignoreManagedFieldsFromRequestObject bool) (Managed, error) {\n\tliveAccessor, err := meta.Accessor(liveObj)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\t// We take the managedFields of the live object in case the request tries to\n\t// manually set managedFields via a subresource.\n\tif ignoreManagedFieldsFromRequestObject {\n\t\treturn emptyManagedFieldsOnErr(DecodeManagedFields(liveAccessor.GetManagedFields()))\n\t}\n\n\t// If the object doesn't have metadata, we should just return without trying to\n\t// set the managedFields at all, so creates/updates/patches will work normally.\n\tnewAccessor, err := meta.Accessor(newObj)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tif isResetManagedFields(newAccessor.GetManagedFields()) {\n\t\treturn NewEmptyManaged(), nil\n\t}\n\n\t// If the managed field is empty or we failed to decode it,\n\t// let's try the live object. This is to prevent clients who\n\t// don't understand managedFields from deleting it accidentally.\n\tmanaged, err := DecodeManagedFields(newAccessor.GetManagedFields())\n\tif err != nil || len(managed.Fields()) == 0 {\n\t\treturn emptyManagedFieldsOnErr(DecodeManagedFields(liveAccessor.GetManagedFields()))\n\t}\n\treturn managed, nil\n}\n\nfunc emptyManagedFieldsOnErr(managed Managed, err error) (Managed, error) {\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn NewEmptyManaged(), nil\n\t}\n\treturn managed, nil\n}\n\n// Update is used when the object has already been merged (non-apply\n// use-case), and simply updates the managed fields in the output\n// object.\nfunc (f *FieldManager) Update(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, manager string) (object runtime.Object, err error) {\n\t// First try to decode the managed fields provided in the update,\n\t// This is necessary to allow directly updating managed fields.\n\tisSubresource := f.subresource != \"\"\n\tmanaged, err := decodeLiveOrNew(liveObj, newObj, isSubresource)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn newObj, nil\n\t}\n\n\tRemoveObjectManagedFields(newObj)\n\n\tif object, managed, err = f.fieldManager.Update(liveObj, newObj, managed, manager); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tif err = EncodeObjectManagedFields(object, managed); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode managed fields: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn object, nil\n}\n\n// UpdateNoErrors is the same as Update, but it will not return\n// errors. If an error happens, the object is returned with\n// managedFields cleared.\nfunc (f *FieldManager) UpdateNoErrors(liveObj, newObj runtime.Object, manager string) runtime.Object {\n\tobj, err := f.Update(liveObj, newObj, manager)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tatMostEverySecond.Do(func() {\n\t\t\tns, name := \"unknown\", \"unknown\"\n\t\t\tif accessor, err := meta.Accessor(newObj); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\tns = accessor.GetNamespace()\n\t\t\t\tname = accessor.GetName()\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tklog.ErrorS(err, \"[SHOULD NOT HAPPEN] failed to update managedFields\", \"versionKind\",\n\t\t\t\tnewObj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind(), \"namespace\", ns, \"name\", name)\n\t\t})\n\t\t// Explicitly remove managedFields on failure, so that\n\t\t// we can't have garbage in it.\n\t\tRemoveObjectManagedFields(newObj)\n\t\treturn newObj\n\t}\n\treturn obj\n}\n\n// Returns true if the managedFields indicate that the user is trying to\n// reset the managedFields, i.e. if the list is non-nil but empty, or if\n// the list has one empty item.\nfunc isResetManagedFields(managedFields []metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry) bool {\n\tif len(managedFields) == 0 {\n\t\treturn managedFields != nil\n\t}\n\n\tif len(managedFields) == 1 {\n\t\treturn reflect.DeepEqual(managedFields[0], metav1.ManagedFieldsEntry{})\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// Apply is used when server-side apply is called, as it merges the\n// object and updates the managed fields.\nfunc (f *FieldManager) Apply(liveObj, appliedObj runtime.Object, manager string, force bool) (object runtime.Object, err error) {\n\t// If the object doesn't have metadata, apply isn't allowed.\n\taccessor, err := meta.Accessor(liveObj)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"couldn't get accessor: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Decode the managed fields in the live object, since it isn't allowed in the patch.\n\tmanaged, err := DecodeManagedFields(accessor.GetManagedFields())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to decode managed fields: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tobject, managed, err = f.fieldManager.Apply(liveObj, appliedObj, managed, manager, force)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tif conflicts, ok := err.(merge.Conflicts); ok {\n\t\t\treturn nil, NewConflictError(conflicts)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tif err = EncodeObjectManagedFields(object, managed); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode managed fields: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn object, nil\n}\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields/internal/fieldmanager.go",

  },
